The moments you left the phone plugged in another room always were more memorable than when you kept the screen close to your face making everything feel as nebulous as it does. Those moments are prized for your attention. You argued whether one had to lie to dream, the tensions between charm & narcissism, & the existence of Time with everyone younger than you. Your niece, son, & daughter: for Thanksgiving: remember what it felt like having them in the house when the bombs are ubiquitous. Some moments were propelled by fantastic performances by the offspring of others. Sometimes a moment suddenly put you directly inside another moment that cut back & forth across multiple moments & you got lost trying to reconnect with twentysomethings whose childhoods were set around you. At moments like that, it’s important to know you’re not alone. Your top ten good moments of the year include seeing “Sinners” once with your son & once with your daughter. The central conflict was between creation & consumption on & off our screens. Real people were real characters. This year gave you new perspectives on parenting. Some moments held the best physical comedy in years. The less inventive through lines & missteps had everything to do with gravity. You defined space in terms of boundaries when space operates outside boundaries. You defined Time by beginnings & endings when Time has no beginning & ending. Remember you only have to be good & clear & kind.

articles.title_en: Letters from Our Readers
articles.rubric_en: At a Price

paragraphs.en: Readers respond to Melanie Thernstrom’s piece about a concierge women’s-health doctor, Burkhard Bilger’s piece about the lone-star tick, and Adeline Goss’s piece about the potential connections between frontotemporal dementia and criminality.

It was hard for me to read Melanie Thernstrom’s piece about fabulously wealthy women spending thousands of dollars on a gynecologist who coaches them on optimizing their orgasms, because it arrived the same week that my town’s small community hospital, in southeastern Vermont, announced that it anticipates having to close its birthing center this year (“Billionaires’ Vagina Club,” July 6th & 13th). If that comes to pass, then many women will have to travel out of state, often across long distances, for obstetric care. This week, I also read that the average annual compensation for a C.E.O. of a major national health-insurance company is more than twenty million dollars. I also encountered a poll, no doubt instituted to mark our nation’s two-hundred-and-fiftieth birthday, asking: Are you proud to be an American? I’m thinking about how proud I would be if our country made serious strides to eliminate this level of economic injustice.
Karen Tyler Brattleboro, Vt.

Burkhard Bilger’s piece about how lone-star ticks are spreading alpha-gal syndrome, a condition that causes meat allergies, is terrifying and informative (“The Great Infestation,” July 6th & 13th). What’s troubling to me, besides the obvious, is the insistence on finding a way to continue to eat animals if your body starts to reject them. “Vegan” may be a curse word to some, but there’s never been a better, and easier, time to be one than now. Brands like Beyond and Impossible make delicious burger patties without meat. Of course, there are critics who say that these products don’t pass their purity tests, but alternative meats have the advantage of not requiring factory farming or breaking you out in a rash. Another plus is that you don’t have to raise cows or pigs only to kill them, just because you prefer the taste of red meat.
Robert Howard Madisonville, Ky.

Bilger’s article struck a chord with me. Fifty years ago, when I was thirty, I passed out with a swollen face and hives a few hours after eating prime rib at a celebratory dinner. My family doctor, in Tennessee, thought I had an allergic reaction, but he couldn’t tell what had triggered it, and implored me to see a specialist. All the allergy tests doctors later performed came back negative. It was only after I started eliminating foods that I realized my reactions were caused by red meat.

Many years later, I was diagnosed with alpha-gal syndrome. My journey with this condition has entailed visiting the emergency room three times, always carrying an EpiPen, travelling with a card that describes my allergy in several languages, and constantly alerting restaurant chefs and staff that I can have no food that might contain meat by-products or be contaminated by them. Last year, after a lovely dinner in San Francisco that I thought was safe, I was in an emergency room explaining alpha-gal to doctors who had never even heard of it.

I am waiting for the day when there might be a preventive remedy. In the meantime, I appreciate the exposure The New Yorker has given to this unusual allergy, which is prevalent in the South and now moving across the country.
Sarah Hunt Nashville, Tenn.

I was troubled by Adeline Goss’s recent article about Ray Howell, a doctor whose criminal actions may have been caused by frontotemporal dementia (Annals of Science, newyorker.com/goss-on-ray-howell, June 11th). Howell’s F.T.D. was linked to a genetic mutation known as a C9orf72 (C9) hexanucleotide repeat expansion. I write from a place of concern for the well-being of those living with this mutation, as I fear that many readers will be left with the impression that there is strong evidence that having it is definitively and consistently associated with profoundly negative characteristics, including criminality. As a statistician who has partnered with A.L.S./F.T.D. advocacy groups and researchers to interrogate the published literature on the genetically at-risk community, I believe that the evidence does not support this impression.

Goss cites a study that found half of F.T.D. patients who were C9-mutation carriers had fixed behavioral patterns, like rigid meal schedules and excessive exercise routines. Research indicates that the majority of mutation carriers will never develop F.T.D., however, so it is unclear how these findings would apply to the broader C9-carrier population. Furthermore, this study should not be used to draw conclusions because its sample size for carriers was very small: only twenty. The authors also describe exercising more than four times a week as excessive. Surely most people would agree that four times a week is not necessarily excessive. What’s more, the authors do not present any formal analysis for the observation, which is based on relatives’ accounts, that the study’s subjects became (as Goss quotes) “bad tempered” if they couldn’t exercise. Finally, the paper acknowledges that the study’s interviewer was not blind to the C9 status of its participants and went in with a specific hypothesis—circumstances that risk bias.

More broadly, it’s also worth remembering how far Dr. Howell’s actions are from the norm: even among C9 carriers who do develop F.T.D., serious crimes of this kind are rare. We should be careful how we communicate the implications of C9 mutations so as not to stigmatize carriers, or discourage them from participating in research or getting clinical care. C9 carriers deserve to be treated with respect—and to know that a sequence in their DNA does not define who they are.
Daniel Brickman San Francisco, Calif.

Letters should be sent with the writer’s name, address, and daytime phone number via e-mail to themail@newyorker.com. Letters may be edited for length and clarity, and may be published in any medium. We regret that owing to the volume of correspondence we cannot reply to every letter.

articles.title_en: Politics
articles.title_zh: 政治

paragraphs.en: The ceasefire between America and Iran all but collapsed, after Donald Trump reinstated a blockade of Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and ordered fresh strikes to degrade Iran’s ability to attack commercial shipping. Iran continued to target countries in the region, including Kuwait and Jordan, and struck two United Arab Emirates’ oil tankers with cruise missiles. America hit a tanker heading towards Kharg island, Iran’s oil-export hub. Mr Trump raised, and then quickly backed down from, the possibility of America charging a 20% fee on ships that traverse the strait. Oil prices jumped.
paragraphs.zh: 美国与伊朗之间的停火几乎宣告破裂。此前，唐纳德·特朗普恢复了对霍尔木兹海峡伊朗船只的封锁，并下令发动新一轮打击，以削弱伊朗攻击商业航运的能力。伊朗继续把该地区国家作为目标，包括科威特和约旦，并用巡航导弹击中了两艘阿拉伯联合酋长国的油轮。美国袭击了一艘驶向哈尔克岛的油轮，哈尔克岛是伊朗的石油出口枢纽。特朗普曾提出美国可能对通过该海峡的船只收取20%的费用，但随后很快退让。油价大涨。

paragraphs.en: Iraq’s new prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi, visited the White House. Mr Trump was effusive in his praise, describing Mr al-Zaidi as a “great leader”. The president had in effect blocked the return of Nouri al-Maliki as prime minister, viewing him as too close to Iran. Iraq’s new leader is under intense pressure to disarm Iranian-aligned militias that have launched attacks on American facilities.
paragraphs.zh: 伊拉克新任总理阿里·扎伊迪访问了白宫。特朗普对他大加赞赏，称扎伊迪是“伟大的领导人”。总统实际上阻止了努里·马利基重新出任总理，因为他认为马利基与伊朗走得太近。伊拉克新领导人正面临巨大压力，要求他解除亲伊朗民兵组织的武装；这些民兵曾对美国设施发动袭击。

paragraphs.en: Israel’s general election is to be held on October 27th. Binyamin Netanyahu’s government will have served a full term when the Knesset is dissolved on July 17th.
paragraphs.zh: 以色列大选将于10月27日举行。当以色列议会于7月17日解散时，贝尼亚明·内塔尼亚胡政府将已完成一个完整任期。

paragraphs.en: Oxford University launched the first trial of a vaccine against the strain of Ebola that has broken out in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Nearly 800 people have died and over 2,000 are infected. Scientists think that the current outbreak could be worse than the one that killed more than 11,000 people in west Africa ten years ago, unless there is an improvement in efforts to combat the disease.
paragraphs.zh: 牛津大学启动了首个针对刚果民主共和国暴发的埃博拉病毒毒株的疫苗试验。已有近800人死亡，超过2,000人感染。科学家认为，除非抗击这种疾病的努力有所改善，否则当前疫情可能比十年前在西非造成逾11,000人死亡的那次疫情更严重。

paragraphs.en: Lindsey Graham, a senior American senator from South Carolina, died unexpectedly at the age of 71. He had just returned from Ukraine and was pushing a bill to widen sanctions against Russia. Mr Trump described Mr Graham as a “great politician”, though the senator opposed Mr Trump’s first run for president, becoming a steadfast ally only after his victory. Mr Graham also supported Israel’s war aims and advocated for a tough stand against Iran.
paragraphs.zh: 来自南卡罗来纳州的美国资深参议员林赛·格雷厄姆意外去世，享年71岁。他刚从乌克兰返回，并正推动一项扩大对俄罗斯制裁的法案。特朗普称格雷厄姆是“伟大的政治家”，尽管这位参议员曾反对特朗普首次竞选总统，只是在特朗普胜选后才成为坚定盟友。格雷厄姆还支持以色列的战争目标，并主张对伊朗采取强硬立场。

paragraphs.en: A suspect was arrested in the murder of Ann Widdecombe, a former MP and Conservative government minister in Britain who switched to the populist-right Reform UK and was its justice spokeswoman. Ms Widdecombe, a devout Christian who converted to Catholicism and fiercely resisted the transgender movement, was found dead at her home. Reform called for better security protection for MPs after a man was arrested for threatening on social media to shoot its leader, Nigel Farage.
paragraphs.zh: 英国前议员、保守党政府大臣安·威德科姆遇害案中，一名嫌疑人被逮捕。威德科姆后来转投民粹右翼的英国改革党，并担任该党的司法事务发言人。威德科姆是一名虔诚基督徒，后来改宗天主教，并强烈抵制跨性别运动；她被发现死于家中。此前一名男子因在社交媒体上威胁要枪击改革党领袖奈杰尔·法拉奇而被捕，改革党随后呼吁为议员提供更好的安全保护。

paragraphs.en: Andy Burnham easily secured the support of enough Labour MPs to become the party’s new leader, ensuring that he will take over from Sir Keir Starmer. Sir Keir took his last prime minister’s questions in the House of Commons. “I am proud of everything that we have achieved,” said the man with the worst satisfaction ratings ever recorded for a British prime minister.
paragraphs.zh: 安迪·伯纳姆轻松获得足够多工党议员的支持，成为该党新领袖，这确保了他将接替基尔·斯塔默爵士。斯塔默爵士在下议院最后一次接受首相质询。“我为我们取得的一切成就感到自豪，”这位民意满意度为英国首相有记录以来最低的人说道。

paragraphs.en: El Salvador’s president, Nayib Bukele, was nominated by his New Ideas party to run for a third term at next year’s election. When Mr Bukele was first elected in 2019 presidents were limited to one term. But in 2021 the country’s top court ruled that he could run a second time. Last year the legislative assembly passed a constitutional amendment scrapping term limits for the presidency altogether and extending the term in office from five years to six. Some observers fear that El Salvador is slipping back to the old Latin American model of one-man rule.
paragraphs.zh: 萨尔瓦多总统纳伊布·布克尔获得其所属的新思想党提名，将在明年选举中竞选第三个任期。布克尔2019年首次当选时，总统任期被限制为一届。但在2021年，该国最高法院裁定他可以再次参选。去年，立法议会通过了一项宪法修正案，彻底取消总统任期限制，并把任期从五年延长到六年。一些观察人士担心，萨尔瓦多正滑回拉丁美洲旧式的一人统治模式。

paragraphs.en: The death toll from two recent earthquakes in Venezuela approached 5,000. The government is in discussions with the IMF about tapping its special drawing rights at the fund for humanitarian uses.
paragraphs.zh: 委内瑞拉近期两次地震造成的死亡人数接近5,000人。政府正与国际货币基金组织讨论动用其在该基金的特别提款权，用于人道主义用途。

paragraphs.en: At least 32 people died when a fire engulfed a bar in Bangkok. Survivors described how they encountered locked doors and poor signage when they tried to escape the inferno.
paragraphs.zh: 曼谷一家酒吧被大火吞噬，至少32人死亡。幸存者描述说，他们试图逃离火海时遇到了上锁的门和糟糕的指示标识。

paragraphs.en: More violent clashes broke out in Pakistan-administered Kashmir over seats in the local assembly that are reserved for refugees who live in other parts of Pakistan. A grassroots organisation, the Joint Action Committee, has been banned for stirring the protests; it claims the reserved seats weaken local representation. Nine people were killed in the recent trouble. Around 30 have died over the past few weeks.
paragraphs.zh: 在巴基斯坦管辖的克什米尔地区，围绕地方议会中为居住在巴基斯坦其他地区的难民保留的席位，又爆发了更严重的暴力冲突。一个草根组织“联合行动委员会”因煽动抗议已被取缔；该组织声称，保留席位削弱了当地代表性。最近的动乱造成9人死亡。过去几周约有30人死亡。

paragraphs.en: Almost 2m people were evacuated in China from the path of Typhoon Bavi, a gigantic storm that spanned 1,000km (620 miles) at its widest point. Bavi made landfall at Taizhou, around 300km south of Shanghai, before turning on Wenzhou, which is a little farther south.
paragraphs.zh: 在中国，近200万人因躲避台风巴威而被疏散。巴威是一场巨型风暴，最宽处横跨1,000公里，即620英里。巴威在距离上海以南约300公里的台州登陆，随后转向更靠南一些的温州。

paragraphs.en: Volodymyr Zelensky shook up his cabinet, which resulted in Yuliia Svyrydenko stepping down as prime minister after just a year in the job. “Ukraine is changing its political strategy,” said Mr Zelensky. Mykhailo Fedorov, the defence minister who is widely credited with Ukraine’s successful use of new wartime technology, was also ousted after clashing with generals. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Kyiv with another heavy bombardment, its fifth this month.
paragraphs.zh: 沃洛德米尔·泽连斯基重组内阁，导致尤利娅·斯维里登科在担任总理仅一年后下台。“乌克兰正在改变其政治战略，”泽连斯基说。国防部长米哈伊洛·费多罗夫也被免职；他因乌克兰成功运用新型战时技术而广受赞誉，但此前与将领们发生冲突。与此同时，俄罗斯又对基辅发动了一轮猛烈轰炸，这是本月第五次。

paragraphs.en: Lithuania’s ruling coalition approved Mindaugas Sinkevicius as the new prime minister. The coalition in parliament voted for his manifesto, which includes keeping spending on defence to at least 5% of GDP and an aim to keep American troops in the country for the long term. Although Russia is suffering heavy losses in Ukraine, it would be a mistake to think its “military threat is subsiding”, said Mr Sinkevicius.
paragraphs.zh: 立陶宛执政联盟批准明道加斯·辛克维丘斯出任新总理。议会中的执政联盟投票支持了他的施政纲领，其中包括把国防开支保持在国内生产总值至少5%的水平，并争取让美国军队长期留驻该国。辛克维丘斯说，尽管俄罗斯在乌克兰遭受重大损失，但如果认为其“军事威胁正在减弱”，那将是错误的。

paragraphs.en: The Hungarian parliament, now with the centrists holding a supermajority after April’s election, passed a constitutional amendment that would remove Tamas Sulyok from the presidency and Peter Polt as head of the Constitutional Court. Peter Magyar, the new prime minister, said both men had put their loyalty to Viktor Orban, the previous right-wing prime minister, above the interests of Hungary.
paragraphs.zh: 匈牙利议会通过了一项宪法修正案。该议会在4月选举后由中间派掌握超级多数。这项修正案将把陶马什·舒约克赶下总统职位，并解除彼得·波尔特宪法法院院长职务。新任总理彼得·马扎尔表示，两人都把对前右翼总理维克托·欧尔班的忠诚置于匈牙利利益之上。

paragraphs.en: Border controls between Gibraltar, a British overseas territory, and Spain were abolished. Checks had been administered on people crossing to and from Spain, and thus the European Union, but during the Brexit negotiations with Britain the EU insisted on allowing free movement. Spaniards and Gibraltarians can now cross their border with ease. But as Gibraltar is now in effect part of the EU’s Schengen free-movement area, non-Gibraltarians will have to endure the EU’s new and technically challenged electronic border system at the airport.
paragraphs.zh: 英国海外领土直布罗陀与西班牙之间的边境管制被取消。此前，对往返西班牙、也就是往返欧洲联盟的人员一直实施检查，但在英国脱欧谈判期间，欧盟坚持要求允许自由流动。西班牙人和直布罗陀人现在可以轻松跨越边境。不过，由于直布罗陀如今实际上已成为欧盟申根自由流动区的一部分，非直布罗陀居民将不得不在机场忍受欧盟新的、技术上问题重重的电子边境系统。

articles.title_en: Business
articles.title_zh: 商业

paragraphs.en: Kevin Warsh gave his first report to Congress as chairman of the Federal Reserve. Mr Warsh said the Fed was committed to “restoring price stability”, which would entail a “regime change in policy” to make the inflation surge of the previous five years “a thing of the past”. Mr Warsh has appointed task forces to assess each of the five areas that are central to the Fed’s policy. The task forces’ members include Lord Mervyn King, a former governor of the Bank of England, and Greg Mankiw, who led George W. Bush’s Council of Economic Advisers.
paragraphs.zh: 凯文·沃什以美联储主席身份首次向国会提交报告。沃什表示，美联储致力于“恢复价格稳定”，这将需要一场“政策体制转变”，使过去五年的通胀飙升“成为过去”。沃什已任命多个工作组，评估美联储政策核心的五个领域。工作组成员包括英格兰银行前行长默文·金勋爵，以及曾领导乔治·W·布什经济顾问委员会的格雷格·曼昆。

paragraphs.en: Mr Warsh gave his testimony as the latest figures on American consumer-price inflation showed the annual rate falling in June to 3.5%, from 4.2% in May. Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, also fell, to 2.6%. Energy prices declined by 5.7%, month on month, after rising for three months.
paragraphs.zh: 沃什作证之际，美国消费者价格通胀的最新数据显示，6月份年通胀率从5月份的4.2%降至3.5%。剔除波动较大的食品和能源价格的核心通胀率也降至2.6%。能源价格在连续三个月上涨后，环比下降5.7%。

paragraphs.en: Brisk activity in equities trading during the second quarter led to huge profits at America’s big banks. Net income at JPMorgan Chase was up by 41%, year on year, to $21.2bn. Profits surged by 80% at Goldman Sachs, by 45% at Citigroup and by 27% at Bank of America.
paragraphs.zh: 第二季度股票交易活跃，推动美国大型银行获得巨额利润。摩根大通净利润同比增长41%，至212亿美元。高盛利润飙升80%，花旗集团增长45%，美国银行增长27%。

paragraphs.en: South Korea’s central bank raised interest rates for the first time in three years, lifting its base rate to 2.75%. The boom in exports from the country’s mighty chipmakers is expected to “spill over into domestic demand”, said the bank. Higher oil prices, coupled with a weaker won to pay for energy imports, are also adding to inflationary pressures.
paragraphs.zh: 韩国央行三年来首次加息，将基准利率上调至2.75%。该行表示，韩国强大芯片制造商的出口繁荣预计将“外溢至国内需求”。油价上涨，加上韩元走弱导致能源进口成本上升，也在增加通胀压力。

paragraphs.en: SK Hynix’s debut on the Nasdaq exchange was a success. The South Korean chipmaker raised $26.5bn through a sale of stock in American depositary receipts (allowing American investors to buy its shares). In the days following the flotation the share price fell as investors banked profits from the sale, but it then surged again in another rollercoaster week for the KOSPI, South Korea’s main stock-market index.
paragraphs.zh: SK海力士在纳斯达克交易所首秀成功。这家韩国芯片制造商通过出售美国存托凭证股票筹集了265亿美元（美国存托凭证允许美国投资者购买其股票）。上市后的几天里，随着投资者从此次发行中获利了结，股价下跌，但随后又再次飙升，韩国主要股市指数KOSPI又经历了跌宕起伏的一周。

paragraphs.en: EasyJet changed its mind about a proposal to be taken private by Castlelake, an American investment firm, and instead accepted an offer from Apollo, a private-equity firm, that values the European airline at £5.7bn ($7.6bn). Castlelake had not finalised its proposal.
paragraphs.zh: 易捷航空改变了接受美国投资公司Castlelake私有化提议的想法，转而接受私募股权公司Apollo的报价，该报价对这家欧洲航空公司的估值为57亿英镑（76亿美元）。Castlelake尚未敲定其提议。

paragraphs.en: Uber agreed to buy Delivery Hero, a German online food-ordering company that operates in 65 countries. The acquisition values Delivery Hero, which will sell its Turkish business and make other divestments, at €13bn ($14.8bn).
paragraphs.zh: 优步同意收购Delivery Hero，这是一家业务遍及65个国家的德国在线订餐公司。此次收购对Delivery Hero的估值为130亿欧元（148亿美元）；该公司将出售其土耳其业务，并进行其他资产剥离。

paragraphs.en: China’s economy grew by 4.3% in the second quarter, year on year. Apart from the pandemic era it was the country’s slowest growth rate since the early 1990s, when its GDP statistics were standardised. The domestic economy has remained subdued during a long slowdown in the property market. Exports are booming, however. Separate figures showed the value of goods sent abroad rising by 27% in June. Shipments of cars were up by 71% to more than 1m for the first time.
paragraphs.zh: 中国经济第二季度同比增长4.3%。除疫情时期外，这是自20世纪90年代初中国国内生产总值统计标准化以来的最低增速。房地产市场长期放缓，使国内经济一直较为低迷。不过，出口正在蓬勃增长。另一组数据显示，6月份出口商品价值增长27%。汽车出口量首次增长71%，超过100万辆。

paragraphs.en: Apple lodged a lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing it and two former employees of stealing trade secrets. The complaint relates to OpenAI’s expansion into consumer devices, the development of which is now “rotten to the core”, according to Apple. OpenAI denies the claims. OpenAI’s first device will be a portable speaker.
paragraphs.zh: 苹果对OpenAI提起诉讼，指控其以及两名前员工窃取商业机密。起诉内容涉及OpenAI向消费设备领域的扩张；苹果称，这些设备的开发如今已“烂到了根子里”。OpenAI否认这些指控。OpenAI的首款设备将是一款便携式音箱。

paragraphs.en: The AI boom powered ASML to another bumper quarter. The Dutch maker of lithography machines used to produce high-end chips recorded a 21% jump in sales, year on year, to €9.3bn ($10.7bn) and raised its annual outlook. TSMC, the world’s biggest contract-manufacturer of chips, reported a 77% rise in net profit and also lifted its revenue projections.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智能热潮推动ASML又迎来一个表现强劲的季度。这家荷兰公司生产用于制造高端芯片的光刻机，其销售额同比增长21%，至93亿欧元（107亿美元），并上调了全年展望。全球最大的芯片代工制造商台积电报告净利润增长77%，并同样上调了营收预期。

paragraphs.en: New York imposed a one-year moratorium on building large AI data centres, the first American state to do so. Residents are objecting to the facilities, claiming, often on dubious evidence, that they use too much water and raise electricity prices.
paragraphs.zh: 纽约州对大型人工智能数据中心建设实施为期一年的暂停令，成为美国首个这样做的州。居民反对这些设施，声称它们耗水过多并推高电价，尽管这些说法往往证据不足。

paragraphs.en: SpaceX’s stock slipped below the $135 it was listed at in its initial public offering on June 12th. More than $1trn has been wiped off the company’s market value since the share price hit a peak in mid-June.
paragraphs.zh: SpaceX的股价跌破了6月12日首次公开募股时135美元的发行价。自其股价在6月中旬触及峰值以来，该公司的市值已蒸发超过1万亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Speculation that artificial intelligence is causing a market bubble was dismissed as “absurd” by Masayoshi Son, the boss of SoftBank. Mr Son was an early backer of the technology. He indulged in some speculation of his own. By 2040 humans will no longer be the “highest life form”, predicted Mr Son, $5trn a year will be spent on AI, and data centres will be powered by nuclear fusion. In June Mr Son compared worries about an AI bubble to “blasphemy”.
paragraphs.zh: 软银老板孙正义驳斥了人工智能正在造成市场泡沫的猜测，称这种说法“荒谬”。孙正义是这项技术的早期支持者。他自己也做了一番预测。孙正义预测，到2040年，人类将不再是“最高等的生命形式”；每年将有5万亿美元用于人工智能；数据中心将由核聚变供能。6月，孙正义把对人工智能泡沫的担忧比作“亵渎”。

paragraphs.en: Sir Demis Hassabis, the co-founder of Google DeepMind, called for the establishment of a regulatory body to oversee potential threats from the most-advanced AI models to national security. The organisation should be led by America, said Sir Demis, and modelled on the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, an American non-governmental body that protects investors.
paragraphs.zh: Google DeepMind联合创始人德米斯·哈萨比斯爵士呼吁设立一个监管机构，监督最先进人工智能模型可能对国家安全构成的威胁。德米斯爵士表示，该组织应由美国牵头，并仿照美国金融业监管局；后者是一个保护投资者的美国非政府机构。

articles.title_en: Donald Trump’s gutting of the Department of Justice
articles.title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普对司法部的掏空
articles.rubric_en: What the degraded institution means for America
articles.rubric_zh: 这个被削弱的机构对美国意味着什么

paragraphs.en: THE PURsUIT of enemies grabs all the attention, and for good reason. Markets were shocked to see President Donald Trump set the Department of Justice (DoJ) on Jerome Powell when he was still the chairman of the Federal Reserve. Champions of press freedom cried foul last week when the department issued subpoenas to New York Times journalists for explaining the truth about Qatar’s gift of a jet to the president. But Mr Trump’s grievance agenda isn’t the half of it.
paragraphs.zh: 追击敌人吸引了所有注意力，而且理由充分。市场震惊地看到，唐纳德·特朗普总统在杰罗姆·鲍威尔仍担任美联储主席时，就指使司法部（DoJ）对他下手。上周，司法部向《纽约时报》记者发出传票，原因是他们讲清了卡塔尔向总统赠送一架飞机的真相，新闻自由的捍卫者对此大呼不公。但特朗普先生的怨恨议程还远不止这些。

paragraphs.en: In a sign of how much he treats the department as his own personal law firm, he wants his actual lawyer to become attorney-general. Senate hearings begin this week for Todd Blanche, who is currently the acting attorney-general. Those on Mr Trump’s enemies list are not the only ones who should be worried. The president is also inflicting less-noticed harms on the DoJ that are as bad as the attention-grabbing ones. The damage is likely to be profound.
paragraphs.zh: 一个迹象显示，他多么把司法部当成自己的私人律师事务所：他想让自己真正的律师出任司法部长。托德·布兰奇目前是代理司法部长，参议院本周开始就他的任命举行听证。该担心的不只是特朗普先生敌人名单上的人。总统还在对司法部造成一些不那么受关注的伤害，其严重程度不亚于那些吸引眼球的做法。损害很可能会非常深远。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ is the government’s lawyer, but it also serves as the guardian of the law, especially since Watergate. In 2019 Bill Barr, then Mr Trump’s pick for attorney-general, said that Americans “have to know that there are places in the government where the rule of law—not politics—holds sway” and that the Department of Justice “must be such a place”. Mr Trump has no time for that. Less than halfway through his second term, he has turned the DoJ from an arm of the law into a muscular limb of the presidency.
paragraphs.zh: 司法部是政府的律师，但它也承担着法律守护者的角色，尤其是在水门事件之后。2019年，当时由特朗普先生挑选出任司法部长的比尔·巴尔说，美国人“必须知道，政府中有一些地方是法治而非政治占据主导”，而司法部“必须就是这样的地方”。特朗普先生对此毫无兴趣。在第二任期尚未过半之际，他已经把司法部从法律的一条臂膀，变成了总统权力的一块强健肌肉。

paragraphs.en: For a start, he has dramatically redefined the department’s priorities, which is legitimate, often by setting goals that blur policy and politics, which is not. Health-care fraud is being chased with particular zeal in states run by Democrats, such as California and Minnesota, where it can be used to discredit Mr Trump’s opponents, including the states’ governors, Gavin Newsom and Tim Walz.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，他大幅重新界定了司法部的优先事项。重新设定优先事项本身是正当的，但他往往设定一些模糊政策与政治界限的目标，这就不正当了。在加利福尼亚和明尼苏达等由民主党执政的州，医疗欺诈正受到格外积极的追查，因为这可以用来抹黑特朗普先生的对手，包括这些州的州长加文·纽森和蒂姆·沃尔兹。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ is also an effective tool for pursuing his political agenda. Election fraud is consuming ever more of its resources—not because it is a real problem, but because it is a presidential obsession. The DoJ has sued states for access to their voter rolls. In January the FBI seized hundreds of boxes filled with ballots and other documents in Georgia’s most populous county, part of an investigation of the presidential election in 2020. More recently, some 260 FBI analysts were dispatched to pore over Georgia’s files, with a deadline to review records by July 17th. At the very least, this will shake voters’ faith that elections are trustworthy—indeed, that may be its sinister design.
paragraphs.zh: 司法部也是推进其政治议程的有效工具。选举舞弊正在消耗它越来越多的资源，不是因为这是真正的问题，而是因为这是总统的执念。司法部已起诉多个州，要求获取其选民名册。今年1月，联邦调查局（FBI）在佐治亚州人口最多的县查扣了数百箱装有选票和其他文件的箱子，这是对2020年总统选举调查的一部分。更近一些时候，约260名FBI分析员被派去仔细审查佐治亚州的文件，并被要求在7月17日前完成记录审查。至少，这会动摇选民对选举可信度的信心；事实上，这也许正是其阴险设计所在。

paragraphs.en: Matters of genuine public interest are left to languish. About a quarter of the DoJ’s lawyers have left. Divisions that investigated cryptocurrency fraud and public corruption have withered. Financial-fraud indictments by prosecutors at DoJ headquarters and in Manhattan are down by 30% from the ten-year average. About 300 special agents who specialise in national security have quit the FBI, taking decades of experience in counterterrorism and cyber-warfare with them.
paragraphs.zh: 真正关系公共利益的事务却被搁置不理。司法部约四分之一的律师已经离职。曾调查加密货币欺诈和公共腐败的部门已经萎缩。司法部总部和曼哈顿检察官提出的金融欺诈起诉，比十年平均水平低了30%。约300名专门负责国家安全的特工已经离开FBI，同时带走了数十年的反恐和网络战经验。

paragraphs.en: The department has also become more chaotic. Too often, cases encounter problems in court, though it is hard to distinguish sloppiness by DoJ staff from deliberate ill-intent. Nearly 100 times in Mr Trump’s first 14 months, the DoJ supplied courts with inaccurate information. It is quite something for the state to lose the benefit of the doubt in its own courtrooms.
paragraphs.zh: 司法部也变得更加混乱。案件在法庭上遇到问题的情况太多了，尽管很难区分这是司法部工作人员的草率，还是蓄意的不良意图。在特朗普先生执政的头14个月里，司法部向法院提供不准确信息的次数接近100次。国家在自己的法庭上失去被善意推定的资格，这可不是小事。

paragraphs.en: More than 61,000 petitions from detained immigrants have bogged down courts and frustrated judges and federal prosecutors, who have moved lawyers from criminal divisions to help. By September last year, about a fifth of FBI agents had been diverted to immigration enforcement.
paragraphs.zh: 被拘押移民提交的逾61,000份申请让法院不堪重负，也令法官和联邦检察官感到挫败；他们已经从刑事部门调来律师帮忙。到去年9月，约五分之一的FBI探员已被转去执行移民执法任务。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, the president’s powers are increasing. In Trump v Slaughter last month the Supreme Court ruled that the president could sack leaders of semi-independent agencies, such as the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). That gives the president the capacity to force agencies to work in league with the DoJ. Imagine a co-ordinated campaign of pressure, in which the DoJ opens an antitrust inquiry, the FTC explores consumer fraud and the SEC investigates corporate disclosures.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，总统的权力正在扩大。上个月，在“特朗普诉斯劳特案”中，最高法院裁定，总统可以解雇半独立机构的负责人，例如联邦贸易委员会（FTC）和证券交易委员会（SEC）。这使总统有能力迫使各机构与司法部协同行动。可以想象一场协调一致的施压行动：司法部启动反垄断调查，FTC调查消费者欺诈，SEC调查企业信息披露。

paragraphs.en: Unfortunately, the permanent appointment of Mr Blanche is unlikely to mark an improvement. He has done as much as anyone to advance Mr Trump’s agenda of prosecuting his enemies and protecting his friends—a powerful combination for encouraging people to comply with Mr Trump’s wishes. Under Mr Blanche, the DoJ has recently threatened state election officials with criminal prosecution if they knowingly let non-citizens remain on voting rolls.
paragraphs.zh: 不幸的是，布兰奇先生获得正式任命不太可能意味着情况会改善。在推进特朗普先生起诉敌人、保护朋友的议程方面，他的作用不亚于任何人；这种组合威力强大，会促使人们顺从特朗普先生的意愿。在布兰奇先生领导下，司法部最近威胁各州选举官员说，如果他们明知非公民仍在选民名册上却任其保留，就可能面临刑事起诉。

paragraphs.en: Democrats and more than 1,200 former DoJ lawyers have demanded that the Senate reject Mr Blanche’s nomination. The Senate now has the choice of confirming him, and thereby seeming to endorse Mr Trump’s broader agenda, or blocking him in a rare rebuke to the president. Unfortunately, a rejection may not accomplish all that much. Mr Trump can retain Mr Blanche as acting attorney-general for months or nominate someone just as pliable.
paragraphs.zh: 民主党人和1,200多名前司法部律师已要求参议院否决布兰奇先生的提名。参议院现在面临选择：确认他的任命，从而看似为特朗普先生更广泛的议程背书；或者阻止他，以一种罕见方式斥责总统。不幸的是，即便否决，也未必能取得多大效果。特朗普先生可以让布兰奇先生继续担任数月代理司法部长，或提名一个同样顺从的人。

paragraphs.en: The best Americans can hope for, in the next two years, is that courts stand firm. So far they have generally checked the DoJ’s worst impulses. On July 7th a federal judge blocked the department’s effort to subpoena the names of election workers in Georgia. On July 13th another federal judge nullified a settlement organised by the DoJ in response to a civil case brought by Mr Trump that would have protected the president and his family from tax audits.
paragraphs.zh: 在接下来的两年里，美国人所能寄望的最好结果，是法院坚守立场。到目前为止，法院总体上遏制了司法部最糟糕的冲动。7月7日，一名联邦法官阻止了司法部传唤佐治亚州选举工作人员姓名的努力。7月13日，另一名联邦法官撤销了一项由司法部安排的和解；该和解是为回应特朗普先生提起的一桩民事案件而达成的，本会保护总统及其家人免受税务审计。

paragraphs.en: Even if Americans elect a president who wants to restore the DoJ, the damage will be hard to reverse. Mr Trump’s acolytes would see the ejection of his partisan lawyers as a witch hunt that justifies the next purge when they take back power. Once the arrival of any new administration routinely entails a fresh round of sackings, professionals who care about the rule of law will think twice about signing up. After Watergate, statesmanship and a bipartisan effort were needed to create the modern DoJ. Today the stakes are as high, and the task is harder. ■
paragraphs.zh: 即使美国人选出一位想要恢复司法部正常状态的总统，损害也很难逆转。特朗普先生的追随者会把驱逐他的党派律师视为一场政治迫害，并以此为下一次他们重新掌权后的清洗辩护。一旦任何新政府上台都例行意味着新一轮解雇，关心法治的专业人士在加入之前就会三思。水门事件之后，建立现代司法部需要政治家的担当和两党共同努力。如今利害关系同样重大，而任务更加艰难。■

paragraphs.en: For subscribers only: to see how we design each week’s cover, sign up to our weekly Cover Story newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 仅限订阅者：想了解我们每周如何设计封面，请订阅我们的每周“封面故事”新闻简报。

articles.title_en: It’s too darn hot. Blame global dimming
articles.title_zh: 天实在太热了。要怪就怪全球变暗
articles.rubric_en: Earth is absorbing a lot more sunshine
articles.rubric_zh: 地球正在吸收多得多的阳光

paragraphs.en: Cole Porter had it right. It is too darn hot. On July 14th the surface temperature of Earth’s tropical and mid-latitude oceans was higher than on any other July 14th in modern records. The same was true for every other day in July, and for almost all of the days in June, too. Europe has gone through three heatwaves since May, and though the continent is better prepared for such things than it used to be, tens of thousands will have died as a result.
paragraphs.zh: 科尔·波特说对了。天实在太热了。7月14日，地球热带和中纬度海洋的表面温度高于现代记录中任何一个7月14日。7月的其他每一天都是如此，6月几乎所有日子也是如此。欧洲自5月以来经历了三轮热浪，尽管这个大陆如今比过去更能应对这类情况，仍会有数万人因此死亡。

paragraphs.en: America’s Fourth of July celebrations took place under a sweltering “heat dome”. Supertyphoon Bavi, which saw well over a million people evacuated from the Chinese coast, owed much of its power to the rate at which it was able to suck up energy from the overheated sea. Fires are spreading far and wide. Worse is to come. The chances of this year being the hottest on record are growing; the El Niño getting under way in the Pacific all but guarantees next year will be.
paragraphs.zh: 美国的独立日庆祝活动是在令人闷热难耐的“热穹顶”下举行的。超级台风巴威迫使中国沿海远超100万人撤离；它之所以威力巨大，很大程度上是因为它能够从过热的海水中快速汲取能量。火灾正在大范围蔓延。更糟的还在后面。今年成为有记录以来最热年份的可能性正在上升；太平洋中正在形成的厄尔尼诺几乎可以保证明年会是最热的一年。

paragraphs.en: Climate change is feeding on itself. The warming effects of greenhouse gases are driving processes that heat up the planet yet further. The one which is exciting—and alarming—scientists most is an unexpected reduction in an obscure but fundamental aspect of Earth: its albedo.
paragraphs.zh: 气候变化正在自我强化。温室气体的增温效应正在推动一些过程，使地球进一步升温。其中最令科学家兴奋又担忧的，是地球一个冷门却根本的属性出现了意外下降：反照率。

paragraphs.en: The albedo is the fraction of incoming sunlight that a surface or a planet reflects back whence it came. High albedos, whether they are created by white roofs or ice-covered seas, keep things cool; by contrast, when albedos are low, the incoming energy stays here on Earth, raising the planet’s surface temperatures. Robust satellite measurements of Earth’s albedo have now been taken for over two decades. They show it falling with surprising speed. The oceans, which are responsible for most of the sunshine’s absorption, have been accumulating much more heat as a result.
paragraphs.zh: 反照率是指一个表面或一颗行星把入射阳光反射回来源方向的比例。无论是白色屋顶还是冰层覆盖的海面造成的高反照率，都能让事物保持凉爽；相比之下，当反照率较低时，入射的能量会留在地球上，推高地表温度。对地球反照率的可靠卫星测量如今已经持续了二十多年。数据显示，它正以惊人的速度下降。海洋吸收了大部分阳光，因此也积累了多得多的热量。

paragraphs.en: This is partly because of a scarcity of cooling clouds over the seas, a widely predicted effect of greenhouse warming. It is also owing to something else entirely. In 2006—in fact, 20 years ago this month—the late Paul Crutzen, a world-renowned atmospheric chemist, wrote an article pointing out the “policy dilemma” embedded in efforts to control emissions of sulphur dioxide. Sulphur dioxide, which comes from the burning of some fossil fuels, creates little particles in the atmosphere after it is emitted. They wreak havoc with human health. They also reflect sunlight back into space, thereby reducing the amount of energy that reaches Earth’s surface. Health agencies, Crutzen said, thought controlling the particles could save 500,000 lives a year; at the same time, by diminishing the sulphur’s cooling effect, the controls might increase global warming by perhaps 1°C (1.8°F).
paragraphs.zh: 这在一定程度上是因为海洋上方缺少能降温的云层，而这正是温室效应变暖被广泛预测会带来的影响。另一个原因则完全不同。2006年，也就是本月的20年前，已故的世界知名大气化学家保罗·克鲁岑写了一篇文章，指出控制二氧化硫排放的努力中存在一个“政策困境”。二氧化硫来自某些化石燃料的燃烧，排放后会在大气中形成微小颗粒。它们严重危害人类健康。同时，它们也会把阳光反射回太空，从而减少到达地表的能量。克鲁岑说，卫生机构认为控制这些颗粒物每年可挽救50万人的生命；但与此同时，由于削弱了硫的降温效应，这些控制措施也许会使全球变暖增加约1°C（1.8°F）。

paragraphs.en: Both albedo-reducing trends look set to continue, adding yet more to the heat. The industrial cities of the developing world are hardly likely to sacrifice the health of their smog-choked inhabitants in order to slow warming for the world at large, so sulphur emissions will continue to fall. The climate feedbacks which reduce cloud cover will not stop while carbon-dioxide levels continue to rise. And even when the transition to renewable energy eats into the use of fossil fuels, as it is starting to do, what matters most is the cumulative level of greenhouse gases.
paragraphs.zh: 这两种降低反照率的趋势看来都会持续下去，让热量进一步增加。发展中世界的工业城市几乎不可能为了放缓全球整体变暖，而牺牲本地雾霾笼罩下居民的健康，因此硫排放会继续下降。只要二氧化碳水平继续上升，减少云量的气候反馈就不会停止。即使向可再生能源转型开始削减化石燃料的使用，正如现在已经开始发生的那样，最重要的仍是温室气体的累积水平。

paragraphs.en: Around a decade ago the need to stabilise that cumulative level led lots of countries to embrace “net zero” goals. The idea’s proponents see putting no more greenhouse gases into the atmosphere than you are taking out as good planetary housekeeping. They are right. But it is an unavoidably slow process. What’s more, its critics are also correct when they say that only for the largest economies does a single country’s progress towards net-zero goals have any discernible effect on the world’s overall trajectory. The idea was conceived when America was a believer in international agreements and the level of idealism needed to imagine countries spurring each other on to better things was merely high—not, as it is now, delusional.
paragraphs.zh: 大约十年前，稳定这一累积水平的需要促使许多国家接受“净零”目标。这个理念的支持者认为，向大气中排放的温室气体不超过你移除的数量，是一种良好的地球治理。他们是对的。但这不可避免是一个缓慢的过程。而且，批评者说，单个国家迈向净零目标的进展，只有在最大经济体身上才会对世界总体轨迹产生可察觉的影响；这一点他们也说得没错。这个理念诞生时，美国还相信国际协议，而想象各国会相互激励、共同变得更好的理想主义程度只是很高而已，不像现在，已经近乎妄想。

paragraphs.en: There are still places where climate change is understood to be important and where action can be co-ordinated. Being committed to net zero should buy a country a certain standing when it is negotiating other, more near-term options for limiting climate change, such as curbing methane emissions and the production of fluorinated gases used in cooling systems, both of which bring benefits faster than cutting carbon dioxide.
paragraphs.zh: 仍有一些地方理解气候变化的重要性，也仍有可能协调行动。承诺实现净零，应当能让一个国家在谈判其他更近期的限制气候变化方案时获得一定地位，比如限制甲烷排放，以及限制冷却系统所用氟化气体的生产；这两项措施都比削减二氧化碳更快带来好处。

paragraphs.en: No form of emissions reduction, though, can quickly bend the current trajectory. Endurance is what remains. Making air conditioning more efficient, cheap and widespread saves lives and fits well with other policy goals, like making clean electricity cheap to produce and consume. Ten years ago a commitment to phase out the fluorinated gases in cooling systems was reached in Kigali, the capital of Rwanda. There is scope for extra international efforts to improve the machinery that uses them. This could double the cooling benefit of phasing out the gases.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，没有任何一种减排方式能迅速扭转当前轨迹。剩下的只能是承受与适应。让空调更高效、更便宜、更普及可以挽救生命，也与其他政策目标相契合，比如让清洁电力的生产和消费变得便宜。十年前，各方在卢旺达首都基加利达成了承诺，要逐步淘汰冷却系统中的氟化气体。国际社会还有空间加大努力，改进使用这些气体的设备。这可能使逐步淘汰这些气体带来的降温收益翻倍。

paragraphs.en: The most radical response is to make Earth a bit more reflective again. Crutzen’s provocative suggestion for how to resolve his policy dilemma over sulphur emissions was to replace harmful aerosols with benign ones. The brightening of cooling clouds has been tried over the Great Barrier Reef, but the technology needed to do it at scale remains elusive. An Israeli-American company, Stardust Solutions, has raised money to develop “safe” aerosols that it thinks a country or set of countries could use to cool the planet from the stratosphere.
paragraphs.zh: 最激进的应对办法，是让地球重新变得稍微更能反射阳光。对于如何解决他所说的硫排放政策困境，克鲁岑提出了一个挑衅性的建议：用无害气溶胶取代有害气溶胶。人们已经在大堡礁上空尝试过让具有降温作用的云层变亮，但要大规模做到这一点，所需技术仍难以实现。一家以色列-美国公司Stardust Solutions已经筹资开发它认为“安全”的气溶胶；该公司设想，一个国家或一组国家可以从平流层利用这些气溶胶给地球降温。

paragraphs.en: Some oppose such geoengineering fiercely; hardly anyone endorses it as a welcome path forward. But if nations can act in concert to warm the planet—as those of the International Maritime Organisation did when they enforced new restrictions on ships’ sulphur emissions in 2020—they ought to be able to discuss the conditions under which they might cool it. Keeping silent about geoengineering does not stop someone from trying it. The more warming quickens, the sooner it may reach a point where, to quote Cole Porter again, anything goes. ■
paragraphs.zh: 有些人强烈反对这种地球工程；几乎没有人把它赞成为什么受欢迎的前进道路。但是，如果各国能够一致行动使地球变暖，就像国际海事组织成员国在2020年执行船舶硫排放新限制时所做的那样，它们也应该能够讨论在什么条件下可能给地球降温。对地球工程保持沉默，并不能阻止某些人尝试它。变暖加速得越快，它就越可能更早到达一个临界点；再次引用科尔·波特的话，到那时，什么都可能发生。■

paragraphs.en: For subscribers only: to see how we design each week’s cover, sign up to our weekly Cover Story newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 仅限订户：如想了解我们每周如何设计封面，请订阅我们的每周“封面故事”新闻简报。

articles.title_en: Donald Trump’s blind alley
articles.title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普的死胡同
articles.rubric_en: America’s president looks bereft of good options for solving the stand-off in the Gulf
articles.rubric_zh: 美国总统似乎找不到解决海湾僵局的好办法

paragraphs.en: WHEN DONALD TRUMP proposed peace with Iran, he could hardly have offered better terms. In return for Iran opening the Strait of Hormuz and forswearing all ambitions for a nuclear bomb, America held out the prospect of hundreds of billions of dollars of income and investment in an economy ravaged by sanctions and war. The horrified reaction of Iran hawks in America and Israel tells you that no other American leader would have surrendered so much.
paragraphs.zh: 当唐纳德·特朗普向伊朗提出和平方案时，他几乎不可能开出更优厚的条件了。作为交换，伊朗要开放霍尔木兹海峡，并放弃一切制造核弹的野心；而美国则提出，让这个饱受制裁和战争摧残的经济体有望获得数千亿美元的收入和投资。美国和以色列的伊朗鹰派对此反应惊恐，这说明没有哪位美国领导人会让步到这种程度。

paragraphs.en: The bleak message from the upsurge in fighting over the past week is that, for Iran, money alone is not enough. The hardliners are in charge. They want something more, and it cannot be good—be it revenge, control over the strait, regional dominance or a nuclear programme. America must not yield.
paragraphs.zh: 过去一周战事升级传递出的惨淡信息是：对伊朗来说，光有钱还不够。强硬派掌权。他们想要更多东西，而那不可能是什么好事——无论是复仇、控制海峡、地区主导权，还是核计划。美国不能让步。

paragraphs.en: The memorandum of understanding (MoU), signed a month ago, allows 60 days to bring peace. Halfway through, it has itself become the focus of conflict. It asks Iran to “make arrangements to ensure the safe passage of commercial vessels free of charge for 60 days”. Iran takes that to imply it is in charge; for America it means that Iran must not restrict sea traffic.
paragraphs.zh: 一个月前签署的谅解备忘录允许双方用60天时间实现和平。如今期限过半，它本身却成了冲突的焦点。备忘录要求伊朗“作出安排，确保商船在60天内免费安全通行”。伊朗认为这意味着由它说了算；而在美国看来，这意味着伊朗不得限制海上交通。

paragraphs.en: The two sides are exchanging missile and drone strikes, and tankers are wary of sailing even with American offers of protection. Thankfully, these military exchanges have so far stopped short of a return to war. But the oil price is creeping back up. Meanwhile, there has been no progress in talks on tricky matters, including nuclear materials and Iranian efforts to enrich uranium.
paragraphs.zh: 双方正在相互发动导弹和无人机袭击，即便美国提出提供保护，油轮也对航行心存顾虑。谢天谢地，到目前为止，这些军事交锋还没有升级为重新开战。但油价正在悄然回升。与此同时，在核材料和伊朗铀浓缩努力等棘手问题的谈判上，仍然没有进展。

paragraphs.en: After decades of hostility, trust between America and Iran is in desperately short supply. But America was honouring its side of the MoU, by allowing Iranian oil to be sold on international markets. If ever the two countries might have been able to put relations on a more steady footing, this was the moment.
paragraphs.zh: 在几十年的敌对之后，美国和伊朗之间的信任极其匮乏。但美国一直在履行谅解备忘录中自己的义务，允许伊朗石油在国际市场上出售。如果说两国曾有机会把关系置于更稳定的基础之上，那就是这个时刻。

paragraphs.en: Moderates in Tehran (a relative term) are said to grasp what a favourable offer they had. But the hardliners won the argument. The new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, remains unseen, but his pronouncements are hardline, too. Either he agrees with squeezing America for more, or he is under the thumb of those who want war.
paragraphs.zh: 据说德黑兰的温和派（这是一个相对说法）明白，他们得到的是多么有利的提议。但强硬派在争论中胜出。新任最高领袖莫杰塔巴·哈梅内伊仍未露面，但他的表态同样强硬。要么他赞同向美国施压以争取更多东西，要么他受制于那些想要战争的人。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump seems to have no plan. This week he said America would itself start levying tolls on ships in the strait, until wiser heads in the administration pointed out how foolish that idea was. Thankfully he reversed it.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普似乎没有计划。本周他说，美国将自己开始向通过海峡的船只征收通行费，直到政府中更明智的人指出这个想法有多愚蠢。幸好他撤回了这个主意。

paragraphs.en: He has also issued more threats to destroy Iranian bridges and energy plants. But a return to all-out war does not hold much promise. After all, the intense fighting in February was supposed to topple the regime, but ended up strengthening its hardliners. The president also drops hints about sending in troops to seize Kharg island, the export terminal for almost all of Iran’s oil. American forces on Kharg would be a sitting target for Iranian missiles and drones.
paragraphs.zh: 他还发出更多威胁，要摧毁伊朗的桥梁和能源设施。但重新陷入全面战争并没有多少希望可言。毕竟，2月的激烈战斗原本被认为会推翻伊朗政权，结果却增强了其强硬派的力量。总统还暗示可能派兵夺取哈尔克岛，那里是伊朗几乎所有石油的出口终端。驻扎在哈尔克岛的美军将成为伊朗导弹和无人机的活靶子。

paragraphs.en: Giving Iran what it wants would be terrible, too. Acquiescing to its control of international waters in the Gulf would not only be bad in itself, but would set a dire precedent. Abandoning Gulf countries to Iran’s predations would be against America’s direct interests and send a bad signal to allies everywhere. And Iran’s nuclear programme poses a real danger that needs close monitoring and control by international inspectors.
paragraphs.zh: 按伊朗的要求让步也会很糟糕。默许它控制海湾国际水域，不仅本身就是坏事，还会开创一个可怕的先例。把海湾国家丢给伊朗任其掠夺，将违背美国的直接利益，并向各地盟友发出糟糕信号。而伊朗的核计划构成真实危险，需要国际核查人员密切监控和约束。

paragraphs.en: The options are not good, therefore. All of them involve demonstrating to Iran’s hardliners that America has the resolve to impose a sustained blockade on Iranian oil exports—even if that raises the price of petrol before the midterm elections in November. America has restored the embargo against Iran, which is a start. To show its will, it should also continue to match Iranian strikes. Mr Trump made a foolish mistake by starting this war. However much he twists and turns, he now has little choice but to stick it out. ■
paragraphs.zh: 因此，可选项都不好。所有选项都必须向伊朗强硬派表明，美国有决心对伊朗石油出口实施持续封锁——即使这会在11月中期选举前推高汽油价格。美国已经恢复了对伊朗的禁运，这是一个开端。为显示意志，它还应继续对伊朗的袭击作出对等回应。特朗普发动这场战争是一个愚蠢的错误。无论他如何辗转腾挪，如今也几乎别无选择，只能硬撑到底。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订户可以订阅我们的“观点”新闻简报，其中汇集了我们最精彩的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。

articles.title_en: China’s rulers have a woman problem
articles.title_zh: 中国的统治者有一个女性问题
articles.rubric_en: Antagonism between the sexes does nothing to address China’s demographic problems
articles.rubric_zh: 性别对立无助于解决中国的人口问题

paragraphs.en: CHINA HAS no direct translation of the word “manosphere” but it is plagued by one nonetheless—indeed, the abuse of women on social media is every bit as poisonous there as it is in democracies. The difference is that, in a country where censors rush to silence posts they dislike, the authorities tolerate the manosphere’s vitriol and instead focus their energy on women. The cyberspace regulator’s crackdown on harmful content specifically lists “extreme feminism”, including posts that promote singledom.
paragraphs.zh: 中文里没有“manosphere”（厌女男性网络圈层）这个词的直接对应译法，但中国同样深受其害。事实上，社交媒体上对女性的辱骂在那里和在民主国家一样恶毒。不同之处在于，在一个审查者会迅速压制他们不喜欢的帖子的国家，当局却容忍这种男性圈层的刻毒言论，反而把精力集中在女性身上。网信监管机构打击有害内容的行动明确列出“极端女权主义”，其中包括宣扬单身的帖子。

paragraphs.en: This is just one symptom of a much broader problem that China’s old, male rulers have with the opposite sex. Unlike Asia’s other two big powers, Japan and India, China has never been led by a woman in modern times. As China’s leader, Xi Jinping, has consolidated power, he has also sidelined women. The powerful Politburo, which usually has 24 members, has been exclusively male since 2022. No woman has ever made it to the more important Politburo Standing Committee. No wonder China has been tumbling down international rankings of gender equality.
paragraphs.zh: 这只是中国那些年迈的男性统治者在面对异性时所暴露出的一个更广泛问题的症状。不同于亚洲另外两个大国日本和印度，中国现代以来从未由女性领导。随着中国领导人习近平巩固权力，他也把女性排挤到了边缘。通常有24名成员、权力很大的政治局，自2022年以来一直清一色为男性。更重要的政治局常委会从未有女性进入。难怪中国在国际性别平等排名中一路下滑。

paragraphs.en: At the heart of Communist Party policies towards women is a crushing error. The party is worried about the country’s demography. But it imagines the solution lies in browbeating women and telling them, in traditional tones, to be “virtuous wives and good mothers”. The result is an approach to women that will not only fail to realise the party’s narrow aims, but will also make countless women miserable.
paragraphs.zh: 共产党对女性政策的核心，是一个沉重的错误。党担心国家的人口问题。但它想象中的解决办法，是用传统口吻训诫女性，要求她们做“贤妻良母”。结果，这种对待女性的方式不仅无法实现党那些狭隘目标，还会让无数女性痛苦不堪。

paragraphs.en: Mao Zedong famously claimed that women hold up “half the sky”, but you would never know it today. The party’s disastrous one-child policy, in place until 2015, led to millions of female fetuses being aborted. The inevitable result is that millions of women are now missing. Their absence leads to other terrible outcomes. Counting men aged 23-37 and women aged 22-36, China has 22.5m more men than women. To be a catch, a man needs an education, a solid job, a flat and, in many regions, to pay a hefty fee to his bride’s family. Around a third of young male migrants who intend to marry think they have only a 50% or lower chance of doing so by the age of 30.
paragraphs.zh: 毛泽东有句名言，说妇女能顶“半边天”，但今天你几乎看不出这一点。党灾难性的一孩政策一直实施到2015年，导致数以百万计的女性胎儿被流产。不可避免的结果是，如今有数以百万计的女性“缺失”。她们的缺席又导致其他可怕后果。按23至37岁的男性和22至36岁的女性计算，中国男性比女性多出2250万。一个男人若想成为抢手的结婚对象，需要有教育背景、稳定工作、一套房；在许多地区，还要向新娘家庭支付一大笔彩礼。约三分之一有结婚打算的年轻男性流动人口认为，自己到30岁时结婚的概率只有50%或更低。

paragraphs.en: As a result, resentments abound: many men, especially rural ones, are chauvinists. The sense of bitterness is likely to spread. Women make up more than half of those in higher education; men who lack education or economic prospects risk falling ever further behind.
paragraphs.zh: 结果是怨气四处弥漫：许多男性，尤其是农村男性，抱有大男子主义观念。这种苦涩感很可能继续扩散。接受高等教育的人中，女性占比超过一半；缺乏教育或经济前景的男性则有可能越来越落后。

paragraphs.en: The Chinese government cannot suddenly conjure up millions of missing women to alleviate the situation. But it could introduce policies that boost the marriage rate among Chinese women while improving the choices available to them.
paragraphs.zh: 中国政府无法突然凭空变出数以百万计缺失的女性来缓解局面。但它可以出台一些政策，在提高中国女性结婚率的同时，也改善她们可获得的选择。

paragraphs.en: One idea is to ensure that women have proper rights within marriages. The enforcement of laws on harassment, marital rape and domestic violence is woefully inadequate. More might marry if they were assured of fair treatment in cases of divorce, including payment of child support. If it were easier for wives to divorce bad husbands, more might remarry.
paragraphs.zh: 一个想法是确保女性在婚姻中拥有切实的权利。有关骚扰、婚内强奸和家庭暴力的法律执行严重不足。如果女性确信离婚时能获得公平对待，包括子女抚养费能够得到支付，或许会有更多人愿意结婚。如果妻子更容易与糟糕的丈夫离婚，或许也会有更多人再婚。

paragraphs.en: Fairer divorce settlements might also hasten the end of bride prices. Research suggests a young male migrant must save for six years to afford a bride price of 127,300 yuan ($18,780). A law bans extortionate bride prices already, but it is ambiguous and poorly enforced. Many parents still set great store by the price a daughter can command; the desperation of men to find a partner means that those with money will pay; female divorcees sometimes retain a chunk of it if marriages fail. But the payment both commodifies women and disadvantages impecunious men in rural areas in particular.
paragraphs.zh: 更公平的离婚财产安排也可能加速彩礼的终结。研究显示，一名年轻男性流动人口必须储蓄六年，才负担得起127300元人民币（18780美元）的彩礼。法律已经禁止索要过高彩礼，但规定含糊，执行不力。许多父母仍然非常看重女儿能要到多少彩礼；男性寻找伴侣的迫切感意味着有钱的人会支付；如果婚姻破裂，离异女性有时会保留其中一大部分。但这种支付既把女性商品化，也让缺钱的男性处于不利地位，尤其是在农村地区。

paragraphs.en: More generally, the government could lower the hurdles to home ownership and work that stand in the way of couples. It should buy more of China’s vast tracts of unsold housing, converting it into affordable rental homes favoured by young families. Recent relaxations in the hukou system of internal passports, which make it easier for newcomers to register themselves and their families for schools and other public services in cities, are welcome.
paragraphs.zh: 更广泛地说，政府可以降低挡在情侣面前的购房和工作门槛。它应当购买中国大面积未售出的住房，将其改造成受年轻家庭欢迎的可负担租赁住房。最近对户口这一内部通行证制度的放宽值得欢迎，因为这让新来者更容易在城市为自己和家人登记，获得学校和其他公共服务。

paragraphs.en: Traditionalist men, including those at the top of the Communist Party, may see some of these ideas as foreign impositions. If they stopped chiding Chinese women and tried asking them for their opinions, they might hear a different story. ■
paragraphs.zh: 传统主义男性，包括共产党最高层的一些人，可能会把其中一些想法视为外来的强加之物。如果他们停止斥责中国女性，转而试着询问她们的意见，或许会听到另一种说法。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》订户可以订阅我们的Opinion新闻简报，其中汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。

articles.title_en: How to make AI safe—and lessen dependence on America and China
articles.title_zh: 如何让 AI 更安全，并减少对美国和中国的依赖
articles.rubric_en: State-backed efforts to catch up with frontier models are doomed
articles.rubric_zh: 由国家支持、试图追赶前沿模型的努力注定会失败

paragraphs.en: PROGRESS IS rapidly being made towards regulating the release of powerful new AI models in America. Its government is set to soon outline its plan, after taking suggestions from the industry. Speaking to The Economist this week Sir Demis Hassabis, the boss of Google DeepMind, pitched a hybrid public-private regulator for the industry, echoing our own thoughts. The regulator is modelled on the agency that regulates brokers and stock markets.
paragraphs.zh: 美国在监管强大新 AI 模型发布方面正迅速取得进展。美国政府在听取行业建议后，预计很快将公布其计划。本周，Google DeepMind 负责人德米斯·哈萨比斯爵士在接受《经济学人》采访时，提出为该行业设立一个公私混合监管机构，这与我们的想法相呼应。这个监管机构以监管经纪商和股票市场的机构为蓝本。

paragraphs.en: Rules are needed because the newest frontier models possess some dangerous powers—from hacking critical digital infrastructure to writing recipes for new bioweapons. Without any organised way of dealing with such risks, the government has taken to making up the rules as it goes along in order to control access to Mythos and Sol, Anthropic and OpenAI’s latest offerings. Ideally, predictable rules would be agreed on internationally, especially between America and China, whose models surpass everyone else’s. But time is short. Sir Demis thinks that, if America moves unilaterally, the rest of the world will sign up to its system.
paragraphs.zh: 之所以需要规则，是因为最新的前沿模型具备一些危险能力，从入侵关键数字基础设施到编写新型生物武器的配方，不一而足。在没有任何有组织方式来应对这些风险的情况下，政府为了控制对 Mythos 和 Sol 的访问，开始边走边制定规则；这两者分别是 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的最新产品。理想情况下，各国应就可预期的规则达成国际共识，尤其是美国和中国之间，因为它们的模型领先于其他所有国家。但时间紧迫。德米斯爵士认为，如果美国单方面行动，世界其他国家会加入其体系。

paragraphs.en: He may be right. Yet safety is only one part of the problem. It is increasingly clear that America and China will also restrict access to their models for economic and strategic reasons. Under President Donald Trump, America has had no qualms about using its allies’ military dependence as negotiating leverage; it could one day try to exploit their AI dependence, too. China has treated other exports, such as rare earths, as sources of geopolitical advantage, and probably views AI the same way.
paragraphs.zh: 他也许是对的。然而，安全只是问题的一部分。越来越明显的是，美国和中国还会出于经济和战略原因限制他国使用自己的模型。在唐纳德·特朗普总统任内，美国毫不避讳地把盟友对其军事上的依赖当作谈判筹码；有朝一日，它也可能试图利用盟友对其 AI 的依赖。中国曾把稀土等其他出口品视为地缘政治优势来源，并且很可能也以同样方式看待 AI。

paragraphs.en: The control that these two governments exert over frontier AI puts other countries in a very tight spot, especially as the technology spreads. If America cuts off a country from its models and data centres, “Are your factories going to still run?” asks Arthur Mensch, head of Mistral AI, Europe’s best model maker, on this week’s “Inside Tech” show. Access to AI will also be increasingly important for security.
paragraphs.zh: 这两个政府对前沿 AI 的控制，使其他国家处于非常被动的境地，尤其是在这项技术不断扩散之际。如果美国切断一个国家对其模型和数据中心的访问，“你的工厂还能继续运转吗？”欧洲最佳模型制造商 Mistral AI 的负责人阿瑟·门施在本周的《Inside Tech》节目中问道。获得 AI 的能力对安全也将变得越来越重要。

paragraphs.en: What, then, can other countries do? A state-backed effort to catch up with America’s frontier models would be doomed. OpenAI and Anthropic have each raised well over $100bn in funding so far. Any effort that spends much less is not credible. Even if politicians find the cash, AI research is the sort of endeavour—involving expensive researchers and risky bets—in which governments have a feeble record. Who today remembers Quaero, the rival to Google that the French and German governments announced in 2005?
paragraphs.zh: 那么，其他国家能做什么？由国家支持、试图追赶美国前沿模型的努力注定会失败。到目前为止，OpenAI 和 Anthropic 各自筹集的资金都远超 1000 亿美元。任何投入远低于这一水平的努力都难以令人信服。即便政客们能找到这笔钱，AI 研究也属于那种政府记录乏善可陈的事业：它需要昂贵的研究人员，还要承担高风险押注。今天还有谁记得 Quaero 呢？那是法国和德国政府在 2005 年宣布推出的 Google 竞争对手。

paragraphs.en: Other countries can, though, build data centres. Most will need some of them, to run AI on sensitive data. Local data centres offer insurance against being suddenly cut off from processing power, or “compute”. They also make it easier to maintain a credible ability to switch to open-weight models, which anyone can run if access to frontier capabilities is cut off.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，其他国家可以建设数据中心。多数国家都需要一些数据中心，以便在敏感数据上运行 AI。本地数据中心可以作为一种保险，防止突然被切断处理能力，也就是“算力”。它们还使一国更容易保持一种可信的切换能力：如果前沿能力的访问被切断，就转向开放权重模型，因为任何人都可以运行这类模型。

paragraphs.en: Unfortunately, much of the world is far behind the data-centre buildouts in America and China. Usually, the biggest problems are regulation and access to power. By delaying revenues and locking up capital, procedural snafus can have enormous effects: a nine-month delay worsens the economics of a data centre as much as would doubling its lifetime electricity bill, according to the Carnegie Endowment, a think-tank. The average delay before being connected to the grid is three years in India and Britain and three-and-a-half in Germany and South Korea, compared with two years in America.
paragraphs.zh: 遗憾的是，世界许多地区在数据中心建设方面远远落后于美国和中国。通常，最大的问题是监管和电力获取。程序上的混乱会推迟收入并占用资本，因此可能造成巨大影响：智库卡内基国际和平基金会称，九个月的延误对数据中心经济性的损害，相当于其整个生命周期电费翻倍。接入电网前的平均等待时间在印度和英国为三年，在德国和韩国为三年半，而美国为两年。

paragraphs.en: Speeding up regulatory approval, offering fast-tracks through connection queues and allowing power generation that bypasses the grid would all help. There is no need for subsidies: AI companies are desperate for compute and will pay to get it. There is even the enticing possibility of agreements where AI developers promise to offer a country the same models as America in return for data centres. Simply becoming a big customer of America’s AI labs, by encouraging the diffusion of the technology, could itself build leverage. Model makers need revenue to justify their gigantic investments; they will have a reason to lobby against American protectionism that hurts foreign buyers.
paragraphs.zh: 加快监管审批、为电网接入排队提供快速通道，以及允许绕过电网的发电方式，都会有所帮助。没有必要提供补贴：AI 公司极度渴求算力，并且愿意为此付费。甚至还有一种诱人的可能性：达成协议，让 AI 开发商承诺向某个国家提供与美国相同的模型，以换取数据中心。只要通过鼓励这项技术扩散，成为美国 AI 实验室的大客户，本身就可能建立谈判筹码。模型制造商需要收入来证明其巨额投资是合理的；因此，他们会有理由游说反对损害外国买家的美国保护主义。

paragraphs.en: Some countries are in the fortunate position of having their own choke points. Taiwan has chipmaking; the Netherlands has ASML; South Korea has memory technology. These specialisms should be prized. But even the lucky few cannot build fully sovereign AI capabilities. So countries must prepare to deal transactionally with the AI superpowers. Democratic America is a better partner than authoritarian China. Even so, every source of negotiating leverage will count. ■
paragraphs.zh: 一些国家幸运地拥有自己的关键卡点。台湾有芯片制造；荷兰有 ASML；韩国有存储技术。这些专长应当受到珍视。但即便是少数幸运者，也无法建立完全自主的 AI 能力。因此，各国必须准备好以交易方式同 AI 超级大国打交道。民主的美国是比威权的中国更好的伙伴。即便如此，每一种谈判筹码都将很重要。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订阅用户可以订阅我们的 Opinion 新闻简报，该简报汇集了我们社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信中的最佳内容。

articles.title_en: America should stop making it so hard to have fun
articles.title_zh: 美国不该再把找乐子搞得这么难
articles.rubric_en: Bureaucracy is nightlife’s toughest bouncer
articles.rubric_zh: 官僚主义是夜生活最难缠的门卫

paragraphs.en: In 1660 the laws of the Puritan Massachusetts Bay Colony declared that its nascent watchmen should “examine all night walkers after ten of the clock at night”. This year Boston’s largest police union offered up a modern equivalent, opposing the extension of bar hours to 3am on the grounds that: “Truth be told, not a lot of good happens after midnight.” For a country that prides itself on defending individual liberty, America has long been surprisingly prissy about nightlife and the boozy, exuberant and sometimes disorderly fun that comes with it. It should learn to lighten up.
paragraphs.zh: 1660年，清教徒统治的马萨诸塞湾殖民地法律规定，新设的巡夜人应“盘查所有晚上十点之后在夜间行走的人”。今年，波士顿最大的警察工会提出了一个现代版说法，反对把酒吧营业时间延长到凌晨3点，理由是：“说实话，午夜之后没多少好事发生。”美国以捍卫个人自由自豪，但长期以来，对夜生活以及随之而来的饮酒作乐、兴高采烈和有时有些失序的欢乐，却出人意料地拘谨。它该学着放松一点了。

paragraphs.en: Rules about alcohol are often to blame. When Prohibition ended in 1933 a patchwork of laws emerged in its place, from caps on the number of liquor licences for pubs and restaurants to state monopolies over wholesale liquor distribution.
paragraphs.zh: 酒精相关规则往往是罪魁祸首。1933年禁酒令结束后，取而代之的是一套拼凑而成的法律体系，从限制酒馆和餐厅酒类许可证数量，到由州政府垄断酒类批发分销，不一而足。

paragraphs.en: Over the next decades zoning laws, neighbourhood review boards and other municipal restrictions layered on additional limits for venues that serve alcohol. This bureaucracy was partly motivated by understandable fears over crime and public health. Cities are not just places for drinking and revelry, but also for growing families and people who want safe streets and a good night’s sleep. Drinking too much causes disease, accidents and crime.
paragraphs.zh: 在接下来的几十年里，分区法规、社区审查委员会以及其他市政限制，又给供应酒精的场所叠加了更多约束。这些官僚规定的部分动机，是对犯罪和公共健康的担忧，这可以理解。城市不只是饮酒和狂欢的地方，也是家庭成长、以及人们希望街道安全并睡个好觉的地方。过量饮酒会导致疾病、事故和犯罪。

paragraphs.en: But bureaucracy also takes on a life of its own. The good reasons to regulate nightlife are not a reason to throttle it. The consequence of all this red tape is that in the land of the free, it is simpler in some places for an 18-year-old to buy a semi-automatic rifle than it is for an entrepreneur to open a neighbourhood bar. Government-imposed caps have the predictable effect of driving up prices and smothering competition. The average cost of a liquor licence in New Jersey is estimated to be around $350,000.
paragraphs.zh: 但官僚主义也会自行膨胀。监管夜生活有充分理由，并不等于有理由扼杀它。所有这些繁文缛节的后果是，在这个“自由之邦”，有些地方一个18岁的人购买半自动步枪，比一名创业者开一家社区酒吧还要简单。政府施加的数量上限会推高价格、压制竞争，这是可以预见的结果。据估计，新泽西州一张酒类许可证的平均成本约为35万美元。

paragraphs.en: Thriving nightlife is good for both economic and social reasons. Economically, livelier streets tend to make cities better places to live, helping create productive clusters of skilled young workers who wish to socialise as well as build careers. As a side-benefit, the extra foot traffic can make streets safer.
paragraphs.zh: 繁荣的夜生活无论在经济还是社会层面都有好处。从经济上看，更有活力的街道往往会让城市变得更宜居，有助于形成由熟练年轻劳动者组成的高效集群；他们既想社交，也想发展事业。作为附带好处，更多的行人流量还能让街道更安全。

paragraphs.en: A bustling late-night economy also makes for good social policy. Bars, music venues and nightclubs are the sorts of places where young people can have fun together, regardless of whether alcohol is involved. Official data show that, between 2003 and 2023, the average amount of time 18- to 30-year-old Americans spent socialising each day fell by 38%. According to one survey, roughly one-third of single people aged 22 to 35 say they have not been out on a date in the past year. Dancing is, according to our fact-checked analysis, a far superior activity to doomscrolling.
paragraphs.zh: 热闹的深夜经济也有利于社会政策。酒吧、音乐场所和夜总会，是年轻人可以一起开心玩乐的地方，不管是否涉及饮酒。官方数据显示，2003年至2023年间，18至30岁的美国人每天用于社交的平均时间下降了38%。一项调查显示，22至35岁的单身人士中，约有三分之一表示自己过去一年没有出去约会过。根据我们经过事实核查的分析，跳舞远比沉迷刷负面信息要好得多。

paragraphs.en: American cities should make it easier to create places for people to gather and have fun. Many of those places will be open late into the night and involve alcohol. That means speeding up permits so that applications do not languish as business owners rack up costs; simplifying licensing rules that only a specialist can navigate; and getting rid of arbitrarily low quotas on liquor licences. Cities should also invest more in measures that actually improve safety. Better late-night public transport would be a good start. More police resources would help, too.
paragraphs.zh: 美国城市应该让人们更容易创造聚会和娱乐的场所。其中许多场所会营业到深夜，并涉及酒精。这意味着要加快审批，让申请不至于在企业主不断累积成本时迟迟没有结果；简化只有专家才看得懂的许可规则；并取消对酒类许可证任意设定的过低配额。城市还应该加大对真正改善安全措施的投入。更好的深夜公共交通会是一个不错的起点。投入更多警力也会有所帮助。

paragraphs.en: The good news is that more American cities are now taking an enlightened view of the night. In New York City, red tape that limits dancing in bars has been cut. San Francisco has begun simplifying its permit process for entertainment venues. The debate over Boston’s later closing hours was prompted by hordes of good-natured Scottish football fans who descended on the city for the World Cup in June. The Scots may not be that good at football but they did show the city how wonderful the wee hours can be. Even before then, Boston had increased its supply of liquor licences.
paragraphs.zh: 好消息是，越来越多美国城市现在正以更开明的眼光看待夜晚。在纽约市，限制酒吧跳舞的繁文缛节已经被削减。旧金山已开始简化娱乐场所的许可流程。关于波士顿延迟打烊时间的讨论，是由6月为世界杯涌入该市的大批友善苏格兰球迷引发的。苏格兰人足球或许踢得没那么好，但他们确实向这座城市展示了凌晨时分可以多么美妙。甚至在那之前，波士顿就已经增加了酒类许可证的供应。

paragraphs.en: Keeping up this momentum is good economics and even better social policy. And if that sounds a little dry, there’s a simpler argument: a night out is a lot of fun. More American cities should embrace it. ■
paragraphs.zh: 保持这种势头是明智的经济政策，也是更好的社会政策。如果这听起来有点枯燥，还有一个更简单的理由：晚上出去玩非常有趣。更多美国城市应该拥抱这一点。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订阅用户可以订阅我们的 Opinion 新闻通讯，其中汇集了我们最精彩的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。

articles.title_en: The United States is once again in a state of rebellion
articles.title_zh: 美国再次处于反叛状态
articles.rubric_en: Also this week, the backlash against AI, homelessness, Eisenhower and roads, oil predictions
articles.rubric_zh: 本周还谈到：对人工智能的反弹、无家可归问题、艾森豪威尔与公路、石油预测

paragraphs.en: Letters are welcome via email to letters@economist.comFind out more about how we process your letter
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎通过电子邮件 letters@economist.com 来信。了解更多关于我们如何处理来信的信息。

paragraphs.en: Your sweeping analysis of “The Wrecking-ball revolution” (July 4th) in geopolitics initiated by Donald Trump concluded by contemplating a new balance-of-power regime that nevertheless remains grounded in the principles established by the peace of Westphalia in 1648. Those treaties recognised the sovereign state as “the fundamental notion on which geopolitics is founded”, you said.
paragraphs.zh: 你们对唐纳德·特朗普在地缘政治上发起的“破坏球革命”（7月4日）所作的宏观分析，最后设想了一种新的均势体制，但它仍然植根于1648年《威斯特伐利亚和约》确立的原则。你们说，那些条约承认主权国家是“地缘政治赖以建立的基本概念”。

paragraphs.en: Otto von Bismarck and Henry Kissinger were the masters of balancing power. Both assessed national interests on a truly grand strategic scale. Mr Trump has replaced that with the art of the deal. A good example of this is the Abraham accords between Israel and the United Arab Emirates. The accords bring parties together to negotiate an agreement that serves their mutual interests. Although no overarching geopolitical blueprint may be guiding the process, the parties must still consider the wider consequences that any agreement may have within their respective spheres of influence and among their partners.
paragraphs.zh: 奥托·冯·俾斯麦和亨利·基辛格是平衡权力的大师。两人都能从真正宏大的战略尺度评估国家利益。特朗普先生用交易的艺术取而代之。以色列与阿拉伯联合酋长国之间的《亚伯拉罕协议》就是一个很好的例子。这些协议把各方带到一起，谈判达成一项符合彼此利益的安排。尽管整个过程未必有一套总体地缘政治蓝图作为指引，各方仍必须考虑任何协议可能在各自势力范围内以及在伙伴之间产生的更广泛后果。

paragraphs.en: This emphasis on one-to-one dealmaking may be seen as a new way of rethinking the international order, as you suggested, without losing sight of the enduring logic of the balance of power. Rather than relying on grand designs, bilateral deals are themselves a mechanism for creating a balance of power.
paragraphs.zh: 正如你们所指出的，这种对一对一交易的强调，可以被视为重新思考国际秩序的一种新方式，同时并不忽视均势的持久逻辑。双边交易本身就是一种创造均势的机制，而不是依赖宏大设计。

paragraphs.en: Josef ErnstBremen, Germany
paragraphs.zh: Josef Ernst 德国不来梅

paragraphs.en: The revolt you describe is real enough, but the diagnosis is too convenient. Americans have not suddenly decided that immigration, foreigners and liberalism are intolerable. They have noticed that borders, universities, labour markets and public services are often managed as if citizens were merely one constituency among many, and not necessarily the preferred one.
paragraphs.zh: 你们描述的反叛确实存在，但诊断过于方便。美国人并不是突然认定移民、外国人和自由主义令人无法容忍。他们注意到，边境、大学、劳动力市场和公共服务的管理方式，往往像是把公民只当作众多群体中的一个，而且还不一定是优先考虑的那个。

paragraphs.en: The distinctions matter. Opposition to illegal immigration is not opposition to immigration. Nor is America plainly succumbing to a wave of Christian nationalism. Much of what you call nativism is closer to a demand for cultural confidence, a belief that liberal societies should be able to say what their norms are before importing large numbers of people from countries shaped by very different religious and legal traditions.
paragraphs.zh: 这些区别很重要。反对非法移民并不等于反对移民。美国也并非显然正在屈服于一股基督教民族主义浪潮。你们所谓的本土主义，其中很大一部分更接近于对文化自信的要求，即认为自由社会在从宗教和法律传统截然不同的国家大量引入人口之前，应当能够说清自己的规范是什么。

paragraphs.en: This is not a uniquely American spasm. Britain faces much the same argument, though perhaps with more queuing and less ammunition. One suspects that at certain London brunch tables “racism” remains the most socially efficient explanation for voters who have noticed that the old settlement is not working.
paragraphs.zh: 这并不是美国独有的一阵痉挛。英国面临的争论大体相同，只不过也许排队更多、枪弹更少。有人会怀疑，在伦敦某些早午餐餐桌上，对那些已经发现旧安排行不通的选民来说，“种族主义”仍是社交上最省事的解释。

paragraphs.en: The post-war order is indeed under strain. But the wrecking ball was handed to voters by institutions that stopped asking for consent.
paragraphs.zh: 战后秩序确实承受着压力。但那只破坏球，是由那些不再寻求同意的机构交到选民手里的。

paragraphs.en: Michael LauckHouston
paragraphs.zh: Michael Lauck 休斯敦

paragraphs.en: “Do not compute” (June 27th) highlighted the public backlash to AI data centres. But your example of a mid-size data centre using only as much water as two golf courses was a mild injustice for those of us who enjoy spending our weekends out on the links.
paragraphs.zh: 《不要计算》（6月27日）重点谈到了公众对人工智能数据中心的反弹。不过，你们举例说一个中型数据中心用水量仅相当于两个高尔夫球场，这对我们这些喜欢周末去球场挥杆的人来说，多少有点小小的不公。

paragraphs.en: The sources of the water for a data centre and a golf course differ in ways that matter locally. Golf courses mainly rely on non-potable water from ponds, reclaimed wastewater and the like. By contrast, data centres often tap potable or near-potable freshwater, often from public utilities or aquifers. This distinction is crucial. The issue is not just how much water is used, but whether this competes directly with households.
paragraphs.zh: 数据中心和高尔夫球场的水源不同，而这种差异在当地很重要。高尔夫球场主要依靠池塘、再生废水等非饮用水。相比之下，数据中心往往取用可饮用或接近可饮用的淡水，而且常常来自公共供水系统或含水层。这一区别至关重要。问题不只是用了多少水，而是这种用水是否直接与家庭用水竞争。

paragraphs.en: Corbin DorfnerScottsdale, Arizona
paragraphs.zh: Corbin Dorfner 亚利桑那州斯科茨代尔

paragraphs.en: The conflict around AI and data centres is not so much about the technology itself as it is the far larger contest between those who wish to live in a world ruled by CAESARE (Credentialed Academic Experts Should Always Run Everything) and those who don’t. CAESARE and his loyal followers should first consider how best to go about gaining the trust of the rest of Earth’s inhabitants. Humility and empathy would be a good start.
paragraphs.zh: 围绕人工智能和数据中心的冲突，与其说关乎技术本身，不如说关乎一场更大的较量：一方希望生活在由 CAESARE（“有资质的学术专家应当永远掌管一切”）统治的世界里，另一方则不愿如此。CAESARE 及其忠实追随者应当首先想想，怎样才能最好地赢得地球上其他居民的信任。谦逊和同理心会是一个不错的开端。

paragraphs.en: Steve AcetoMontreat, North Carolina
paragraphs.zh: Steve Aceto 北卡罗来纳州蒙特里特

paragraphs.en: There was one important detail missing from your article on Denver’s progress towards solving its homelessness problem (“Down, not out”, June 20th). Providing temporary shelter and eventually permanent housing to 100% of the currently homeless will not fully solve the problem. The reservoir of potentially new homeless people is an order of magnitude greater than the number of homeless. About half of poverty households spend more than 90% of their income on housing. Each is one small misfortune away from homelessness.
paragraphs.zh: 你们关于丹佛在解决无家可归问题上取得进展的文章（《境况好转，尚未脱困》，6月20日）遗漏了一个重要细节。为目前无家可归者中的100%提供临时住所，并最终提供永久住房，并不能彻底解决问题。潜在新增无家可归者的蓄水池，比现有无家可归者人数高出一个数量级。约有一半贫困家庭把超过90%的收入花在住房上。每一个家庭都只差一次小小的不幸，就可能沦为无家可归。

paragraphs.en: The cost to create housing at this scale is significantly larger than any city can afford, so the short answer to your question about how much progress cities can make without broader support is not much. The economic and political will does not exist to make the substantial investment necessary to truly solve Denver or any city’s homelessness problem.
paragraphs.zh: 按这样的规模建造住房，其成本远超任何城市所能负担。因此，对于你们提出的城市在没有更广泛支持的情况下能取得多少进展这个问题，简短的答案是：不多。无论是丹佛还是任何城市，要真正解决无家可归问题，都需要大量投资，而目前并不存在作出这种投资的经济和政治意愿。

paragraphs.en: Tom RosenthalProfessor emeritus at the David Geffen School of MedicineUniversity of California, Los Angeles
paragraphs.zh: Tom Rosenthal 加州大学洛杉矶分校大卫·格芬医学院荣休教授

paragraphs.en: “The wheel deal” (July 4th) looked at the history of Route 66 and mentioned that Dwight Eisenhower created the federal-highways act after being so impressed with Germany’s autobahn during the second world war. Eisenhower perceived the need for America’s interstate freeways long before that. As a lieutenant-colonel in 1919 he witnessed the American army’s gruelling two-month, 3,200 mile (5,100km) coast-to-coast convoy from Washington, DC, to San Francisco over what had been named the Lincoln Highway.
paragraphs.zh: 《轮上交易》（7月4日）回顾了66号公路的历史，并提到德怀特·艾森豪威尔在第二次世界大战期间对德国高速公路印象深刻，后来制定了联邦公路法。其实，艾森豪威尔早在那之前就已经意识到美国需要州际高速公路。1919年，身为陆军中校的他亲眼见证了美国陆军一次艰苦的、历时两个月、横跨3,200英里（5,100公里）的海岸到海岸车队行军，从华盛顿特区沿着当时被命名为林肯公路的路线前往旧金山。

paragraphs.en: Much of the route west of Chicago was a rutted dirt road. The ordeal deeply influenced the future president regarding the national security imperative for modern road networks, culminating in the Federal Aid Highway Act of 1956. Michael Owen’s splendid book, “After Ike”, recounts the convoy’s ordeal.
paragraphs.zh: 芝加哥以西的大部分路段都是布满车辙的土路。这场磨难深刻影响了这位未来总统对现代道路网络作为国家安全必需品的看法，并最终促成了1956年的《联邦援助公路法》。Michael Owen 的精彩著作《After Ike》讲述了那支车队的艰难经历。

paragraphs.en: DAVID SMOLLARSan Diego
paragraphs.zh: DAVID SMOLLAR 圣迭戈

paragraphs.en: I was surprised to see The Economist acknowledging it was wrong about its prediction that oil prices would remain very high (“We woz wrong about oil”, July 4th). The year is far from over. No one can accurately predict what Donald Trump will do next (except for his insiders) or how those actions may drive prices. You may have lost only the battle and not the war, especially as the ceasefire is unravelling. Maybe you were a bit too quick in claiming defeat.
paragraphs.zh: 看到《经济学人》承认自己关于油价将维持高位的预测错了（《俺们对石油判断错了》，7月4日），我感到惊讶。今年还远未结束。没有人能准确预测唐纳德·特朗普下一步会做什么（他的内部人士除外），也无法预测这些行动会如何推动价格。你们也许只是输掉了一场战斗，而不是整场战争，尤其是停火正在瓦解。也许你们有点太快认输了。

paragraphs.en: Jamal Russell BlackLondon
paragraphs.zh: Jamal Russell Black 伦敦

paragraphs.en: Morris Adelman, the Adam Smith of the oil industry, once said that oil prices are bound to fluctuate between infinity and $2 per barrel. Even he was wrong, because oil prices fell below zero during the pandemic in April 2020. This is a cautionary tale for anyone who makes predictions and the reason why I reject your mea culpa. Why the oil-price shock never materialised is still a mystery. It seems the over-optimism of financial investors in stock markets has leaked into energy and pushed prices lower. It is too early to dismiss the “la-la land” hypothesis.
paragraphs.zh: 被称为石油业亚当·斯密的 Morris Adelman 曾说，油价必然会在无穷大和每桶2美元之间波动。即便是他也错了，因为在2020年4月疫情期间，油价跌到了零以下。对任何做预测的人来说，这是一个警示故事，也是我不接受你们认错的原因。油价冲击为何从未出现，仍然是个谜。似乎是股市金融投资者的过度乐观蔓延到了能源市场，并把价格推低。现在就排除“异想天开”假说还为时过早。

paragraphs.en: Davide TabarelliNE Nomisma EnergiaBologna, Italy
paragraphs.zh: Davide Tabarelli NE Nomisma Energia 意大利博洛尼亚

paragraphs.en: Admitting you made a mistake brought to mind the claim made by Stephen Dedalus in James Joyce’s “Ulysses”. Some “errors are volitional and are the portals of discovery”.
paragraphs.zh: 你们承认自己犯了错误，让人想起詹姆斯·乔伊斯《尤利西斯》中斯蒂芬·迪达勒斯提出的说法。有些“错误是出于意志的，是发现之门”。

paragraphs.en: Gerald SmithWellington, New Zealand
paragraphs.zh: Gerald Smith 新西兰惠灵顿

paragraphs.en: It was a big relief to learn that The Economist is not always wrong and gets the future right from time to time. May I make my own modest prediction? In another quarter of a century, The Economist will still call the OECD “a club of mostly rich countries”.
paragraphs.zh: 得知《经济学人》并不总是错，而且时不时也能正确预见未来，真是让人大大松了一口气。我可以作一个自己小小的预测吗？再过四分之一个世纪，《经济学人》仍会把经合组织称为“一个主要由富国组成的俱乐部”。

paragraphs.en: Michael BildeJaegerspris, Denmark
paragraphs.zh: Michael Bilde 丹麦耶厄斯普里斯

articles.title_en: Would Europeans fight, if it came to that?
articles.title_zh: 如果真到那一步，欧洲人会参战吗？
articles.rubric_en: Putting boots on the ground would not help Ukraine, but it might help Europe find the stomach for war, writes Nathalie Tocci
articles.rubric_zh: 派遣地面部队无助于乌克兰，但也许能帮助欧洲鼓起面对战争的勇气，娜塔莉·托奇写道

paragraphs.en: EUROPEAN LEADERS understand that conflict is closer than ever—hence the sharp rise in defence budgets, as showcased at NATO’s recent summit in Ankara. Spending is not increasing because European armies are planning to fight Russia in Ukraine: Europeans are now the main military backers of Kyiv, but such support is a small fraction of their total military spending. Nor are Europeans spending simply because Donald Trump has demanded it. They are doing so because they consider a kinetic war in Europe beyond Ukraine to be increasingly plausible. This explains why spending is proportionally higher in eastern and northern Europe, including Germany, than in southern and western European countries farther from the front line.
paragraphs.zh: 欧洲领导人明白，冲突比以往任何时候都更近了，因此国防预算急剧上升，北约最近在安卡拉举行的峰会就展示了这一点。开支增加，并不是因为欧洲军队正计划在乌克兰与俄罗斯作战：欧洲人如今是基辅的主要军事支持者，但这种支持只占其军费总支出的一小部分。欧洲人增加开支，也不只是因为唐纳德·特朗普提出了要求。他们这样做，是因为他们认为，乌克兰之外的欧洲发生一场动用武力的战争，可能性正越来越大。这也解释了为什么东欧和北欧，包括德国，国防开支按比例高于离前线更远的南欧和西欧国家。

paragraphs.en: Since Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022, European leaders have often expressed awe at Ukrainian resilience. But there is something else behind Europeans’ amazement: fear that they may not show the same resolve. This is not because of some inherent courage in Ukrainians which Europeans lack. It boils down to the fundamentally different predicaments, physically and psychologically, of nations in war and peace. Can the spirit needed to deter war be summoned in Europe without fully entering one?
paragraphs.zh: 自俄罗斯在2022年发动全面入侵以来，欧洲领导人常常对乌克兰人的韧性表示惊叹。但欧洲人的惊讶背后还有另一层东西：他们担心自己未必能表现出同样的决心。这并不是因为乌克兰人天生具有某种欧洲人缺乏的勇气。归根结底，这是战争中的国家与和平中的国家在身体处境和心理状态上的根本不同。欧洲能否在不完全卷入战争的情况下，唤起遏止战争所需的精神？

paragraphs.en: Spending on defence is one thing, fighting is another. A Gallup poll in 2024 revealed that around a third of Europeans would be unwilling to fight if their own country were attacked. My own country, Italy, was at the bottom of the pack, with a whopping 78% saying they would refuse.
paragraphs.zh: 花钱搞国防是一回事，真正上战场则是另一回事。盖洛普在2024年的一项民调显示，如果自己的国家遭到攻击，约三分之一的欧洲人不愿参战。我的祖国意大利在各国中垫底，高达78%的人说他们会拒绝参战。

paragraphs.en: These figures reveal several things at once. Worryingly, they show the growing strength of far-right and far-left forces openly sympathetic to Russia and susceptible to its propaganda demonising Europe’s rearmament. The figures may, though, also indicate that surveyed Europeans simply don’t believe war is coming their way. Indeed, the number of Europeans ready to defend their country is much higher in northern and eastern European states closer to Ukraine, especially those that are in neither the European Union nor NATO, and thus presumably feel more vulnerable.
paragraphs.zh: 这些数字同时揭示了几件事。令人担忧的是，它们显示，公开同情俄罗斯、并容易受其宣传影响的极右翼和极左翼力量正在壮大；俄罗斯的宣传把欧洲重新武装妖魔化。不过，这些数字也可能表明，受访的欧洲人只是并不相信战争会降临到自己头上。事实上，在距离乌克兰更近的北欧和东欧国家，愿意保卫自己国家的欧洲人比例要高得多，尤其是在那些既不属于欧盟也不属于北约、因而大概感到更脆弱的国家。
