paragraphs.en: Geography, then, plays a crucial role. But so do politics, institutions and the media. It is hardly surprising that Estonians are more willing to fight than Italians, if tens of thousands of Estonian volunteers regularly train for a potential attack from its vastly larger next-door neighbour, while Italians are drugged with TV talk shows—heavily infiltrated by pro-Russia propagandists—which deride European attempts to bolster societal resilience. When Sauli Niinisto, a former Finnish president, penned an EU report on preparedness, the public response in countries like mine ranged from indifference to scorn. If institutions, politics and the media don’t take the possibility of war seriously, little wonder that citizens don’t either, rejecting calls for conscription or cuts to social spending for the sake of defence. If war is not seen as plausible, military spending is easily portrayed as a mere ploy to enrich the defence-industrial complex at citizens’ expense.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，地理位置起着关键作用。但政治、制度和媒体也同样重要。爱沙尼亚人比意大利人更愿意参战，这并不令人意外：成千上万的爱沙尼亚志愿者定期训练，以应对来自其大得多的邻国的潜在攻击；而意大利人则被电视谈话节目麻痹，这些节目严重渗透着亲俄宣传者，他们嘲笑欧洲增强社会韧性的努力。当前芬兰总统绍利·尼尼斯托撰写一份关于备战准备的欧盟报告时，在像我的国家这样的地方，公众反应从冷漠到讥讽不等。如果制度、政治和媒体都不认真对待战争的可能性，那么公民也不认真对待就毫不奇怪了，他们会拒绝征兵呼吁，也会拒绝为了国防而削减社会开支。如果战争不被视为可能发生，军费开支就很容易被描绘成只是让国防工业复合体以公民为代价发财的把戏。

paragraphs.en: But the plausibility of war is exactly the point. During a recent meeting, a former high-ranking Ukrainian official put it bluntly: “We’re not superhuman, we’re just at war.” In war, cognition and emotion become geared towards survival. Sacrifices are made, bravery wins out over exhaustion and creativity is unleashed. Without war, the bright minds behind Ukraine’s myriad recent military innovations would probably have trodden the same path as most of the continent’s promising young professionals, forging careers that were rewarding but unremarkable. Because of war, they were spurred to make technological advances that will go down in history. So yes, today Europeans express their reluctance to fight, but they are probably indicating more their abhorrence of war than their cowardly indifference to it.
paragraphs.zh: 但战争是否可能发生，正是问题的关键。在最近一次会议上，一位乌克兰前高级官员直截了当地说：“我们不是超人，我们只是在打仗。”在战争中，认知和情绪都会转向生存。人们作出牺牲，勇敢战胜疲惫，创造力被释放出来。如果没有战争，乌克兰近年来无数军事创新背后的那些聪明头脑，很可能会走上欧洲大陆大多数有前途的年轻专业人士同样的道路，打造一份有回报但并不出众的事业。正因为战争，他们才被激励去取得将载入史册的技术进步。所以，是的，如今欧洲人表达了他们不愿参战的态度，但他们所表明的，可能更多是对战争的憎恶，而不是对战争的懦弱冷漠。

paragraphs.en: Which leads to what may be the key point about deterrence: Europeans are less likely to have to fight if they are prepared to do so physically and psychologically. It is that preparedness that can make deterrence against Russia work. But it is precisely the absence of war that reduces preparedness and deterrence. So can societal resilience be substantially hardened in peacetime?
paragraphs.zh: 这就引出了关于威慑也许最关键的一点：如果欧洲人在身体和心理上都准备好参战，他们真正不得不参战的可能性反而更低。正是这种准备，能让针对俄罗斯的威慑发挥作用。但恰恰是没有战争，削弱了准备和威慑。那么，在和平时期，社会韧性能否得到大幅增强？

paragraphs.en: One way to toughen up populations is to put boots on the ground in Ukraine. For months, a European coalition of the willing has drawn up plans to deploy a “reassurance force” to Ukraine after a ceasefire. These discussions have not led anywhere because a truce is not in sight. Their aim was mainly political: to show European solidarity with Ukraine and encourage American engagement. But Ukraine is under no illusions about the fact that deploying a few thousand European soldiers far from the front line in a hypothetical post-ceasefire scenario would make no difference to its defence. As for Mr Trump, by now few if any Europeans believe he can be kept steadily on side, despite his unexpectedly warm words in Ankara.
paragraphs.zh: 让民众变得更坚韧的一种办法，是向乌克兰派遣地面部队。数月来，一个由愿意行动的欧洲国家组成的联盟，一直在制定计划，准备在停火后向乌克兰部署一支“安抚部队”。这些讨论没有取得进展，因为停战仍看不到希望。它们的目标主要是政治性的：展示欧洲对乌克兰的团结，并鼓励美国参与。但乌克兰并不幻想，在一个假设的停火后场景中，把几千名欧洲士兵部署到远离前线的地方，会对其防务产生什么影响。至于特朗普先生，到目前为止，尽管他在安卡拉出人意料地说了些热情的话，几乎没有欧洲人还相信他能被稳定地拉在同一边。

paragraphs.en: A European reassurance force would, then, do little for Ukraine. But deploying it now, before a hypothetical ceasefire, could benefit Europe, encouraging citizens to develop a mindset that would better prepare them for war. Yes, it would put European soldiers in harm’s way—any such move would infuriate the Kremlin—but the continent is already in a hybrid conflict, even if many refuse to recognise it. Were European soldiers to be stationed in Ukraine, it would bring reality closer to home. And perhaps a fraction of Ukraine’s courage, humility and creativity could infect other European societies. European troops would not be put in harm’s way out of sheer generosity, but to help prepare for war—and prevent it from spilling into the rest of the continent.■
paragraphs.zh: 因此，一支欧洲安抚部队对乌克兰帮助不大。但如果现在就部署它，而不是等到某个假设的停火之后，可能会让欧洲受益，促使公民形成一种更能为战争做好准备的心态。是的，这会让欧洲士兵置身危险之中，任何这样的举动都会激怒克里姆林宫，但欧洲大陆已经处在一场混合冲突之中，即便许多人拒绝承认。如果欧洲士兵驻扎在乌克兰，现实就会离欧洲人的日常生活更近一些。也许乌克兰的勇气、谦卑和创造力中会有一小部分感染其他欧洲社会。让欧洲军队置身危险，并不是出于纯粹的慷慨，而是为了帮助欧洲为战争做好准备，并防止战争蔓延到欧洲大陆其他地方。■

paragraphs.en: Nathalie Tocci is a professor at the Johns Hopkins School of Advanced International Studies, Europe, and a senior fellow at Bocconi University’s Institute for European Policymaking.
paragraphs.zh: 娜塔莉·托奇是约翰斯·霍普金斯大学高级国际研究学院欧洲校区教授，也是博科尼大学欧洲政策制定研究所高级研究员。

articles.title_en: Israel’s future hangs in the balance
articles.title_zh: 以色列的未来悬而未决
articles.rubric_en: The country’s coming election may be its most important ever
articles.rubric_zh: 该国即将举行的选举或许是其有史以来最重要的一次

paragraphs.en: “Israel is stronger than ever!” After four years, a devastating terrorist attack, multiple wars and international opprobrium, Israel’s prime minister, Binyamin Netanyahu, is nothing if not bullish. Launching his 12th election campaign as leader of the Likud party on June 30th, he told a friendly interviewer and rapturous audience that Israel’s wars “never end. Do you want to live? In the Middle East and the world? Be strong.” Israel is militarily and financially strong. But it is stuck.
paragraphs.zh: “以色列比以往任何时候都更强大！”经历了四年、一场毁灭性的恐怖袭击、多场战争和国际社会的谴责之后，以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡依然十分乐观。6月30日，他以利库德党领袖身份启动自己的第12次竞选活动时，对一位立场友好的采访者和热烈的观众说，以色列的战争“永远不会结束。你想活下去吗？在中东和这个世界上？那就要强大。”以色列在军事和财政上确实强大。但它陷入了僵局。

paragraphs.en: The Knesset, Israel’s parliament, is set to dissolve on July 17th. The country will vote for a new government on October 27th in one of the most consequential elections of its history. It will be voters’ first chance to pass judgment on their leaders since the terrorist attacks of October 7th 2023. But more than that, they will have to determine what kind of future they want for their country. And so far, that seems likely to be a choice between an ultranationalist and religious coalition that insists that only hardline militarism can secure the Jewish state’s future, and a gaggle of opposition parties offering an alternative vision that they have yet to fully define.
paragraphs.zh: 以色列议会克奈塞特定于7月17日解散。该国将在10月27日投票选出新政府，这将是其历史上最具影响力的选举之一。这将是自2023年10月7日恐怖袭击以来，选民第一次有机会评判他们的领导人。但更重要的是，他们必须决定希望自己的国家拥有什么样的未来。到目前为止，这似乎很可能是在两者之间作出选择：一边是极端民族主义和宗教联盟，坚称只有强硬的军事主义才能保障这个犹太国家的未来；另一边是一群反对党，它们提出了另一种愿景，但尚未完全界定清楚。

paragraphs.en: None of the wars triggered by the atrocities of October 7th, which were committed on Mr Netanyahu’s watch, has ended. Instead, America has imposed uneasy and inadequate ceasefires on Israel. Its army, still deployed in Gaza, southern Lebanon and Syria, is overstretched. Israel has battered its enemies, but Hamas in Gaza and Hizbullah in Lebanon both remain entrenched and are rearming. And over two wars, even backed by America’s military might, Israel has failed to destroy Iran’s nuclear and ballistic-missile programmes or to topple its regime.
paragraphs.zh: 10月7日的暴行发生在内塔尼亚胡执政期间，由此引发的多场战争没有一场已经结束。相反，美国向以色列强加了不稳固且不充分的停火。以色列军队仍部署在加沙、黎巴嫩南部和叙利亚，已经不堪重负。以色列重创了敌人，但加沙的哈马斯和黎巴嫩的真主党仍然根基稳固，并在重新武装。经过两场战争，即便有美国军事力量支持，以色列也未能摧毁伊朗的核计划和弹道导弹计划，或推翻其政权。

paragraphs.en: What is more, Israel has paid a heavy diplomatic price. Its international standing is hugely diminished because of its brutality in Gaza. Before October 7th it was on the brink of a regional breakthrough, including, perhaps, a historic deal with Saudi Arabia. It still has allies in the Middle East, but they do their utmost to conceal that. Even the United Arab Emirates, which defended itself against Iranian attacks with Israeli missile-defence systems, was quick to deny Mr Netanyahu’s claims in March of a visit to Abu Dhabi.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，以色列付出了沉重的外交代价。由于其在加沙的残酷行径，以色列的国际地位大幅下降。10月7日之前，它正处在区域突破的边缘，其中也许包括与沙特阿拉伯达成一项历史性协议。它在中东仍有盟友，但这些盟友竭力掩饰这一点。即使是曾用以色列导弹防御系统抵御伊朗袭击的阿拉伯联合酋长国，也迅速否认了内塔尼亚胡3月关于访问阿布扎比的说法。

paragraphs.en: That is a loss but more worrying for Israel is the erosion in support from its Western allies, notably America. According to the most recent polling by the Pew Research Centre, 62% of Americans view the Israeli government unfavourably, up from 43% in 2022. Donald Trump, whom Mr Netanyahu once described as “the greatest friend that the State of Israel has ever had in the White House”, has left Israel out of America’s talks with Iran, while calling the Israeli leader “fucking crazy” for disrupting the ceasefire in Lebanon.
paragraphs.zh: 这是一个损失，但对以色列来说更令人担忧的是，西方盟友，尤其是美国，对它的支持正在减弱。根据皮尤研究中心最新民调，62%的美国人对以色列政府持负面看法，高于2022年的43%。唐纳德·特朗普曾被内塔尼亚胡形容为“以色列国在白宫拥有过的最伟大的朋友”，如今却把以色列排除在美国与伊朗的谈判之外，并因内塔尼亚胡破坏黎巴嫩停火而称这位以色列领导人“他妈的疯了”。

paragraphs.en: Israel and the world around it have changed profoundly but on October 27th Israel’s prime minister, who was first elected in 1996 and has spent 19 years in office, will offer more of the same. To win, he needs to detach himself from the failures that led to October 7th. Mr Netanyahu repeatedly lists the leaders of Hamas, Hizbullah and Iran assassinated by Israel, the military infrastructure it has destroyed and “security zones” it occupies. But none of this has yielded his oft-promised “total victory”. Israel under his continuing rule would face more war and isolation.
paragraphs.zh: 以色列及其周边世界都已发生深刻变化，但在10月27日，这位1996年首次当选、累计执政19年的以色列总理仍将拿出更多老一套。要赢得选举，他需要把自己同导致10月7日袭击的失败切割开来。内塔尼亚胡反复列举以色列暗杀的哈马斯、真主党和伊朗领导人，列举它摧毁的军事基础设施，以及它占领的“安全区”。但这一切都没有带来他屡屡承诺的“彻底胜利”。在他继续统治下的以色列将面临更多战争和孤立。

paragraphs.en: Might his opponents set the country on a new path? In recent weeks the new vaguely centrist party of Gadi Eisenkot, Yashar (meaning “honest”) has begun to overtake Likud in the polls. Mr Eisenkot, a former head of the Israel Defence Forces (idf), said at the launch of his party’s manifesto that Israel needed “to know when to go to war”, rather than seeing in war “an aim to itself”. He and other opposition leaders are united in criticising Mr Netanyahu for the inconclusive outcomes of Israel’s wars. They highlight the strain this has put on the idf, especially its reservists, many of whom have served for hundreds of days. But thus far none has presented a coherent alternative to Mr Netanyahu’s vision.
paragraphs.zh: 他的对手能否把国家带上新道路？最近几周，加迪·艾森科特新成立的、立场大致中间派的政党Yashar（意为“诚实”）开始在民调中超过利库德党。艾森科特曾任以色列国防军（IDF）总参谋长，他在发布该党宣言时说，以色列需要“知道何时开战”，而不是把战争视为“目的本身”。他和其他反对派领导人在批评内塔尼亚胡方面立场一致，认为以色列多场战争的结果都没有定论。他们强调，这给以色列国防军，尤其是预备役军人带来了压力，其中许多人已经服役数百天。但到目前为止，还没有人提出一种连贯的、可替代内塔尼亚胡愿景的方案。

paragraphs.en: Their reticence may be explained by the fact that, although Mr Netanyahu’s coalition is lagging behind in most polls, the Israeli population as a whole has shifted rightward. In a recent survey, 38% said they have moved to the right since October 7th; only 7% have shifted leftward.
paragraphs.zh: 他们的沉默或许可以这样解释：尽管内塔尼亚胡联盟在大多数民调中落后，但以色列整体民意已经向右转。在最近一项调查中，38%的受访者说自己自10月7日以来更加右倾；只有7%转向左翼。

paragraphs.en: “The focus on the personnel issues reflects a reluctance to address the most critical issues of this election, the missions themselves,” says Omer Dank, a former air-force lieutenant colonel and strategic planner in the idf. Mr Netanyahu’s opponents will not refer directly to the reasons the idf is so overstretched: the lack of a resolution in Gaza or Lebanon; the “security zones” strategy; and the massive expansion of settlements in the occupied West Bank which are guarded by the army.
paragraphs.zh: “对人事问题的关注反映出一种不愿触及本次选举最关键议题的态度，也就是这些任务本身，”前空军中校、以色列国防军战略规划人员奥默·丹克说。内塔尼亚胡的对手不会直接谈及以色列国防军如此不堪重负的原因：加沙或黎巴嫩问题缺乏解决方案；“安全区”战略；以及被占领的约旦河西岸定居点大规模扩张，而这些定居点由军队守卫。

paragraphs.en: Ending Israel’s wars will be the most urgent task for next government. But doing so will get barely a mention in the campaign. “It’s very hard to have that conversation in Israel after October 7th,” explains Mr Dank. Gil Murciano, the head of Mitvim, a foreign-policy think-tank in Israel warns: “In private all the politicians are aware that the next prime minister, assuming it’s not Mr Netanyahu, will have to immediately start addressing the situations in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria. The alternative will be Israel becoming a pariah state.” The opposition parties do not talk about this in public. “They don’t want to scare away their voters,” explains Mr Dank, by giving Mr Netanyahu the chance to portray them as weak and defeatist.
paragraphs.zh: 结束以色列的战争将是下一届政府最紧迫的任务。但在竞选中，这一点几乎不会被提及。“10月7日之后，在以色列很难进行那样的谈话，”丹克解释说。以色列外交政策智库Mitvim负责人吉尔·穆尔恰诺警告说：“私下里，所有政治人物都清楚，下一任总理，假设不是内塔尼亚胡，就必须立即开始处理加沙、黎巴嫩和叙利亚的局势。另一种结果将是以色列变成一个贱民国家。”反对党不会公开谈论此事。丹克解释说，“他们不想吓跑自己的选民”，因为那会给内塔尼亚胡机会，把他们描绘成软弱和失败主义。

paragraphs.en: Still, he believes that, should Mr Netanyahu lose, his successors could act differently. To the prime minister, ending the wars would mean admitting defeat. A new government could bring them to a close. But if it fails to act quickly, he fears that, like Mr Netanyahu, it would become “invested” in them too.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，他相信，如果内塔尼亚胡败选，其继任者可以采取不同做法。对总理来说，结束战争就意味着承认失败。一个新政府可以让这些战争收场。但他担心，如果新政府不迅速行动，它也会像内塔尼亚胡一样对这些战争产生“利益绑定”。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps most terrifying for the voters, at least judging by their politicians’ silence on the matter, are Israel’s relations with the Palestinians. Israel’s next government will need to end the current wars but it will also need to prevent the outbreak of another—in the occupied West Bank. Security officials warn that Palestinian anger about the spread of Jewish settlements and increasing settler violence against Palestinians with the support of the idf could boil over in a new intifada (uprising).
paragraphs.zh: 至少从政客们对此保持沉默来看，对选民来说或许最可怕的问题，是以色列与巴勒斯坦人的关系。以色列下一届政府需要结束当前战争，但也需要防止另一场战争在被占领的约旦河西岸爆发。安全官员警告说，犹太定居点扩张以及定居者在以色列国防军支持下对巴勒斯坦人的暴力不断增加，已经激起巴勒斯坦人的愤怒，可能会引发新的“因提法达”（起义）。

paragraphs.en: The expansion has been part of a policy of de facto annexation led by Mr Netanyahu’s hard-right allies, in particular the finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich. He has funnelled ever more public money to the settlements. “Netanyahu’s government has empowered a radical minority to call the shots in the West Bank,” says Rami Hod, head of the Berl Katznelson Centre, a think-tank aligned with the Labour Party.
paragraphs.zh: 这种扩张一直是内塔尼亚胡极右翼盟友推行的事实吞并政策的一部分，尤其是财政部长贝扎莱尔·斯莫特里赫。他把越来越多公共资金输送到定居点。“内塔尼亚胡政府让一个激进少数派在约旦河西岸发号施令，”与工党关系密切的智库贝尔·卡茨内尔森中心负责人拉米·霍德说。

paragraphs.en: Mr Hod admits that even if his party, which has only four Knesset seats and has now merged with Meretz, an even smaller Zionist-left party to form a new group, the Democrats, is part of a coalition replacing Mr Netanyahu, ending Israel’s occupation of the West Bank will not be on its agenda. “The most immediate threat is that the current government’s annexation plan will plunge us into another war. We have to insist that at the very least a new government rewinds that, dismantles illegal settler farms and curbs settler violence,” he argues. On July 13th the leader of the Democrats, Yair Golan, publicly called for such steps. He was angrily rebuffed by Avigdor Lieberman, the head of Yisrael Beitenu, an ultranationalist party also opposed to Mr Netanyahu, who recommended “focusing on what’s in consensus”. To have any chance of forming a new government, these parties will have to co-operate.
paragraphs.zh: 霍德承认，即便他的政党加入一个取代内塔尼亚胡的联盟，结束以色列对约旦河西岸的占领也不会列入议程。该党在克奈塞特只有四个席位，如今已与更小的锡安主义左翼政党梅雷兹合并，组成一个名为“民主党人”的新团体。他认为：“最迫在眉睫的威胁是，现政府的吞并计划会把我们拖入另一场战争。我们必须坚持，新政府至少要倒转这一进程，拆除非法定居者农场，并遏制定居者暴力。”7月13日，“民主党人”领导人亚伊尔·戈兰公开呼吁采取这些措施。他遭到以色列是我们的家园党领袖阿维格多·利伯曼的愤怒驳斥。这个极端民族主义政党也反对内塔尼亚胡，利伯曼建议“专注于有共识的事情”。这些政党若想有任何机会组建新政府，就必须合作。

paragraphs.en: The exchange highlighted the opposition’s greatest weakness. In nearly every poll since October 2023 Mr Netanyahu’s opponents have maintained a small but constant lead over his coalition of far-right and religious parties. But they remain diverse and divided, as do their voters.
paragraphs.zh: 这场交锋凸显了反对派最大的弱点。自2023年10月以来，几乎每一项民调中，内塔尼亚胡的对手都对他的极右翼和宗教政党联盟保持着微弱但稳定的领先。但他们依然构成多元、彼此分裂，他们的选民也是如此。

paragraphs.en: On average, The Economist’s poll tracker predicts Mr Netanyahu’s opponents will secure 66 of the Knesset’s 120 seats in the next Knesset. But these numbers represent a wide range of voters, including many right-wingers who not only support Israel’s occupation of the West Bank, but are also adamantly opposed to forming a government with its Arab parties. To dislodge Mr Netanyahu, the opposition not only needs to win a majority. It needs to find a way to work together.
paragraphs.zh: 平均来看，《经济学人》的民调追踪器预测，内塔尼亚胡的对手将在下一届克奈塞特120个席位中获得66席。但这些数字代表着范围很广的选民，其中包括许多右翼人士，他们不仅支持以色列对约旦河西岸的占领，也坚决反对与阿拉伯政党组建政府。要赶走内塔尼亚胡，反对派不仅需要赢得多数席位，还需要找到共同合作的办法。

paragraphs.en: Once that would have meant finding some consensus about the Palestinians. Resolving Israel’s deepest conflict used to be the defining split in its politics. Today, the trauma of October 7th means no mainstream politician is interested—in public—in any resolution. No one is talking about a two-state solution. Instead, the wars have created a new fault line—between those who are willing to fight on Israel’s behalf and those who are not.
paragraphs.zh: 在过去，这会意味着要在巴勒斯坦人问题上找到某种共识。解决以色列最深层的冲突曾经是其政治中的决定性分野。如今，10月7日的创伤意味着，没有主流政治人物有兴趣在公开场合谈论任何解决方案。没有人在谈“两国方案”。相反，战争制造了一条新的断层线：一边是愿意代表以色列参战的人，另一边是不愿意的人。

paragraphs.en: Mr Netanyahu’s most dependable partners are the ultra-Orthodox—or Haredi—parties. His alliance with them goes back decades. They have been a mainstay of nearly all his coalitions but now, along with his responsibility for the October 7th attacks, they may be his biggest liability.
paragraphs.zh: 内塔尼亚胡最可靠的伙伴是极端正统派，也就是哈雷迪派政党。他与他们的联盟可以追溯到几十年前。他们一直是他几乎所有执政联盟的支柱，但现在，除了他对10月7日袭击负有责任之外，他们也可能成为他最大的包袱。

paragraphs.en: Anger towards the Haredim is rooted in their refusal to serve in the idf. In 1948, when Israel was founded, 400 religious scholars were granted an exemption from military service. It applies to as many as 90,000 today. In 2012 the Supreme Court ruled that the exemption was unconstitutional. The ultra-Orthodox parties have demanded its restoration but Mr Netanyahu has failed to muster a majority for that at a time when Israel’s conscript army is approaching its third year of multiple wars.
paragraphs.zh: 对哈雷迪派的愤怒源于他们拒绝在以色列国防军服役。1948年以色列建国时，400名宗教学者获得兵役豁免。如今，这一豁免适用于多达90,000人。2012年，最高法院裁定该豁免违宪。极端正统派政党要求恢复豁免，但在以色列征兵制军队即将进入多场战争的第三年之际，内塔尼亚胡未能为此凑足多数。

paragraphs.en: The promise to ensure the ultra-Orthodox serve animates the campaigns of all the main opposition parties, but they want to go further. Voters may well support that. The Haredim currently constitute around 14% of Israel’s 10m-strong population, but high birth-rates make them its fastest-growing group. A wide range of benefits and public funding, which have only increased over the years, subsidises a community where many men spend their lives studying the Torah. Around half do not work. Mr Netanyahu says Iran and the Palestinians imperil the Jewish state’s future, but to many Israelis this shift in the social fabric represents the existential threat.
paragraphs.zh: 确保极端正统派服役的承诺推动着所有主要反对党的竞选，但他们还想走得更远。选民很可能支持这一点。哈雷迪派目前约占以色列1,000万人口的14%，但高出生率使他们成为增长最快的群体。范围广泛的福利和公共资金补贴了这个群体，而且多年来只增不减；在这个群体中，许多男性终生学习《托拉》。大约一半人不工作。内塔尼亚胡说，伊朗和巴勒斯坦人危及这个犹太国家的未来，但对许多以色列人来说，社会结构的这种变化才是生存性威胁。

paragraphs.en: “This is not just about the draft or participation in the workforce,” wails Eli Paley, an influential ultra-Orthodox publisher. “If the opposition wanted to solve these issues, there are pragmatic solutions we could reach through dialogue which would allow more Haredim to serve in the security forces and work, while guaranteeing our core ideology of Torah study.” He accuses the opposition of “trying to dismantle Haredi autonomy as part of a hidden progressive agenda of social-engineering”.
paragraphs.zh: “这不只是征兵或参与劳动力市场的问题，”有影响力的极端正统派出版人埃利·佩利哀叹道。“如果反对派想解决这些问题，我们可以通过对话达成务实方案，让更多哈雷迪人服役于安全部队并参加工作，同时保证我们以研习《托拉》为核心的意识形态。”他指责反对派“试图把拆解哈雷迪自治作为一项隐藏的进步主义社会工程议程的一部分”。

paragraphs.en: A new government, without Mr Netanyahu, is unlikely to include the ultra-Orthodox. That would probably mean that Torah students would be drafted. It would also almost certainly mean such communities would get less government money, perhaps most crucially, for their schools.
paragraphs.zh: 一个没有内塔尼亚胡的新政府不太可能包括极端正统派。这大概意味着《托拉》学生会被征召入伍。几乎可以肯定的是，这也意味着这些社群将得到更少政府资金，也许最关键的是，他们的学校会少拿钱。

paragraphs.en: Nearly half the children who began school in Israel last year either went to ultra-Orthodox institutions, which barely teach “secular” subjects such as maths, or were Israeli-Arabs whose schools have been neglected by successive governments. They are receiving what Dan Ben-David, head of the Shoresh Institute for Socioeconomic Research in Tel Aviv, describes as a “third-world education”. “A new government, backed by the public’s anger with the Haredim, will have the opportunity to enact radical reform. The good news is that there’s still time,” he argues. It would mean restructuring the entire education and benefits systems and, he suggests, diverting funding away from schools that do not teach a new national curriculum and that discourage participation in the workforce.
paragraphs.zh: 去年在以色列入学的儿童中，近一半要么进入极端正统派机构，这些机构几乎不教授数学等“世俗”科目；要么是以色列阿拉伯人，他们的学校被历届政府忽视。特拉维夫Shoresh社会经济研究所负责人丹·本-戴维称，他们接受的是“第三世界教育”。他认为：“一个新政府在公众对哈雷迪人的愤怒支持下，将有机会推行激进改革。好消息是，现在仍有时间。”这将意味着重组整个教育和福利体系；他建议，把资金从那些不教授新国家课程、并且不鼓励参与劳动力市场的学校转走。

paragraphs.en: “These elections may be Israel’s last opportunity to pivot toward a sustainable long-term future,” says Mr Ben-David. Just over 10% of Israel’s workforce is employed in the tech sector, perhaps the highest share in the world. But it relies on a core of fewer than 300,000 researchers, academics and doctors. “We’re a small country which has put all its eggs in one tech basket,” says Mr Ben-David. “It’s not just our economy which is dependent on this small and highly mobile group; it’s also our very survival in a hostile region. These are the people developing Israel’s advanced defence systems.” He is afraid not only that they will leave, but that Israel is failing to produce its next generation of researchers.
paragraphs.zh: “这些选举可能是以色列转向可持续长期未来的最后机会，”本-戴维说。以色列劳动力中略高于10%的人受雇于科技行业，这一比例也许是全球最高的。但它依赖的核心群体不足300,000人，包括研究人员、学者和医生。“我们是一个小国，却把所有鸡蛋都放进了科技这一个篮子里，”本-戴维说。“依赖这个规模小且高度流动群体的不只是我们的经济；在一个敌对地区，我们的生存本身也依赖他们。这些人正在开发以色列的先进防御系统。”他担心的不只是他们会离开，还担心以色列正在无法培养下一代研究人员。

paragraphs.en: For decades Mr Ben-David was a lone voice but in this election the opposition seems to be listening. “Israel is not viable if we don’t integrate the Haredi community into society,” says Liran Avisar Ben-Horin, the policy chief of Together, a centre-right party led by Naftali Bennett, a former prime minister.Together’s educational programme, “From Tribes to Nation”, envisages bringing ultra-Orthodox and Arab schools into a single regulatory system.
paragraphs.zh: 几十年来，本-戴维一直是孤独的声音，但在这次选举中，反对派似乎开始倾听。“如果我们不把哈雷迪社群融入社会，以色列就无法维持下去，”中右翼政党Together的政策主管利兰·阿维萨尔·本-霍林说。该党由前总理纳夫塔利·贝内特领导。Together的教育方案“从部落到国家”设想把极端正统派学校和阿拉伯学校纳入一个统一的监管体系。

paragraphs.en: After countless elections gripped solely by the question of whether Mr Netanyahu would win or lose, there is a refreshing ferment of policy ideas, at least on domestic matters, to this campaign. “After so many years when the country was run according to what was good for Netanyahu, we’re now working out Israel’s strategy,” says Shaul Meridor, a candidate for Yashar and the former head of the Treasury’s budget department. “It means basic things like re-establishing norms, checks and balances, rebuilding the demoralised civil service and Israel’s international relations.”
paragraphs.zh: 在无数次选举都只被内塔尼亚胡会赢还是会输这一问题所主导之后，这次竞选至少在国内事务上出现了一股令人耳目一新的政策思想活力。“这么多年来，这个国家一直按照对内塔尼亚胡有利的方式运转；现在我们正在制定以色列的战略，”Yashar候选人、前财政部预算司负责人绍尔·梅里多尔说。“这意味着一些基本事项，比如重建规范、制衡机制，重建士气低落的公务员体系以及以色列的国际关系。”

paragraphs.en: The reports of a growing brain-drain have brought a fresh urgency, bordering on desperation, to such discussions. Many Israelis already felt as if they were losing their country in 2023 when the government tried, unsuccessfully, to push through judicial reforms that would have drastically weakened the Supreme Court. In a recent survey a majority of liberal Israelis said a victory in this election for the Netanyahu camp would be “intolerable”.
paragraphs.zh: 关于人才外流加剧的报道，使这些讨论有了新的紧迫感，几近绝望。2023年，当政府试图强行推进司法改革、大幅削弱最高法院却未成功时，许多以色列人已经感觉自己仿佛正在失去自己的国家。在最近一项调查中，多数自由派以色列人表示，内塔尼亚胡阵营若在本次选举中获胜，将是“无法忍受的”。

paragraphs.en: The prime minister’s supporters are dismissive. “Israelis like to live in existential angst,” says Michael Eisenberg, a successful venture capitalist who advises the prime minister. “That doesn’t mean Israel isn’t facing serious challenges,” but given time they will largely solve themselves. Israel’s tech economy is resilient, he insists, and remains attractive enough to foreign investors to survive both the brain-drain and Israel’s international isolation. One way or another, the desire to be part of Israeli society will, he believes, ultimately draw young Haredim into the workforce “Sociological change takes time. The world wants what Israel is selling and the changing geopolitical order will be much more sympathetic to countries with clear ethnic identity, like Israel.”
paragraphs.zh: 总理的支持者对此不以为然。“以色列人喜欢生活在生存焦虑之中，”为总理提供建议的成功风险投资家迈克尔·艾森伯格说。“这并不意味着以色列没有面临严峻挑战”，但只要有时间，这些挑战大体上会自行解决。他坚称，以色列科技经济具有韧性，对外国投资者仍有足够吸引力，能够挺过人才外流和以色列的国际孤立。他认为，无论如何，成为以色列社会一部分的愿望最终会把年轻哈雷迪人吸引进劳动力市场。“社会学变化需要时间。世界想要以色列正在出售的东西，而不断变化的地缘政治秩序将会更同情像以色列这样拥有清晰族群身份的国家。”

paragraphs.en: Israel’s economy has proved resilient. Its gdp grew by 2.9% in 2025 and growth is forecast to accelerate (see chart). It has made such recoveries before. Thanks to its knowledge- and tech-based economy, its growth rates were among the fastest to rebound after covid and the 2008 global recession.
paragraphs.zh: 事实证明，以色列经济具有韧性。其GDP在2025年增长2.9%，预计增长还将加速（见图表）。它以前也曾实现这样的复苏。得益于以知识和科技为基础的经济，它在新冠疫情和2008年全球经济衰退之后的增长率反弹速度都位居前列。

paragraphs.en: But not all tech titans are convinced this will last. Eyal Waldman, founder of Mellanox, a chipmaker sold to Nvidia in 2019 for $7bn, argues “Mr Netanyahu’s government leading us into further isolation from the world may harm the growth of companies wanting to work and invest in Israel. There are already major international companies that don’t want to work or invest in Israel.”
paragraphs.zh: 但并非所有科技巨头都相信这种局面会持续。芯片制造商Mellanox创始人埃亚尔·瓦尔德曼认为，“内塔尼亚胡政府正把我们带向与世界进一步隔绝，这可能损害那些想在以色列工作和投资的公司的增长。已经有一些大型国际公司不愿在以色列工作或投资。”Mellanox于2019年以70亿美元出售给英伟达。

paragraphs.en: Mr Netanyahu seems unmoved by such concerns. Last year he said that Israel should become “Athens and super-Sparta”. By his telling and under his stewardship, Israel combines the democratic wisdom of Athens and the military might of Sparta. Victory has proved elusive but eventually Israel will triumph and regain the world’s respect. But the story of Athens is one of a democracy that ultimately failed. Sparta’s militaristic autarky, too, ended in collapse.
paragraphs.zh: 内塔尼亚胡似乎没有被这些担忧打动。去年他说，以色列应该成为“雅典和超级斯巴达”。按照他的说法，在他的领导下，以色列结合了雅典的民主智慧和斯巴达的军事力量。胜利一直难以实现，但以色列最终会获胜，并重新赢得世界的尊重。然而，雅典的故事是一种民主最终失败的故事。斯巴达的军事化自给自足也以崩溃告终。

paragraphs.en: The prime minister’s opponents seem to understand that a radical change of course is imperative to draw Israel back from the brink. But so far they have shied away from telling Israeli voters some hard truths: that its enemies cannot simply be destroyed, that Israel cannot keep itself safe with “security zones” and, most crucially, that a just settlement with the Palestinians is essential for Israel’s future. Uniting Israel’s deeply divided society on such matters will be hard enough, but Israelis must have that reckoning. Even if Mr Netanyahu’s foes oust him on October 27th, their work will be only starting. ■
paragraphs.zh: 总理的对手似乎明白，必须彻底改变方向，才能把以色列从悬崖边拉回来。但到目前为止，他们一直回避向以色列选民说明一些残酷事实：它的敌人无法被简单摧毁；以色列无法靠“安全区”保障自身安全；最关键的是，与巴勒斯坦人达成公正解决方案对以色列未来至关重要。要在这些问题上团结以色列这个严重分裂的社会已经足够困难，但以色列人必须进行这场清算。即便内塔尼亚胡的敌人在10月27日把他赶下台，他们的工作也才刚刚开始。■

articles.title_en: Donald Trump’s transformation of the Department of Justice will be hard to undo
articles.title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普对司法部的改造将很难逆转
articles.rubric_en: Political prosecutions are just part of a more radical reinvention
articles.rubric_zh: 政治起诉只是更激进重塑的一部分

paragraphs.en: Anyone who has ever worked as a lawyer for Donald Trump makes a bet. There is the possibility that you, too, will run afoul of the law—eight of the president’s lawyers have themselves been indicted. Alternatively, your work might pay dividends. Such was Todd Blanche’s calculus three years ago when his white-shoe firm gave him a choice: represent Mr Trump, then a candidate and a criminal defendant, or remain a partner. Mr Blanche chose Mr Trump. Now the president has selected him to be America’s top cop.
paragraphs.zh: 任何曾为唐纳德·特朗普担任律师的人，都是在下注。一种可能是，你自己也会触犯法律——总统的律师中已有八人被起诉。另一种可能是，你的工作会带来回报。三年前，陶德·布兰奇的白鞋律所给了他一个选择：要么代理当时既是候选人又是刑事被告的特朗普先生，要么继续留任合伙人。这就是布兰奇先生当时的盘算。布兰奇先生选择了特朗普先生。如今，总统已选中他出任美国最高执法官。

paragraphs.en: On July 15th the Senate began hearings to consider Mr Blanche’s nomination to lead the Department of Justice (DoJ). Whether lawmakers confirm him as attorney-general will test their willingness to endorse Mr Trump’s legal agenda, but even a rebuke would be only symbolic. The president will still get his way at the DoJ; Mr Blanche can stay in the job on an acting basis for months and be replaced by someone similar after.
paragraphs.zh: 7月15日，参议院开始举行听证会，审议布兰奇先生出任司法部（DoJ）部长的提名。议员们是否确认他担任司法部长，将考验他们是否愿意为特朗普先生的法律议程背书，但即便否决，也只具有象征意义。总统仍会在司法部得偿所愿；布兰奇先生可以以代理身份留任数月，之后再由类似的人接替。

paragraphs.en: With or without Mr Blanche, the president will continue to lead a transformation of dramatic scale. With warrants and subpoenas, the DoJ is pursuing Mr Trump’s critics and his hobby-horses, from political adversaries to election fraud to leaks in the press. The number of lawyers has dwindled by 20%; former prosecutors point to an incompatibility between the president’s agenda and the fair application of law. The DoJ is retreating from certain types of criminal enforcement. And, increasingly, judges are telling government lawyers that they cannot be taken at their word.
paragraphs.zh: 无论有没有布兰奇先生，总统都将继续主导一场规模巨大的转型。司法部正通过搜查令和传票追查特朗普先生的批评者及其执念所在，从政治对手到选举舞弊，再到媒体泄密。律师人数已减少20%；前检察官指出，总统的议程与法律的公正适用互不相容。司法部正在退出某些类型的刑事执法。而且，法官们越来越多地告诉政府律师，不能只凭他们的话就采信。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ’s duties, at least as historically defined, include enforcing the law and defending the government in court. Ever since Richard Nixon urged his attorney-general to interfere with an investigation, politicians of both parties have generally agreed that the DoJ should operate at some distance from the presidency. Mr Trump has bucked that consensus, claiming that Democrats weaponised the law against him. Joe Biden’s DoJ indicted him twice, elected Democratic district-attorneys indicted him twice more and Letitia James, New York’s attorney-general, sued him in civil court.
paragraphs.zh: 至少按照历史定义，司法部的职责包括执法以及在法庭上为政府辩护。自理查德·尼克松敦促其司法部长干预一项调查以来，两党政客大体上都认同，司法部应与总统保持一定距离。特朗普先生打破了这一共识，声称民主党人把法律武器化，用来对付他。乔·拜登领导下的司法部两次起诉他，民选的民主党地方检察官又两次起诉他，纽约州总检察长利蒂希娅·詹姆斯则在民事法庭起诉他。

paragraphs.en: Pam Bondi, Mr Trump’s first attorney-general, was fired in April for being insufficiently aggressive. Mr Blanche told the Senate this week that he is not the president’s “yes man”. But shortly after assuming his current post, Mr Blanche was touting a second indictment against James Comey, a former FBI director and antagonist of the president. Mr Comey had posted a photo of seashells on a beach arranged like “86 47”. To “86” someone means to get rid of them; Mr Trump is the 47th president. The DoJ contends that Mr Comey was threatening to kill Mr Trump.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生的首任司法部长帕姆·邦迪因不够强硬，于4月被解职。布兰奇先生本周告诉参议院，他不是总统的“唯命是从者”。但在就任现职后不久，布兰奇先生就在宣扬对詹姆斯·科米的第二项起诉。科米是联邦调查局前局长，也是总统的对头。科米曾发布一张照片，照片中海滩上的贝壳被摆成“86 47”的样子。在英语里，“86”某人意为除掉此人；特朗普先生是第47任总统。司法部主张，科米是在威胁杀害特朗普先生。

paragraphs.en: This prosecution is almost certain to fail, just like the president’s cases against Ms James; Jerome Powell, the former chair of the Federal Reserve; and six Democratic lawmakers. Last month a court tossed subpoenas targeting Tim Walz, Minnesota’s governor, and other Democratic officials in that state. The judge wrote that the subpoenas were “not issued to investigate, but to harass, coerce and retaliate” for the officials’ refusal to aid the president’s immigration crackdown.
paragraphs.zh: 这项起诉几乎肯定会失败，就像总统针对詹姆斯女士、前美联储主席杰罗姆·鲍威尔以及六名民主党议员的案子一样。上个月，一家法院撤销了针对明尼苏达州州长蒂姆·沃尔兹以及该州其他民主党官员的传票。法官写道，这些传票“不是为了调查而发出，而是为了骚扰、胁迫和报复”，因为这些官员拒绝协助总统的移民严打行动。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump complements prosecutions of political foes with lenient treatment of his friends, through pardons and dropped charges. Last year political appointees at the DoJ ordered prosecutors to abandon a corruption case against Eric Adams, then New York’s mayor. Unlike Mr Walz, he had agreed to co-operate on immigration. A judge in New York wants to know whether a quid pro quo inspired the DoJ to drop fraud charges against Gautam Adani, an Indian billionaire who has promised to invest $10bn in America.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生一边起诉政敌，一边通过赦免和撤销指控宽待朋友。去年，司法部的政治任命官员命令检察官放弃对时任纽约市长埃里克·亚当斯的腐败案。与沃尔兹先生不同，亚当斯同意在移民问题上合作。纽约一名法官想知道，司法部撤销针对印度亿万富翁高塔姆·阿达尼的欺诈指控，是否出于某种交换条件；阿达尼曾承诺在美国投资100亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump had sought to reward allies most explicitly through a $1.8bn fund for supposed victims of government lawfare. Even Republican senators balked at that; in June Mr Blanche said he had given up the idea. But it loomed over his confirmation hearing, with John Cornyn, a Republican senator, observing that his answers “don’t lead inevitably to the conclusion that it’s dead”.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生曾试图通过一个18亿美元的基金，最明目张胆地奖赏盟友；该基金据称用于补偿政府“法律战”的受害者。即便共和党参议员也对此退缩了；6月，布兰奇先生说他已经放弃这一想法。但这件事仍笼罩着他的确认听证会，共和党参议员约翰·科宁指出，他的回答“并不必然导向它已经死了这一结论”。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ is vast; last year it charged 81,000 people. Politicised probes mark a radical departure from past norms, but they are not enough to upend that work. Yet broader change is underway, too, with the department itself depleted and redirected, at remarkable speed.
paragraphs.zh: 司法部规模庞大；去年它起诉了8.1万人。政治化调查标志着对过往规范的激进背离，但还不足以颠覆这些工作。然而，更广泛的变化也在进行：该部门本身正以惊人的速度被削弱和改道。

paragraphs.en: Mass attrition means that some of the most experienced lawyers have left, often for better pay in the private sector. A former prosecutor in the Virginia office that first indicted Mr Comey recounts how, after charges were issued, nearly everyone in his unit started looking for new work. “These people have job options.” Now the DoJ is struggling to fill its ranks, lowering hiring standards to take applicants straight from law school and offering some recruits $25,000 signing bonuses.
paragraphs.zh: 大规模人员流失意味着一些最有经验的律师已经离开，往往是去了薪酬更高的私营部门。曾在最早起诉科米先生的弗吉尼亚办公室任职的一名前检察官回忆说，指控提出后，他所在部门几乎所有人都开始找新工作。“这些人有工作选择。”如今，司法部正苦于填补人员空缺，不得不降低招聘标准，直接从法学院招收申请者，并向一些新员工提供2.5万美元的签约奖金。

paragraphs.en: Units specialising in cryptocurrency fraud and corruption by public officials have been gutted. (As it happens, Mr Trump made more than $1.4bn from cryptocurrency last year.) The group focused on counterintelligence and enforcing export controls has warned Congress of “unprecedented personnel constraints”, with a 40% drop in prosecutors from a year and a half ago. Up to a third of the counterterrorism section has left, says a former prosecutor in that unit. The FBI, which sits within the DoJ, has lost about 300 special agents who worked on national security.
paragraphs.zh: 专门处理加密货币欺诈和公职人员腐败的部门已被大幅削弱。（巧的是，特朗普先生去年从加密货币中赚了逾14亿美元。）负责反情报和执行出口管制的小组已向国会警告称，他们面临“前所未有的人员限制”，检察官人数较一年半前下降40%。一名曾在反恐部门任职的前检察官说，该部门多达三分之一的人员已经离开。隶属于司法部的联邦调查局也流失了约300名从事国家安全工作的特别探员。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ’s national-security division has expertise that most prosecutors lack, in handling classified information and charging complex statutes. They sift through dozens of FBI referrals and decide which to pursue. “What feels real versus which ones are idle chatter? When is the right time to disrupt a plot? Do we go now? Prosecutors learn that only by working these cases over many years,” says one who left last year.
paragraphs.zh: 司法部国家安全司拥有多数检察官所不具备的专业能力，能够处理机密信息并依据复杂法规提出指控。他们会筛查数十份联邦调查局移送材料，并决定追查哪些案件。“哪些感觉是真的，哪些只是空谈？什么时候才是破坏一起阴谋的正确时机？我们现在就行动吗？检察官只有多年办理这些案件，才能学会这一点，”一位去年离职的人说。

paragraphs.en: Fewer resources mean less enforcement in some domains. White-collar defence lawyers remark how work has slowed. Last year the number of financial-fraud indictments out of DoJ headquarters and the US attorney’s office in Manhattan fell by 30% from the ten-year average. Indictments are a lagging indicator of enforcement activity. Subpoenas of financial firms, which precede them, are “not happening, basically,” says a white-collar lawyer in New York, who expects even fewer indictments to come. “Nobody’s investigating those things.” Cases targeting political graft have largely dried up. Mr Trump is notably lax about that, having granted clemency to at least 20 politicians convicted of self-dealing over his two terms.
paragraphs.zh: 资源减少意味着某些领域的执法力度下降。白领犯罪辩护律师注意到，相关工作已经放缓。去年，司法部总部和曼哈顿联邦检察官办公室提出的金融欺诈起诉数量，较十年平均水平下降30%。起诉数量是执法活动的滞后指标。纽约一名白领犯罪律师说，作为起诉前置步骤的对金融公司的传票“基本上没有发生”，他预计未来起诉还会更少。“没人再调查那些事了。”针对政治腐败的案件基本枯竭。特朗普先生在这方面尤其宽松；在他的两个任期内，他已对至少20名因以权谋私被定罪的政客给予宽宥。

paragraphs.en: Instead the DoJ has made a big show around the president’s bugbears: health-care and benefits fraud; anything connected to cartels; transgender care; diversity, equity and inclusion; and election fraud. Recently the FBI dispatched 260 analysts to investigate debunked claims of vote-rigging in the 2020 election in Georgia, which Mr Trump maintains he won. In January the FBI seized records related to that race in Georgia’s Fulton County. Last week a judge quashed the DoJ’s subpoena seeking names of poll workers there: an “overly broad fishing expedition is bad and is not allowed”, he said.
paragraphs.zh: 相反，司法部围绕总统的心头大患大张旗鼓：医疗保健和福利欺诈；任何与贩毒集团有关的事项；跨性别医疗；多元、公平和包容；以及选举舞弊。最近，联邦调查局派出260名分析员，调查有关2020年佐治亚州选举舞弊的已被驳倒说法；特朗普先生坚称自己赢得了该州。1月，联邦调查局查封了佐治亚州富尔顿县与那场选举有关的记录。上周，一名法官撤销了司法部要求提供当地投票工作人员姓名的传票；他说，一场“过于宽泛的摸鱼式搜查是糟糕的，也是不被允许的”。

paragraphs.en: The DoJ will be at the tip of the spear if Mr Trump attempts to intervene in the midterm elections in November. Ominously, it has all but shut the unit that normally monitors election-related crimes. A prosecutor who left that section last year says he thinks the administration is “taking steps to be in a position to put its thumb on the scale in 2026 or 2028”.
paragraphs.zh: 如果特朗普先生试图干预11月的中期选举，司法部将站在最前线。令人不安的是，它几乎关闭了通常负责监督选举相关犯罪的部门。一名去年离开该部门的检察官说，他认为本届政府正在“采取步骤，让自己处于能够在2026年或2028年偏袒一方的位置”。

paragraphs.en: Election-year training for FBI agents and DoJ staff, once mandatory, has been cancelled. The department seems to have no intention to stand up the National Election Command Post, which normally monitors irregularities. Instead, election deniers populate the DoJ, including several who worked to overturn the 2020 result. Recently they threatened to prosecute election officials who let non-citizens vote.
paragraphs.zh: 曾经强制要求的联邦调查局探员和司法部工作人员选举年培训已被取消。司法部似乎无意设立通常用于监测异常情况的全国选举指挥站。相反，司法部内充斥着否认选举结果的人，其中包括数名曾参与推翻2020年结果的人。最近，他们威胁要起诉允许非公民投票的选举官员。

paragraphs.en: No issue has consumed the attention of the Trump administration like immigration. By September the FBI had diverted a fifth of its roughly 14,000 agents to immigration enforcement. What is striking is not that the president has made immigration a priority—he said he would do as much—but the manner in which DoJ lawyers are pursuing those cases.
paragraphs.zh: 没有哪个议题像移民一样占据特朗普政府的注意力。到9月，联邦调查局已将其约1.4万名探员中的五分之一转用于移民执法。引人注目的并不是总统把移民列为优先事项——他早就说过会这么做——而是司法部律师推进这些案件的方式。

paragraphs.en: In the autumn Mr Blanche showed up in Chicago, the site of a surge of agents dubbed “Operation Midway Blitz”, and decried “an organised effort by domestic terrorists to actually injure and hurt” those agents. Then his deputy told prosecutors to “go big and go loud” against protesters.
paragraphs.zh: 秋天，布兰奇先生现身芝加哥，那里正经历一波被称为“中途闪击行动”的探员增派。他谴责称，有“国内恐怖分子有组织地实际伤害并袭击”这些探员。随后，他的副手告诉检察官，要对抗议者“声势浩大地大干一场”。

paragraphs.en: A prosecutor in Chicago who left earlier this year says that every unit there, including ones focused on cyber and national-security crime, was dragooned into protester cases, which became a “dreaded thing”. He says the pressure from the front office to file charges was so great that prosecutors had to present a compelling argument not to do so. That is the inverse of how decisions are usually made.
paragraphs.zh: 一名今年早些时候离开芝加哥的检察官说，当地每个部门，包括专注网络犯罪和国家安全犯罪的部门，都被强行拉去处理抗议者案件，而这些案件成了“令人畏惧的事情”。他说，前台办公室施加的起诉压力大到检察官必须提出有说服力的理由，才能不提起指控。这与通常的决策方式正好相反。

paragraphs.en: Agents arrested nearly 4,000 immigrants during Midway Blitz. The crackdown in Chicago also became a stark example of how to drive away lawyers. In the US attorney’s office, which has seven criminal sections, each chief serving at the start of Midway Blitz has quit. Seven of their 15 deputies and at least a quarter of the 90 or so staff prosecutors have left, too.
paragraphs.zh: 在中途闪击行动期间，探员逮捕了近4000名移民。芝加哥的严打也成为赶走律师的鲜明例子。在拥有七个刑事部门的联邦检察官办公室中，中途闪击行动开始时任职的每一位部门主管都已辞职。他们15名副手中的7人，以及约90名一线检察官中至少四分之一的人，也已经离开。

paragraphs.en: Across the country, there have been hundreds of shaky cases brought against protesters. Many collapse before trial. A jury rebellion awaits those that do make it that far. Ten of 13 resulted in an acquittal as of March, according to Steven Salky, a defence lawyer who tracks unusual charging decisions. By contrast, across all federal trials last year, the acquittal rate was 12%.
paragraphs.zh: 在全国各地，针对抗议者提起的站不住脚的案件已有数百起。许多在审判前就崩溃了。那些真正走到审判阶段的案件，则面临陪审团的反抗。据追踪异常起诉决定的辩护律师史蒂文·索尔基统计，截至3月，13起案件中有10起以无罪判决告终。相比之下，去年所有联邦审判的无罪判决率为12%。

paragraphs.en: Some cases have veered into the absurd. A jury took 35 minutes to acquit a man accused of pointing a laser at the president’s helicopter. Once unusual practices are more common. In October prosecutors charged six Democratic activists and politicians in Chicago with conspiring to impede a federal agent, in a case known as the “Broadview Six”. After a judge reviewed transcripts from the grand-jury sitting—held in secret, without judges or defence lawyers—she said she had never seen such misbehaviour by prosecutors. Among other no-nos, they had dismissed sceptical jurors (including one who called the case “a crock of shit”) who might have been unlikely to return an indictment. In May prosecutors dropped the case.
paragraphs.zh: 有些案件已经滑向荒唐。一个陪审团只用了35分钟，就宣告一名被控用激光照射总统直升机的男子无罪。曾经少见的做法如今更常见了。10月，检察官指控六名芝加哥民主党活动人士和政客密谋阻碍一名联邦探员，该案被称为“布罗德维尤六人案”。一名法官审阅了大陪审团听证的记录后表示，她从未见过检察官有如此不当行为；大陪审团听证秘密进行，没有法官或辩护律师在场。除其他禁忌行为外，他们还排除了持怀疑态度的陪审员，其中包括一名称该案是“一派胡言”的陪审员；这些陪审员本来可能不太可能同意起诉。5月，检察官撤销了该案。

paragraphs.en: A consequence of all this is that the DoJ is losing credibility in the courts. Increasingly judges are calling out lapses by government lawyers, saying they cannot take them at face value. The judge overseeing the Broadview Six case said she believed that “most government attorneys are doing the best they can to do the right thing”. Then she added: “That trust has been broken.” More judges are now requiring depositions and documents to verify that the government’s claims are true, and threatening sanctions when its lawyers obfuscate or fail to comply. In the first 14 months of Mr Trump’s second term, according to Just Security, a site for legal commentary, the DoJ gave courts inaccurate information in nearly 100 instances.
paragraphs.zh: 这一切的一个后果是，司法部正在法庭上失去公信力。法官们越来越多地点名指出政府律师的失误，称不能照单全收他们的说法。负责布罗德维尤六人案的法官说，她相信“大多数政府律师都在尽最大努力做正确的事”。接着她补充道：“这种信任已经破裂。”现在，更多法官要求通过证词录取和文件来核实政府主张是否属实，并在其律师含糊其词或不遵守要求时威胁施加制裁。法律评论网站Just Security称，在特朗普先生第二任期的前14个月里，司法部向法院提供不准确信息近100次。

paragraphs.en: The problem is acute in immigration cases. The administration’s mass-detention policy led to a twentyfold spike in “habeas” petitions by detained immigrants suing for release between 2024 and 2025. Just Security found nearly 800 instances of non-compliance with court orders in habeas cases, and 13 sanctions and contempt-of-court findings against DoJ lawyers. The administration’s response has been to label any judge who disagrees with it a “rogue activist”. Mr Blanche has called it a “war” on the judiciary.
paragraphs.zh: 这个问题在移民案件中尤为严重。本届政府的大规模拘押政策，导致被拘押移民为寻求获释而提起的“人身保护令”申请在2024年至2025年间激增20倍。Just Security发现，在人身保护令案件中，有近800起不遵守法院命令的情形，并有13项针对司法部律师的制裁和藐视法庭认定。政府的回应是把任何不同意它的法官都称为“流氓活动分子”。布兰奇先生称这是对司法机构的“战争”。

paragraphs.en: Seen one way, that fighting talk reflects something positive: the guardrails in the judicial system holding up, to the administration’s dismay. Already the department’s alumni are asking what it will take to reconstitute it when Mr Trump leaves office. A former prosecutor says he and his former colleagues want to return. Still, the appeal of the department diminishes somewhat with the prospect that the next person overseeing it may sack you, or harness the law for their personal ends. For decades the DoJ enjoyed some protection from the politics that have fractured America. That era seems over. ■
paragraphs.zh: 从一个角度看，这种好斗言辞反映了一件积极的事：司法体系中的护栏仍在发挥作用，令政府懊恼。司法部的前成员们已经在询问，特朗普先生离任后，重建该部门需要什么。一名前检察官说，他和昔日同事都想回去。不过，如果想到下一位主管司法部的人可能会解雇你，或把法律用于个人目的，这个部门的吸引力仍会有所下降。几十年来，司法部在一定程度上免受撕裂美国的政治影响。那个时代似乎结束了。■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 通过我们的每日简报《美国简讯》及时掌握美国政治动态，它会快速分析最重要的政治新闻；还可阅读每周一期的《制衡》，了解美国民主状况以及选民关心的问题。

articles.title_en: Democrats challenge a big Hollywood tie-up
articles.title_zh: 民主党人挑战好莱坞一桩大型合并案
articles.rubric_en: A dozen states are suing to block Paramount’s merger with Warner Bros
articles.rubric_zh: 12个州正提起诉讼，试图阻止派拉蒙与华纳兄弟合并

paragraphs.en: THE LONG-RUNNING corporate dramedy over Paramount Skydance’s merger with Warner Bros Discovery has entered a new act. On July 13th Rob Bonta (pictured), California’s top prosecutor, announced that a coalition of 12 attorneys-general would sue to halt the studios’ megadeal. At a press conference in front of the Hollywood sign, he argued that the $111bn merger was illegal and insinuated that David Ellison, Paramount’s boss, was acting like a mafia don. “David Ellison may think this is an offer we can’t refuse,” said Mr Bonta. “But I am here to say he’s wrong.”
paragraphs.zh: 围绕派拉蒙天舞与华纳兄弟探索合并案上演已久的企业悲喜剧，又进入了新一幕。7月13日，加州最高检察官罗布·邦塔（见图）宣布，由12名州总检察长组成的联盟将提起诉讼，阻止这笔电影公司之间的巨额交易。在好莱坞标志前举行的新闻发布会上，他称这笔1110亿美元的合并交易违法，并暗示派拉蒙老板戴维·埃里森行事像黑手党教父。“戴维·埃里森也许以为这是一个我们无法拒绝的提议，”邦塔说。“但我在这里要说，他错了。”

paragraphs.en: Paramount claims that its merger with Warner Bros would allow it to compete with streamers, such as Netflix, which have been eating the studios’ lunch. But instead of considering this broader competition, the states focused on legacy markets in which they say the merger would reduce competition. The complaint, filed in federal court in California, alleges that the deal would give the combined company greater leverage over cinemas and cable distributors, allowing it to extract more favourable terms that could ultimately lead to higher ticket prices and cable bills.
paragraphs.zh: 派拉蒙声称，与华纳兄弟合并将使其能够同Netflix等流媒体平台竞争；这些平台一直在抢走传统电影公司的饭碗。但这些州没有考虑这种更广泛的竞争，而是把重点放在它们所说的、合并将削弱竞争的传统市场上。这份提交至加州联邦法院的起诉书称，该交易将使合并后的公司在面对影院和有线电视分销商时拥有更大议价能力，从而能够争取到更有利的条款，最终可能导致票价和有线电视账单上涨。

paragraphs.en: The legal merits will be considered in court, but the lawsuit is part of a bigger saga, too. Antitrust enforcement has long been characterised by bipartisan co-operation. However it is an increasingly political endeavour, on the local and national level.
paragraphs.zh: 该案在法律上是否站得住脚，将由法院审理判断，但这起诉讼也是一场更大戏码的一部分。长期以来，反垄断执法一直以两党合作为特征。然而，无论在地方还是全国层面，它正变成一项越来越政治化的工作。

paragraphs.en: States are pursuing more aggressive antitrust action. Although that trend predates Mr Trump’s return to office, it has gathered pace as Democratic attorneys-general argue that his administration has become less willing to challenge mergers. “The federal government is no longer a partner in this work,” said Oregon’s attorney-general, Dan Rayfield, in May. States are responding by expanding their own capabilities. An exodus of lawyers from Washington, DC, has strengthened their teams, while California, Colorado and Washington state have begun requiring advance notice of many large mergers. Legislatures elsewhere may follow suit.
paragraphs.zh: 各州正在采取更激进的反垄断行动。尽管这一趋势早在特朗普重返白宫之前就已出现，但随着民主党籍州总检察长称特朗普政府越来越不愿挑战并购，它的步伐已经加快。俄勒冈州总检察长丹·雷菲尔德在5月说：“联邦政府不再是这项工作的伙伴。”各州的回应是扩充自身能力。华盛顿特区律师外流增强了各州团队的实力，而加州、科罗拉多州和华盛顿州已开始要求许多大型并购提前申报。其他地方的立法机构也可能效仿。

paragraphs.en: That alone does not explain why all 12 attorneys-general who brought the Paramount lawsuit are Democrats. Republican attorneys-general still frequently join such cases. Only recently Tennessee’s Jonathan Skrmetti helped lead a bipartisan antitrust suit against Live Nation, a big concert promoter. But antitrust enforcement is increasingly being used as a way to convey a broader political message, allowing Democratic officials to argue that they are protecting consumers while Mr Trump looks after wealthy allies.
paragraphs.zh: 但仅凭这一点，并不能解释为何提起派拉蒙诉讼的12名州总检察长全都是民主党人。共和党籍州总检察长仍经常加入此类案件。就在不久前，田纳西州的乔纳森·斯克梅蒂还协助牵头，对大型演唱会推广公司Live Nation发起了一起两党共同参与的反垄断诉讼。但反垄断执法正越来越多地被用来传递更广泛的政治信息，让民主党官员得以主张，他们在保护消费者，而特朗普则在照顾富有盟友。

paragraphs.en: In announcing the suit, Mr Bonta claimed that “antitrust enforcement is a check on billionaires currying favour with the president so he will do their bidding.” Larry Ellison, David’s tech-billionaire father, donated $45m to a non-profit supporting Mr Trump’s re-election in 2024, according to the Wall Street Journal. On “The Town”, a podcast beloved by Hollywood insiders, Mr Bonta also said the lawsuit was about “affordability”, one of the Democratic Party’s favourite buzzwords ahead of the midterm elections.
paragraphs.zh: 在宣布诉讼时，邦塔称，“反垄断执法是对亿万富翁讨好总统、以便让总统听命于他们的一种制衡。”据《华尔街日报》报道，戴维的父亲、科技亿万富翁拉里·埃里森曾向一家支持特朗普2024年连任的非营利组织捐赠4500万美元。在深受好莱坞圈内人士喜爱的播客节目“The Town”上，邦塔还说，这起诉讼关乎“可负担性”；这是中期选举前民主党最喜欢的流行词之一。

paragraphs.en: It is notable, then, that Mr Bonta and the other attorneys-general have made little reference to one aspect of the deal that has worried Democrats: the shift in ownership of two of America’s best known news brands, CBS News and CNN, to the Ellisons. Mr Bonta has denied that a sale of CNN by the combined company could result in a settlement, though he suggested on “The Town” that some members of his party may welcome that idea.
paragraphs.zh: 因此值得注意的是，邦塔和其他州总检察长几乎没有提及这笔交易中令民主党人担忧的一个方面：美国两个最知名新闻品牌CBS News和CNN的所有权将转到埃里森家族手中。邦塔否认合并后的公司出售CNN可能换来和解，尽管他在“The Town”上暗示，党内一些人也许会欢迎这个想法。

paragraphs.en: Californians have a particular interest in the deal. State politicians face pressure to help Hollywood, where the merger has turbocharged fears of job losses. Hollywood’s workforce is shrinking as production migrates to cheaper locations such as Canada and Britain. Last year the legislature responded by more than doubling the state’s film and television tax credits, to $750m. Now candidates for governor and mayor of Los Angeles are proposing further incentives. Opposing the Paramount deal is considered another way to help. Hollywood’s powerful labour unions lobbied for months for a legal challenge to the tie-up. The Writers Guild of America West was quick to celebrate the states’ lawsuit, calling the deal “one of the worst proposed mergers we’ve seen”.
paragraphs.zh: 加州人对这笔交易尤其关切。州内政治人物面临压力，要帮助好莱坞；在那里，这桩合并案加剧了人们对失业的担忧。随着制作迁往加拿大和英国等成本更低的地点，好莱坞的从业队伍正在缩小。去年，加州议会作出回应，将该州影视税收抵免额度提高了一倍多，达到7.5亿美元。如今，竞选加州州长和洛杉矶市长的候选人正提出进一步激励措施。反对派拉蒙交易被视为另一种帮助方式。好莱坞强大的工会为对这桩合并发起法律挑战游说了数月。美国西部编剧工会很快对各州的诉讼表示庆祝，称该交易是“我们见过的最糟糕的拟议合并之一”。

paragraphs.en: Paramount, for its part, has dismissed the lawsuit as “wrong on both the facts and the law”. Semafor, an online news outlet, reported that Mr Ellison’s advisers are nudging him to consider moving Paramount’s headquarters out of California. The rumour could be a bluff to force Mr Bonta to the negotiating table. But the Ellisons have done this before. Larry moved the offices of Oracle, his software giant, from Silicon Valley to Texas in 2020.
paragraphs.zh: 派拉蒙方面则驳斥这起诉讼，称其“在事实和法律上都是错误的”。在线新闻媒体Semafor报道称，埃里森的顾问正劝他考虑把派拉蒙总部迁出加州。这一传闻可能是在虚张声势，意在迫使邦塔坐到谈判桌前。但埃里森家族以前做过这种事。2020年，拉里把他的软件巨头甲骨文的办公室从硅谷迁到了得克萨斯州。

paragraphs.en: The possibility of a Paramount move raises a broader question about how many regulations and costs California can impose before more companies choose to opt out. “We support businesses”, insists Mr Bonta. “We are pro-business.” If that were unquestionably true, perhaps he wouldn’t need to say it twice. ■
paragraphs.zh: 派拉蒙可能搬迁的前景，引出了一个更广泛的问题：在更多公司选择退出之前，加州还能施加多少监管和成本？“我们支持企业，”邦塔坚称。“我们是亲商的。”如果这点无可置疑地真实，也许他就不需要说两遍了。■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 想随时掌握美国政治动态，请阅读我们的每日简报The US in brief，其中快速分析最重要的政治新闻；以及每周一期的Checks and Balance，它审视美国民主状况和选民关心的议题。

articles.title_en: Cities are rethinking what happens after dark
articles.title_zh: 城市正在重新思考夜幕降临后会发生什么
articles.rubric_en: Making it easier to party is becoming serious public policy
articles.rubric_zh: 让人们更容易尽兴聚会，正成为严肃的公共政策

paragraphs.en: THE JOB OF nightlife czar sounds almost hedonistic. Then Corean Reynolds explains what it entails. Officially Boston’s director of the nightlife economy, Ms Reynolds can sometimes be found standing outside a bar at 2am, watching how staff manage crowds spilling into the street. When residents complain about noise, she may be nearby with a decibel meter, with a plan not to kill the buzz but measure it. “I’m like, hey, actually it didn’t seem that loud,” she says she tells them. Her task is, essentially, to help more people have fun without making nearby residents miserable. She is the first person to hold the job in Boston and one of a rising number of officials across the country tasked with livening up life after dark.
paragraphs.zh: “夜生活沙皇”这个职位听起来几乎有点享乐主义色彩。可当科里安·雷诺兹解释它到底要做什么时，情况就不一样了。雷诺兹女士的正式头衔是波士顿夜间经济主管；有时凌晨2点，你会在一家酒吧门外看到她，观察工作人员如何管理涌到街上的人群。居民投诉噪音时，她可能就在附近，手里拿着分贝仪；她的计划不是扼杀热闹气氛，而是把它测量出来。她说自己会告诉居民：“我会说，嘿，其实听起来并没有那么吵。”从本质上说，她的任务是帮助更多人玩得开心，同时不让附近居民苦不堪言。她是波士顿第一个担任这一职位的人，也是全美越来越多负责让夜间生活活跃起来的官员之一。

paragraphs.en: For decades cities were interested mainly in enforcing strict rules, some dating to Prohibition, on pubs, music venues and dance halls. Now roughly a dozen American municipalities, from Iowa City to Philadelphia, have an official responsible for promoting the nightlife economy.
paragraphs.zh: 几十年来，城市主要关心的是对酒吧、音乐场所和舞厅执行严格规定，其中一些规定可追溯到禁酒令时期。如今，从艾奥瓦城到费城，美国大约十几个市政当局都设有一名负责推动夜间经济的官员。

paragraphs.en: This evolution in thinking can be seen in New York. A measure known as the Cabaret Law, which required venues to obtain special licences before allowing more than three people to dance, was introduced in 1926. In the 1990s Rudy Giuliani, then the city’s mayor, revived the law as part of his administration’s “Broken Windows” strategy, an approach to policing that sought to deter serious crime by cracking down on minor offences.
paragraphs.zh: 这种思路的演变可以在纽约看到。一项名为《歌舞厅法》的措施于1926年出台，要求场所在允许三人以上跳舞前必须取得特别许可证。20世纪90年代，时任纽约市长鲁迪·朱利安尼重新启用这项法律，将其作为其政府“破窗”策略的一部分；这种治安治理方式试图通过严厉打击轻微违法行为来遏制严重犯罪。

paragraphs.en: The law was finally repealed in 2017, and the city’s first nightlife mayor, Ariel Palitz, was appointed to her post the year after. Her office set up mediation services to help residents and business owners resolve noise complaints (Ms Palitz cautions that the correct term is “sound—not noise”). The prior system was “purely punitive”, she says, with each complaint automatically dispatching police. Her office also assisted bars, clubs and restaurants navigate permits and city bureaucracy. But enacting change has been arduous. Until May applicants for liquor licences in New York City still had to notify state officials if they wanted to allow dancing.
paragraphs.zh: 这项法律最终于2017年被废除，纽约市第一位夜生活市长阿丽尔·帕利茨在次年获任。她的办公室设立了调解服务，帮助居民和商家解决噪声投诉（帕利茨女士提醒说，正确的说法是“声音，而不是噪音”）。她说，此前的制度“完全是惩罚性的”，每一次投诉都会自动派警察出动。她的办公室还帮助酒吧、俱乐部和餐馆办理许可，并应对市政官僚程序。但推动改变一直十分艰难。直到5月以前，纽约市的酒类许可证申请者如果想允许跳舞，仍必须通知州政府官员。

paragraphs.en: For officials like Ms Reynolds, the biggest limits to her agenda remain red tape surrounding liquor licences, zoning and permitting. Boston is one of many cities that caps the number of liquor licences well below market demand. But in 2024, after Ms Reynolds pushed for change, the city created 225 new licences. According to the local government, it was “the single largest addition to Boston’s liquor licence quota since the end of Prohibition”.
paragraphs.zh: 对雷诺兹女士这样的官员来说，她议程面临的最大限制仍是围绕酒类许可证、分区规划和许可审批的繁文缛节。波士顿是许多城市之一，其酒类许可证数量上限远低于市场需求。但在2024年，经雷诺兹女士推动改革后，该市新增了225张许可证。当地政府称，这是“自禁酒令结束以来，波士顿酒类许可证配额单次最大规模的增加”。

paragraphs.en: Other cities are experimenting with new approaches that, if successful, could spread. San Francisco has created entertainment zones where patrons may consume alcohol on streets and sidewalks. Boston and some neighbouring municipalities have done the same as part of the World Cup. The tournament has become an opportunity to test looser rules, with several host cities temporarily extending bar hours. Meanwhile Austin, Texas, known for its live music, has adopted a policy that protects venues from noise complaints by occupants of new residential buildings, putting the onus on developers to provide adequate soundproofing.
paragraphs.zh: 其他城市正在尝试新做法，如果成功，可能会推广开来。旧金山设立了娱乐区，顾客可以在街道和人行道上饮酒。波士顿及一些邻近市镇也借世界杯之机采取了同样做法。这项赛事已成为测试更宽松规则的机会，多个主办城市临时延长了酒吧营业时间。与此同时，以现场音乐闻名的得克萨斯州奥斯汀通过了一项政策，保护演出场所免受新建住宅楼住户的噪声投诉，把提供充分隔音措施的责任放在开发商身上。

paragraphs.en: Smaller cities are following suit. Syracuse, a college town of 145,000 in upstate New York, recently announced plans to hire a nightlife co-ordinator. A thriving after-dark scene, city officials hope, will keep young professionals from moving away. It may also create jobs not just in hospitality and entertainment, but in sanitation, transport and security, as well as generate more tax revenue. At a time when remote work has left downtown areas quieter, encouraging nightlife is one way to sustain commercial activity after office hours while giving people more reasons to gather in person.
paragraphs.zh: 较小的城市也在跟进。雪城是纽约州北部一座拥有14.5万人口的大学城，最近宣布计划聘请一名夜生活协调员。市政官员希望，一个繁荣的夜间场景能留住年轻专业人士，避免他们迁往他处。它还可能创造就业岗位，不仅限于餐饮接待和娱乐行业，还包括环卫、交通和安保领域，并带来更多税收收入。在远程办公让市中心变得更安静之际，鼓励夜生活是在办公时间之后维持商业活动的一种方式，同时也给人们更多理由线下相聚。

paragraphs.en: Nightlife czars have a different challenge now, too. Between 2003 and 2023 data from the Bureau of Labour Statistics’ American Time Use Survey shows that the average amount of time 18- to 30-year-olds spent socialising each day fell by 38%. Andreina Seijas, an international nightlife consultant, says her industry is now in a war against the couch. In Boston, Ms Reynolds’s mission to reclaim the night continues. Next on her to-do list is persuading the city’s public-transport operator to run later into the night. The job of a nightlife czar is not always fun, but hopefully the cities they oversee will be. ■
paragraphs.zh: 如今，夜生活沙皇们还面临另一项挑战。美国劳工统计局“美国时间使用调查”的数据显示，2003年至2023年间，18至30岁人群平均每天用于社交的时间下降了38%。国际夜生活顾问安德烈娜·塞哈斯说，她所在的行业如今正在与沙发作战。在波士顿，雷诺兹女士夺回夜晚的使命仍在继续。她待办清单上的下一项，是说服该市公共交通运营机构把运营时间延长到更晚。夜生活沙皇的工作并不总是有趣，但希望他们所管理的城市会变得有趣。■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 通过我们的每日简报《The US in brief》持续掌握美国政治动态，快速分析最重要的政治新闻；也可阅读每周通讯《Checks and Balance》，了解美国民主的状况以及选民关心的议题。

articles.title_en: America’s other elections problem
articles.title_zh: 美国的另一个选举难题
articles.rubric_en: No other rich democracy hands as many decisions directly to its voters
articles.rubric_zh: 没有哪个富裕民主国家把如此多的决定直接交给选民

paragraphs.en: IT IS OFTEN said that Americans love to work. Perhaps that is why they have made one of their fundamental obligations as citizens—voting—feel like work, too. A civically minded person in Houston, Texas, may already have voted six times this year. When voters in California cast their ballots in November, they will consider a wealth tax from three different angles. In Idaho, home to just 2m people, voters will elect 49 judges, a slew of county coroners and decide which of six firearms should become the state’s official gun.
paragraphs.zh: 人们常说，美国人热爱工作。也许正因如此，他们把公民的一项基本义务——投票——也弄得像工作一样。在得克萨斯州休斯敦，一个有公民意识的人今年可能已经投了六次票。加利福尼亚州选民在11月投票时，将从三个不同角度审议一项财富税。在仅有200万人口的爱达荷州，选民将选出49名法官、一大批县验尸官，并决定六种枪支中的哪一种应成为该州的官方枪支。

paragraphs.en: Much attention is paid to whether the rules of democracy are being broken. Donald Trump claims that American elections are marred by voter fraud, despite scant evidence. This month his administration dispatched more than 250 federal investigators to probe Fulton County’s handling of the 2020 election, which he lost. But there is a more prosaic question: does America’s voting system make sense? No other rich democracy hands as many decisions directly to its people.
paragraphs.zh: 人们很关注民主规则是否遭到破坏。唐纳德·特朗普声称美国选举受到选民舞弊的玷污，尽管证据寥寥。本月，他的政府派出250多名联邦调查人员，调查富尔顿县对2020年选举的处理情况；那场选举他输了。但还有一个更平实的问题：美国的投票制度合理吗？没有哪个富裕民主国家把如此多的决定直接交给人民。

paragraphs.en: Americans are expected to vote for judges, sheriffs, coroners and school-board members. Through ballot initiatives they also weigh in on everything from whether actors in pornographic films must wear condoms to arcane details of prescription-drug pricing. The assumption is that more voting makes for a healthier democracy. But ballots of biblical length often do the opposite, making government less efficient and less accountable.
paragraphs.zh: 美国人被要求投票选出法官、警长、验尸官和学校董事会成员。通过公投提案，他们还要对各种事项表态，从成人电影演员是否必须戴安全套，到处方药定价的晦涩细节。其假设是，投票越多，民主就越健康。但篇幅如《圣经》般漫长的选票往往适得其反，使政府效率更低、问责更弱。

paragraphs.en: America’s electoral sprawl happened in two stages. The first came under Andrew Jackson, the country’s seventh president, who handed more power to non-landowning white men by allowing them to elect more officials. The second stage came at the turn of the 20th century, when reformers gave citizens more direct influence through ballot initiatives and referendums. Both moments reflected concern that elites and political parties had amassed too much control over government, says David Schleicher of Yale University. The solution, he says, was to “diffuse power to the people as a check” on those groups.
paragraphs.zh: 美国选举范围的膨胀分两个阶段发生。第一阶段出现在美国第七任总统安德鲁·杰克逊时期，他让无地产的白人男性能够选举更多官员，从而把更多权力交给他们。第二阶段出现在20世纪之交，当时改革者通过公投提案和全民复决，让公民拥有更直接的影响力。耶鲁大学的戴维·施莱歇尔说，这两个时刻都反映出一种担忧：精英和政党对政府的控制过多。他说，解决办法是“把权力分散给人民，作为对这些群体的制衡”。

paragraphs.en: In total Americans choose some 500,000 officials, roughly five times as many per person as in Britain. Even with a population of 340m, America has struggled to find enough candidates to contest all of these races. Between 2018 and 2025 nearly two-thirds of candidates ran unopposed, according to analysis by Ballotpedia, an online resource.
paragraphs.zh: 美国人总共要选出约50万名官员，按人均计算大约是英国的五倍。即便拥有3.4亿人口，美国也一直难以找到足够的候选人来竞争所有这些职位。在线资料库Ballotpedia的分析显示，2018年至2025年间，近三分之二的候选人没有竞争对手。

paragraphs.en: About half of states also allow citizens who gather enough signatures to place initiatives on the ballot. These ask voters to decide questions that in most other democracies are left to legislatures. The Economist scraped data from Ballotpedia covering 1,728 ballot measures from the past three decades. They cover everything from taxation to contentious social issues (see chart).
paragraphs.zh: 大约一半的州还允许收集到足够签名的公民把提案列入选票。这些提案要求选民决定一些问题，而在大多数其他民主国家，这些问题通常交由立法机构处理。《经济学人》从Ballotpedia抓取了过去三十年1728项公投措施的数据。它们涵盖了从税收到有争议的社会议题等各种事项（见图表）。

paragraphs.en: No voter can reasonably be expected to make an informed decision about every item on the ballot. The sheer number of elected officials makes it difficult to know who is responsible for what. Even an expert steeped in local governance might struggle to judge whether a county comptroller has succeeded or failed in office, says Mr Schleicher. Yet on many ballots, voters face that challenge dozens of times. When they reach the more obscure races, they are “flying blind”, says Todd Donovan of Western Washington University.
paragraphs.zh: 不能合理地期待任何选民都能对选票上的每一项作出知情决定。民选官员数量之多，使人很难知道谁该对什么负责。施莱歇尔先生说，即便是深谙地方治理的专家，也可能难以判断一名县审计长在任内是成功还是失败。然而在许多选票上，选民要面对这种挑战几十次。西华盛顿大学的托德·多诺万说，当他们看到那些更冷门的竞选项目时，就是在“盲飞”。

paragraphs.en: Ballot initiatives can be even harder to understand. The Economist analysed the text of 130 initiatives in California since 2000. Using a metric that scores text based on sentence and word complexity, we found that most required college-level reading comprehension (see chart). One in four demanded the reading comprehension of someone with a graduate degree. Yet only a third of Americans have at least a bachelor’s degree.
paragraphs.zh: 公投提案可能更难理解。《经济学人》分析了加州自2000年以来130项提案的文本。我们使用一种根据句子和词语复杂度给文本打分的指标发现，大多数提案需要大学水平的阅读理解能力（见图表）。四分之一的提案要求读者具备研究生水平的阅读理解能力。然而，只有三分之一的美国人至少拥有学士学位。

paragraphs.en: After deciphering the language of initiatives, voters weigh their merits. This year Californians must become health economists as they decide whether federally funded community clinics should be required to devote a set share of revenue to their charitable mission rather than overhead costs. Obscure decisions like this can have lasting consequences. In 1992 Colorado voters approved the cleverly named Taxpayer Bill of Rights, a constitutional amendment that ties state revenue growth to inflation. As a result, Colorado must in some years refund taxpayers while cutting services because the cost of things such as health care rises faster than inflation.
paragraphs.zh: 在读懂提案文字之后，选民还要权衡其利弊。今年，加州人必须化身为卫生经济学家，决定是否应要求由联邦资助的社区诊所把固定比例的收入用于其慈善使命，而不是管理开支。像这样晦涩的决定可能产生持久后果。1992年，科罗拉多州选民批准了一个名称巧妙的《纳税人权利法案》，这是一项宪法修正案，把州收入增长与通胀挂钩。因此，在某些年份，科罗拉多州必须一边削减服务，一边向纳税人退款，因为医疗保健等项目的成本上涨速度快于通胀。

paragraphs.en: In principle, ballot initiatives allow groups unable to persuade legislatures to appeal directly to voters. But most are no longer the homespun, volunteer campaigns of yesteryear. They are often sponsored by corporations, unions, ideological organisations or wealthy patrons, which hire signature-gathering firms and specialised consultants. “You can’t really get on the ballot anymore without substantial resources,” says Mr Donovan. Even the original wealth-tax measure in California was not a grassroots effort. It was sponsored by a health-care labour union and drafted with the assistance of experienced lawyers. The campaign against it is largely financed by billionaires. Sergey Brin, Google’s co-founder, has poured $82m into the effort.
paragraphs.zh: 原则上，公投提案让那些无法说服立法机构的团体得以直接诉诸选民。但大多数提案早已不再是过去那种本土自发、依靠志愿者的竞选活动。它们往往由企业、工会、意识形态组织或富有赞助人发起，这些人会雇用签名收集公司和专业顾问。多诺万先生说：“没有大量资源，如今基本不可能把提案送上选票。”即便加州最初的财富税提案也不是一场草根努力。它由一个医疗保健行业工会赞助，并在经验丰富的律师协助下起草。反对该提案的运动主要由亿万富翁资助。谷歌联合创始人谢尔盖·布林已向这项行动投入8200万美元。

paragraphs.en: As ballots grow longer, more complex and more susceptible to organised interests, some states are looking to rein them in. Massachusetts, for example, has proposed a commission to reform the ballot-initiative process. But politicians tend to be wary of taking power away from voters—or, at least, of being seen to do so. Few elected officials are keen to reduce the number of elected offices, not least because some would be eliminating their own jobs. Making government more efficient and officials more accountable will not be easy. Perhaps such reforms should be put before voters. ■
paragraphs.zh: 随着选票越来越长、越来越复杂，也越来越容易受到有组织利益集团的影响，一些州正试图加以约束。例如，马萨诸塞州已提议成立一个委员会，改革公投提案流程。但政治人物往往对从选民手中拿走权力心存顾虑——或者至少担心被视为这样做。很少有民选官员热衷于减少民选职位的数量，尤其是因为其中一些人等于是在取消自己的工作。让政府更有效率、让官员更可问责并不容易。也许这样的改革应该交给选民表决。■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 通过我们的每日新闻简报《美国简报》及时了解美国政治，该简报快速分析最重要的政治新闻；还可阅读每周一期的《制衡》，它考察美国民主的状况以及选民关心的问题。

articles.title_en: Lindsey Graham tried to save the wrong countries from Donald Trump
articles.title_zh: 林赛·格雷厄姆试图从唐纳德·特朗普手中拯救错误的国家
articles.rubric_en: He should have put American democracy first
articles.rubric_zh: 他本该把美国民主放在首位

paragraphs.en: Anyone anxious that the American establishment can no longer speak loudly with one voice should be heartened by the outpouring of adulation from Washington’s elite for Senator Lindsey Graham, who died from heart disease on July 11th. In part, the reaction reveals real affection for a politician nimble enough to charm senators of both parties, along with the press—its most experienced, jaded members in particular. The reaction also illuminates what establishment Washington prizes most: relevance, a quality honoured there above honour itself, as Donald Trump intuited from the start of his own political career.
paragraphs.zh: 任何担心美国建制派再也无法用一个响亮声音齐声发言的人，都应当从华盛顿精英对参议员林赛·格雷厄姆的如潮赞美中感到宽慰。格雷厄姆于7月11日死于心脏病。在一定程度上，这种反应显示出人们对这位政治人物的真切喜爱：他足够机敏，能让两党参议员以及新闻界，尤其是其中最资深、最见多识广也最世故的成员，都对他产生好感。这种反应也照亮了华盛顿建制派最看重的东西：相关性，或者说仍然有用、有影响力。在那里，这种品质受到的尊崇甚至高于荣誉本身，而唐纳德·特朗普从自己政治生涯一开始就直觉地明白这一点。

paragraphs.en: Republicans who put their principles ahead of loyalty to Mr Trump, such as Mitt Romney and Liz Cheney, live on. But they are forgotten by Republicans on Capitol Hill as anything but cautionary tales about the folly of crossing him. By contrast Mr Graham remade himself as so “relevant”—the term he used—that his sudden disappearance from the scene, at 71, creates problems rather than opportunities for the president, from confirming nominees to passing laws. The bigger question for Washington, and America, is whether Mr Graham’s legacy of compromise may yet prove the true cautionary tale. As Mr Graham himself warned in 2015, back when he was instructing Republicans that Mr Trump was a demagogue they should shun: “I’m not afraid of losing an election. I’m afraid of losing our soul.”
paragraphs.zh: 那些把原则置于对特朗普忠诚之上的共和党人，例如米特·罗姆尼和莉兹·切尼，仍然活着。但在国会山的共和党人眼中，他们除了作为“不要与特朗普作对”的反面教材之外，已经被遗忘。相比之下，格雷厄姆把自己重塑得如此“相关”——这是他自己使用的词——以至于他在71岁时突然退出舞台，对总统而言带来的不是机会，而是问题，从确认提名人选到推动立法都是如此。对华盛顿和美国来说，更大的问题是，格雷厄姆的妥协遗产是否最终才会被证明是真正的警示故事。正如格雷厄姆本人在2015年曾警告的那样，当时他还在告诫共和党人，特朗普是一个他们应当避开的煽动家：“我不怕输掉一场选举。我怕的是失去我们的灵魂。”

paragraphs.en: Mr Graham’s indefatigable pursuit of relevance made not just his conversion to Trumpism but his whole career a map of the trajectory of the Republican Party. It was completing its consolidation of the new South when, as part of the Republican Revolution led by Newt Gingrich in 1994, Mr Graham was elected as the first Republican in 117 years to represent South Carolina’s third congressional district. He helped lead the impeachment of President Bill Clinton over an affair with an intern. When Mr Graham was elected to the Senate in 2002, he succeeded Strom Thurmond, who had held the seat since 1954. Mr Graham represented not just a new generation of southern Republican but, after the attacks of September 11th 2001, a more internationalist, interventionist conservatism. He allied himself with Senator John McCain of Arizona to champion the wars led by President George W. Bush.
paragraphs.zh: 格雷厄姆不知疲倦地追求“相关性”，使得不仅他转向特朗普主义的过程，而且他的整个职业生涯，都成了共和党轨迹的一幅地图。1994年，在纽特·金里奇领导的“共和党革命”中，格雷厄姆当选为南卡罗来纳州第三国会选区117年来第一位共和党众议员；当时共和党正在完成对新南方的整合。他曾帮助领导对总统比尔·克林顿因与一名实习生的婚外情而进行的弹劾。2002年格雷厄姆当选参议员时，他接替的是自1954年以来一直占据该席位的斯特罗姆·瑟蒙德。格雷厄姆代表的不只是一代新的南方共和党人；在2001年9月11日袭击之后，他还代表了一种更具国际主义和干预主义色彩的保守主义。他与亚利桑那州参议员约翰·麦凯恩结盟，支持总统乔治·W·布什领导的战争。

paragraphs.en: Mr Graham was in the thick of the action again when McCain won the Republican nomination in 2008. After his friend’s defeat, he found ways to make himself relevant to Barack Obama. He worked with John Kerry, a Democratic senator, on climate legislation in 2009 and voted for Mr Obama’s successful nominees to the Supreme Court, Sonia Sotomayor and Elena Kagan. Mr Graham became one of the Gang of Eight, a bipartisan group of senators who proposed far-reaching immigration reform. (Lexington’s brother, Michael Bennet, was also a member.) That bill passed the Senate in 2013 by a filibuster-proof majority, but the Republican speaker, John Boehner, refused to bring it to a vote in the House.
paragraphs.zh: 2008年麦凯恩赢得共和党总统候选人提名时，格雷厄姆再次身处行动中心。朋友败选后，他找到了让自己对贝拉克·奥巴马也变得有用的办法。2009年，他与民主党参议员约翰·克里合作推动气候立法，并投票支持奥巴马成功提名的最高法院大法官索尼娅·索托马约尔和埃琳娜·卡根。格雷厄姆成为“八人帮”之一，这是一个由两党参议员组成的小组，提出了影响深远的移民改革方案。（Lexington的兄弟迈克尔·贝内特也是成员。）该法案于2013年在参议院以足以阻止冗长辩论阻挠的多数通过，但共和党籍众议院议长约翰·博纳拒绝将其提交众议院表决。

paragraphs.en: Even among the Republicans who made early criticisms of Mr Trump, Mr Graham stands out for his ferocity. Ever loquacious, he was prodigal on attack in 2015 and 2016: he warned that Mr Trump might order troops to commit war crimes; that he preyed on fear; that he was “completely unhinged”; that he would cost “the heart and soul of the conservative movement”; that his foreign policy was “gibberish”; that he was a “race-baiting, xenophobic, religious bigot”; that he “represents the worst in America”. Mr Graham did not vote for Mr Trump.
paragraphs.zh: 即便在那些早早批评特朗普的共和党人中，格雷厄姆也因其猛烈程度而格外突出。他一向滔滔不绝，在2015年和2016年的攻击中毫不吝惜火力：他警告说，特朗普可能命令军队犯下战争罪；说特朗普利用恐惧；说他“完全失控”；说他会让“保守主义运动的核心和灵魂”付出代价；说他的外交政策是“胡言乱语”；说他是一个“煽动种族情绪、排外、宗教偏执的人”；说他“代表了美国最糟糕的一面”。格雷厄姆没有投票给特朗普。

paragraphs.en: But that was when the senator believed Mr Trump would lose. After he won, Mr Graham became one of the president’s staunchest defenders. Where once Mr Graham claimed that stopping Mr Trump was worth losing an election, he now argued winning was what mattered: democracy had rendered his own judgments about ideas and character void. The American people “rejected my analysis”, he told CBS in 2017. Mr Graham never so surrendered his views when Americans chose a Democrat. But now he abided even Mr Trump’s attacks on his dear friend McCain, one of whose last wishes, before he died in 2018, was that the president not attend his memorial service. In his winking way Mr Graham continued to criticise Mr Trump’s ethics and motives off-the-record to the press. He contended he was currying the president’s favour not just to cling to the Senate seat he loved, but to put his relevance to work restraining Mr Trump’s worst impulses, particularly on foreign policy. Mr Graham wanted to sustain support for Ukraine, Israel and NATO, and to confront Iran.
paragraphs.zh: 但那是在这位参议员相信特朗普会输的时候。特朗普获胜后，格雷厄姆成了总统最坚定的捍卫者之一。格雷厄姆曾经声称，阻止特朗普即使输掉一场选举也值得；现在他却主张，取胜才是重要的：民主已经使他自己对理念和品格的判断作废。2017年他对CBS说，美国人民“拒绝了我的分析”。当美国人选择一名民主党人时，格雷厄姆从未这样放弃自己的观点。但现在，他甚至容忍特朗普攻击自己亲爱的朋友麦凯恩；麦凯恩在2018年去世前的最后愿望之一，就是总统不要出席他的追悼会。格雷厄姆以他那种心照不宣的方式，继续在不具名背景下向媒体批评特朗普的道德和动机。他辩称，自己讨好总统，不只是为了保住他热爱的参议院席位，也是为了利用自己的相关性来约束特朗普最糟糕的冲动，尤其是在外交政策上。格雷厄姆希望维持对乌克兰、以色列和北约的支持，并对抗伊朗。

paragraphs.en: But publicly, Mr Graham had become such a minion by the 2020 election that he endorsed Mr Trump’s claims of fraud. Then, after rioters attacked the Capitol on January 6th 2021, Mr Graham acknowledged the electoral outcome was legitimate. “Trump and I, we’ve had a hell of a journey,” he said on the Senate floor. “I hate it to end this way.” But by late February he was promoting Mr Trump for president in 2024. Referring to Ms Cheney, he told Fox News that May, “The people who try to erase him are going to wind up getting erased. It’s impossible for this party to move forward without President Trump.” That judgment has always been, for Mr Trump, usefully self-fulfilling. The fewer Republican leaders who stood up to Mr Trump, the more powerful he became.
paragraphs.zh: 但到了2020年大选时，在公开场合，格雷厄姆已经变成了如此忠实的附庸，以至于他支持特朗普关于选举舞弊的说法。随后，在2021年1月6日暴徒袭击国会大厦之后，格雷厄姆承认选举结果合法。“特朗普和我，我们一路走来真是不容易，”他在参议院发言时说。“我讨厌它以这种方式结束。”但到2月底，他已经在为特朗普2024年竞选总统造势。那年5月，在谈到切尼女士时，他对福克斯新闻说：“那些试图抹掉他的人，最后会被抹掉。这个党没有特朗普总统就不可能向前走。”对特朗普来说，这一判断一直以一种有用的方式自我实现。站出来反对特朗普的共和党领导人越少，他就变得越强大。

paragraphs.en: When applied to politics, the definition of courage turns slippery. There is bravery in courting criticism, as Mr Graham did, by compromising to achieve some progress, even when principle is at stake. The challenge is in choosing where to draw the line. Mr Graham never did. Though he had some success in protecting his causes, by the time of his death he had changed Mr Trump’s politics far less than Mr Trump had changed his. And in his bid to promote democracy abroad, he helped corrode it at home. ■
paragraphs.zh: 当“勇气”的定义被用于政治时，它就变得难以把握。像格雷厄姆那样，通过妥协来取得一些进展，即便原则也处于利害攸关之中，仍然愿意招致批评，这里面确有勇敢。挑战在于选择把界线划在哪里。格雷厄姆从未划出这条线。尽管他在保护自己关心的事业方面取得过一些成功，但到他去世时，他改变特朗普政治的程度远远小于特朗普改变他的程度。而在他努力推动海外民主的过程中，他帮助腐蚀了国内的民主。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订户可以订阅我们的Opinion新闻简报，该简报汇集了我们社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信中的精华。

articles.title_en: The Panama Canal is growing more important
articles.title_zh: 巴拿马运河正变得更加重要
articles.rubric_en: And the challenges to it are getting more acute
articles.rubric_zh: 而它面临的挑战也愈发严峻

paragraphs.en: When Donald Trump’s war with Iran first closed the Strait of Hormuz this year, the effects were felt swiftly on the other side of the world. Demand for passage through the Panama Canal, the 80km waterway linking the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans, shot up to near its maximum capacity of 36 to 40 transits a day. The number of ships carrying fuel through the canal rose sharply as countries turned to American energy. The number of liquefied-natural-gas (LNG) cargoes transiting almost doubled in April, compared with the same month last year.
paragraphs.zh: 今年，唐纳德·特朗普与伊朗的战争首次导致霍尔木兹海峡关闭时，世界另一端很快就感受到了影响。巴拿马运河这条连接大西洋和太平洋、全长80公里的水道，通行需求飙升，接近每天36至40次过境的最大运力。随着各国转向美国能源，经由运河运输燃料的船只数量大幅增加。4月份通过运河的液化天然气（LNG）货运量与去年同期相比几乎翻了一番。

paragraphs.en: The Panama Canal carries just 5-6% of world maritime trade. That is less than some other chokepoints, such as the Strait of Malacca near Singapore. Yet its importance is growing, both because of the Middle East war and because of enduring changes to flows of goods and energy. This could be a boon to the country of 4.6m people that hosts it, but only if it can avoid becoming a pawn in the rivalry between China and the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 巴拿马运河承载的海上贸易量仅占全球的5-6%。这低于其他一些咽喉要道，例如新加坡附近的马六甲海峡。然而，由于中东战争以及货物和能源流向的持久变化，它的重要性正在上升。对拥有460万人口、坐拥这条运河的巴拿马来说，这可能是一大利好，但前提是它能避免成为中国与美国竞争中的棋子。

paragraphs.en: The goods that pass along the canal run the gamut, from toys to cars, fertilisers to fruit. But its significance as a route for energy shipments is especially striking, largely due to the United States’ rise as an exporter of LNG and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). The canal built the infrastructure needed to accept this traffic in 2016. It currently moves around 1m barrels of LPG a day, much of it travelling from the Gulf Coast to petrochemical plants in Asia.
paragraphs.zh: 通过运河的货物种类繁多，从玩具到汽车，从化肥到水果，应有尽有。但它作为能源运输路线的重要性尤其引人注目，这在很大程度上是因为美国崛起为液化天然气和液化石油气（LPG）出口国。2016年，运河建成了接纳这类运输所需的基础设施。目前，它每天运输约100万桶液化石油气，其中大部分从美国墨西哥湾沿岸运往亚洲的石化工厂。

paragraphs.en: The Economist met Ricaurte Vásquez Morales, the canal’s administrator, in a control room overlooking the waterway. He says the canal authority has launched $8.5bn worth of projects on his watch.
