The boldest is a pipeline to carry LpG across the isthmus, which could raise its energy capacity by up to 2.5m barrels a day. In addition, two new port terminals will expand container capacity. The aim, he says, is to make Panama not merely a passage between oceans but a broader market for trade.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》在一间俯瞰运河的控制室里见到了运河管理局局长里考尔特·巴斯克斯·莫拉莱斯。他说，在他任内，运河管理局已启动价值85亿美元的项目。其中最大胆的是一条把液化石油气输送穿越地峡的管道，这可能使其能源运输能力每天最多增加250万桶。此外，两个新的港口码头将扩大集装箱运力。他说，目标是让巴拿马不仅仅成为两大洋之间的通道，而是成为更广泛的贸易市场。

paragraphs.en: The extra traffic because of the war in the Gulf may help pay for that. The auctioned cost of passage through the canal (some passages are pre-booked but the most in-demand slots are auctioned) nearly tripled from a pre-Hormuz average of $135,000-140,000 to about $385,000-425,000 in April and May. One company paid $4m for a single slot. In the fiscal year to September 2025 the canal put almost $3bn into Panama’s treasury, a record amount that accounts for over a fifth of state revenue.
paragraphs.zh: 海湾战争带来的额外交通量可能有助于支付这些项目的费用。运河通行的拍卖价格（部分通行名额可提前预订，但最抢手的时段会进行拍卖）从霍尔木兹海峡关闭前平均13.5万至14万美元，几乎涨了两倍，在4月和5月达到约38.5万至42.5万美元。一家公司为单个通行时段支付了400万美元。在截至2025年9月的财政年度，运河为巴拿马国库贡献了近30亿美元，创下纪录，占国家收入的五分之一以上。

paragraphs.en: Mr Vásquez is aware that the canal’s current good fortune may prove to be “a blip”. But some of it may last. It is far from clear when Hormuz will be fully open, given the resumption of strikes between Iran and America in recent weeks. Even if it does open, trade patterns will not revert quite to how they were before, reckon researchers at Goldman Sachs, a bank.
paragraphs.zh: 巴斯克斯先生意识到，运河眼下的好运可能只是“昙花一现”。但其中一部分影响也许会持续。鉴于最近几周伊朗与美国之间再次相互打击，霍尔木兹海峡何时能够完全开放还远不明朗。高盛这家银行的研究人员认为，即便它重新开放，贸易格局也不会完全回到从前。

paragraphs.en: Yet Mr Vásquez—and his designated successor, Ilya Espino de Marotta, who is due to take over in October—face two big challenges. One is the canal’s vulnerability to climate change. Its locks are supplied by lakes which depend on rainfall for replenishment. A severe drought in 2023-24 forced the authority to cut daily transits to as few as 18. It also caused many LNG carriers to switch to a route around the Cape of Good Hope, despite it taking longer; many of them have not yet returned to the canal, says Francis Zeimetz of Panama’s Maritime Chamber. This year’s El Niño could cause similar disruption.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，巴斯克斯先生以及他指定的继任者伊利娅·埃斯皮诺·德·马罗塔面临两大挑战；后者预计将在10月接任。其一是运河容易受到气候变化影响。运河船闸由湖泊供水，而湖泊依靠降雨补给。2023-24年的严重干旱迫使管理局将每日通行次数削减至最低18次。巴拿马海事商会的弗朗西斯·蔡梅茨说，这也导致许多液化天然气运输船改走绕行好望角的路线，尽管这条路线耗时更长；其中许多船至今尚未回到巴拿马运河。今年的厄尔尼诺现象可能造成类似干扰。

paragraphs.en: Hence another big project that is at last getting under way: a reservoir on the Indio river, damming it to secure the canal’s water supply. The decades-old plan languished because it was costly and controversial. It will displace some 2,000 people. Finally approved in 2025, works are due to start next year and should be completed by 2032. Mr Vásquez says it will supply enough water for roughly 11.5 extra transits a day in a dry year, ensuring the canal’s operation for the next half-century.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，另一个大型项目终于开始推进：在因迪奥河上修建水库，通过筑坝保障运河供水。这个已有数十年历史的计划曾因成本高昂且争议不断而搁置。它将导致约2,000人迁离。该项目于2025年最终获批，工程计划明年启动，并应在2032年前完成。巴斯克斯先生说，在干旱年份，它将提供足够水量，使每天通行次数大约增加11.5次，确保运河未来半个世纪的运营。

paragraphs.en: The most pressing problem, however, is geopolitics. Panama has recently found itself in the middle of a rivalry between the United States and China. Since returning to the White House in January 2025, Mr Trump has repeatedly threatened to “take back” the Panama Canal, which the United States built and operated from 1914 until it handed it to Panama to run in 1999. It seems like bluster, but it is a theme he returns to. This month he again ruminated on it in two speeches. The attention is a shock after years when Panama appeared to fall off the maps in Washington. The United States did not post an ambassador to Panama between 2018 and 2022.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，最紧迫的问题是地缘政治。巴拿马最近发现自己处在美国和中国竞争的夹缝中。自2025年1月重返白宫以来，特朗普先生多次威胁要“收回”巴拿马运河。美国从1914年起修建并运营这条运河，直到1999年才把它交给巴拿马管理。这听起来像是虚张声势，但却是他反复提起的主题。本月，他又在两次演讲中琢磨起这件事。对巴拿马来说，这种关注令人震惊，因为多年来它似乎已从华盛顿的地图上消失。2018年至2022年间，美国没有向巴拿马派驻大使。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump is worried about the security of America’s supply chains (more than 70% of traffic in the Panama Canal is going to or coming from the United States). In 2025 his administration opened an investigation into global chokepoints. His interest also results from his revival of the 19th-century Monroe Doctrine, which holds the Americas to be the backyard of the United States and considers outside powers unwelcome. He has repeatedly said that China is “running” the canal.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普先生担心美国供应链的安全（巴拿马运河超过70%的交通量是驶往美国或来自美国）。2025年，他的政府对全球咽喉要道展开调查。他的兴趣也源于他重新唤起19世纪的门罗主义；该主义认为美洲是美国的后院，并认为外部势力不受欢迎。他多次声称中国正在“运营”这条运河。

paragraphs.en: Foreign firms do not run the canal, but some hold concessions in the canal’s hinterland, notes Mr Vásquez. Alonso Illueca, a Panamanian lawyer, notes that China’s footprint has increased rapidly in Panama in recent years.
paragraphs.zh: 巴斯克斯先生指出，外国公司并不运营运河，但有一些公司在运河腹地持有特许经营权。巴拿马律师阿隆索·伊柳埃卡指出，近年来中国在巴拿马的存在迅速扩大。

paragraphs.en: In 1996 the Panama Ports Company, a subsidiary of the Hong Kong-based conglomerate CK Hutchison, won concessions to run the ports of Balboa, at the Pacific end of the canal, and Cristóbal, at the Atlantic end. Other Chinese firms clustered around the waterway. Panama too often treats strategic assets as ordinary holdings that may be bought and sold, says Mr Illueca. Some also allege that Chinese companies, bound more by the needs of the Chinese Communist Party than rule of law, have found it easier than their American rivals to strike deals in a country that is notorious for corruption.
paragraphs.zh: 1996年，总部位于香港的企业集团长江和记旗下的巴拿马港口公司获得特许经营权，经营运河太平洋端的巴尔博亚港和大西洋端的克里斯托瓦尔港。其他中国企业也聚集在这条水道周围。伊柳埃卡先生说，巴拿马太常把战略资产当作可买可卖的普通持有资产。也有人指称，中国企业受中国共产党的需求约束多于受法治约束，因此在这个以腐败著称的国家，比美国竞争对手更容易达成交易。

paragraphs.en: American pressure has already had an impact. In January Panama’s Supreme Court ruled that the Panama Ports Company’s contract was unconstitutional. José Raúl Mulino, Panama’s president, handed the ports to two European firms until new contracts can be tendered. Last year Panama also left the Belt and Road Initiative, China’s international infrastructure-investment scheme, shortly after Mr Trump returned to the White House.
paragraphs.zh: 美国的压力已经产生影响。1月，巴拿马最高法院裁定巴拿马港口公司的合同违宪。巴拿马总统何塞·劳尔·穆利诺将这些港口交给两家欧洲公司管理，直到新合同可以招标。去年，在特朗普先生重返白宫后不久，巴拿马也退出了中国的国际基础设施投资计划“一带一路”倡议。

paragraphs.en: Mr Vásquez notes that the canal never closed during the pandemic; nor has it during drought or geopolitical turmoil. Panama has done a “tremendous job” running it, says Louis Sola, who used to head the Federal Maritime Commission, an independent regulator in Washington. But Panama and the canal’s administrator will have a hard time navigating the next few years, stuck between an American president who wants more control and a Chinese government that will make life hard for those who hurt its interests.
paragraphs.zh: 巴斯克斯先生指出，运河在疫情期间从未关闭；在干旱或地缘政治动荡期间也没有关闭。曾领导美国联邦海事委员会这一华盛顿独立监管机构的路易斯·索拉说，巴拿马在运营运河方面做得“非常出色”。但在未来几年，巴拿马和运河管理者将很难周旋：一边是想要更多控制权的美国总统，另一边是会让损害其利益者日子不好过的中国政府。

paragraphs.en: China has started to retaliate for Panama’s moves to appease Mr Trump. In March, 91 of 123 ships detained at Chinese ports were Panama-flagged; that figure climbed higher in April to 136 vessels. American officials say the detentions show no sign of ending. China is “trying to make Panama hurt”, says Mr Illueca. Asked what sums up his seven years at the helm, Mr Vásquez says “volatility”. His successor can expect more of that. ■
paragraphs.zh: 中国已经开始报复巴拿马为安抚特朗普先生而采取的举措。3月，在中国港口被扣留的123艘船中，有91艘悬挂巴拿马旗；4月这一数字进一步升至136艘。美国官员说，这些扣留看不到结束迹象。伊柳埃卡先生说，中国是在“试图让巴拿马吃苦头”。当被问及如何概括自己掌舵七年的经历时，巴斯克斯先生说：“波动。”他的继任者可以预期，还会有更多这样的波动。■

paragraphs.en: Correction (July 16th 2026): An earlier version of this story mistakenly said the pipeline would carry lng. It will carry lpg. Sorry.
paragraphs.zh: 更正（2026年7月16日）：本文早前版本误称该管道将输送液化天然气。它将输送液化石油气。谨致歉意。

paragraphs.en: Sign up to El Boletín, our subscriber-only newsletter on Latin America, to understand the forces shaping a fascinating and complex region.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《El Boletín》，这是我们面向订户的拉丁美洲新闻通讯，帮助你理解塑造这个迷人而复杂地区的各种力量。

articles.title_en: The biggest schools training America’s doctors are in the Caribbean
articles.title_zh: 培养美国医生的最大学校在加勒比海
articles.rubric_en: The industry has a seedy underbelly
articles.rubric_zh: 这个行业有着肮脏的阴暗面

paragraphs.en: Free shuttle buses run from St George’s University (SGU) to the white-sand beach at Grand Anse, the best in Grenada. American students in swimwear crowd the seafront bar. This post-exam ritual prompts envy from peers back home, says Daniel Nathan from Virginia. He was rejected by a few medical schools in the United States before he got his place at SGU, and didn’t fancy waiting a year to try again. “Grenada is pretty cool,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: 免费穿梭巴士从圣乔治大学（SGU）开往格兰德安斯的白沙滩，那里是格林纳达最好的海滩。穿着泳装的美国学生挤满了海滨酒吧。来自弗吉尼亚州的丹尼尔·内森说，这种考后仪式让美国国内的同龄人羡慕不已。他在美国申请几所医学院都被拒后，才获得了SGU的录取，而且不想等一年再试一次。“格林纳达挺酷的，”他说。

paragraphs.en: Mr Nathan is not alone. Seventy per cent of the students at SGU are American. Its intake is large, more than 1,000 students a year where the average American medical school admits 200. Its fees are higher than Harvard’s. Despite this, a growing cohort of would-be doctors is opting for island training. In 2010, Americans made up 48% of the graduates from Caribbean medical schools. By 2022 that share had risen to 67%. SGU provides more doctors to the United States than any other school in the world, including American ones. “The legacy system just simply does not produce enough doctors for the US health-care system,” says Scott Liles, who oversees Ross University School of Medicine in Barbados, where 98% of students are American or Canadian. Ross is the second-largest provider of doctors to the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 内森并不是个例。SGU的学生中有70%是美国人。它的招生规模很大，每年超过1,000名学生，而美国普通医学院平均录取200人。它的学费比哈佛还高。尽管如此，越来越多有志成为医生的人选择到这些岛上接受培训。2010年，美国人占加勒比医学院毕业生的48%。到2022年，这一比例已升至67%。SGU向美国输送的医生比世界上任何其他学校都多，包括美国本土学校在内。“传统体系根本无法为美国医疗保健系统培养足够的医生，”斯科特·莱尔斯说。他负责巴巴多斯的罗斯大学医学院，该校98%的学生是美国人或加拿大人。罗斯是向美国输送医生数量第二多的学校。

paragraphs.en: The love-in is mutual: SGU contributes a good chunk of Grenada’s GDP and is the largest private employer. Charitable donations endear it to the local population. “Everything depends on St George’s,” says Lincus Baptiste, a take-away vendor who has worked on the campus for 11 years.
paragraphs.zh: 这种亲密关系是双向的：SGU为格林纳达的GDP贡献了相当大一部分，也是当地最大的私营雇主。慈善捐赠让它深受当地民众欢迎。“一切都依赖圣乔治大学，”林克斯·巴普蒂斯特说。他是一名外卖摊贩，已在校园里工作了11年。

paragraphs.en: Sadly, the prominence of Ross and SGU belies the existence of less scrupulous actors. The allure of the opportunity to practise medicine in the United States is a powerful incentive. There are around 100 medical schools in the Caribbean. On the other side of Barbados from Ross is Queen’s University College of Medicine. On the evening The Economist visited the site—a nondescript house at the top of a hill—no one was there. Discussion threads on Reddit, a chat board, tell of online-only courses and broken promises to provide legitimate qualifications. Queen’s website says it is listed on the World Directory of Medical Schools, but a listing does not imply accreditation. The school did not reply to requests for comment.
paragraphs.zh: 遗憾的是，罗斯和SGU的显赫地位掩盖了不那么守规矩的参与者的存在。能够在美国行医这一机会有着强大的吸引力。加勒比海地区约有100所医学院。在巴巴多斯与罗斯相隔岛屿另一端的地方，是女王大学医学院。《经济学人》傍晚到访该校所在地时，那里没有人在场；那只是山顶上一栋不起眼的房子。聊天论坛Reddit上的讨论帖称，该校只提供线上课程，并且未能兑现提供合法资格的承诺。女王大学的网站称，它被列入《世界医学院名录》，但被列入名录并不意味着获得认证。该校没有回复置评请求。

paragraphs.en: The path from the Caribbean to licensed doctor in the United States is complex. Before applying for a residency, a graduate must certify their qualification with the Educational Commission for Foreign Medical Graduates, an American NGO. It only certifies graduates of schools that have in turn been accredited by an agency approved by a committee of the US Department of Education, or its international equivalent.
paragraphs.zh: 从加勒比海地区走向美国持证医生的道路很复杂。毕业生在申请住院医师培训之前，必须通过美国非政府组织“外国医学毕业生教育委员会”认证自己的资格。该委员会只认证那些学校毕业生，而这些学校本身必须已获得某个机构的认证；该机构还必须得到美国教育部一个委员会或其国际同等机构的批准。

paragraphs.en: But those agencies can operate from anywhere in the world. “It kind of breaks our brains a little bit to see an island that is in the Caribbean, that is a constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands, getting its medical accreditation from Kyrgyzstan,” says Rosalie Hancock of Bellevue College in Washington state, co-author of a report on Caribbean medical schools. “You don’t really see that in any other field of education.”
paragraphs.zh: 但这些认证机构可以在世界任何地方运作。“看到一个位于加勒比海、又是荷兰王国组成国的岛屿，却从吉尔吉斯斯坦获得医学认证，这多少让我们有点难以理解，”华盛顿州贝尔维尤学院的罗莎莉·汉考克说。她是一份关于加勒比医学院报告的共同作者。“在教育的其他任何领域，你真的看不到这种情况。”

paragraphs.en: This complexity can make it hard to identify illegitimate operators. Some students end up saddled with debt and no closer to being a doctor. Rashed, 32, from Trinidad, cried with happiness when he was accepted into All Saints University College of Medicine in St Vincent. He had been rejected by nearly 50 other medical schools. But the school, which later changed its name to Richmond Gabriel University, suddenly moved all courses online and told students that a new campus was opening in Curaçao. It never did. In April, one of the founders appeared in court in Chicago to face federal charges of fraud and embezzlement related to the city’s Loretto Hospital.
paragraphs.zh: 这种复杂性可能使人难以识别非法经营者。一些学生最终背上债务，却离成为医生毫无进展。来自特立尼达、32岁的拉希德在被圣文森特的众圣大学医学院录取时，喜极而泣。他此前已被近50所其他医学院拒绝。但这所学校后来改名为里士满加布里埃尔大学，突然把所有课程转到线上，并告诉学生，一个新校区将在库拉索开设。这个校区从未开成。今年4月，该校一名创始人在芝加哥出庭，面对与该市洛雷托医院有关的联邦欺诈和挪用公款指控。

paragraphs.en: Rashed says he is “totally broke”. He has managed to enroll in a different, accredited medical school. His peers at SGU and Ross are a mainstay of America’s medical system. The allure of a career in medicine in the United States will probably get more like him into trouble. ■
paragraphs.zh: 拉希德说自己“彻底破产了”。他设法进入了另一所获得认证的医学院就读。他在SGU和罗斯的同辈则是美国医疗体系的重要支柱。在美国从医这一职业的吸引力，可能会让更多像他一样的人陷入麻烦。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to El Boletín, our subscriber-only newsletter on Latin America, to understand the forces shaping a fascinating and complex region.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《El Boletín》，这是我们面向订户的拉丁美洲新闻简报，帮助你理解塑造这个迷人而复杂地区的各种力量。

articles.title_en: Argentina v England is one of the most intense rivalries in sport
articles.title_zh: 阿根廷对英格兰是体育界最激烈的宿敌对决之一
articles.rubric_en: The two countries have not played each other in over two decades
articles.rubric_zh: 这两个国家已有二十多年没有交手

paragraphs.en: THE SUMMER of 1966 is remembered differently in two of the world’s football powerhouses. For England it was euphoric: on home turf they beat West Germany in the final to win their first—and so far only—World Cup title. For Argentina it was different. Their exit from the tournament after losing to England in the quarter-finals is a bitter memory. The contested game, in which Argentina’s captain had to be forcibly removed from the pitch, saw England score a disputed goal. England’s coach called his opponents “animals”. The game is known as el robo del siglo (the robbery of the century) in Argentina.
paragraphs.zh: 1966年的那个夏天，在世界两大足球强国的记忆中截然不同。对英格兰来说，那是令人狂喜的时刻：他们在本土举行的决赛中击败西德，赢得了他们第一座，也是迄今唯一一座世界杯冠军。对阿根廷来说，情况则不同。他们在四分之一决赛中输给英格兰后出局，成了一段苦涩的记忆。那场充满争议的比赛中，阿根廷队长不得不被强行带离球场，英格兰则打进了一粒有争议的进球。英格兰主教练称对手为“动物”。在阿根廷，这场比赛被称为 el robo del siglo（世纪大劫案）。

paragraphs.en: Six decades on, with a German coach, Thomas Tuchel, at the helm, England came as close to reclaiming the title as they have ever been. The squad lost to Argentina, the reigning world champions, 2-1 in a semi-final on July 15th. The game in Atlanta was the first time the teams had faced each other in two decades. It was still testy and foul-ridden throughout.
paragraphs.zh: 六十年后，在德国籍主教练托马斯·图赫尔（Thomas Tuchel）执掌帅印的情况下，英格兰距离重新夺回冠军头衔已是前所未有地接近。7月15日，这支球队在半决赛中以1比2输给了卫冕世界冠军阿根廷。亚特兰大的这场比赛，是两队二十年来首次交手。整场比赛依然火药味十足，犯规不断。

paragraphs.en: Lionel Scaloni, Argentina’s head coach, had insisted that it was “a football game and that is all”. Every Argentine knew that was not true. Atlanta’s police force increased security ahead of the game. Opposing fans entered from different ends of the stadium. Such was the depth, even after a long hiatus, of perhaps the most intense transcontinental rivalry in sport.
paragraphs.zh: 阿根廷主教练莱昂内尔·斯卡洛尼（Lionel Scaloni）曾坚称，这“只是一场足球比赛，仅此而已”。每个阿根廷人都知道这并不是真的。赛前，亚特兰大警方加强了安保。双方球迷从体育场不同入口入场。即便经历了漫长的间隔，这或许仍是体育界最激烈的跨洲宿敌关系，其积怨之深可见一斑。

paragraphs.en: Argentina and England have now played six times at the World Cup since it began in 1930. Although animosity took off in 1966, 1986 was the year it transcended sport. Argentina had invaded the Falkland Islands four years earlier, laying claim to the British overseas territory in the South Atlantic over which both countries assert sovereignty. Argentina lost the 74-day war. Nearly one thousand people died. The Falklands remain British territory today.
paragraphs.zh: 自1930年世界杯创办以来，阿根廷和英格兰如今已在世界杯上交手六次。虽然敌意是在1966年开始升温，但1986年才是它超越体育本身的一年。四年前，阿根廷曾入侵福克兰群岛，宣称对这片位于南大西洋、英阿两国都声称拥有主权的英国海外领土拥有主权。阿根廷输掉了这场持续74天的战争。近一千人丧生。福克兰群岛如今仍是英国领土。

paragraphs.en: When the two countries played each other in the quarter-final in Mexico City in 1986, emotions ran high. Argentine striker Diego Maradona scored two historic goals, one using la mano de Dios (the hand of God, pictured), punching the ball into the net. Argentina went on to win the tournament. Maradona later admitted to the handball, and claimed that it was revenge for Las Malvinas, as the Falklands are known in Argentina. Tension persisted in games in 1998 and 2002.
paragraphs.zh: 1986年，两国在墨西哥城的四分之一决赛中交手时，情绪高度紧张。阿根廷前锋迭戈·马拉多纳（Diego Maradona）打进两粒载入史册的进球，其中一粒借助 la mano de Dios（上帝之手，见图），他用拳头将球打入网窝。阿根廷随后赢得了那届赛事冠军。马拉多纳后来承认了手球，并声称这是为 Las Malvinas 复仇，也就是阿根廷对福克兰群岛的称呼。1998年和2002年的比赛中，紧张气氛仍然延续。

paragraphs.en: The territory remains a sticking point. Javier Milei, Argentina’s populist president, who has previously been criticised for a lack of support for the cause, has become more vocal. In April, after a leaked memo showed that the United States could review its position on British sovereignty of the islands, Mr Milei said they would “always be Argentine”.
paragraphs.zh: 这片领土仍是一个争议焦点。阿根廷民粹主义总统哈维尔·米莱（Javier Milei）此前曾因对这一事业支持不足而受到批评，如今他的表态变得更加强硬。今年4月，一份泄露的备忘录显示，美国可能重新审视其对英国拥有这些岛屿主权的立场；随后米莱表示，这些岛屿将“永远属于阿根廷”。

paragraphs.en: After their win, some Argentine players celebrated by holding up a banner that read: “Las Malvinas son Argentinas.” They and the fans have been singing “The Fourth Star”, an anthem that has gone viral online. One lyric goes: “For the Malvinas, for Diego (Maradona) and for Leo’s last run (Lionel Messi, Argentina’s star player); Argentina I want to see you become back-to-back champions.” Glory is on offer in the final against Spain on July 19th. But an Argentine win won’t change anything in the Falklands. ■
paragraphs.zh: 获胜后，一些阿根廷球员举起一条横幅庆祝，上面写着：“Las Malvinas son Argentinas.”（马尔维纳斯群岛属于阿根廷。）他们和球迷一直在唱《第四颗星》（“The Fourth Star”），这首歌已在网上走红。其中一句歌词唱道：“为了马尔维纳斯，为了迭戈（马拉多纳），也为了莱奥的最后一舞（莱昂内尔·梅西，阿根廷球星）；阿根廷，我想看到你卫冕成功。”7月19日对阵西班牙的决赛将带来争夺荣耀的机会。但阿根廷即便获胜，也不会改变福克兰群岛的任何现实。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to El Boletín, our subscriber-only newsletter on Latin America, to understand the forces shaping a fascinating and complex region.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅 El Boletín，这是我们面向订阅用户推出的拉丁美洲新闻通讯，帮助你理解塑造这一迷人而复杂地区的各种力量。

articles.title_en: Is Aung San Suu Kyi dead?
articles.title_zh: 昂山素季去世了吗？
articles.rubric_en: Myanmar’s jailed leader has not been seen since 2022
articles.rubric_zh: 这位被监禁的缅甸领导人自2022年以来再未公开露面

paragraphs.en: TAICHITO HAD followed Aung San Suu Kyi everywhere. But the dog was not allowed to accompany Myanmar’s leader into prison after a military junta overthrew her government five years ago. Last month, the floppy-eared mongrel died at her home in Yangon, the former capital, aged 15, still awaiting her return. He had been a gift from Kim Aris, her son, on her release from an earlier spell in prison in 2010. “I think he was the best thing I ever gave her. He was very faithful to her,” says Mr Aris in an interview in London.
paragraphs.zh: 泰奇托曾经寸步不离地跟着昂山素季。但五年前，一个军政府推翻她的政府后，这只狗未被允许陪同缅甸领导人入狱。上个月，这只耷拉着耳朵的混种犬在前首都仰光她的家中去世，享年15岁，至死仍在等待她归来。它是她的儿子金·阿里斯在她2010年结束此前一段囚禁获释时送给她的礼物。“我觉得它是我送给她的最好的东西。它对她非常忠诚，”阿里斯在伦敦接受采访时说。

paragraphs.en: Mr Aris has a bigger worry, however: his aged mother has disappeared. He has been travelling the world in recent months, pushing presidents and ministers to demand that Myanmar’s military government provide “proof of life”. The last official appearance of Ms Suu Kyi, who would now be 81, came at the conclusion of her show trial at the end of 2022. Since then, the army has consistently refused her lawyers’ requests to see her. Reports of sightings in Myanmar’s prison system have trickled out of the war-torn country in recent years, but are impossible to verify.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，阿里斯有一个更大的担忧：他年迈的母亲失踪了。近几个月来，他一直在世界各地奔走，敦促各国总统和部长要求缅甸军政府提供“存活证明”。现年本应81岁的昂山素季最后一次官方露面，是在2022年底对她的作秀审判结束时。此后，军方一直拒绝她的律师提出的会见请求。近年来，关于她在缅甸监狱系统中被人看见的消息陆续从这个饱受战争蹂躏的国家传出，但无法核实。

paragraphs.en: Ms Suu Kyi ranks as one of the world’s most famous political prisoners. She spent many years under house arrest while leading peaceful democratic protests against Myanmar’s military regime during the 1980s to the 2010s, and won the Nobel peace prize in 1991. After her party finally came to power in 2015, however, her reputation was damaged by her defence of the armed forces’ treatment of the Rohingyas, a persecuted Muslim minority. In 2021 a military coup removed her from power, before sending her to prison.
paragraphs.zh: 昂山素季是世界上最著名的政治犯之一。20世纪80年代至2010年代，她领导反对缅甸军政府的和平民主抗议，期间多年遭软禁，并于1991年获得诺贝尔和平奖。然而，在她的政党终于于2015年上台后，她因维护武装部队对受迫害的穆斯林少数族群罗兴亚人的处置而名誉受损。2021年，一场军事政变将她赶下台，随后把她送进监狱。

paragraphs.en: The military government claimed in April that it had moved Ms Suu Kyi to house arrest, but has turned down repeated requests from diplomats who ask to visit. Pressed by diplomats about her condition, regime officials invariably reply that she is in good health, but say little else. A photo published at the time of her supposed move to house arrest showed her chatting with a policeman and an army officer inside an unidentifiable building. There is no indication that it is of recent vintage, however, and Mr Aris doubts its authenticity. He says that if she is indeed under house arrest, it is not at her home in Yangon and that her house in Naypyidaw, the new capital, has been torn down.
paragraphs.zh: 军政府4月声称已将昂山素季转为软禁，但多次拒绝外交官提出的探访请求。当外交官追问她的状况时，政权官员总是一成不变地回答说她身体健康，除此之外几乎不再透露。她据称转为软禁时公布的一张照片显示，她在一栋无法辨认的建筑内与一名警察和一名军官交谈。然而，没有迹象表明这张照片是近期拍摄的，阿里斯也怀疑其真实性。他说，如果她确实遭到软禁，也不是在她位于仰光的家中；而她在新首都内比都的住所已经被拆除。

paragraphs.en: Senior General Min Aung Hlaing, the man who led the coup against her, had himself declared president in March. He has been asked about Ms Suu Kyi at least twice recently. Narendra Modi, India’s prime minister, brought her up in talks with him in Delhi last month. And in May, Julie Bishop, the UN special envoy on Myanmar, asked to see her when she met the general. Diplomats privy to those conversations say that he responded with anger at the mention of her name.
paragraphs.zh: 敏昂莱大将，也就是领导针对她发动政变的人，已于3月宣布自己出任总统。最近至少有两次，有人向他问起昂山素季。印度总理纳伦德拉·莫迪上个月在德里与他会谈时提到了她。5月，联合国缅甸问题特使朱莉·毕晓普会见这名将军时，也要求见她。知情外交官说，他一听到她的名字就愤怒回应。

paragraphs.en: Some of those briefed on these conversations worry that the coup-leader’s reaction could indicate that he is unable to produce the requested proof of life—because she is either dead or in bad shape. Others are sceptical. “To keep that under wraps would be impossible,” says Morgan Michaels, a Myanmar expert at the International Institute for Strategic Studies, a think-tank in London. The general’s profound dislike of his political rival, as one ambassador puts it, may be sufficient explanation for her isolation.
paragraphs.zh: 一些听取过这些谈话通报的人担心，政变领导人的反应可能表明他无法提供所要求的存活证明，因为她要么已经去世，要么状况很糟。也有人对此持怀疑态度。“要把这种事捂住是不可能的，”伦敦智库国际战略研究所的缅甸问题专家摩根·迈克尔斯说。正如一位大使所说，这名将军对其政治对手的深切厌恶，或许已足以解释她为何被隔绝。

paragraphs.en: Foreign ministers from other members of the Association of South-East Asian Nations (ASEAN) have repeatedly raised Ms Suu Kyi’s welfare with their opposite number in the junta, including at a meeting in Bangkok on July 12th. The generals want to fully rejoin ASEAN, which suspended Myanmar from high-level meetings after the coup. They also want their seat at the UN. Myanmar has been represented there since the coup by an official of the ousted civilian government. Releasing Ms Suu Kyi, or just giving foreign diplomats access to her, might melt opposition in both institutions to normalisation.
paragraphs.zh: 东南亚国家联盟（东盟）其他成员国的外长曾多次向军政府中与他们对口的官员提及昂山素季的安危，包括在7月12日曼谷的一次会议上。将军们希望全面重返东盟；政变后，东盟暂停缅甸参加高级别会议。他们还想要缅甸在联合国的席位。政变以来，缅甸在联合国一直由被推翻的文官政府的一名官员代表。释放昂山素季，或者仅仅允许外国外交官接触她，都可能软化这两个机构中反对关系正常化的立场。

paragraphs.en: Doing either would have less predictable consequences inside Myanmar. Ms Suu Kyi has consistently advocated non-violent resistance. Since the coup, a nationwide campaign of armed groups representing many of Myanmar’s ethnic minorities has emerged to challenge military rule; for the first time they have joined forces with revolutionaries from the Burman ethnic majority. But Ms Suu Kyi, a Burman nationalist who in office gave minorities short shrift, lacks support from many armed groups. Freeing her would be “the easiest way to break up” unity among them, says a foreign diplomat who has been working to stitch them together.
paragraphs.zh: 这两种做法中的任何一种，在缅甸国内造成的后果都不那么可预测。昂山素季一直主张非暴力抵抗。政变以来，一场由代表缅甸许多少数民族的武装组织组成的全国性运动兴起，挑战军方统治；他们首次与来自缅族多数族群的革命者联手。但昂山素季是一名缅族民族主义者，执政时对少数族群重视不足，因此缺乏许多武装组织的支持。一名一直在努力把这些组织联合起来的外国外交官说，释放她将是“拆散”它们团结的“最容易办法”。

paragraphs.en: Mr Michaels sees the armed forces as less worried about the armed uprising than they are about non-violent movements of the sort that Ms Suu Kyi has advocated since 1988. She retains an almost talismanic power over many members of the Burman majority, who continue to take personal risks to show their fealty.
paragraphs.zh: 迈克尔斯认为，相比武装起义，武装部队更担心昂山素季自1988年以来一直倡导的那类非暴力运动。她对许多缅族多数族群成员仍保有近乎护身符般的影响力，这些人继续冒着个人风险来表示对她的忠诚。

paragraphs.en: The diplomatic focus on Ms Suu Kyi’s plight risks eclipsing that of Myanmar’s 55m people, who continue to suffer under the junta. She is but one of 14,517 political prisoners confirmed to be held by the government, says the Assistance Association for Political Prisoners (AAPP), an advocacy group. Medical care in prison is poor, air conditioning in the sweltering hot season unavailable. (Ms Suu Kyi is reported to have refused an air-conditioned cell because others do not have one.) This year alone, the AAPP confirmed the deaths of over 60 political prisoners in custody. Even as Mr Aris seeks news of his own mother, he says she would not want their plight to be forgotten. ■
paragraphs.zh: 外交界对昂山素季困境的关注，可能会让缅甸5500万人民的处境相形失色；他们仍在军政府统治下受苦。倡议组织政治犯援助协会（AAPP）表示，她只是已确认被政府关押的14,517名政治犯之一。监狱里的医疗条件很差，在酷热季节也没有空调。（据报道，昂山素季曾拒绝入住有空调的牢房，因为其他人没有。）仅今年一年，AAPP就确认有60多名政治犯死于羁押期间。即便阿里斯在寻找自己母亲的消息，他也说，她不会希望这些人的苦难被遗忘。■

paragraphs.en: For exclusive coverage of Asian politics, economics and security, sign up to Asia Bulletin, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取关于亚洲政治、经济与安全的独家报道，请订阅《亚洲简报》，这是我们每周一期、仅面向订阅者的新闻简报。

articles.title_en: An unreliable America is drawing Asia’s middle powers closer
articles.title_zh: 一个不可靠的美国正让亚洲中等强国彼此靠得更近
articles.rubric_en: A burst of Indian diplomacy exemplifies the trend
articles.rubric_zh: 印度外交的一阵密集行动体现了这一趋势

paragraphs.en: NARENDRA MODI is often seen pressing flesh at bilateral meetings, or embracing his favourite counterparts with a bear hug. A visit to France and Slovakia last month was the peripatetic Indian prime minister’s 100th foreign trip since taking office in 2014. Shortly after returning to India, he jetted off to the Seychelles. He was then in Delhi to host Japan’s prime minister, Takaichi Sanae, before heading to Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand.
paragraphs.zh: 人们常看到纳伦德拉·莫迪在双边会晤中与人握手寒暄，或用熊抱拥抱他最喜欢的同行。上个月访问法国和斯洛伐克，是这位奔波不停的印度总理自2014年上任以来第100次出访。回到印度不久后，他又飞往塞舌尔。随后他在德里接待日本首相高市早苗，之后又前往印度尼西亚、澳大利亚和新西兰。

paragraphs.en: The flurry of activity in the Indo-Pacific illustrates a trend: Asia’s middle powers want an insurance policy. Mr Modi aired deals on defence, maritime security, semiconductors and rare earths—all in the shadow of the two superpowers. Like other Asian countries, India has long had to contend with the economic heft and territorial ambitions of China. But as America has become less reliable the balancing act has got rather harder.
paragraphs.zh: 印度洋-太平洋地区这一连串活动体现出一个趋势：亚洲的中等强国想要一份保险。莫迪公布了涉及国防、海上安全、半导体和稀土的多项协议，而这一切都发生在两个超级大国的阴影之下。和其他亚洲国家一样，印度长期以来不得不应对中国的经济分量和领土野心。但随着美国变得不那么可靠，这种平衡术变得相当更难。

paragraphs.en: C. Raja Mohan, an Indian foreign-policy analyst, has described Mr Modi’s approach as “G minus 2”, referring to the so-called G2 pact that Donald Trump at times talks of striking with China. Asian countries are not turning their backs on America or seeking to contain China, he says: they know they cannot do either. Instead, they are working out what they can do together, putting aside old differences.
paragraphs.zh: 印度外交政策分析人士C. Raja Mohan把莫迪的做法称为“G减2”，指的是唐纳德·特朗普有时谈到要与中国达成的所谓G2协议。他说，亚洲国家并不是在背弃美国，也不是在寻求遏制中国：它们知道这两件事都做不到。相反，它们正在弄清楚彼此能一起做些什么，同时搁置旧有分歧。

paragraphs.en: For India the shift in America’s position has been particularly jarring. Though it has managed to get off the “punishment tariffs” Mr Trump imposed last August, few see how it can repair its relations with America while he is in office. Diplomats elsewhere in the region are aghast at the deterioration of such a vital relationship.
paragraphs.zh: 对印度来说，美国立场的转变尤其令人震动。尽管印度已经设法摆脱了特朗普去年8月施加的“惩罚性关税”，但很少有人看得出，在他任内印度如何能修复与美国的关系。该地区其他地方的外交官对这样一种至关重要关系的恶化感到震惊。

paragraphs.en: Such worries were further aggravated in June when America said that it was rebranding its Indo-Pacific Command, as it terms its military structure in the region, as the Pacific Command. The small textual change carried a big message. “Indo” had been added in 2018 to signal maritime co-operation between countries in the Quad—America, Australia, India and Japan—and portray India in particular as a critical partner. Its removal was seen as a snub in Delhi and another sign of American retrenchment.
paragraphs.zh: 6月，美国表示将把其所谓的地区军事架构“印度洋-太平洋司令部”重新命名为“太平洋司令部”，这进一步加剧了这些担忧。这个小小的文字变化传递了重大信号。“印度洋”一词是在2018年加入的，意在表明“四方安全对话”国家——美国、澳大利亚、印度和日本——之间的海上合作，并尤其把印度塑造成关键伙伴。它被删去，在德里被视为一次冷落，也是美国收缩的又一个信号。

paragraphs.en: What did Mr Modi’s tour yield? Most promising is a convergence between India and Australia. Their relationship has long been constrained by Australia’s opposition to nuclear proliferation and India’s suspicion of Western alliances. Politicians from Australia’s Labor Party have also raised concerns about Kashmir. Such differences are now forgotten. On July 9th Australia agreed to supply India with uranium for nuclear energy. Notably, the two will also co-ordinate action on maritime security. “India has long resisted that kind of language,” says Ian Hall of Griffith University.
paragraphs.zh: 莫迪此行取得了什么成果？最有希望的是印度与澳大利亚之间的趋同。两国关系长期受制于澳大利亚反对核扩散，以及印度对西方联盟的疑虑。澳大利亚工党的政界人士也曾对克什米尔问题表示担忧。如今这些分歧已被抛诸脑后。7月9日，澳大利亚同意向印度供应核能用铀。值得注意的是，两国还将协调海上安全行动。格里菲斯大学的Ian Hall说：“印度长期以来一直抗拒这种表述。”

paragraphs.en: India’s ties with Japan are also getting stronger, with the focus shifting from infrastructure to defence and technology. The two have agreed to work together on defence production for the first time, even if their inaugural project (making radio antennae) is small-bore. In New Zealand Mr Modi spoke of plans for more naval exercises and information sharing. In Jakarta he signed a deal to supply Indonesia with India’s BrahMos missile system.
paragraphs.zh: 印度与日本的关系也在加强，重点正从基础设施转向国防和技术。两国首次同意在国防生产方面开展合作，尽管它们的首个项目（制造无线电天线）规模不大。在新西兰，莫迪谈到了增加海军演习和信息共享的计划。在雅加达，他签署了一项协议，向印度尼西亚供应印度的“布拉莫斯”导弹系统。

paragraphs.en: Mr Modi is “essentially implementing Mark Carney’s idea of middle powers coming together in Asia”, says Ajay Bisaria, a former Indian high commissioner to Canada. Yet for all his frenetic activity, mutual concerns will not inevitably lead to deep co-operation. Asia’s middle powers still differ on many issues, especially over how to handle Mr Trump, and in some cases attitudes towards China.
paragraphs.zh: 印度前驻加拿大高级专员Ajay Bisaria说，莫迪“本质上是在亚洲落实马克·卡尼关于中等强国走到一起的想法”。然而，尽管他活动频繁，共同的担忧并不必然会带来深度合作。亚洲的中等强国在许多问题上仍有分歧，尤其是在如何应对特朗普方面，在某些情况下还包括对中国的态度。

paragraphs.en: Compared with those in Europe, Asian countries may be more divided over how to respond to a shifting balance between the two superpowers. But they are also a lot more exposed. Expect the energetic diplomacy to continue. ■
paragraphs.zh: 与欧洲国家相比，亚洲国家在如何应对两个超级大国之间力量平衡的变化上可能更加分裂。但它们也暴露在更大的风险之下。可以预期，这种积极的外交活动还会继续。■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of our India coverage by signing up to Essential India, our free weekly newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅我们的免费每周通讯《Essential India》，持续关注我们的印度报道。

articles.title_en: Haunted houses are in demand in Japan
articles.title_zh: 日本的“凶宅”正变得抢手
articles.rubric_en: What’s behind the growing desire for once-shunned “incident properties”?
articles.rubric_zh: 曾经避之不及的“事故房”，为何越来越受欢迎？

paragraphs.en: For more than a decade, Matsubara Tanishi, a Japanese comedian, has lived in houses where others met gruesome ends. One tenant murdered his brother. Another hanged herself. In a third an old man died alone and lay undiscovered for two years. “I used to think I might get cursed,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: 十多年来，日本喜剧演员松原田螺一直住在那些曾有人惨死的房子里。有一名住户杀害了自己的兄弟。另一名住户上吊自杀。第三处房子里，一位老人孤独死去，尸体两年后才被发现。“我以前总觉得自己可能会被诅咒，”他说。

paragraphs.en: In Japan such unfortunate houses are known as jiko bukken, or “incident properties”. Though many recoil at the thought of living in one, they have long fascinated the public. Mr Matsubara has built a career from inhabiting dozens of them; his memoirs have become best-sellers and inspired films. Now rising housing costs are making these properties look less unappealing to the wider public.
paragraphs.zh: 在日本，这类不幸的房子被称为 jiko bukken，即“事故房”。尽管许多人一想到住进去就退避三舍，但它们长期以来一直令公众着迷。松原先生靠住进几十处这样的房子做成了一番事业；他的回忆录成了畅销书，还被改编成电影。如今，住房成本不断上涨，使这些房子在更广泛公众眼中显得没那么令人反感了。

paragraphs.en: Incident properties are priced anywhere from 10% to 50% below market value, depending on the grisliness of the previous occupant’s demise. Such discounts of course exist elsewhere, but Japan’s culture around them is unusually elaborate. Oshima Teru, a property investor, runs a popular website mapping jiko bukken across the country, marking each with a fire icon and a description of the death. “It frightens me, but I can’t stop checking it,” says Kobayashi Yoko, a housekeeper in Tokyo. If she discovers that a client’s house is on the map, she invents an excuse not to go.
paragraphs.zh: 事故房的价格通常比市场价低 10% 到 50%，折扣幅度取决于上一位住户死亡方式的惨烈程度。当然，类似折价在别处也存在，但日本围绕这类房产形成的文化格外复杂。房地产投资者大岛照经营着一个热门网站，绘制全国各地 jiko bukken 的地图，用火焰图标标出每一处，并附上死亡情况说明。“这让我害怕，但我又忍不住去看，”东京家政员小林洋子说。如果她发现客户的房子在地图上，就会编个理由不去。

paragraphs.en: Japan is strikingly superstitious for a country where few profess a religion. A survey in 2024 showed that a third of Japanese believe in spirits. Social media teems with tales of ghosts lingering in incident properties. Some firms cater to those fears. Buddhist monks perform cleansing rituals. Kachimode, a firm in Tokyo, inspects jiko bukken with thermal cameras and other devices, purportedly searching for signs of paranormal activity. If none appear, it issues a document certifying the house as not haunted.
paragraphs.zh: 日本是一个颇为迷信的国家，尽管自称有宗教信仰的人并不多。2024 年的一项调查显示，三分之一的日本人相信灵魂存在。社交媒体上充斥着鬼魂徘徊在事故房里的故事。一些公司专门迎合这些恐惧。佛教僧侣会举行净化仪式。东京公司 Kachimode 会用热成像摄像机和其他设备检查 jiko bukken，据称是在寻找超自然活动的迹象。如果没有发现异常，它就会出具一份文件，证明这所房子没有闹鬼。

paragraphs.en: Demography is making such properties harder to ignore. The government recorded more than 20,000 kodokushi or “lonely deaths” in 2025, defining them as people who died alone and went unnoticed for at least eight days. In 2021 it issued a guideline saying that landlords need not disclose them, except in case of extraordinary circumstances, such as severe decomposition. The change also aimed to make it easier for older people to rent, since many landlords fear a lonely death taking place in their property.
paragraphs.zh: 人口结构变化正使这类房产越来越难以被忽视。日本政府记录显示，2025 年有超过 20,000 起 kodokushi，即“孤独死”；其定义是独自死亡且至少八天无人发现。2021 年，政府发布指导方针称，房东无需披露这类情况，除非存在特殊情形，例如尸体严重腐烂。此举也旨在让老年人更容易租到房子，因为许多房东担心自己的房产里发生孤独死。

paragraphs.en: Niki Hidenori, who runs a property firm focused on jiko bukken in Kobe, says more tenants are “weighing the price and taking a pragmatic approach”. In March average studio rents in central Tokyo rose by 13% year on year, a record high for the 22nd consecutive month. A recent survey suggests a majority of Japanese would consider living in a jiko bukken. Investors are drawn too; legally, a death need not be disclosed after three years have elapsed, so a property bought at a discount can later be sold at full price.
paragraphs.zh: 在神户经营一家专注于 jiko bukken 的房地产公司的仁木秀典说，越来越多租客正在“权衡价格，并采取务实态度”。今年 3 月，东京市中心单间公寓平均租金同比上涨 13%，连续第 22 个月创下历史新高。最近一项调查显示，多数日本人会考虑住进 jiko bukken。投资者也被吸引而来；从法律上说，死亡事件过了三年后就无需披露，因此以折扣价买入的房产日后可以按全价出售。

paragraphs.en: Mr Matsubara says he has never seen a ghost, but he has developed a habit of sleepwalking. He doubts malicious spirits are to blame, suspecting instead that years of living in such spaces have taken a psychological toll. Ryua, a young woman living in a jiko bukken in Tokyo, is at peace with it herself. But when friends visit, “they start getting nervous and leave quickly,” she says. “That’s a bit sad.” ■
paragraphs.zh: 松原先生说自己从未见过鬼，但他养成了梦游的习惯。他怀疑罪魁祸首并非恶灵，而是多年住在这种空间里给心理造成了负担。住在东京一处 jiko bukken 里的年轻女性 Ryua 自己对此很坦然。但她说，朋友来访时，“他们会开始紧张，然后很快离开。”“这有点让人难过。”■

paragraphs.en: For exclusive coverage of Asian politics, economics and security, sign up to Asia Bulletin, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取亚洲政治、经济与安全事务的独家报道，请订阅 Asia Bulletin，这是我们每周发布、仅面向订阅者的新闻简报。

articles.title_en: A real but selective crackdown on Cambodia’s scam industry
articles.title_zh: 对柬埔寨诈骗产业的一场真实但有选择性的打击
articles.rubric_en: Sanctions from America and pressure from China have forced a reckoning
articles.rubric_zh: 来自美国的制裁和来自中国的压力迫使其面对清算

paragraphs.en: IN JANUARY CAMBODIA extradited Chen Zhi, believed to be the boss of one of Asia’s most powerful criminal organisations, to China. Mr Chen, notorious for online fraud, was an adviser to Hun Sen, Cambodia’s longtime strongman. His fate has spooked scam moguls across the country: if the most powerful man in Cambodia could not protect Mr Chen, nobody was safe. Since then, tens of thousands of workers trapped in compounds ringed by barbed wire and armed guards, forced to defraud people around the world, have flooded onto Cambodia’s streets after their bosses fled, leaving the gates open.
paragraphs.zh: 今年1月，柬埔寨将陈志引渡到中国。陈志被认为是亚洲最强大犯罪组织之一的头目。陈志因网络诈骗而臭名昭著，曾是柬埔寨长期强人洪森的顾问。他的命运让柬埔寨全国的诈骗大亨们心惊胆战：如果柬埔寨最有权势的人都保护不了陈志，那就没有人是安全的。自那以后，数以万计被困在铁丝网和武装警卫环绕的园区里、被迫诈骗世界各地民众的工人，在老板逃跑并敞开大门后涌上了柬埔寨街头。

paragraphs.en: The global online fraud industry is estimated to steal more than $500bn a year from victims worldwide, putting it on a par with the illicit drug trade. Nowhere in Asia are politics and cybercrime more deeply enmeshed than in Cambodia, where online scams may generate up to $19bn a year, equivalent to 40% of the country’s formal GDP and more than garment manufacturing, its largest legitimate industry.
paragraphs.zh: 据估计，全球网络诈骗产业每年从世界各地受害者手中窃取超过5,000亿美元，规模可与非法毒品贸易相提并论。在亚洲，没有哪个地方的政治与网络犯罪比柬埔寨纠缠得更深；在那里，网络诈骗每年可能产生高达190亿美元的收入，相当于该国正式GDP的40%，也超过了其最大的合法产业成衣制造业。

paragraphs.en: For years Cambodia was known for staging fake crackdowns, with police raiding empty buildings after the bosses had been tipped off. The past six months have been different, but not as different as the government claims. Cambodia is under greater international pressure than ever before. The crackdown “is not fake; but it is selective and strategic,” says Jacob Sims of Inca Digital, a data-analytics firm. The risks to the elite are multiplying. After America and Britain slapped sanctions on Mr Chen last October, Hong Kong, Singapore, South Korea, Taiwan and Thailand seized assets belonging to him and imposed sanctions of their own. “Our country’s image has been damaged and tarnished by online scam syndicates,” said Mr Hun Sen in May. In April he signed Cambodia’s first anti-fraud statute. Over the past year the government has closed more than 90 casinos, according to Neth Pheaktra, the information minister. Scam operations are often run from inside casinos, which also help launder the proceeds.
paragraphs.zh: 多年来，柬埔寨以假装打击诈骗而闻名：老板们事先得到风声后，警方才去搜查空楼。过去六个月情况有所不同，但并没有政府声称的那么不同。柬埔寨正承受前所未有的国际压力。数据分析公司Inca Digital的雅各布·西姆斯说，这场打击“不是假的；但它是有选择的，也是有策略的”。精英阶层面临的风险正在增加。去年10月美国和英国对陈志实施制裁后，香港、新加坡、韩国、台湾和泰国扣押了他的资产，并实施了各自的制裁。“我国形象已被网络诈骗集团损害和玷污，”洪森5月说。今年4月，他签署了柬埔寨首部反诈骗法规。信息部长内斯·皮克特拉称，过去一年政府已关闭90多家赌场。诈骗活动常常在赌场内部运作，而赌场也帮助洗钱。

paragraphs.en: American pressure continues to mount. Last October the Department of Justice seized around $15bn in cryptocurrency from Mr Chen, its largest-ever seizure of foreign assets anywhere. In April the Treasury sanctioned Kok An, a Cambodian senator who controls scam compounds across the country. In June it imposed sanctions on nine people and 26 entities linked to Mr Chen’s Prince Group, a Cambodian conglomerate spanning property, finance and consumer services. It also blocked subsidiaries of Huione Group, another conglomerate believed to have links to Mr Chen, from using America’s financial system. Huione Group is the firm behind “the largest-ever illicit online marketplace”, according to Elliptic, a blockchain-analytics firm based in London.
paragraphs.zh: 美国的压力还在继续加大。去年10月，美国司法部从陈志手中扣押了约150亿美元的加密货币，这是该部有史以来在任何地方扣押的最大一笔外国资产。今年4月，美国财政部制裁了柬埔寨参议员郭安，他控制着柬埔寨各地的诈骗园区。6月，财政部又对与陈志的太子集团有关的9名个人和26个实体实施制裁；太子集团是一家横跨房地产、金融和消费服务的柬埔寨企业集团。财政部还禁止汇旺集团旗下子公司使用美国金融体系；汇旺集团是另一家被认为与陈志有关联的企业集团。总部位于伦敦的区块链分析公司Elliptic称，汇旺集团是“有史以来最大的非法网络市场”背后的公司。

paragraphs.en: Fighting fraud has become one of America’s foreign-policy priorities in South-East Asia. It fits into an America-first mindset better than many other issues, since it can easily be characterised as dealing with criminals who are making America weaker. Even as America’s presence in the region shrinks, its Scam Centre Strike Force is tackling the biggest form of financial crime ever to hit Americans, who are reckoned by their government to lose at least $10bn a year to scams, and probably considerably more.
paragraphs.zh: 打击诈骗已成为美国在东南亚的外交政策重点之一。与许多其他议题相比，它更符合“美国优先”的思维方式，因为它很容易被描述为是在对付那些削弱美国的犯罪分子。即便美国在该地区的存在感正在下降，其“诈骗中心打击力量”仍在应对有史以来冲击美国人的最大规模金融犯罪。美国政府估计，美国人每年至少因诈骗损失100亿美元，实际金额很可能高得多。

paragraphs.en: China began squeezing the industry years earlier: its citizens are both the scammers’ prime victims and also supply much of the workforce inside the compounds. Liu Zhongyi of China’s Ministry of Public Security visited Cambodia just as the current crackdown began. China, Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand and Vietnam now share intelligence and mount joint operations against the industry. Scams cost Cambodia in other ways, too. Chinese tourists, increasingly fearful of being grabbed and forced to work in the scam farms, numbered just 850,000 in 2024, down from 2.4m in 2019. Cambodia now offers them visa-free entry in the hope of encouraging a revival.
paragraphs.zh: 中国早在数年前就开始挤压这一产业：其公民既是诈骗者的主要受害者，也为园区内部提供了大量劳动力。在当前这轮打击刚开始时，中国公安部的刘忠义访问了柬埔寨。如今，中国、柬埔寨、老挝、缅甸、泰国和越南共享情报，并对这一产业开展联合行动。诈骗也以其他方式让柬埔寨付出代价。中国游客越来越担心被抓走并被迫在诈骗农场工作，2024年赴柬中国游客仅为85万人，低于2019年的240万人。柬埔寨现在向他们提供免签入境，希望推动旅游复苏。

paragraphs.en: Yet the industry is adapting rather than dying. Operations are shifting yet again, this time to Laos and Myanmar, the other main hosts of the world’s scam compounds; Sri Lanka and Indonesia are home to yet others, says John Wojcik of Infoblox, a security firm. Cybercrime will also continue in Cambodia. Selective enforcement has eliminated some scam compounds, but not the system that protects them. No sitting or former ministers have been charged by Cambodia’s officers, despite a number of donations that Prince Group has been documented as having made to the ruling party. ■
paragraphs.zh: 然而，这个产业并不是在消亡，而是在适应。相关活动再次转移，这一次转向老挝和缅甸，它们是全球诈骗园区的另外两个主要容身地；安全公司Infoblox的约翰·沃伊奇克说，斯里兰卡和印度尼西亚也有其他诈骗园区。网络犯罪也将继续存在于柬埔寨。有选择的执法清除了部分诈骗园区，但没有清除保护它们的体系。尽管已有文件显示太子集团曾向执政党捐款多次，柬埔寨执法人员并未起诉任何现任或前任部长。■

paragraphs.en: For exclusive coverage of Asian politics, economics and security, sign up to Asia Bulletin, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取亚洲政治、经济与安全领域的独家报道，请订阅《亚洲简报》，这是我们每周一期、仅面向订阅者的新闻通讯。

articles.title_en: An incomplete list of falling objects in India
articles.title_zh: 印度坠落物不完全清单
articles.rubric_en: Prosperity and infrastructure development have not come with improved safety
articles.rubric_zh: 繁荣和基础设施发展并没有带来更好的安全

paragraphs.en: NO METROPOLIS IN India and few big cities anywhere receive as much rain in as little time as Mumbai. Even by the city’s own standards it has been an exceptionally soggy July, the wettest bit of the monsoon. It rained as much in the first week as it normally does over the whole month. Wind speeds of over 75kph wreaked havoc. By July 7th, 830 trees had fallen (compared with 855 in all of 2025) in addition to 1,238 branches. An 11-year-old was crushed inside his school bus; an 18-year-old on his bike; a 63-year-old outside his shop.
paragraphs.zh: 在印度，没有哪座大都市，也几乎没有世界上哪座大城市，会像孟买这样在如此短的时间里降下如此多的雨。即便按这座城市自己的标准来看，今年7月也异常潮湿，而7月本就是季风季里最湿的一段时间。第一周的降雨量就相当于通常整个月的雨量。超过每小时75公里的风速造成了严重破坏。到7月7日，已有830棵树倒下（相比之下，2025年全年为855棵），另有1,238根树枝折断。一名11岁儿童在校车内被压死；一名18岁青年骑车时遇难；一名63岁男子在自己的店外丧生。

paragraphs.en: Citizens blame the authorities for blocking the roots of trees with concrete. Officials counter that no city in the world could weather this weather. They have a point. Strong winds in Beijing last year knocked down 843 trees and 2,572 branches in a single day. But the problem with that explanation is that the phenomenon of objects falling on and killing people is not restricted to the monsoon, to trees or to Mumbai.
paragraphs.zh: 市民指责当局用混凝土封住了树根。官员则反驳说，世界上没有哪座城市能扛住这样的天气。他们说得也有道理。去年北京的强风曾在一天之内吹倒843棵树和2,572根树枝。但这种解释的问题在于，物体坠落砸死人这一现象并不限于季风季，不限于树木，也不限于孟买。

paragraphs.en: Everything that can crush someone has done so in India—including an actual anvil (in 2024). On just one day this month a jamun tree cracked a man’s skull, a tanker overturned and squashed two people in an autorickshaw and a trash mountain tumbled onto a building, which then collapsed, killing nine. India is the land of death from above.
paragraphs.zh: 在印度，凡是能压死人的东西，几乎都已经压死过人，其中甚至包括一块真正的铁砧（发生在2024年）。就在本月的某一天，一棵蒲桃树砸裂了一名男子的头骨，一辆油罐车侧翻压扁了一辆机动三轮车里的两个人，一座垃圾山倾倒到一栋建筑上，随后建筑坍塌，造成9人死亡。印度是“天降横祸”的国度。

paragraphs.en: The good news is that death by tree should become less common. India is cutting them down to make way for flyovers, metro lines and other infrastructure. The bad news is that this brings its own hazards. Among the things that have fallen on people in recent years are slabs of concrete, iron rods, iron pipes, iron plates, a 40,000kg block of iron, a piling rig, a crane’s trolley and various sections of under-construction bridges. Airport-terminal canopies have collapsed in Delhi, Jabalpur and Rajkot. As one newspaper put it recently, “Yet another unsuspecting citizen walks past an infra-project and nearly pays with his life.” That India is at last creating much-needed infrastructure is laudable. If it could do so without killing passers-by that would be even more so.
paragraphs.zh: 好消息是，被树砸死的情况应该会变少。印度正在砍树，为立交桥、地铁线路和其他基础设施让路。坏消息是，这又带来了新的危险。近些年来，砸到人身上的东西包括混凝土板、铁杆、铁管、铁板、一块重达40,000公斤的铁块、一台打桩机、一台起重机的小车，以及多段在建桥梁构件。德里、贾巴尔普尔和拉杰果德的机场航站楼雨棚都曾坍塌。正如一家报纸最近所说：“又一个毫无防备的市民路过一项基础设施工程，差点付出生命代价。”印度终于在建设亟需的基础设施，这值得称道。若能在建设时不杀死路人，就更值得称道了。

paragraphs.en: When it is not the new stuff that is collapsing, it is the ageing and decrepit. The roof of a school in Rajasthan—which gets almost no rain—crushed seven children last year. Two more school roofs in the same state fell the next day. Another two in the past few weeks. In April a student died when a basketball post toppled over onto him, the third such incident in six months. Nothing is too absurd or tragic. In 2024 an illegal billboard, three times the maximum permitted size, collapsed and killed 17 people at a petrol station. Nine workers were killed at a steel plant last month when a bucket dropped molten metal at 1,600°C on them. The authorities’ response is usually to suspend a few officials, arrest a scapegoat or offer “ex gratia” compensation and move on.
paragraphs.zh: 如果倒塌的不是新建项目，那就是老旧破败的东西。去年，在几乎不下雨的拉贾斯坦邦，一所学校的屋顶压死了7名儿童。第二天，该邦又有两所学校的屋顶坍塌。过去几周又发生了两起。今年4月，一名学生被倒下的篮球架砸死，这是六个月内第三起此类事件。没有什么荒诞或悲惨到不会发生。2024年，一块非法广告牌坍塌，在一座加油站造成17人死亡；这块广告牌的尺寸是最高许可尺寸的三倍。上个月，一家钢厂有9名工人遇难，原因是一个料桶将1,600摄氏度的熔融金属倾倒在他们身上。当局的反应通常是停职几名官员、逮捕一个替罪羊，或提供“恩恤”赔偿，然后继续往前走。

paragraphs.en: For citizens, the answer to this crisis is not to walk around looking up, forever fretting about what might befall them. For there is another hazard: that the ground beneath simply crumbles away. Well before the monsoon started, motorcycles and whole cars were being swallowed up by unmarked open pits in streets across India. In May two dozen candidates outside an exam centre fell into a sewer when the pavement gave way. This month the staircase collapsed in the offices of one northern city’s development authority. Last month a Mumbai municipal official fell into a drain while accompanying the mayor to inspect drainage.
paragraphs.zh: 对市民来说，应对这场危机的办法并不是走路时一直抬头看，永远担心会有什么东西落到自己身上。因为还有另一种危险：脚下的地面可能会直接塌陷。早在季风季开始之前，印度各地街道上没有标识的开放坑洞就已经在吞没摩托车，甚至整辆汽车。5月，一处考试中心外的人行道塌陷，两打考生掉进了下水道。本月，北部某市发展局办公室里的楼梯坍塌。上个月，孟买一名市政官员陪同市长检查排水情况时掉进了排水沟。

paragraphs.en: One response would be for the state to do a better job of enforcing health-and-safety regulations and road-traffic rules designed to prevent exactly this sort of needless loss of life. Another is to marvel at the sheer absurdity of it all, as Ameet Satam, a legislator and the Mumbai president of the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party, was no doubt doing when he was captured on video laughing, “Yesterday it was because of a tree, today it is a manhole.”
paragraphs.zh: 一种应对办法，是让政府更好地执行健康与安全法规以及道路交通规则；这些规则本来就是为了防止这类不必要的生命损失。另一种反应，则是惊叹于这一切荒诞到何种程度。执政党印度人民党议员、孟买市主席阿米特·萨塔姆无疑就是这样想的；一段视频拍到他笑着说：“昨天是因为一棵树，今天是一个窨井。”

paragraphs.en: In 1996 Steve McCurry, a photographer, took a picture that captured the essence of Mumbai at the time: framed against the city’s magnificent Indo-Gothic railway terminus, a taxi driver reclines on the bonnet of his car reading a local tabloid. The front-page headline reads: “BLDG CRASHES IN FORT, 2 DIE”. Much has changed in the decades since, not least India’s income per person, which has quadrupled. But some things remain the same. Earlier this year another newspaper summed up in a front-page headline what many Indians know to be true: “NOWHERE IS SAFE”. ■
paragraphs.zh: 1996年，摄影师史蒂夫·麦凯瑞拍下了一张照片，捕捉到了当时孟买的精髓：在这座城市宏伟的印度哥特式火车总站映衬下，一名出租车司机斜躺在汽车引擎盖上，读着一份当地小报。头版标题写着：“堡垒区建筑倒塌，2人死亡”。此后几十年里，许多事情发生了变化，尤其是印度的人均收入已经增长到原来的四倍。但有些事情依然如故。今年早些时候，另一家报纸用头版标题概括了许多印度人知道的事实：“没有哪里是安全的”。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订阅者可以订阅我们的“观点”新闻通讯，其中汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。

articles.title_en: China is dealing with its own manosphere
articles.title_zh: 中国正在应对自己的“男性圈”
articles.rubric_en: But online misogyny is only one corner of a much bigger problem
articles.rubric_zh: 但网络厌女只是一个更大问题的一角

paragraphs.en: To get a flavour of how some university-educated male influencers in China think, log on to Zhihu, a social-media platform, and find the account of someone who calls himself Peng Huitang. Mr Peng has 50,000 followers and regularly spews vitriol at modern women. “Young women brainwashed by feminist thinking became increasingly man-hating, which then evolved into the idea that men are born owing women something,” he rants.
paragraphs.zh: 要想感受一下中国一些受过大学教育的男性网红是如何思考的，可以登录社交媒体平台知乎，找到一个自称彭惠堂的账号。彭先生有5万名粉丝，经常对现代女性大肆攻击。“被女权思想洗脑的年轻女性变得越来越仇男，进而演变成男人天生欠女人东西的观念，”他愤愤地写道。

paragraphs.en: Another man, who goes by the name Ying Yueyong, in a post viewed 4m times on Bilibili, a video platform, issued “a rallying cry to men to no longer remain silent in the face of years of bullying and to rise up in resistance”. He slips in some of the nationalism popular with such influencers: “Extreme feminism is a common choice favoured by academic cliques and foreign forces.” Welcome to the Chinese manosphere. (Neither Mr Peng nor Mr Ying accepted a request for an interview.)
paragraphs.zh: 另一名网名为应岳勇的男子，在视频平台哔哩哔哩上一条浏览量达400万次的帖子中，发出“号召男性在多年被欺凌面前不再保持沉默、起来反抗的战斗檄文”。他还夹带了一些这类网红中流行的民族主义论调：“极端女权是学阀和境外势力共同青睐的选择。”欢迎来到中国的“男性圈”。（彭先生和应先生均未接受采访请求。）

paragraphs.en: Like most countries, China has lots of online communities of unmarried men. Some are angry nationalists. Some are hardcore misogynists. They post messages claiming that society favours women, who they believe should stay in the kitchen and the bedroom. There is plenty of overlap. Many of them remain anonymous. Tellingly, however, there is no direct translation of the word “manosphere” in Chinese. That is because the online anger of Chinese men is only the most hostile expression of a more widespread frustration felt by tens of millions of men, online and offline. Traditional patriarchal values remain much more deeply rooted than in the West, and feminism is a more recent phenomenon. Involuntarily celibate men are struggling to adapt to the changing dynamics of gender relations.
paragraphs.zh: 和大多数国家一样，中国有许多由未婚男性组成的网络社群。有些人是愤怒的民族主义者。有些人则是铁杆厌女者。他们发帖声称社会偏袒女性，而他们认为女性应该待在厨房和卧室里。这些群体之间有大量重叠。他们中的许多人保持匿名。不过很说明问题的是，中文里并没有“manosphere”这个词的直接译法。这是因为中国男性在网上的愤怒，只是数以千万计男性在线上和线下普遍挫败感中最具敌意的一种表达。传统父权价值观在中国仍比在西方根深蒂固得多，而女权主义则是更晚近的现象。非自愿独身男性正艰难适应性别关系不断变化的格局。

paragraphs.en: In Western democracies, right-wing populists aside, there is plenty of opprobrium towards the manosphere. In China, traditional masculinity and anti-feminist rhetoric have long had substantial support from the Communist Party. The Communist Youth League has said “extreme feminism” (which it does not define, but generally includes anything that encourages activism or conflict and discourages women from getting married and having children) is a “cancer” afflicting the internet. Despite the fact that Mao Zedong famously said that “women hold up half the sky”, the party has regularly cast feminism as a destabilising, insidious foreign force.
paragraphs.zh: 在西方民主国家，撇开右翼民粹主义者不谈，社会上对“男性圈”有大量谴责。在中国，传统男子气概和反女权话语长期以来得到了共产党相当程度的支持。共青团曾称“极端女权”（它并未给出定义，但通常包括任何鼓励行动主义或冲突、并劝阻女性结婚生子的内容）是侵蚀互联网的“毒瘤”。尽管毛泽东有句名言说“妇女能顶半边天”，但党经常把女权主义描绘成一种破坏稳定、暗中渗透的外国势力。

paragraphs.en: China does have unique problems. The most obvious is the effect of its one-child policy, which ran from 1980 to 2015. This led to the abortion of many female fetuses and thus a surplus of adult men today, many of whom struggle to couple up. The number of surplus men, known as guanggun, or bare branches, is huge. By 2027 there will be 22.5m more marriage-age men than women. That translates into 119 men of marriage age for every 100 women of that age; in 2012 the ratio was 105 to 100.
paragraphs.zh: 中国确实有独特的问题。最明显的是独生子女政策的影响，该政策从1980年持续到2015年。这导致许多女性胎儿被堕胎，也因此造成如今成年男性过剩，其中许多人难以找到伴侣。过剩男性被称为“光棍”，也就是“bare branches”，人数十分庞大。到2027年，适婚年龄男性将比女性多2250万。这意味着每100名适婚年龄女性对应119名适婚年龄男性；2012年这一比例为105比100。

paragraphs.en: Young women are also increasingly well educated: they often have more qualifications than men. By 2024 they made up 51% of higher-education students, 14 percentage points more than 20 years earlier. This younger generation of women benefited from the one-child policy, as resources were funnelled towards only daughters. And, while urban Chinese women have been able to shed more of their burdensome traditional roles, men are expected to continue upholding many of their own. To marry, a man must first own a flat; many will be asked to pay a “bride price” to the bride’s family.
paragraphs.zh: 年轻女性受教育程度也越来越高：她们往往比男性拥有更多资历。到2024年，女性占高等教育学生的51%，比20年前高出14个百分点。这一代年轻女性受益于独生子女政策，因为资源被集中投向独生女。而且，尽管中国城市女性已经能够摆脱更多沉重的传统角色束缚，男性却仍被期待继续承担许多属于他们自己的传统责任。要结婚，男人首先必须有一套房；许多人还会被要求向新娘家庭支付“彩礼”。

paragraphs.en: Lan Zhaoxia, a matchmaker in Langfang, just outside Beijing, says the demographic shift has thrown the marriage market off kilter. She builds databases of young men and women that include details such as their parents’ pension status. Now, however, the odds are so stacked against rural male migrants that she does not accept them as clients. “Since all the village girls have gone to school, there are now more ordinary boys in rural areas and more outstanding girls in the city,” she says. Women want to marry up, while men want to marry down or just find an obedient girl. “The gap is huge.”
paragraphs.zh: 北京郊外廊坊的一名婚介兰朝霞说，人口结构变化已经让婚姻市场失衡。她建立年轻男女的数据库，其中包括他们父母养老金状况等细节。然而现在，农村男性流动人口处于如此不利的境地，以至于她不再接受他们作为客户。“因为村里的女孩都去上学了，所以现在农村普通男孩更多，城市里优秀女孩更多，”她说。女性想向上择偶，而男性想向下择偶，或者只是找一个听话的女孩。“差距太大了。”

paragraphs.en: One of those frustrated rural men is Mr Zhao, who is tucking into a lunch of noodles near the car-parts factory in Hebei province where he works. The unmarried 27-year-old often scrolls through social-media posts arguing about the status of men and women. He points to a big debate about a widely publicised ruling on marital rape last year. “When something happens, it’s always the woman who’s portrayed as the wronged party.” Another discussion prompts him to ask why, if the sexes are equal, he should have to give up his bus seat to a woman. He appears resigned to the fact that he may not find a wife.
paragraphs.zh: 赵先生就是这些受挫的农村男性之一，他正在河北省自己工作的汽车零部件工厂附近吃一顿面条午餐。这位27岁的未婚男子经常刷社交媒体上关于男女地位争论的帖子。他提到去年一项受到广泛报道的婚内强奸裁决所引发的大辩论。“一出事，总是女人被塑造成受害方。”另一场讨论让他发问：如果两性平等，为什么他必须在公交车上给女性让座？他似乎已经认命，觉得自己可能找不到妻子。

paragraphs.en: Debates about China’s manosphere focus a lot on economic issues. Poorer men like Mr Zhao face immense economic pressure. Online, they use the term baojinbi, or “exploding gold coins”, to describe women wringing them dry by demanding expensive dates, gifts and bride prices. Research by Minhee Chae of Nankai University and Zhang Dandan of Peking University found that around a third of young male migrants who intend to marry think their chances of doing so by the age of 30 are only 50% at best. They estimate that a young migrant worker would have to save every penny for six years to afford the average bride price of 127,300 yuan ($18,780).
paragraphs.zh: 关于中国“男性圈”的讨论很大程度上聚焦于经济问题。像赵先生这样较贫穷的男性面临巨大经济压力。在网上，他们用“爆金币”一词来形容女性通过要求昂贵约会、礼物和彩礼把他们榨干。南开大学的蔡敏熙和北京大学的张丹丹的研究发现，打算结婚的年轻男性流动人口中，约三分之一认为自己到30岁结婚的机会最多只有50%。她们估算，一名年轻农民工必须把每一分钱都存下来六年，才能负担平均12.73万元人民币（18,780美元）的彩礼。

paragraphs.en: The backlash is not just about women, says Sara Liao of Pennsylvania State University. Men want “to vent their anger, frustrations, precarities regarding their life status, regarding the general economic and cultural environment”. The safest outlet is attacking the opposite sex; doing so aligns with what the government itself is saying.
paragraphs.zh: 宾夕法尼亚州立大学的廖莎拉说，这种反弹并不只是针对女性。男性想要“发泄他们对自身生活状态、对总体经济和文化环境的愤怒、挫败和不安全感”。最安全的出口就是攻击异性；这样做与政府本身的说法相一致。

paragraphs.en: Under President Xi Jinping, “sissy men” have been banned from appearing on television, while traditional marriage and child-rearing have been promoted. Belatedly, however, leaders are also becoming aware that intensifying xingbie duili, or “gender antagonism”, may be setting the scene for broader social instability. They are trying to tackle some of the root causes of male anger, for instance by intensifying campaigns against the practice of bride prices. Most of all they worry about plunging marriage and birth rates. In the first quarter of this year China registered just 1.7m marriages, half as many as in the same period in 2017.
paragraphs.zh: 在习近平主席治下，“娘炮”男性被禁止出现在电视上，同时传统婚姻和育儿受到提倡。不过，领导层也终于开始意识到，不断加剧的“性别对立”可能正在为更广泛的社会不稳定铺路。他们正试图处理男性愤怒的一些根源，例如加大力度开展反对彩礼习俗的运动。最令他们担忧的是婚姻率和出生率急剧下降。今年第一季度，中国仅登记了170万对婚姻，只有2017年同期的一半。

paragraphs.en: And it is not just the government that blames feminism and gender antagonism on foreign forces. Griffin Liu, a sports teacher, who is 35, is still looking for a wife. “Chinese people receive Chinese education and ideas,” he says. But now the internet is bringing new Western thinking. “The two are in conflict.”
paragraphs.zh: 而且，把女权主义和性别对立归咎于境外势力的并不只是政府。35岁的体育教师刘格里芬仍在寻找妻子。“中国人接受的是中国教育和中国思想，”他说。但现在互联网带来了新的西方思想。“两者发生了冲突。”

paragraphs.en: Gender antagonism is frequently in the news. Last year, a legal ruling in favour of a male Wuhan University student accused by a female student of masturbating in front of her became a lightning rod for men’s concerns about feminist overreach and women’s concerns about a lack of clarity in defining sexual harassment. In 2024, after a gaming influencer nicknamed “fat cat” committed suicide, netizens “doxxed” his former girlfriend and cast her as a gold-digger who sent him to his grave.
paragraphs.zh: 性别对立频频成为新闻。去年，一项有利于武汉大学一名男学生的法律裁决成为男性担忧女权越界、女性担忧性骚扰定义不清的导火索；此前，一名女学生指控该男学生在她面前自慰。2024年，一名昵称“胖猫”的游戏博主自杀后，网民“人肉”了他的前女友，并把她描绘成把他逼上绝路的拜金女。

paragraphs.en: In a country where much is censored online, misogyny is less likely to be silenced. Instead online censors are more likely to target feminist posts that are judged to be advocating buhun buyu (unmarried and childless) lifestyles. Last year the cyberspace regulator’s campaign to crack down on harmful content listed “extreme feminism”, including posts that promoted singledom or gender antagonism. Weibo, China’s answer to X, last year added “inciting gender antagonism” (usually blamed on feminists) as grounds for reporting content; the company said it punished 15,000 accounts for this offence and deleted 640,000 relevant posts.
paragraphs.zh: 在一个网络上许多内容都会受到审查的国家，厌女言论反而不太可能被噤声。相反，网络审查者更可能针对那些被认定为宣扬“不婚不育”生活方式的女权帖子。去年，网信监管机构打击有害内容的专项行动列出了“极端女权”，其中包括宣扬单身或性别对立的帖子。中国版X微博去年新增“煽动性别对立”（通常被归咎于女权主义者）作为举报内容的理由；该公司表示，已因这一违规行为处罚1.5万个账号，并删除64万条相关帖子。

paragraphs.en: Xiong Xiaoneng, a 40-year-old consultant in Beijing, is one more man yet to find a wife. He partly blames the rise of microdramas, which he says create unrealistic expectations. He thinks women want to be like Wu Zetian, a seventh-century empress portrayed as the archetypal girl boss on screen. His hometown of Guilin has seen a boom in businesses catering to single women, such as shows with shirtless male performers. Men like him are trapped unhappily in their singlehood, he says, while women seem to be enjoying theirs. ■
paragraphs.zh: 北京40岁的咨询顾问熊小能也是又一名尚未找到妻子的男性。他将此部分归咎于微短剧的兴起，称这些剧制造了不切实际的期待。他认为女性都想成为武则天，这位七世纪女皇在荧幕上被塑造成典型的“大女主”。他的家乡桂林出现了一波迎合单身女性的生意热潮，例如有赤膊男性表演者的演出。他说，像他这样的男性痛苦地困在单身状态中，而女性似乎正在享受她们的单身生活。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers can sign up to Drum Tower, our new weekly newsletter, to understand what the world makes of China—and what China makes of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅用户可以注册订阅《鼓楼》，这是我们的全新每周通讯，帮助读者理解世界如何看待中国，以及中国如何看待世界。

articles.title_en: Xi Jinping expels another leader from his top team
articles.title_zh: 习近平将又一名高层团队成员逐出局
articles.rubric_en: He has set a record for booting out members of the Politburo
articles.rubric_zh: 他创下了将政治局成员清除出局的纪录

paragraphs.en: CHINA’S POLITICAL system prides itself on stability. Reshuffles of core leaders normally happen only every five years. But Xi Jinping has been taking the axe to members of his team at a rate not seen since the days of Mao Zedong. The latest to fall is Ma Xingrui, who until last year was the Communist Party boss of the far-western region of Xinjiang. State media said he had been stripped of his party membership for “extremely serious” corruption and his case handed to prosecutors. Another seat on the ruling Politburo—now down to 21 active members from its initial 24—has thus fallen vacant.
paragraphs.zh: 中国的政治体制以稳定自许。核心领导层的改组通常每五年才发生一次。但习近平一直在以毛泽东时代以来未见的速度，对自己团队成员“大动干戈”。最新倒下的是马兴瑞，他直到去年还是中国最西部地区新疆的中共党委书记。官方媒体称，他因“极其严重”的腐败问题被开除党籍，其案件已移送检察机关。执政的政治局因此又空出一个席位；政治局最初有24名成员，如今在任成员已降至21人。

paragraphs.en: Mr Ma’s downfall is no surprise. Once a celebrated aerospace engineer who helped oversee lunar-exploration projects, he has long been absent from major events. In April the party said he was being investigated for “serious violations” of its rules and the law. Now it has publicly accused him of accepting “huge amounts” in bribes, engaging in “large-scale family corruption” and using his power and money to gain sexual favours.
paragraphs.zh: 马先生的落马并不令人意外。他曾是一位备受赞誉的航天工程师，参与负责过探月工程项目，但长期缺席重大活动。今年4月，中共表示他涉嫌“严重违纪违法”，正接受调查。如今，官方公开指控他收受“巨额”贿赂，搞“大规模家族式腐败”，并利用权力和金钱谋取性服务。

paragraphs.en: Since the Mao era, charges of graft have almost always been laid against those expelled from the Politburo. Such wrongdoing is easier to explain to the public than revealing what is often suspected to be the main reason: political tension with the party chief. In this case, however, there has been little official hint or plausible rumour of serious political friction between Mr Ma and Mr Xi. Egregious corruption may be enough to explain his ousting—not that Mr Ma will be able to comment publicly or deny the charges against him.
paragraphs.zh: 自毛泽东时代以来，被逐出政治局的人几乎总会被扣上贪腐指控。与其揭示人们常常怀疑的主因，即与党首之间的政治紧张，贪腐问题更容易向公众解释。不过在这个案例中，官方几乎没有暗示，外界也没有可信传闻显示马先生与习近平先生之间存在严重政治摩擦。严重腐败或许已足以解释他被罢黜的原因，尽管马先生将无法公开置评，也无法否认对他的指控。

paragraphs.en: His removal from the Politburo is a reminder of Mr Xi’s willingness to lash out. Since the most recent five-yearly party congress in 2022, two defence ministers, a foreign minister and dozens of high-ranking military officers have been purged. The armed forces’ two most senior generals have fallen: He Weidong was expelled from the Politburo and Zhang Youxia is under investigation and all but certain to lose his Politburo seat, too. Since Mr Xi took power in 2012, three Politburo members have been dismissed—the most under any leader post-Mao. (The other was Sun Zhengcai in 2017, once considered to be a potential successor to Mr Xi.)
paragraphs.zh: 他被移出政治局，再次提醒人们习近平先生愿意出重手。自2022年最近一次五年一度的党代会以来，两名国防部长、一名外交部长以及数十名高级军官已被清洗。军队两名级别最高的将领也已倒下：何卫东被逐出政治局，张又侠正在接受调查，几乎肯定也将失去政治局席位。自习近平先生2012年掌权以来，已有三名政治局成员被撤职，这是毛泽东之后任何领导人任内最多的一次。（另一人是2017年的孙政才，他曾被认为可能接替习近平先生。）

paragraphs.en: Mr Ma can expect, at a minimum, to spend a very long time behind bars. No former Politburo member has been executed, but that is an unreliable guide to his likely fate. Mr Xi is a norm-breaker. ■
paragraphs.zh: 马先生至少可以预期，自己将在狱中度过很长一段时间。尚无前政治局成员被处决，但这并不能可靠地预示他可能的命运。习近平先生是一个打破惯例的人。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers can sign up to Drum Tower, our new weekly newsletter, to understand what the world makes of China—and what China makes of the world.
