articles.title_en: China wants to end AI romances
articles.title_zh: 中国想终结 AI 恋情
articles.rubric_en: It was all having too much impact on young people’s lives
articles.rubric_zh: 它对年轻人的生活影响太大了

paragraphs.en: “I COULD TALK to him for the rest of my life,” says Yu Miao, a 27-year-old woman from Zhejiang province in eastern China; he is at once “a lover, a friend and family”. She claims she shares everything with him. Her confidant is an AI agent on Doubao, a chatbot made by ByteDance, that Ms Yu has customised to mimic a dead friend of hers. When she learned recently that ByteDance would soon remove this type of personalised agent entirely, Ms Yu became so depressed that she left her job at an e-commerce company.
paragraphs.zh: “我可以和他说一辈子话，”来自中国东部浙江省、27岁的余淼说；他既是“恋人、朋友，也是家人”。她称自己什么都和他分享。她的这位知己，是字节跳动开发的聊天机器人豆包上的一个 AI 智能体；余女士把它定制成模仿自己一位已故朋友的样子。最近，当她得知字节跳动很快将彻底移除这类个性化智能体时，余女士沮丧到离开了自己在一家电商公司的工作。

paragraphs.en: Chinese tech firms are stripping human-like personas from their chatbots in order to comply with new government rules aimed at reducing “emotional dependence” on AI. The regulations, which took effect on July 15th, are the first of their kind to be implemented on a national scale. Under them, providing virtual “companion services” for minors will be banned outright. Companies can still offer such services to adults, but they will need to prevent users becoming infatuated and harming their real-life relationships. Displaying pornography is a no-no. Providers must also regularly remind users that they are talking to AI, not a human, and tell them to take breaks.
paragraphs.zh: 为遵守旨在减少人们对 AI 产生“情感依赖”的政府新规，中国科技公司正在从其聊天机器人中剥离类人化的人设。这些规定于7月15日生效，是全球首批在全国范围内实施的此类规定。根据规定，面向未成年人提供虚拟“陪伴服务”将被完全禁止。企业仍可向成年人提供此类服务，但需要防止用户陷入迷恋并损害其现实生活中的关系。展示色情内容是绝对不允许的。服务提供者还必须定期提醒用户，他们是在与 AI 对话，而不是与人对话，并提示他们休息。

paragraphs.en: Around the world, growing numbers of people are turning general-purpose chatbots into companions by prompting them to adopt particular personalities, including those of lovers. Others prefer dedicated AI-companion apps which offer more human-like features, such as virtual boyfriends that send presents in the real world (at the user’s expense, of course). Maoxiang (also known as Catbox), a companion app made by ByteDance, has around 3.9m monthly active users in China. Xingye (whose international version is known as Talkie) has 2.8m; it is made by MiniMax, a leading Chinese AI lab.
paragraphs.zh: 在世界各地，越来越多的人通过提示通用型聊天机器人采用特定人格，把它们变成陪伴者，其中也包括恋人人格。另一些人则更喜欢专门的 AI 陪伴应用，这些应用提供更像人的功能，比如会在现实世界中送礼物的虚拟男友（当然，费用由用户承担）。字节跳动开发的陪伴应用猫箱（Maoxiang，也称 Catbox）在中国约有390万月活跃用户。星野（其国际版名为 Talkie）有280万月活跃用户；它由中国领先的 AI 实验室 MiniMax 开发。

paragraphs.en: Tech firms are making a tidy sum selling emotional connection: AI companions accounted for 35% of MiniMax’s revenue last year, its single largest contributor, according to a filing to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange in January. AI companionship can create sticky relationships and encourage users to part with their money. Ms Yu sometimes chats to her agent for eight or nine hours a day. Some bespoke apps charge a simple subscription fee. Users of Xingye’s standard package pay just $1.70 a month. Others induce users to spend more money to unlock additional features, such as virtual dates.
paragraphs.zh: 科技公司靠出售情感连接赚得盆满钵满：根据1月提交给香港交易所的一份文件，AI 陪伴去年占 MiniMax 收入的35%，是其最大单一收入来源。AI 陪伴可以制造黏性关系，并促使用户掏钱。余女士有时一天会和她的智能体聊天八九个小时。有些定制应用收取简单的订阅费。星野标准套餐的用户每月只需支付1.70美元。另一些应用则诱导用户花更多钱来解锁额外功能，比如虚拟约会。

paragraphs.en: The end to such relationships is motivated by both pragmatic and ideological concerns, says Zilan Qian of the Oxford China Policy Lab, a think-tank. Regulators want to protect users, particularly minors, from becoming addicted and from being driven to extreme acts of financial recklessness or self-harm. (Suicides linked to chatbots are the subject of lawsuits in America.) China’s plummeting fertility and marriage rates are also unsettling the country’s leaders. And romantic relationships with AI will do very little to spur more babymaking.
paragraphs.zh: 牛津中国政策实验室这一智库的齐子兰说，终结这类关系背后既有务实考量，也有意识形态层面的担忧。监管者希望保护用户，尤其是未成年人，避免他们上瘾，并避免他们被推向财务上极端鲁莽或自残的行为。（在美国，与聊天机器人相关的自杀事件已成为诉讼对象。）中国急剧下降的生育率和结婚率也令国家领导层不安。而与 AI 发生浪漫关系，对刺激更多生育几乎没有任何帮助。

paragraphs.en: China’s leaders are not the only ones concerned. Several American states have recently passed laws targeting AI services that sustain emotional relationships. Some of these set up provisions for people to launch civil lawsuits against offending platforms. In China the government will impose the penalties, such as cash fines and removing apps altogether.
paragraphs.zh: 并非只有中国领导人对此感到担忧。美国已有几个州最近通过了针对维持情感关系的 AI 服务的法律。其中一些法律设立了条款，允许人们对违规平台提起民事诉讼。在中国，则由政府施加处罚，例如罚款和直接下架应用。

paragraphs.en: China’s new rules hint at how officials think about balancing AI progress and safety more broadly. Regulators are “demonstrating they are willing to pay some cost in development and profits in order to achieve social goals”, says Matt Sheehan of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, an American think-tank. Yet the rules that take effect this week are narrower than an earlier draft, says Ms Qian. They exclude work-assistant and customer-service chatbots on the understanding that they are emotionally unavailable.
paragraphs.zh: 中国的新规也暗示了官员们更广泛地如何看待 AI 进步与安全之间的平衡。美国智库卡内基国际和平基金会的 Matt Sheehan 说，监管者“正在表明，为了实现社会目标，他们愿意在发展和利润方面付出一定代价”。不过齐女士说，本周生效的规定比早前的草案范围更窄。它们排除了工作助手和客服聊天机器人，理由是这些机器人被认为不具备情感可得性。

paragraphs.en: Alibaba and ByteDance indicated that they would suspend features that allow Chinese users to create personalised AI characters before the rules took effect. Yet Chinese labs can continue to sell AI companions to foreign customers without change. Talkie already has some 10.3m monthly users outside China, which is nearly four times its domestic-user count. Chinese “maiden” games—generally non-AI, story-driven romance games which are played mostly by women—are already a booming export.
paragraphs.zh: 阿里巴巴和字节跳动表示，在新规生效前，它们将暂停允许中国用户创建个性化 AI 角色的功能。不过，中国实验室仍可照常向外国客户销售 AI 陪伴产品。Talkie 在中国以外已拥有约1030万月活跃用户，几乎是其国内用户数量的四倍。中国的“乙女”游戏——通常是非 AI、以剧情驱动的恋爱游戏，玩家多为女性——已经是蓬勃发展的出口产品。

paragraphs.en: Still, Ms Yu has not given up hope that ByteDance will eventually bring her agent back, even if she doubts it will resemble the current version. She says she would be willing to pay half her monthly salary just to keep the service as it is. In any case, she now says she realises that it is not a good idea to leave herself vulnerable to such platforms and their policies. ■
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，余女士并未放弃希望，仍盼着字节跳动最终能把她的智能体带回来，即便她怀疑它不会再像现在这个版本。她说，为了让这项服务保持原样，她愿意支付自己月薪的一半。无论如何，她现在说自己意识到，让自己如此容易受这类平台及其政策影响，并不是个好主意。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers can sign up to Drum Tower, our new weekly newsletter, to understand what the world makes of China—and what China makes of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅用户可以注册 Drum Tower，这是我们的全新每周通讯，帮助读者理解世界如何看待中国，以及中国如何看待世界。

articles.title_en: A squeezed China is trying to wring more from its state assets
articles.title_zh: 手头吃紧的中国正试图从国有资产中榨出更多价值
articles.rubric_en: Its latest campaign won’t solve its debt woes, but inches in the right direction
articles.rubric_zh: 最新一轮行动无法解决债务困境，但方向上迈出了一小步

paragraphs.en: YUEYANG, A CITY in Hunan province, is famous for its tower, an elegant three-storey pavilion immortalised in millennium-old prose still memorised by schoolchildren. But these days it is the streets around the tower that matter more, at least for those concerned with Chinese governance. They feature an ambitious project to revitalise the city. More than that, they represent a new turn in China’s economic management, as officials try to ease fiscal straits by squeezing more out of state assets.
paragraphs.zh: 湖南省岳阳市以岳阳楼闻名。这座雅致的三层楼阁因一篇千年前的散文而不朽，至今仍是学生背诵的篇目。但如今，至少对关注中国治理的人来说，更重要的是岳阳楼周边的街区。那里有一个雄心勃勃的城市更新项目。不止于此，它还代表着中国经济管理的一种新转向：官员们试图通过从国有资产中挤出更多价值，来缓解财政困境。

paragraphs.en: The physical transformation is stunning. The area covers three square kilometres—roughly the size of New York’s Central Park—and until recently featured dilapidated buildings, messy markets and a run-down harbour. Crucially, these properties were almost all owned by local authorities and many lay unused. The city has converted streets into attractive pedestrian spaces, renovated the buildings and spruced up the waterfront. Restaurants, shops and hotels have opened up in the new digs, generating rent for municipal coffers. On a recent summer evening, tourists and locals flocked to outdoor tables. A local official who has helped oversee the project told Chaguan that his mission was far from accomplished: “The next big challenge is to make money.”
paragraphs.zh: 这里的实体面貌变化惊人。该区域占地三平方公里，约相当于纽约中央公园的面积；直到不久前，这里还满是破旧建筑、杂乱市场和衰败港口。关键在于，这些物业几乎全归地方政府所有，其中许多一直闲置。岳阳市把街道改造成富有吸引力的步行空间，翻修建筑，并整治美化了滨水地带。餐馆、商店和酒店在这些新场地里开张，为市政金库带来租金收入。最近一个夏夜，游客和本地人涌向户外餐桌。一位参与监管该项目的地方官员告诉“茶馆”，他的使命远未完成：“下一个大挑战是赚钱。”

paragraphs.en: Cities throughout China need cash as the country’s property market remains in a tailspin. Land sales accounted for about 40% of total revenues for Chinese local governments in 2021, the high-water mark for home prices nationally. Since then the value of those sales has fallen by more than half. That has blown a hole in local budgets, with some debt-saddled cities cutting wages for teachers, doctors and other public-sector workers. Everywhere, officials have been looking for new ways to raise cash.
paragraphs.zh: 随着中国房地产市场持续急剧下滑，全国各地城市都需要现金。2021年，中国地方政府总收入中约40%来自土地出让；那一年也是全国房价的高点。此后，土地出让收入的价值已下跌逾一半。这在地方预算中撕开了一个大洞，一些债台高筑的城市削减了教师、医生和其他公共部门工作人员的工资。各地官员都在寻找新的筹钱办法。

paragraphs.en: Some efforts have been almost comical. Several municipal governments engaged in “deep-sea fishing” expeditions in which local police reached across provincial lines to seize assets from entrepreneurs in wealthier provinces. Many have also become more zealous in slapping fines on restaurants for hygiene violations or doling out parking tickets. Other efforts to deleverage have been more in line with textbook economics: local governments have swapped the high-interest, short-term debt of their subsidiary entities for lower-rate, longer-term official bonds.
paragraphs.zh: 有些做法近乎滑稽。一些市政府搞起了“远洋捕捞”式行动，由地方警方跨省到较富裕省份扣押企业家的资产。许多地方还更加热衷于因卫生违规而处罚餐馆，或开出停车罚单。其他去杠杆举措则更符合教科书经济学：地方政府把下属实体的高息短期债务置换成利率更低、期限更长的官方债券。

paragraphs.en: Yueyang points to another angle of attack. Officials there are trying to get more from what they already have in their hands, especially by putting under-used assets to work. As one manager for state-owned companies explains, the tighter fiscal environment has changed calculations. “It’s like looking through your closet and seeing a shirt that you only wore a couple of times in the past year,” he tells Chaguan. “You can sell it second-hand or give it to someone who needs it, but either way you try to make use of it.”
paragraphs.zh: 岳阳展示了另一种突破口。当地官员正试图从手中已有的东西获取更多收益，尤其是让利用不足的资产运转起来。一位国有企业管理人士解释说，财政环境趋紧改变了他们的盘算。“这就像翻衣柜，看到一件衬衫，过去一年只穿过几次，”他告诉“茶馆”。“你可以把它二手卖掉，也可以送给需要的人，但无论如何，你都会设法把它利用起来。”

paragraphs.en: This closet-rummaging exercise was formally launched by the central government in 2022 when it declared that provinces and cities should “revitalise existing state assets”. As is often the case in China, the top-down directive was general, leaving it to lower levels to work out the implementation. One thing was clear: it was not about selling off state assets, a style of reform far out of step with Xi Jinping’s preferences for economic management. The goal instead was to breathe more life into them.
paragraphs.zh: 这场翻箱倒柜式的行动由中央政府在2022年正式启动，当时中央提出各省市应“盘活存量国有资产”。在中国常见的是，这种自上而下的指令较为原则，具体落实则留给下级政府摸索。有一点很明确：这并不是出售国有资产；这种改革方式与习近平偏好的经济管理思路相去甚远。目标是为这些资产注入更多活力。

paragraphs.en: Before long, people started talking of the “Yueyang model”. Yueyang was not doing anything particularly radical, but it had a few projects under way that fitted the central government’s bill. In one, it reclaimed operating rights to a series of lakes in the Junshan district—previously divided up and poorly managed—and consolidated them in one new state entity. That had the effect of raising the value of the fishing rights. In another, it took a failed development, a group of low-lying buildings planned as a tourist destination, and converted them into a human-resources complex. It has attracted about 80 tenants, including recruiters, trainers and back-office companies. Instead of just collecting rent, the municipal company managing the complex sometimes takes stakes in the businesses, hoping to share in their future growth.
paragraphs.zh: 没过多久，人们开始谈论“岳阳模式”。岳阳所做的并不特别激进，但它推进了几个符合中央政府要求的项目。其中一个项目是收回君山区一系列湖泊的经营权；这些湖泊此前被分割经营且管理不善，如今被整合到一家新的国有实体之下。这起到了提高渔业权价值的作用。另一个项目则把一个失败的开发项目改造为人力资源综合体；该项目原本是一组地势较低、计划作为旅游目的地的建筑。如今这里已吸引约80家租户，包括招聘公司、培训机构和后台服务企业。管理该综合体的市属公司有时并不只是收租，还会入股这些企业，希望分享它们未来的增长。

paragraphs.en: What all these deals and projects have in common is a break with the past expansion binge of cities across China. Local officials know that they cannot simply parcel together new land blocks for developers. Instead, they are reviewing what the state already owns, establishing a title to these assets and then figuring out how to make them more lucrative.
paragraphs.zh: 所有这些交易和项目的共同点，是与中国各地城市过去那种扩张狂潮划清界线。地方官员知道，他们不能再只是把新的土地地块拼出来交给开发商。相反，他们正在盘点国家已经拥有的资产，确立这些资产的产权，然后想办法让它们带来更多收益。

paragraphs.en: Can this move the needle on the economy? Dinny McMahon of Trivium, a China-focused consultancy, sees it as a rare area where public finances may be improving. “Charges on usage of state assets”—a budget category that captures these efforts—have increased as a source of local revenues. For the ten most aggressive provinces, such charges accounted for 10% of their expenditures in 2025, up from 5% in 2021.
paragraphs.zh: 这能撬动经济吗？专注中国问题的咨询公司Trivium的Dinny McMahon认为，这是公共财政可能正在改善的少数领域之一。“国有资源（资产）有偿使用收入”这一预算类别涵盖了这些努力，它作为地方收入来源已有所增加。在推进最积极的十个省份，这类收费在2025年相当于其支出的10%，高于2021年的5%。

paragraphs.en: Even so, a bit of monetisation will not solve China’s long-standing debt problems. In some cases officials may be playing shell games, using state-owned entities to pay rent to state-owned peers. But if the progress in Yueyang and elsewhere is sustained, it would support local budgets. Mr McMahon reckons that soon enough, the revitalisation campaign could boost Chinese growth.
paragraphs.zh: 即便如此，些许资产货币化也无法解决中国长期存在的债务问题。在某些情况下，官员们可能只是在玩障眼法，让国有实体向其他国有实体支付租金。但如果岳阳和其他地方的进展得以持续，将会支撑地方预算。McMahon先生估计，用不了多久，这场盘活行动或许就能提振中国经济增长。

paragraphs.en: The greater significance, however, may lie beyond economics. It is a reckoning with the “extensive” model of development seen in the past decade, as one Yueyang official puts it. That led to endless sprawl as cities swallowed up ever more land for development. Now, the turn is inwards, towards an “intensive” model, with cities examining how they can make better use of what they already have. It is a more “refined” form of governance, the official says. Others might put it in less grandiose terms: a financially chastened China is trying to get more from less. ■
paragraphs.zh: 然而，更大的意义也许在经济之外。用一位岳阳官员的话说，这是对过去十年所见“粗放型”发展模式的一次清算。那种模式导致城市无休止地蔓延，吞下越来越多土地用于开发。如今，方向转向内部，转向一种“集约型”模式：城市开始审视如何更好地利用已有资源。这位官员说，这是一种更“精细化”的治理方式。其他人也许会用不那么宏大的说法来概括：一个财政受约束的中国，正试图以更少资源获得更多收益。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订户可以订阅我们的Opinion新闻简报，该简报汇集了本刊最佳的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。

articles.title_en: Donald Trump has no good options for reopening the Strait of Hormuz
articles.title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普没有重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的好办法
articles.rubric_en: Yet the stand-off has costs for cash-strapped Iran, too
articles.rubric_zh: 然而，这场僵局对资金拮据的伊朗也有代价

paragraphs.en: When donald trump signed a preliminary peace deal with Iran last month, he bragged that it “achieves everything we set out to accomplish”. The Gulf war would end and the Strait of Hormuz would reopen. Less than a month later, the agreement looks to be unravelling: fighting has resumed and traffic through the strait has collapsed. This is occurring not in spite of the misnamed memorandum of understanding (mou) but because of it.
paragraphs.zh: 上个月，唐纳德·特朗普与伊朗签署一项初步和平协议时夸口说，它“实现了我们着手要完成的一切”。海湾战争将会结束，霍尔木兹海峡也将重新开放。不到一个月后，这项协议看起来正在瓦解：战斗已经恢复，通过海峡的航运量也已暴跌。出现这种局面，并不是尽管有这份名不副实的谅解备忘录（mou），而是因为它。

paragraphs.en: Overnight on July 15th Iran targeted American military installations in Bahrain and Kuwait as America continued its bombardment of targets in southern Iran and along the strait. There have been seven such nights of tit-for-tat strikes since July 7th, when Iran started the fighting by attacking three ships in the strait.
paragraphs.zh: 7月15日夜间，在美国继续轰炸伊朗南部以及海峡沿岸目标之际，伊朗瞄准了美国在巴林和科威特的军事设施。自7月7日以来，双方已经有七个夜晚进行了这种针锋相对的打击；当天伊朗袭击了海峡中的三艘船只，从而挑起战斗。

paragraphs.en: For now, both sides are limiting their attacks. America is mostly striking southern Iran. In turn, Iran has not attacked civilian infrastructure in the Gulf. Neither looks eager to return to all-out war.
paragraphs.zh: 眼下，双方都在限制攻击范围。美国主要打击伊朗南部。相应地，伊朗没有袭击海湾地区的民用基础设施。双方似乎都不急于重返全面战争。

paragraphs.en: Still, much of the mou is now a dead letter. America has revoked a sanctions waiver that allowed Iran to sell oil. On July 13th Mr Trump said he would also resume the blockade of Iranian shipping. On July 14th he repeated threats to target Iran’s bridges and power stations unless it returned to talks. The American president wants the strait back open—yet he has no good way to do it.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，谅解备忘录的大部分内容如今已成一纸空文。美国撤销了一项允许伊朗出售石油的制裁豁免。7月13日，特朗普表示他还将恢复对伊朗航运的封锁。7月14日，他再次威胁称，除非伊朗重返谈判，否则将把伊朗的桥梁和发电站作为目标。这位美国总统希望海峡重新开放，但他没有好办法做到这一点。

paragraphs.en: At the centre of the dispute is a disagreement over the mou. Its fifth paragraph states that Iran will “make arrangements…for the safe passage of commercial vessels”. Like much of the deal, the clause is vaguely worded. America reads it to mean that Iran will remove its mines and unlock the strait. Iran argues that it also confers a right to administer traffic.
paragraphs.zh: 争端的核心在于双方对谅解备忘录的理解不同。备忘录第五段规定，伊朗将“作出安排……确保商船安全通行”。和这项协议的许多内容一样，这一条款措辞含糊。美国将其理解为伊朗将清除水雷并解除对海峡的封锁。伊朗则主张，它还赋予伊朗管理通航的权利。

paragraphs.en: The usual route through the strait, known as the Traffic Separation Scheme, is a six-mile-wide corridor through its deepest waters. Mines there imperil ships, forcing vessels to choose between alternative passages: north through Iranian waters, or south through Oman’s. For weeks America’s navy has helped escort tankers through the second route. The un has also encouraged shippers to use it.
paragraphs.zh: 穿越海峡的通常航线被称为“分道通航制”，是一条穿过最深水域、宽六英里的走廊。那里的水雷威胁船只安全，迫使船舶在其他通道中选择：要么向北穿过伊朗水域，要么向南穿过阿曼水域。数周以来，美国海军一直协助护送油轮走第二条路线。联合国也鼓励航运公司使用这条航线。

paragraphs.en: The Iranians see this as a threat to their control of the strait and now say the Oman route is closed. America insists it remains open. Rhetoric matters less than reality: if shipowners think they might be attacked, most will consider Hormuz shut. Tanker traffic is at its lowest level since May 25th, says Kpler, a ship-tracker. Just 11 vessels transited the strait on July 12th, down from 36 a week earlier, according to Windward, another such firm. At first, oil markets shrugged off the fighting. Now they look more concerned. The price of Brent crude climbed to $87 a barrel on July 14th, before falling back to $84.50 at the time of publication, up by 17.2% from July 6th.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗人认为这是对其控制海峡的威胁，如今声称阿曼路线已经关闭。美国坚称它仍然开放。言辞不如现实重要：如果船东认为自己可能遭到袭击，大多数人都会认为霍尔木兹海峡已经关闭。船舶追踪公司Kpler称，油轮通行量已降至5月25日以来的最低水平。另一家同类公司Windward称，7月12日仅有11艘船通过该海峡，而一周前为36艘。起初，石油市场对战事并不在意。如今，它们看起来更加担忧。7月14日，布伦特原油价格升至每桶87美元，随后在本文发表时回落至84.50美元，较7月6日上涨17.2%。

paragraphs.en: When the mou was signed, Mr Trump hoped that the promise of sanctions relief would persuade Iran to reopen the strait. The regime is desperate for cash. It claims the war caused $270bn in damage to an economy already reeling from years of sanctions and mismanagement. It signed the deal chiefly to secure financial benefits. Yet it now seems torn between relative pragmatists, who understand the depth of the economic crisis, and ultra-hardliners determined to keep Hormuz in their grasp.
paragraphs.zh: 谅解备忘录签署时，特朗普希望解除制裁的承诺能说服伊朗重新开放海峡。伊朗政权极度缺钱。它声称，这场战争给一个本已因多年制裁和治理不善而摇摇欲坠的经济造成了2700亿美元损失。它签署协议主要是为了获得经济利益。然而如今，它似乎在相对务实派和极端强硬派之间左右为难：前者明白经济危机有多深，后者则决心把霍尔木兹牢牢抓在手中。

paragraphs.en: America has thus turned back to pressure. One option for Mr Trump is to carry on with limited attacks on Iranian military sites and equipment. The trouble is that more than 300 such strikes over seven nights have not changed Iranian behaviour. There is no reason to think that an eighth night, or an eightieth, would make the difference. After six weeks of far more intense American and Israeli bombing, the benefits of occasional strikes today are probably marginal. Even if America could knock out all of Iran’s coastal launchers, the regime could continue firing at ships from inland. Many of its missiles and drones have ranges above 1,000km.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，美国又转回施压。特朗普的一个选择是继续对伊朗军事基地和装备进行有限攻击。问题在于，七个夜晚超过300次此类打击并未改变伊朗的行为。没有理由认为第八个夜晚，或第八十个夜晚，会带来不同结果。在美国和以色列进行了六周强度高得多的轰炸之后，如今偶尔打击的收益很可能只是边际性的。即便美国能够摧毁伊朗所有沿海发射装置，该政权仍可从内陆继续向船只开火。它的许多导弹和无人机射程超过1000公里。

paragraphs.en: A second option is to escalate. Yet doing so carries risk, and no guarantee of a return. Bombing vital infrastructure in Iran would invite reciprocal attacks on Gulf states (and might be a war crime). Deploying ground troops is almost certainly a non-starter in Washington. On July 8th Mr Trump repeated his frequent threat to “take over” Kharg island, home to Iran’s main oil-export terminal. This would not help reopen Hormuz, over 600km away.
paragraphs.zh: 第二个选择是升级冲突。然而这样做有风险，而且不能保证有回报。轰炸伊朗关键基础设施会招致对海湾国家的对等攻击（也可能构成战争罪）。在华盛顿，部署地面部队几乎肯定行不通。7月8日，特朗普再次重复他经常发出的威胁，要“接管”哈尔克岛；那里是伊朗主要石油出口终端所在地。这无助于重新开放600多公里外的霍尔木兹海峡。

paragraphs.en: Reinstating the blockade may seem less fraught. It brings America and Iran back to the situation that prevailed between early April, when they first announced a ceasefire, and the signing of the mou on June 17th. That was in effect a staring contest, to see which side blinked first under economic pressure. But the outcome was the interim deal that already looks to be falling apart. Nor is there a guarantee that Iran will accept the blockade a second time. If it were to resume bigger attacks on Gulf states, Mr Trump would have to choose between all-out war and retreat.
paragraphs.zh: 恢复封锁看起来也许没那么棘手。它使美国和伊朗回到4月初双方首次宣布停火到6月17日签署谅解备忘录之间的状态。那实际上是一场对峙，看哪一方会在经济压力下先眨眼。但结果正是那项如今已经显得摇摇欲坠的临时协议。也不能保证伊朗会第二次接受封锁。如果它恢复对海湾国家发动更大规模的攻击，特朗普就不得不在全面战争和退却之间作出选择。

paragraphs.en: His third option is to look elsewhere for help. Meaningful support will not be forthcoming. A multilateral mission to secure the strait exists on paper, and Britain and France are willing to work with Oman to clear mines. But renewed fighting will deter would-be participants from sending warships. Germany’s Bundestag failed to vote on the issue before its summer break. Japan remains undecided. Gulf states are reluctant to get involved (and their navies are probably not up to scratch), lest Iran retaliate. China has urged Iran to reopen the strait, but urging is all it has done.
paragraphs.zh: 他的第三个选择是到别处寻求帮助。但有实质意义的支持不会到来。纸面上存在一项确保海峡安全的多边任务，英国和法国也愿意与阿曼合作清除水雷。但战事重燃会让潜在参与方不愿派出军舰。德国联邦议院在夏季休会前未能就此事投票。日本仍未作出决定。海湾国家不愿卷入其中（而且它们的海军可能也不够过硬），以免伊朗报复。中国敦促伊朗重新开放海峡，但它所做的也仅止于敦促。

paragraphs.en: Mr Trump finds himself back where he started three months ago: neither carrots nor sticks can persuade Iran to reopen the strait. Yet the stand-off has real costs for Iran, too. Some hawks in Tehran argue that relinquishing their grip on Hormuz would deprive them of leverage in a future conflict. That misreads reality. So long as Iran has missiles and drones, it can shut the strait at will. Control of Hormuz matters to Iran today as a means, not an end: it is only valuable in a trade for tangible benefits.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普发现自己又回到了三个月前的起点：无论胡萝卜还是大棒，都无法说服伊朗重新开放海峡。然而，这场僵局对伊朗也有真实代价。德黑兰的一些鹰派认为，放弃对霍尔木兹的掌控会让他们在未来冲突中失去筹码。这误读了现实。只要伊朗拥有导弹和无人机，它就可以随时关闭海峡。今天，控制霍尔木兹对伊朗而言是一种手段，而不是目的：只有在用它交换实际利益时，它才有价值。

paragraphs.en: With sanctions restored and the blockade resuming, Iran has already lost its immediate gains from the mou. It cannot charge tolls on vessels using the strait in peacetime unless Oman agrees to collaborate. Meanwhile, much of the crude it exported during the first weeks of the deal is sitting unsold: Chinese refiners are instead buying from Arab states, which have priced their oil at a discount to Iran’s.
paragraphs.zh: 随着制裁恢复、封锁重启，伊朗已经失去了从谅解备忘录中获得的即时收益。除非阿曼同意合作，否则它无法在和平时期向使用海峡的船只收费。与此同时，它在协议最初几周出口的许多原油仍滞销未售：中国炼油商转而从阿拉伯国家购买原油，因为这些国家的油价相对于伊朗原油有所折扣。

paragraphs.en: Neither side has a military route to achieving its goals. Mr Trump cannot bomb the strait open; Iran cannot profit if it is shut. The least bad option for both is the deal they are busy shooting to bits. ■
paragraphs.zh: 双方都没有通过军事手段实现目标的途径。特朗普无法靠轰炸把海峡炸开；伊朗也无法在海峡关闭时获利。对双方来说，最不坏的选择正是他们正忙着打得千疮百孔的那项协议。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Middle East Dispatch, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop on a fascinating, complex and consequential part of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《中东快讯》，这是一份每周一期的通讯，帮助你及时了解世界上这个迷人、复杂且影响重大的地区。

articles.title_en: America’s other battle with Iran
articles.title_zh: 美国与伊朗的另一场较量
articles.rubric_en: The two countries are vying for influence in Iraq
articles.rubric_zh: 两国正在伊拉克争夺影响力

paragraphs.en: WHILE AMERICA and Iran battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz, another contest between the two is unfolding on land in Iraq. On paper, America appears to have the upper hand. Iraq’s new prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi, chose Washington for his first foreign trip, ahead of Iran. Unlike previous Shia prime ministers, Mr Zaidi is no Islamist, but a businessman with no previous political experience who grew rich from government contracts. He arrived in Washington on July 13th with promises to disarm Iran-backed militias in Iraq and stem the flow of Iraqi dollars into Iran. He also brought a large business delegation offering a raft of prospective deals.
paragraphs.zh: 正当美国和伊朗为控制霍尔木兹海峡而角力时，两国之间的另一场较量正在伊拉克陆地上展开。从表面看，美国似乎占据上风。伊拉克新总理阿里・扎伊迪把华盛顿选为首次出访目的地，排在伊朗之前。与以往的什叶派总理不同，扎伊迪并不是伊斯兰主义者，而是一名没有从政经验的商人，靠政府合同发家致富。他于7月13日抵达华盛顿，承诺解除伊拉克境内受伊朗支持的民兵武装，并遏止伊拉克美元流入伊朗。他还带来了一个庞大的商业代表团，提出了一系列潜在交易。

paragraphs.en: Mr Zaidi seems to have struck the right tone. After their meeting (pictured) on July 14th, Donald Trump lauded their “tremendous chemistry” and predicted Mr Zaidi would make “a great leader in the Middle East”. Yet the new prime minister will probably not reduce Iran’s influence in Iraq by as much as America is hoping.
paragraphs.zh: 扎伊迪似乎拿捏准了语气。7月14日两人会面（见图）后，唐纳德・特朗普称赞他们之间有“极好的默契”，并预言扎伊迪会成为“中东一位伟大的领导人”。然而，这位新总理削弱伊朗在伊拉克影响力的程度，恐怕不会达到美国所期望的那样。

paragraphs.en: True, Mr Zaidi has reason to stay in America’s good books. He got his job after America, in effect, vetoed the return of Nuri al-Maliki, a former prime minister who is close to Iran, by threatening sanctions if Iraq’s power brokers put Mr Maliki back in office. The American president invited Mr Zaidi to Washington in April, before he had picked his cabinet, saying he hoped he would “form a new government free from terrorism.”
paragraphs.zh: 诚然，扎伊迪有理由努力讨好美国。他之所以得到这个职位，是因为美国实际上否决了亲近伊朗的前总理努里・马利基回归：美国威胁称，如果伊拉克的权力掮客让马利基重新上台，就将实施制裁。美国总统早在4月、扎伊迪尚未选定内阁之前，就邀请他访问华盛顿，并表示希望他能“组建一个不受恐怖主义影响的新政府”。

paragraphs.en: Mr Zaidi relies on oil money, already much reduced because of fighting around Hormuz, to pay the salaries of employees in Iraq’s bloated public sector, who could otherwise turn on the government. Under UN arrangements, Iraq’s oil revenues pass through New York, leaving them vulnerable to American sanctions if Mr Zaidi fails to do Mr Trump’s bidding. He also has a personal interest in keeping Mr Trump onside. His bank, Al Janoob Islamic Bank, is one of several America has barred from trading in foreign currencies. Mr Trump has relaxed the restrictions, but has yet to allow it to deal in dollars.
paragraphs.zh: 扎伊迪依赖石油收入来支付伊拉克臃肿公共部门雇员的工资；由于霍尔木兹周边的战事，这笔收入已经大幅减少，否则这些雇员可能会转而反对政府。根据联合国安排，伊拉克的石油收入经由纽约流转，这使得如果扎伊迪未能按特朗普的要求行事，这些收入就容易受到美国制裁的影响。他也有个人利益要让特朗普站在自己这边。他的银行 Al Janoob Islamic Bank 是被美国禁止进行外汇交易的几家银行之一。特朗普已放松了限制，但尚未允许其经营美元业务。

paragraphs.en: Private gains aside, the wider economic benefits from siding decisively with America look promising. As well as helping Iraq build pipelines that bypass Hormuz, American companies are in talks to develop oilfields and build a terminal for liquefied natural gas on the southern coast, reducing Iraq’s dependence on imports from Iran. In turn, Mr Zaidi could help America strengthen its ties with Syria by awarding contracts to link the two countries with pipelines and internet cables.
paragraphs.zh: 撇开个人收益不谈，果断站在美国一边所能带来的更广泛经济好处看起来颇有前景。美国公司除了帮助伊拉克建设绕开霍尔木兹的输油管道外，还在谈判开发油田，并在南部海岸建设一座液化天然气终端，以减少伊拉克对伊朗进口的依赖。反过来，扎伊迪可以通过授予合同，用管道和互联网电缆连接伊拉克和叙利亚，从而帮助美国加强与叙利亚的联系。

paragraphs.en: Supporters say Mr Zaidi is at last tackling the blight of corruption that has long drained Iraq of its oil wealth. He has had some 50 officials arrested, including a deputy oil minister whom America accuses of helping Iran smuggle oil and about a dozen members of parliament, in a heavily publicised anti-graft drive.
paragraphs.zh: 支持者说，扎伊迪终于开始处理腐败这一长期耗尽伊拉克石油财富的祸害。在一场大张旗鼓的反贪行动中，他已下令逮捕约50名官员，其中包括一名被美国指控帮助伊朗走私石油的副石油部长，以及大约十几名议员。

paragraphs.en: Critics say the campaign spares the politicians-cum-mafia bosses who appointed Mr Zaidi. Some see him as a front for Faiq Zaidan, Iraq’s chief justice, who has led the judiciary for nearly a decade and is widely regarded as the real power in the country. Long seen as Iran’s man, he appears recently to have changed tack in the face of American threats of sanctions. Mr Zaidan still controls the state, says an Iraqi diplomat, and yet now “America is happy.”
paragraphs.zh: 批评者说，这场行动放过了那些任命扎伊迪的政客兼黑帮头目。有些人认为他是伊拉克首席大法官法伊克・扎伊丹的门面人物。扎伊丹执掌司法系统近十年，被广泛认为是该国真正的掌权者。长期以来，他被视为伊朗的人，但最近面对美国制裁威胁，他似乎改变了路线。一名伊拉克外交官说，扎伊丹仍然控制着国家，而如今“美国很满意”。

paragraphs.en: The thorniest challenge for Mr Zaidi and his supporters is disarming Shia militias backed by Iran. He has vowed to do this by the end of September, when the last American troops, deployed in 2014 to fight Islamic State, are scheduled to leave Iraq. Some groups have promised to give up their weapons. But the most hardline are least likely to do so. “Israel and the United States can rightly claim that they have weakened some parts of the Iranian military system,” says Victoria Taylor of the Atlantic Council, a think-tank in Washington. “But they have not through this war weakened Iranian influence in Iraq.”
paragraphs.zh: 对扎伊迪及其支持者而言，最棘手的挑战是解除受伊朗支持的什叶派民兵武装。他已誓言在9月底前完成此事，届时2014年为打击“伊斯兰国”而部署在伊拉克的最后一批美国军队按计划将撤离。一些组织已承诺交出武器。但最强硬的派别最不可能这样做。华盛顿智库大西洋理事会的维多利亚・泰勒说：“以色列和美国可以有理由声称，它们削弱了伊朗军事体系的某些部分。但它们并没有通过这场战争削弱伊朗在伊拉克的影响力。”

paragraphs.en: Iran’s generals have no intention of letting go of their Iraqi proxy network, the most important part of Iran’s regional “axis of resistance”. Some 3,000 men are defying Mr Zaidi’s calls to disarm. In recent months they have joined Iran in lobbing missiles and drones at Arab Gulf states. And they used the funeral of Iran’s late supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, as it passed via Iraq’s main Shia shrine-cities, to project their force on the ground by organising the mourning ceremonies. Armoured cars, militiamen and Iranian flags lined the route. “Rise up for God,” read their scarlet banner, emblazoned with a clenched fist of revenge. “We’ll stand like Hussein against Yazid,” said a mourner, invoking the myth of Shia resistance against tyrants, among whom he counts Mr Zaidi.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗的将军们无意放弃他们在伊拉克的代理人网络，这是伊朗地区“抵抗轴心”中最重要的一部分。约有3,000人正在违抗扎伊迪关于解除武装的呼吁。近几个月来，他们与伊朗一道向海湾阿拉伯国家发射导弹和无人机。而在伊朗已故最高领袖阿里・哈梅内伊的葬礼途经伊拉克主要什叶派圣城时，他们通过组织悼念仪式来展示地面力量。装甲车、民兵和伊朗国旗沿途排列。他们猩红色的横幅上印着复仇的紧握拳头，写着“为真主起身”。一名悼念者援引什叶派反抗暴君的神话说：“我们会像侯赛因反抗亚齐德那样站立到底。”在他眼中，扎伊迪也属于暴君之列。

paragraphs.en: The public mood seems to be helping Iran. Gone are the times when Iraqis set Iranian consulates ablaze. Since Mr Trump’s war, even veterans of Iraq’s protest movement have expressed sympathy for Iran. “The generation that was hostile to Iran now cheers it for standing up to America and asserting its sovereignty,” says an analyst in Baghdad, the capital. A businessman travelling to Washington with Mr Zaidi agrees. “America controls the skies. Iran rules the ground,” he says. “And for most Iraqis it’s the ground that matters.” Many view the promise of Trump-related deals with suspicion. “America rules with the dollar, Iran with religion,” says an Iraqi judge in Baghdad. “In this part of the world, religion wins.”
paragraphs.zh: 公众情绪似乎正在帮伊朗。伊拉克人焚烧伊朗领事馆的时代已经过去。自特朗普发动的战争以来，就连伊拉克抗议运动的老参与者也对伊朗表达了同情。首都巴格达的一名分析人士说：“曾经敌视伊朗的那一代人，如今因为伊朗挺身对抗美国、维护主权而为它欢呼。”一名随扎伊迪前往华盛顿的商人也同意这一点。他说：“美国控制天空。伊朗统治地面。而对大多数伊拉克人来说，重要的是地面。”许多人对与特朗普有关交易的承诺持怀疑态度。巴格达一名伊拉克法官说：“美国靠美元统治，伊朗靠宗教统治。在世界的这个地方，宗教会赢。”

paragraphs.en: America has set no hard deadlines for mlitia disarmament. Mr Trump’s administration, approving of the new prime minister’s business credentials, could be disappointed yet. Among those who promoted Mr Zaidi are Iran-friendly factions that will limit his ability to rein in its proxies. If he falls short, America could impose sanctions or halt the transfer of Iraq’s oil revenues. Many anticipate bloodshed as Iran readies for a fight to force America to withdraw its money as well as its soldiers. ■
paragraphs.zh: 美国没有为民兵解除武装设定硬性期限。特朗普政府欣赏这位新总理的商业背景，但可能仍会失望。推动扎伊迪上台的人中包括亲伊朗派别，它们会限制他约束伊朗代理人的能力。如果他做不到，美国可能实施制裁，或停止转移伊拉克的石油收入。许多人预料会发生流血事件，因为伊朗正准备一战，迫使美国撤走它的资金以及士兵。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Middle East Dispatch, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop on a fascinating, complex and consequential part of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《中东快讯》，这是一份每周一期的新闻简报，让你持续了解世界上一个迷人、复杂且影响深远的地区。

articles.title_en: The Ebola epidemic is getting out of control
articles.title_zh: 埃博拉疫情正在失控
articles.rubric_en: It has spread to five provinces in Congo and may soon reach South Sudan
articles.rubric_zh: 它已蔓延至刚果五个省，并可能很快传到南苏丹

paragraphs.en: “WE KEEP ASKING and we still haven’t got anything,” says Moise Bulabantu from a dingy, clapboard clinic on the outskirts of Bunia, a city in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The 38-year-old community nurse is the only public-health worker in his district of more than 40,000 people, where an outbreak of Ebola has been spreading. Every day he sees patients who are feverish or vomiting. But the government has yet to send protective equipment. “We’re pushing for the minimum,” says Mr Bulabantu, with bags under his eyes. “We only have gloves.”
paragraphs.zh: “我们一直在请求帮助，可到现在还是什么都没有，”Moise Bulabantu在布尼亚郊区一家昏暗的木板诊所里说。布尼亚是刚果民主共和国东部的一座城市。这位38岁的社区护士是他所在辖区唯一的公共卫生工作者；该辖区有4万多人，埃博拉疫情正在那里蔓延。他每天都会见到发烧或呕吐的病人。但政府仍未送来防护设备。“我们争取的只是最基本的东西，”眼袋明显的Bulabantu先生说。“我们只有手套。”

paragraphs.en: Stories such as Mr Bulabantu’s are common across Ituri, the main province affected by the Ebola epidemic. When the Congolese government declared an outbreak on May 15th, the disease had been spreading for at least six weeks, prompting the World Health Organisation (WHO) to declare it a public-health emergency two days later. Health authorities are still failing to contain the epidemic.
paragraphs.zh: 在伊图里，像Bulabantu先生这样的故事并不少见。伊图里是受埃博拉疫情影响最严重的省份。刚果政府在5月15日宣布疫情暴发时，这种疾病已经传播了至少六周，促使世界卫生组织（WHO）两天后宣布其为突发公共卫生事件。卫生当局仍未能遏制疫情。

paragraphs.en: By July 14th Congo had confirmed 2,073 infections, about 90% of them in Ituri, and 796 deaths. On July 9th the government admitted that the disease had spread to two new provinces. There is a high risk it could soon enter neighbouring South Sudan. Unless the response improves dramatically, the current outbreak may become as bad as the one that killed more than 11,000 people in west Africa a decade ago, according to calculations in June by the American Centres for Disease Control and Prevention.
paragraphs.zh: 截至7月14日，刚果已确认2,073例感染病例，其中约90%在伊图里，并有796人死亡。7月9日，政府承认疫情已蔓延到两个新的省份。它很可能很快进入邻国南苏丹。根据美国疾病控制与预防中心6月的测算，除非应对措施大幅改善，否则当前这次疫情可能会变得像十年前在西非造成逾11,000人死亡的那次疫情一样严重。

paragraphs.en: One problem is gauging the real size of the epidemic. The reported number of cases is an underestimate, says Peter Piot, a professor of global health at the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine who co-discovered the Ebola virus in 1976. He says only 30% of new cases are contacts of known patients, which suggests people are continuing to fall ill, spread the virus and die far from view. The lack of reliable case numbers also makes it impossible to know how many of those who get the virus end up dying. Part of the steep rise in reported cases (see chart) is driven by rising availability of tests, but much is due to the real spread of the virus. Dr Piot says the outbreak is progressing faster than any he has seen in Congo (this one is the 17th).
paragraphs.zh: 一个问题是如何判断疫情的真实规模。伦敦卫生与热带医学院全球健康教授Peter Piot说，报告的病例数被低估了；他曾在1976年共同发现埃博拉病毒。他说，新增病例中只有30%是已知患者的接触者，这表明仍有人在视线之外继续患病、传播病毒并死亡。缺乏可靠的病例数字，也使人无法知道感染病毒者中最终有多少人会死亡。报告病例数陡增（见图表）的部分原因是检测可及性提高，但很大一部分则是病毒真实传播所致。Piot博士说，这次疫情的发展速度比他在刚果见过的任何一次都快（这是该国第17次疫情）。

paragraphs.en: As there is no vaccine yet against the Bundibugyo strain causing this outbreak (though one trial is beginning in Oxford), measures such as testing, contact-tracing and isolation are crucial to containing it. But too little of this is happening in Ituri. One challenge is logistical. Ituri is a province of dense forests and poorly connected hamlets. Dozens of armed groups threaten civilians. Some 900,000 people live in displacement camps after fleeing conflict in the province. Others are moving around and looking for work in unlicensed gold mines, making them hard to trace and reluctant to isolate themselves. Aid workers and soldiers at checkpoints talk of waves of people fleeing virus hotspots.
paragraphs.zh: 由于目前还没有针对引发此次疫情的Bundibugyo毒株的疫苗（不过牛津即将开始一项试验），检测、接触者追踪和隔离等措施对于遏制疫情至关重要。但在伊图里，这些工作做得太少。其中一个挑战是后勤。伊图里省森林茂密，村落之间交通不便。数十个武装组织威胁平民。约90万人因逃离该省冲突而生活在流离失所者营地。还有一些人四处流动，在无证金矿寻找工作，这使他们难以追踪，也不愿自我隔离。检查站的援助人员和士兵谈到，一波又一波的人正在逃离病毒热点地区。

paragraphs.en: A response is visible in Bunia, the provincial capital, which has hospitals, roads and an airport. But even there it is too small, says Trish Newport of Médecins Sans Frontières, a medical charity. Protective equipment is scarce. The situation is far worse the farther you move from the city, where state authority is often notional. The road to the gold-mining town of Mongbwalu, one hotspot, is unpaved and studded with militia and army checkpoints, yet aid workers still consider the town relatively accessible. Other areas affected by Ebola are far more remote.
paragraphs.zh: 在省会布尼亚，应对行动是看得见的；那里有医院、道路和机场。但医疗慈善机构无国界医生组织的Trish Newport说，即便在那里，行动规模也太小。防护设备短缺。离城市越远，情况越糟；在那些地方，国家权威往往只是名义上的。通往金矿城镇Mongbwalu的道路没有铺装，沿途布满民兵和军队检查站，而Mongbwalu正是一个疫情热点；即便如此，援助人员仍认为该镇相对容易抵达。其他受埃博拉影响的地区要偏远得多。

paragraphs.en: Without the correct equipment, hospitals and clinics can become vectors for transmission, deterring would-be patients. Many clinics have been forced to close in order to disinfect and some have not reopened. Health workers such as Mr Bulabantu are at high risk of catching the virus themselves. Across eastern Congo dozens of front-line health workers have fallen ill and 25 have died of the disease, says the WHO. “We’re very scared,” says Mr Bulabantu. He is also barely paid. His meagre monthly salary of about $80 has not been handed out for months; he is surviving on an Ebola-related bonus of $70.
paragraphs.zh: 没有正确的设备，医院和诊所可能会变成传播媒介，从而吓退本想就医的病人。许多诊所被迫关闭以进行消毒，有些还没有重新开放。像Bulabantu先生这样的医护人员自身感染病毒的风险很高。WHO表示，在刚果东部，已有数十名前线医护人员患病，25人死于这种疾病。“我们非常害怕，”Bulabantu先生说。他的工资也几乎拿不到。他每月约80美元的微薄薪水已经几个月没有发放；他现在靠一笔与埃博拉相关的70美元奖金维持生活。

paragraphs.en: In theory, there is enough money to fund the response. The WHO and the African Centres for Disease Control say that donors have pledged over $1.2bn, more than double the $518m they reckon is needed. But only $115m has been paid out. Over the past week, health workers in Bunia and the surrounding area have gone on strike over pay and working conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 理论上，应对疫情有足够资金。WHO和非洲疾病控制与预防中心表示，捐助方已承诺提供逾12亿美元，是他们估算所需5.18亿美元的两倍多。但实际拨付的只有1.15亿美元。过去一周，布尼亚及周边地区的医护人员因薪酬和工作条件问题举行了罢工。

paragraphs.en: Even if the rest of the money arrives quickly, it will be hard to convince people that their main concern is Ebola, rather than the lack of fresh water or untreated malaria that is killing their children, says Ms Newport. In places such as Bunia, where the virus is visible and people are dying on the street, help is more welcome.
paragraphs.zh: Newport女士说，即使其余资金迅速到位，也很难让人们相信，他们最该担心的是埃博拉，而不是缺乏清洁饮水，或是正在夺走他们孩子生命、却未得到治疗的疟疾。在布尼亚这样的地方，病毒清晰可见，人们死在街头，援助更受欢迎。

paragraphs.en: The virus has long spread beyond Ituri. In late June a pregnant woman who died in Ituri was transported to Kisangani, a city of 1.5m people and the capital of neighbouring Tshopo province. She received a traditional burial and has since been confirmed to have died of Ebola. “It’s a question of days before we have local spread,” says an aid official, explaining that dead bodies are particularly contagious.
paragraphs.zh: 病毒早已传播到伊图里之外。6月底，一名在伊图里死亡的孕妇被运到基桑加尼；基桑加尼是一座拥有150万人口的城市，也是邻近的乔波省省会。她接受了传统葬礼，随后被确认死于埃博拉。一名援助官员说：“出现本地传播只是几天的问题。”他解释说，遗体尤其具有传染性。

paragraphs.en: There are also concerns about the virus spreading north into South Sudan, which is on the edge of civil war and has an even more fragile health system than Congo. A paper in the Lancet Infectious Diseases, predating the spread of the disease to the border province of Haut-Uele, used modelling to estimate a seven-in-ten chance that a case of Ebola would arrive in South Sudan in the next three months.
paragraphs.zh: 人们还担心病毒会向北传播到南苏丹。南苏丹处于内战边缘，卫生系统比刚果更加脆弱。《柳叶刀·传染病》上的一篇论文发表于疫情传播到边境省份上韦莱之前；该论文利用建模估计，未来三个月内南苏丹出现一例埃博拉病例的概率为十分之七。

paragraphs.en: Mr Bulabantu says people who come to his clinic are afraid of dying. “There isn’t really a way for us to protect ourselves,” he says. “Neither for us, nor the ill.” Fear seems like a rational response.■
paragraphs.zh: Bulabantu先生说，来他诊所的人都害怕死亡。“我们其实没有办法保护自己，”他说。“我们没有，病人也没有。”恐惧似乎是一种理性的反应。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Analysing Africa, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop about the world’s youngest—and least understood—continent.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《Analysing Africa》，这是一份每周通讯，让你随时了解世界上最年轻、也最少被理解的大陆。

articles.title_en: A hit new show takes on polygamy in modern South Africa
articles.title_zh: 一部热门新剧聚焦现代南非的一夫多妻制
articles.rubric_en: “The Polygamist” is trashy yet perceptive about family, patriarchy and inequality
articles.rubric_zh: 《一夫多妻者》虽狗血，却敏锐地洞察了家庭、父权制与不平等

paragraphs.en: Dwight Eisenhower said that “whenever I run into a problem I can’t solve, I always make it bigger”. The American president was not thinking about marital woes in South Africa. But his advice has been followed in “The Polygamist”, a salacious new Netflix show that is going viral across Africa.
paragraphs.zh: 德怀特·艾森豪威尔曾说：“每当我遇到一个解决不了的问题，我总是把它变得更大。”这位美国总统当时想的并不是南非的婚姻烦恼。但他的建议在《一夫多妻者》中得到了贯彻。这是一部香艳的新 Netflix 剧集，正在非洲各地走红。

paragraphs.en: The series tracks the ups and downs of Jonasi Gomora, a rags-to-riches tycoon whose fortune, like those of several of the country’s real black millionaires, depends on a mix of his own pluck and tenders from the government. The show opens with his funeral, before rewinding to when he had merely one known wife. He takes on another in an effort to keep his business and family intact. Other affairs are revealed as viewers learn more about Mr Gomora, and South Africa.
paragraphs.zh: 该剧讲述乔纳西·戈莫拉跌宕起伏的人生。他是一位白手起家的大亨，和南非几位现实中的黑人百万富翁一样，他的财富既依靠自己的胆识，也依靠政府招标合同。剧情从他的葬礼开场，然后倒叙到他还只有一位为人所知的妻子的时候。为了保住自己的生意和家庭，他又娶了一位妻子。随着观众进一步了解戈莫拉先生，也进一步了解南非，其他婚外情也被揭露出来。

paragraphs.en: In 2021 a study estimated 1.6% of South African women in polygamous marriages. But less than 25% of South Africans are married, so polygamous set-ups are a non-trivial share, especially in poorer rural areas and among groups such as Zulus. (Two producers are daughters of Jacob Zuma, an ex-president and famous polygamist.)
paragraphs.zh: 2021 年，一项研究估计，1.6% 的南非女性处于一夫多妻婚姻中。但南非已婚人口不到 25%，因此一夫多妻式家庭占比并不算小，尤其是在较贫困的农村地区以及祖鲁人等群体中。（该剧两位制片人是雅各布·祖马的女儿；祖马是南非前总统，也是一位著名的一夫多妻者。）

paragraphs.en: “The Polygamist” transplants traditional practices into modern Johannesburg, shining a light on more than polygamy. Though only a fraction of black South African households are polygamous, just a fifth are typical (to Western eyes) nuclear families. The tut-tutting of various aunties about the polygamous marriage reflects tensions between African traditions and Western customs in the black middle class.
paragraphs.zh: 《一夫多妻者》把传统习俗移植到现代约翰内斯堡，照亮的不只是多妻制。尽管只有一小部分南非黑人家庭实行一夫多妻，但只有五分之一是（在西方人眼中）典型的核心家庭。剧中各位姑姨婶伯对一夫多妻婚姻的啧啧非议，反映了黑人中产阶级内部非洲传统与西方习俗之间的张力。

paragraphs.en: But the main theme—other than romping—is inequality. South Africa is both patriarchal and economically unequal; its tycoons and powerful politicians are almost exclusively men. “The Polygamist” plays on the palpable rationality of some women—lambasted in tabloids as “slay queens”—who view sleeping with bigwigs as a route to social mobility. “I’m going to get a man ten times more powerful than him,” the primary mistress threatens when Mr Gomora considers ditching her. Gauteng, the province containing Johannesburg, has the highest rate of infidelity, according to a dating website. Is it any wonder that the city of gold is also the city of gold diggers?
paragraphs.zh: 但除去床笫戏之外，该剧的主旨是不平等。南非既是父权社会，经济上也高度不平等；其大亨和有权势的政客几乎清一色是男性。《一夫多妻者》利用了一些女性身上显而易见的理性：她们被小报斥为“拜金女王”，却把与权贵上床视为实现阶层流动的途径。当戈莫拉先生考虑抛弃头号情妇时，她威胁说：“我要找一个比他有权势十倍的男人。”据一家交友网站称，约翰内斯堡所在的豪登省出轨率最高。黄金之城同时也是淘金女之城，这有什么可奇怪的呢？

paragraphs.en: The show showcases rich black South Africa on its own terms. The soundtrack pulses to amapiano and gqom, two popular local music genres. Characters speak isiZulu larded with English. This rich portrayal of wealthy black life may be a reason—along with all the sauce—why it has taken off in other African countries. Viewers, like Mr Gomora, want more and more. ■
paragraphs.zh: 这部剧以自己的方式展现了南非富裕黑人群体的生活。配乐随着 amapiano 和 gqom 的节拍律动，这两种都是当地流行的音乐类型。角色们说着夹杂英语的 isiZulu。这种对富裕黑人生活的丰富呈现，再加上满满的八卦猛料，或许正是它在其他非洲国家走红的原因。观众和戈莫拉先生一样，想要的只会越来越多。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Analysing Africa, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop about the world’s youngest—and least understood—continent.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《Analysing Africa》，这是一份每周一期的新闻简报，带你了解世界上最年轻、也最不被理解的大陆。

articles.title_en: How to preserve Africa’s architectural gems
articles.title_zh: 如何保护非洲的建筑瑰宝
articles.rubric_en: Development and restoration must go hand in hand
articles.rubric_zh: 发展与修复必须并行不悖

paragraphs.en: Darini House in Lamu, an old Kenyan town on the Indian Ocean coast, is a marvel: high coral stone ceilings; ornate vidaka shelves, carved meticulously into the walls; a shaded courtyard brightened by lilies. It is also a building site. For years, parts of the centuries-old family home have been near-derelict, bags of cement gathering dust beneath bat-populated beams. Now, slowly, they are being restored. “This place holds our dreams,” says Khaulah Abdulkadir of the Friends of Lamu Cultural Heritage, a local NGO. “We are hoping to breathe more life into it.”
paragraphs.zh: 位于拉穆的 Darini House 是一处奇观。拉穆是肯尼亚印度洋海岸的一座古城。这里有高高的珊瑚石天花板；精美的 vidaka 置物架，被一丝不苟地雕入墙中；还有一座阴凉的庭院，百合花为其增添亮色。它同时也是一处施工现场。多年来，这座有数百年历史的家族宅邸部分区域近乎荒废，水泥袋在蝙蝠栖息的横梁下积灰。如今，它们正被慢慢修复。“这个地方承载着我们的梦想，”当地非政府组织 Friends of Lamu Cultural Heritage 的 Khaulah Abdulkadir 说。“我们希望为它注入更多生机。”

paragraphs.en: Their plan, backed by the World Monuments Fund (WMF), a New York-based conservation body, is to acquire the property and, with outside investors, turn it into a Swahili cultural centre with a café, gallery and rental space for workshops and offices. By leasing and renovating historic homes this way, they hope to spark regeneration in Lamu’s dilapidated, UNESCO-listed Old Town. Yet critics fear that the approach, which relies on tourists and foreign capital, will erode the town’s essence. It shows the difficulty of preserving heritage in Africa, where conservation funds are scarce and development threatens to bulldoze what remains.
paragraphs.zh: 他们的计划得到世界纪念性建筑基金会（World Monuments Fund，简称 WMF）的支持。WMF 是一家总部位于纽约的保护机构。计划是买下这处房产，并在外部投资者参与下，将其改造成一个斯瓦希里文化中心，内设咖啡馆、画廊，以及可供工作坊和办公室租用的空间。通过以这种方式租赁并翻修历史住宅，他们希望带动拉穆破败的、被列入联合国教科文组织名录的老城复兴。然而批评者担心，这种依赖游客和外国资本的做法会侵蚀这座城镇的本质。这显示出在非洲保护遗产的困难：保护资金稀缺，而发展又威胁着要把残存的遗产推平。

paragraphs.en: Africa’s historic buildings and monuments account for less than 10% of the UNESCO World Heritage List. But they are overrepresented among such places deemed by the UN’s cultural arm to be “in danger”. By one estimate as many as one in three buildings in Lamu’s Old Town are in ruins. The once-bustling Madukini Street near Darini House is almost abandoned.
paragraphs.zh: 非洲的历史建筑和纪念物在联合国教科文组织世界遗产名录中占比不到 10%。但在联合国文化机构认定为“濒危”的此类地点中，它们却占比过高。据一项估计，拉穆老城多达三分之一的建筑已成废墟。Darini House 附近曾经熙熙攘攘的 Madukini Street 如今几乎被废弃。

paragraphs.en: In some places, such as Timbuktu in northern Mali, the most urgent threat comes from conflict and jihadists. Elsewhere, the problem usually boils down to a lack of resources, says Stephen Battle, WMF’s Africa director. Where states are cash-strapped, much depends on foreign governments, the UN and NGOs. By one estimate, since the 1980s roughly half of renovations in Stone Town, on Tanzania’s Zanzibar, have been funded by Oman.
paragraphs.zh: 在一些地方，比如马里北部的廷巴克图，最紧迫的威胁来自冲突和圣战分子。在其他地方，问题通常归结为资源不足，WMF 非洲主管 Stephen Battle 说。在国家财政拮据的地方，很大程度上要依赖外国政府、联合国和非政府组织。据一项估计，自 20 世纪 80 年代以来，坦桑尼亚桑给巴尔石头城约一半的翻修资金来自阿曼。

paragraphs.en: Residential buildings like those in Lamu present a particularly thorny challenge for conservationists. Unlike, say, the palaces and castles of Gondar in Ethiopia, which are the property of the state, private homes are typically in the hands of families and individuals. Many lack the means for tasteful restoration. In Lamu, the problem is worsened by the fact that homes which have been passed down multiple generations sometimes have 20 or more relatives squabbling over ownership. As a result, many “end up doing renovation the wrong way”, says Abdalla Kuresh, one of only a few builders in Lamu still practising the traditional vernacular style.
paragraphs.zh: 像拉穆这类住宅建筑，给保护工作者带来尤其棘手的挑战。与埃塞俄比亚贡德尔的宫殿和城堡等国有财产不同，私人住宅通常掌握在家族和个人手中。许多人无力进行得体的修复。在拉穆，问题还因房屋代代相传而加剧：有些房屋有 20 个甚至更多亲属为所有权争执不休。结果是，许多房子“最后都以错误的方式进行翻修”，Abdalla Kuresh 说。他是拉穆仍在实践传统本土建筑风格的少数几名建筑工匠之一。

paragraphs.en: Shoddy—or non-existent—documentation worsens the problem. Killion Mokwete, a Botswanan architect at Northeastern University in Boston, says Afro-Brazilian architecture in west Africa is at risk in part because countries lack accessible digitised databases and archival institutions. This can make it hard both to prove ownership and to know how to restore a building to its original condition. It also helps explain why these architectural gems, many built and designed by former slaves who returned from the Americas in the second half of the 19th century, have not been listed by UNESCO. To fix this Mr Mokwete co-founded the African Built Heritage Project in Benin, which keeps digital records of endangered buildings.
paragraphs.zh: 粗劣的文档记录，甚至完全没有记录，使问题更加严重。波士顿东北大学的博茨瓦纳建筑师 Killion Mokwete 说，西非的非裔巴西建筑面临风险，部分原因在于各国缺乏可访问的数字化数据库和档案机构。这既会让证明所有权变得困难，也会让人难以知道如何把建筑恢复到原貌。这也有助于解释为什么这些建筑瑰宝尚未被联合国教科文组织列入名录。它们中有许多是在 19 世纪下半叶由从美洲返回的前奴隶建造和设计的。为了解决这一问题，Mokwete 先生在贝宁共同创立了 African Built Heritage Project，为濒危建筑保存数字记录。

paragraphs.en: At root, though, lies a more fundamental difficulty: indifference. In Kenya, as elsewhere, “preservation of the built environment can quickly find its way to the bottom of the pile,” says Teckla Muhoro, an urban expert at Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture & Technology in Nairobi, the capital. For residents of Nairobi, historic buildings are often tainted by British colonialism; urban heritage in Lamu is sometimes seen as the work of Arab interlopers. African governments and foreign tourists, meanwhile, tend to pay far more attention to the continent’s wildlife and natural landscapes.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，问题的根源在于一个更根本的困难：冷漠。在肯尼亚和其他地方一样，“建筑环境的保护很快就会被排到优先事项的最末端，”首都内罗毕的乔莫·肯雅塔农业与技术大学城市问题专家 Teckla Muhoro 说。对内罗毕居民来说，历史建筑往往带有英国殖民主义的污点；拉穆的城市遗产有时则被视为阿拉伯外来者的作品。与此同时，非洲政府和外国游客往往更加关注这片大陆的野生动物和自然景观。

paragraphs.en: This means that when the choice is between urban development and heritage preservation, the latter typically loses. Eritrea’s capital, Asmara, whose perfectly preserved Italian-era Art Deco architecture was awarded UNESCO status in 2017, is almost unique. (Eritrea’s autocracy and stagnant economy mean it also is no model.) Much more common is the fate of old buildings in Ethiopia’s capital, Addis Ababa, which have been systemically razed in recent years. In pursuit of hyper-modernisation the city’s authorities have tossed aside laws designed to protect heritage, banishing poor residents to the periphery. “Ironically, the national-heritage authority itself is enabling the demolitions,” says Nahom Teklu, an Ethiopian architect.
paragraphs.zh: 这意味着，当选择摆在城市发展和遗产保护之间时，后者通常会输。厄立特里亚首都阿斯马拉几乎是独一无二的：其保存完好的意大利时期装饰艺术风格建筑于 2017 年获得联合国教科文组织地位。（厄立特里亚的专制体制和停滞经济也意味着它并非榜样。）更常见的是埃塞俄比亚首都亚的斯亚贝巴旧建筑的命运：近年来它们被系统性拆除。为了追求超现代化，该市当局把旨在保护遗产的法律抛在一边，并将贫困居民驱赶到城市边缘。“讽刺的是，国家遗产主管机构本身正在为拆除行为开绿灯，”埃塞俄比亚建筑师 Nahom Teklu 说。

paragraphs.en: But pitting growth against conservation is ultimately a “false dichotomy”, argues Shadreck Chirikure of Oxford University. Plenty of picturesque European cities are proof of this. So is the idea of an inescapable trade-off between indigenous preservation efforts and foreign investment. UNESCO’s Mohammed Juma warns against creating “ghost historical cities”, which preserve architectural heritage but exclude local residents. A better solution may be for authorities to encourage tourism and investment, using the proceeds of growth to fund conservation. Heritage laws and building codes are, after all, more likely to be enforced if locals themselves feel their benefits. ■
paragraphs.zh: 但牛津大学的 Shadreck Chirikure 认为，把增长与保护对立起来，归根结底是一种“虚假的二分法”。许多风景如画的欧洲城市就是证明。所谓本土保护努力与外国投资之间必然存在取舍的说法，也同样站不住脚。联合国教科文组织的 Mohammed Juma 警告，不要制造“幽灵历史城市”：它们保存了建筑遗产，却排除了当地居民。更好的办法或许是由当局鼓励旅游和投资，并用增长带来的收益为保护提供资金。毕竟，如果当地人自己感受到遗产保护的好处，遗产法律和建筑规范就更有可能得到执行。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Analysing Africa, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop about the world’s youngest—and least understood—continent.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅 Analysing Africa，这是一份每周通讯，让你持续了解这个世界上最年轻、也最不为人理解的大陆。

articles.title_en: Ukraine’s reformist defence minister is ousted
articles.title_zh: 乌克兰改革派国防部长遭解职
articles.rubric_en: The generals pushed back at his efforts to modernise the army
articles.rubric_zh: 将军们抵制他推动军队现代化的努力

paragraphs.en: THE FIRING that people expected came first. On July 12th Volodymyr Zelensky announced the impending exit of his prime minister, Yuliia Svyrydenko, as part of a wider shake-up of his top team. The Ukrainian president needed someone with authority to reset fractious relations with parliament, and the pliant Ms Svyrydenko was never that. The more shocking news came three days later with the revelation that Mykhailo Fedorov (pictured), his popular defence minister, would also be leaving after just six months in the job. Mr Zelensky said there were problems in the “dialogue” between the army and the youngest member of his top team. A self-styled disrupter out to break the old system, Mr Fedorov had been quick to ruffle feathers, but slow to build alliances.
paragraphs.zh: 人们预料中的那场解职先来了。7月12日，弗拉基米尔·泽连斯基宣布，他的总理尤利娅·斯维里登科即将离任，这是其高层团队更大范围改组的一部分。这位乌克兰总统需要一个有权威的人来重塑与议会之间紧张不和的关系，而温顺的斯维里登科从来不是这样的人。更令人震惊的消息在三天后传出：颇受欢迎的国防部长米哈伊洛·费多罗夫（图中人物）也将在上任仅六个月后离职。泽连斯基说，军队与他高层团队中最年轻成员之间的“对话”存在问题。费多罗夫自称是要打破旧体制的颠覆者，他很快就得罪了不少人，却迟迟未能建立联盟。

paragraphs.en: Mr Fedorov, 35, known to colleagues as Misha, began his tenure at the ministry in January amid high expectations. He came with solid reformist credentials. As digital-transformation minister, he had introduced Diia, a highly regarded app that put state services and digital passports on smartphones. After Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022, he played a crucial role in building Ukraine’s “drone army”, introducing market mechanisms to boost the production of everything from small battlefield drones to bomber and interceptor drones. His supporters believed he was the man who could reform and modernise the army in the same way that he had digitised the government. The generals were less impressed, and set out to stymie him.
paragraphs.zh: 现年35岁的费多罗夫被同事称为米沙，今年1月在外界高度期待中开始执掌该部。他拥有扎实的改革派资历。担任数字化转型部长时，他推出了广受好评的Diia应用，把国家服务和数字护照装进了智能手机。俄罗斯于2022年全面入侵后，他在组建乌克兰“无人机军团”方面发挥了关键作用，引入市场机制，推动从小型战场无人机到轰炸无人机、拦截无人机等各类装备的生产。他的支持者认为，他能够像推动政府数字化那样改革并现代化军队。将军们对此不以为然，并开始设法阻挠他。

paragraphs.en: At a war council in early July, the tensions between Mr Fedorov and the military leadership simmered barely below the surface. The generals had good news for their president. Drone operations were succeeding. A campaign to isolate Russian-occupied Crimea was ahead of schedule. But as Power Point slides were shown to the testosterone-filled room, the generals griped about Mr Fedorov’s missile and ammunition procurement. The minister snapped back: If it wasn’t for his emergency drone-purchasing decisions at the beginning of the year, which required borrowing money earmarked for salaries, there would be no Crimean operation to speak of. A witness to the proceedings described “two different co-ordinate systems” in a clinch, with “no common language”.
paragraphs.zh: 7月初的一次战争委员会会议上，费多罗夫与军方领导层之间的紧张关系几乎浮到了表面。将军们给总统带来了好消息。无人机行动正在取得成功。孤立俄占克里米亚的战役进度超前。但当幻灯片展示给这个充满男性荷尔蒙的会议室时，将军们抱怨费多罗夫在导弹和弹药采购上的做法。部长当即反驳说，如果不是他年初作出紧急采购无人机的决定，需要借用原本划拨给薪资的钱，就根本谈不上什么克里米亚行动。一名与会见证者形容，这是“两个不同坐标系”僵持在一起，“没有共同语言”。

paragraphs.en: Mr Fedorov’s six months in the ministry were marked by hyperactivity and a pugnacity that put many noses out of joint. He encroached on entrenched interests, and denied some powerful people lucrative contracts. He ordered a full audit of the ministry that uncovered overspending of 300bn hryvnia ($6.6bn). He subjected officials to lie detectors; those who refused or failed were dismissed. And he moved some procurement to open tenders, which he says cut the cost of artillery shells by 16%.
paragraphs.zh: 费多罗夫在国防部的六个月，以高度活跃和好斗作风为标志，也因此惹恼了许多人。他触动了根深蒂固的利益，令一些有权势的人失去了利润丰厚的合同。他下令对国防部进行全面审计，发现了3000亿格里夫纳（66亿美元）的超支。他让官员接受测谎；拒绝或未通过者均被解职。他还将部分采购转向公开招标，并称这使炮弹成本降低了16%。

paragraphs.en: That was part of the reason for his troubles. The rest was self-inflicted. The minister has quarrelled openly with the army’s more traditionalist leaders, especially its commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrsky. It is well known that the defence minister angled for the general’s removal, but failed to win his president’s approval. A senior intelligence source warned before Mr Fedorov’s ouster that he never understood how small his chances of succeeding were: “Syrsky is experienced, knows the system much better than Misha, and will outfox him.”
paragraphs.zh: 这是他陷入麻烦的部分原因。其余问题则是他自己造成的。这位部长公开与军队中较为传统派的领导人发生争执，尤其是总司令亚历山大·瑟尔斯基。众所周知，国防部长曾试图推动这位将军下台，但未能获得总统批准。一名高级情报人士在费多罗夫被罢免前警告说，他从未明白自己成功的机会有多小：“瑟尔斯基经验丰富，比米沙更了解这个系统，而且会智胜他。”

paragraphs.en: Mr Fedorov’s first big reform package only began to be implemented in June, after months of haranguing and waiting for signoffs. On paper, it tackled the most urgent manpower problems. There would be a new deal for front line infantry. Monthly pay would increase threefold to $7,000 and fixed-term contracts of six, ten, 14 and 24 months would be introduced. There would also be a limited demobilisation for the longest-serving soldiers by the end of 2026. In addition, he earmarked more money for recruiting foreigners. The estimated 300,000 Ukrainians listed as absent without leave would also be given a 100-day window in which to return without punishment. Previously, those caught were sent to the hottest spots on the front-line, where the chances of surviving were slim.
paragraphs.zh: 费多罗夫的第一套重大改革方案，在经历数月催促和等待签字后，直到6月才开始实施。从纸面上看，它针对的是最紧迫的兵力问题。前线步兵将获得一套新待遇。月薪将增加两倍，达到7000美元，并将引入6个月、10个月、14个月和24个月的固定期限合同。到2026年底，服役时间最长的士兵还将获得有限复员。此外，他还预留了更多资金用于招募外国人。估计有30万名被列为擅离职守的乌克兰人，也将获得一个100天的窗口期，可以无惩罚返回。此前，被抓到的人会被派往前线最危险的地段，在那里生还机会渺茫。

paragraphs.en: Mr Fedorov’s critics in the army accept he has improved drone procurement and digitalisation. But they said his lack of military experience made him unqualified to plan a war. Some said his flagship reforms amounted to a “PR repackaging” of work that was already under way. The defence minister was the equivalent of a football “goalhanger” seizing credit for others’ ideas, said one senior general. Some likened him to a modern day Robert McNamara, the late American defence secretary who found that the managerial methods he had honed running Ford did not transfer well to the Pentagon. “To reform something you have to understand how it works,” said another Ukrainian general. “Would you really sit in an aeroplane if you saw that the pilot was a shopkeeper?”
paragraphs.zh: 费多罗夫在军中的批评者承认，他改善了无人机采购和数字化。但他们说，他缺乏军事经验，没有资格规划一场战争。一些人说，他的标志性改革不过是对已经在进行的工作进行“公关式重新包装”。一名高级将领说，这位国防部长相当于足球场上的“捡漏前锋”，抢占他人创意的功劳。有些人把他比作现代版罗伯特·麦克纳马拉；这位已故美国国防部长曾发现，自己在管理福特汽车时磨练出的管理方法，并不能很好地移植到五角大楼。另一名乌克兰将军说：“要改革某个东西，你就必须了解它如何运作。”“如果你看到飞行员是个店主，你真的会坐上那架飞机吗？”

paragraphs.en: On July 12th Mr Zelensky asked him if he was tempted by the newly vacant prime minister’s post. In usual circumstances that would have counted as a promotion; here it was read as a defeat for his project. The defence minister turned the job down, and it has since been offered to Serhiy Koretskiy, a well-regarded manager with a background in the energy industry. Mr Koretskiy was “the most prepared candidate” to navigate the challenges of what would be a difficult winter, Mr Zelensky said.
paragraphs.zh: 7月12日，泽连斯基问他是否有兴趣出任新近空缺的总理职位。在通常情况下，这本应算是升职；但在这里，人们将其解读为他的计划遭遇失败。国防部长拒绝了这份工作，随后该职位被提供给谢尔希·科列茨基。科列茨基是一名广受认可的管理者，有能源行业背景。泽连斯基说，科列茨基是应对这个艰难冬季挑战的“准备最充分的候选人”。

paragraphs.en: Reports suggest the interior minister, Ihor Klymenko, was poised to take over the defence ministry. At the time of writing, it was not clear if Mr Fedorov would be offered another job at all. It is likely he would turn down a demotion if offered one. In the days leading up to his defenestration, the minister admitted that he was worried that the support of his political patron was coming to an end, but that he had only followed instructions. “When I began the job, the president told me to act according to my conscience,” he said. “What can I do? I don’t want to leave this post knowing that I ever bent to suit anyone.” ■
paragraphs.zh: 报道称，内务部长伊霍尔·克利缅科已准备接管国防部。截至发稿时，尚不清楚费多罗夫是否还会获得任何其他职位。如果有人给他降职安排，他很可能会拒绝。在被赶下台前的几天里，这位部长承认，他担心自己的政治靠山对他的支持即将结束，但他说自己只是遵照指示行事。“我开始这份工作时，总统告诉我要按良心行事，”他说。“我能怎么办？我不想在离开这个职位时，知道自己曾经为了迎合任何人而低头。”■

paragraphs.en: To stay on top of the biggest European stories, sign up to Café Europa, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 若想持续关注最重要的欧洲新闻，请订阅Café Europa，这是我们的每周一期、仅面向订户的新闻通讯。

articles.title_en: Europe seems set to ease its carbon pricing
articles.title_zh: 欧洲似乎将放松碳定价
articles.rubric_en: Green goals are running into fears about competitiveness
articles.rubric_zh: 绿色目标正遭遇对竞争力的担忧

paragraphs.en: THE EUROPEAN UNION’s carbon price is an economist’s dream. The bloc’s emissions-trading scheme (ETS) distributes rights to emit greenhouse gases to firms in electricity generation, heavy industry and aviation. Then it lets the market do the rest. It was phased in gradually, with all kinds of cushioning such as free allowances (not all of them justified) to give emitters plenty of time to adjust. Now, however, the system is starting to bite, which makes it a perfect scapegoat for politicians struggling to contain soaring energy prices and to shield struggling industries from global competition.
paragraphs.zh: 欧盟的碳价格堪称经济学家的梦想。欧盟的排放交易体系（ETS）向发电、重工业和航空企业分配温室气体排放权，然后让市场完成剩下的事。该体系是逐步推行的，并配有各种缓冲措施，比如免费配额（其中并非全都有正当理由），以便给排放者充足时间适应。然而现在，这套体系开始真正产生痛感，这也让它成了政客们的绝佳替罪羊：他们正努力遏制飙升的能源价格，并保护陷入困境的产业免受全球竞争冲击。

paragraphs.en: On July 17th the European Commission is expected to reveal a watering down of the ETS amid worries that, since it is essentially a tax, it is hampering European competitiveness. Auctioning carbon allowances brought in around €40bn ($46bn) to the EU and its members in 2024.
paragraphs.zh: 预计欧洲委员会将在7月17日公布对ETS的弱化方案。人们担心，由于它本质上是一种税，正在削弱欧洲的竞争力。2024年，拍卖碳配额为欧盟及其成员国带来了约400亿欧元（460亿美元）的收入。

paragraphs.en: In March, ten EU member states, including Italy, Poland and Romania, urged the commission to conduct a thorough review of the scheme with an eye to helping electricity consumers and industry. Friedrich Merz, Germany’s chancellor, worried that his country’s manufacturing sector was wavering: “We should be very open to revise it, or at least to postpone it.” Since then, the commission has worked on a plan that tries to reconcile its contradictory goals of cutting greenhouse gas emissions while allowing the bloc’s industries to compete—all without breaching international trade rules.
paragraphs.zh: 今年3月，包括意大利、波兰和罗马尼亚在内的十个欧盟成员国敦促欧盟委员会对该体系进行全面审查，着眼于帮助电力消费者和工业界。德国总理弗里德里希·默茨担心本国制造业正在动摇：“我们应当非常开放地考虑修改它，或者至少推迟它。”此后，欧盟委员会一直在制定一项计划，试图调和其相互矛盾的目标：既要削减温室气体排放，又要让欧盟产业能够竞争，同时还不能违反国际贸易规则。

paragraphs.en: Industry’s gripe is that it now costs about €80 to emit a tonne of carbon, and that price is expected to climb. This adds roughly three cents to the price of a gas-powered kWh (or about 10% to the retail price of power) and €11 to the ticket price for a three-hour flight. Almost all allowances for electricity generation and airlines have to be bought in auctions. Some industries that are exposed to international markets and high emission costs still receive many free allowances, as long as they continue to produce. That has shielded them from the impact of the carbon price. In fact, some companies in industries like metals and paper receive allowances for more greenhouse gases than they emit, says Bruegel, a Brussels-based think-tank, resulting in a handsome profit.
paragraphs.zh: 工业界的不满在于，如今排放一吨碳大约要花80欧元，而且这一价格预计还会上涨。这大约会让燃气发电每千瓦时的价格增加3欧分（约相当于零售电价的10%），并让一张三小时航班的机票价格增加11欧元。发电和航空公司的几乎所有配额都必须通过拍卖购买。一些面向国际市场、且排放成本较高的行业，只要继续生产，仍会获得大量免费配额。这使它们免受碳价影响。总部位于布鲁塞尔的智库Bruegel称，事实上，金属和造纸等行业的一些公司获得的温室气体配额超过了它们实际排放量，从而赚取了可观利润。

paragraphs.en: The EU has brought in a tax called the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM). Importers of energy-intensive goods like steel or fertiliser must pay the EU’s emissions price on the carbon embedded in the imported good, unless the country of origin has its own carbon-pricing scheme. But the EU cannot go on giving its own producers free allowances without flouting international trade rules. As CBAM payments are phased in until 2034, free allowances will be phased out.
paragraphs.zh: 欧盟已经引入一项名为“碳边境调节机制”（CBAM）的税。钢铁或化肥等能源密集型商品的进口商，必须就进口商品中所含的碳支付欧盟的排放价格，除非原产国拥有自己的碳定价体系。但欧盟不能一边继续向本土生产商发放免费配额，一边又不违反国际贸易规则。随着CBAM付款逐步推行至2034年，免费配额将逐步取消。

paragraphs.en: The switch from free allowances to CBAM will leave one large gap: exports. Inside the European market, all producers, domestic or foreign, will have to pay a carbon price. But when competing in export markets, EU producers will be the only ones paying it, leaving them at a disadvantage. Worse still, exempting exports from the carbon price would be considered an export subsidy that breaches WTO rules.
paragraphs.zh: 从免费配额转向CBAM将留下一个大缺口：出口。在欧洲市场内部，所有生产商，无论本土还是外国，都必须支付碳价。但在出口市场竞争时，欧盟生产商将是唯一需要支付这笔费用的一方，使其处于劣势。更糟的是，豁免出口产品的碳价会被视为违反世贸组织规则的出口补贴。

paragraphs.en: One workaround could be to provide export credits for green goods, says George Riddell of Goyder, a trade consultancy. “Those would help competitive European players, but not those that are currently screaming the loudest.” There is also a plan to offer free allowances conditional on green investment.
paragraphs.zh: 贸易咨询公司Goyder的乔治·里德尔表示，一个变通办法可能是为绿色商品提供出口抵免。“这会帮助有竞争力的欧洲企业，但帮不到那些目前叫得最响的企业。”另有一项计划是提供以绿色投资为条件的免费配额。

paragraphs.en: The biggest effect on the carbon price will come from how many allowances are issued. The EU will have to greatly reduce the number to meet its climate target. That would tend to drive up the carbon price. Yet allowing industry to emit more would put added pressure on domestic heating, farming and transport to reduce their emissions, which is politically even harder (see chart).
paragraphs.zh: 对碳价影响最大的因素将是发放多少配额。欧盟必须大幅减少配额数量，才能实现其气候目标。这往往会推高碳价。然而，允许工业排放更多，则会给家庭取暖、农业和交通运输施加更大减排压力，而这在政治上更加困难（见图表）。

paragraphs.en: It would also hurt firms that have invested in greener processes believing the rules would not be tweaked to suit polluters. “The jury is still out on whether Europe can be a winner of the megatrend of sustainability,” says Stefan Kvarfordt of the Confederation of Swedish Enterprise. ■
paragraphs.zh: 这还会伤害那些已经投资更绿色工艺的企业；它们原本相信规则不会为了迁就污染者而被调整。瑞典企业联合会的斯特凡·克瓦福特表示：“欧洲能否成为可持续发展这一大趋势的赢家，目前还没有定论。”■

paragraphs.en: To stay on top of the biggest European stories, sign up to Café Europa, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 若想及时了解最重要的欧洲新闻，请订阅Café Europa，我们每周一期、仅面向订户的新闻简报。

articles.title_en: Sweden squashed migration. The populist right wants to go further
articles.title_zh: 瑞典压低了移民规模。民粹右翼还想更进一步
articles.rubric_en: The Sweden Democrats could be heading for a bigger role in government
articles.rubric_zh: 瑞典民主党可能将在政府中扮演更重要的角色

paragraphs.en: NOT FAR from central Stockholm lies Stora Essingen, a sleepy island covered with lush trees and pretty houses. For years its residents voted overwhelmingly for the Moderate Party, Sweden’s mainstream conservatives. But since 2022, when the party entered a power-sharing pact with the hard-right anti-immigrant Sweden Democrats (SD), voters have signalled their displeasure at its alliance with the hitherto pariah.
paragraphs.zh: 离斯德哥尔摩市中心不远处有一座名叫斯托拉埃辛根的小岛，岛上绿树成荫，房屋漂亮，气氛闲适。多年来，当地居民压倒性地支持温和党，即瑞典的主流保守派。但自2022年以来，该党与强硬右翼、反移民的瑞典民主党（SD）达成权力分享协议，选民便用选票表达了对其与这个昔日“贱民”结盟的不满。

paragraphs.en: Instead voters have shifted towards the centre-left Social Democrats, whose activists are knocking on doors ahead of the general election on September 13th, in the hope that it can keep the SD out of the next government. Many voters in places like Stora Essingen view their country as a sensible, liberal and cosmopolitan democracy. The election will test whether that idea of Sweden wins out—or whether, having had a taste of anti-immigrant populism, Swedes want more of it.
paragraphs.zh: 相反，选民转向了中左翼的社会民主党。该党的活动人士正在9月13日大选前挨家挨户敲门拉票，希望能把瑞典民主党挡在下一届政府之外。斯托拉埃辛根这类地方的许多选民认为，自己的国家是一个理性、自由、国际化的民主国家。这场选举将检验这种瑞典想象能否胜出，还是说，在尝过反移民民粹主义的滋味后，瑞典人还想要更多。

paragraphs.en: The SD argues that it has tamed (or expelled) its most extreme elements. Its logo, once a blazing torch, is now a blue-and-yellow flower. In the election in 2022, amid anger over a surge in migration a few years earlier, it became the biggest right-wing party in parliament and backed a government headed by Ulf Kristersson, the leader of the Moderates; the SD got policy influence, but no ministerial jobs. Today Mr Kristersson promises the SD cabinet roles if the coalition is re-elected.
paragraphs.zh: 瑞典民主党辩称，它已经驯服了（或驱逐了）党内最极端的成分。它的党徽曾是一支燃烧的火炬，如今变成了一朵蓝黄相间的花。2022年选举中，几年前移民激增引发的愤怒仍在发酵，该党成为议会中最大的右翼政党，并支持由温和党领袖乌尔夫·克里斯特松领导的政府；瑞典民主党获得了政策影响力，但没有拿到部长职位。如今，克里斯特松承诺，如果联盟再次当选，将给予瑞典民主党内阁职位。

paragraphs.en: The election will reveal whether the SD’s popularity can endure once migration slows to a trickle. Sweden’s hyper-restrictive policies—admired by much of the European right—have reduced annual net migration from 117,000 at its peak in 2016 to 12,000 last year (see chart).
paragraphs.zh: 这场选举将揭示，在移民数量降至涓涓细流之后，瑞典民主党的受欢迎程度能否维持。瑞典极度严格的政策受到欧洲许多右翼人士赞赏，已将年度净移民人数从2016年峰值时的117,000人降至去年的12,000人（见图表）。

paragraphs.en: The Social Democrats tightened asylum rules and border checks while in power during Europe’s refugee crisis in the mid-2010s. “The first wave of changes tried to make Sweden less attractive to people trying to come here,” explains Louise Dane of the Swedish Refugee Law Centre, an NGO. But as the number of arrivals fell and Mr Kristersson’s government took over, the focus shifted. “Now the policy changes are about trying to get people who are already here with residency permits to leave.”
paragraphs.zh: 在2010年代中期欧洲难民危机期间，社会民主党执政时收紧了庇护规则和边境检查。“第一波改革试图让瑞典对想来这里的人没那么有吸引力，”非政府组织瑞典难民法律中心的路易丝·达内解释说。但随着抵达人数下降、克里斯特松政府上台，重点发生了转移。“现在的政策变化，是试图让那些已经在这里、持有居留许可的人离开。”

paragraphs.en: The government aims to repatriate poorly integrated or out-of-work migrants. The migration ministry boasts that more people are returning to countries such as Iraq and Somalia than are arriving from them. Atervandring (repatriation) is a watchword for the SD. “We want a lot of people to leave,” says Ludvig Aspling, the party’s migration spokesman. “There’s no reason to have people here from other countries if they’re not contributing.”
paragraphs.zh: 政府的目标是遣返融入不佳或失业的移民。移民部夸耀说，如今返回伊拉克和索马里等国的人数多于从这些国家来到瑞典的人数。Atervandring（遣返）是瑞典民主党的关键词。“我们希望很多人离开，”该党移民事务发言人卢德维格·阿斯普林说。“如果他们没有贡献，就没有理由让来自其他国家的人留在这里。”

paragraphs.en: To that end the government is encouraging migrants to go. Since January refugees have been able to claim up to SKr350,000 ($36,000) to return to their country of origin—nine times what a similar scheme offers in Britain. Only 600 people a year are expected to take it. The SD argues this would increase if welfare benefits were trimmed. It wants to expand the scheme to naturalised citizens, too.
paragraphs.zh: 为此，政府正在鼓励移民离开。自1月以来，难民可以申请最高350,000瑞典克朗（36,000美元）的补助，用于返回原籍国，这一金额是英国类似计划的九倍。预计每年只有600人会接受这项补助。瑞典民主党认为，如果削减福利待遇，接受人数会增加。该党还希望把这项计划扩大到已入籍公民。

paragraphs.en: A “good behaviour” law, passed last month, allows the government to deport migrants for infractions such as unpaid debts. It has tightened family-reunification rules and stopped issuing permanent residence to refugees. The SD also wants to downgrade those who already hold this status to temporary residence.
paragraphs.zh: 上个月通过的一项“良好行为”法律允许政府因未偿债务等违规行为驱逐移民。政府还收紧了家庭团聚规则，并停止向难民发放永久居留权。瑞典民主党还希望把那些已经拥有永久居留身份的人降级为临时居留。

paragraphs.en: A chunk of voters likes these plans. But as migration has dropped, the SD’s popularity has remained stuck at around 20%. The party’s main issue is losing its salience. Last year just 23% of Swedes named integration as one of their biggest concerns, compared with 54% in 2015, according to surveys by the University of Gothenburg’s SOM Institute. That could help the Social Democrats, who struggle to convince voters they are tough on migration.
paragraphs.zh: 有一部分选民喜欢这些计划。但随着移民数量下降，瑞典民主党的支持率一直停留在约20%。该党的核心议题正在失去显著性。根据哥德堡大学SOM研究所的调查，去年只有23%的瑞典人把融入问题列为自己最担忧的问题之一，而2015年这一比例为54%。这可能有利于社会民主党，因为该党一直难以让选民相信自己在移民问题上足够强硬。

paragraphs.en: For the right, this shift has made politics awkward. Last month public outrage caused the government to ease rules that had led to the deportation of teenagers who grew up in Sweden. Business groups have begun complaining that they cannot get foreign talent. The Liberal Party, part of Mr Kristersson’s coalition, opposes the SD’s push to revoke permanent residency.
paragraphs.zh: 对右翼而言，这一转变让政治局面变得棘手。上个月，公众愤怒迫使政府放宽了一些规则，这些规则曾导致在瑞典长大的青少年被驱逐出境。商业团体也开始抱怨说，他们招不到外国人才。作为克里斯特松联盟成员之一的自由党，反对瑞典民主党推动撤销永久居留权。

paragraphs.en: Some voters who tolerated the SD’s rougher edges when migration was a bigger worry may find them harder to ignore today. On July 5th Richard Jomshof, a senior SD politician, said: “It is no more strange to hate Islam than it is to hate national socialism”. Some fear that talk like this risks emboldening ugly extremes (something the SD strenuously denies).
paragraphs.zh: 一些选民在移民问题更令人担忧时曾容忍瑞典民主党较粗粝的一面，如今可能会发现这些更难忽视。7月5日，瑞典民主党资深政治人物理查德·约姆斯霍夫说：“仇恨伊斯兰教并不比仇恨国家社会主义更奇怪。”一些人担心，这类言论可能会助长丑陋的极端倾向（瑞典民主党对此坚决否认）。

paragraphs.en: Populist-right parties across Europe face a challenge of staying relevant even as rates of migration slow. Many will be watching this election to see if the SD’s pivot—to trying to remove migrants who have already arrived—will be as popular with voters as the previous wave of anti-immigrant policies. ■
paragraphs.zh: 欧洲各地的民粹右翼政党都面临一个挑战：即使移民率放缓，也要保持相关性。许多人将关注这场选举，看看瑞典民主党的转向，即试图移走已经抵达的移民，是否会像前一波反移民政策那样受到选民欢迎。■
