articles.title_en: Opening the border between Spain and Gibraltar
articles.title_zh: 开放西班牙与直布罗陀之间的边界
articles.rubric_en: The deal offers hope for a more pragmatic relationship between Britain and Europe
articles.rubric_zh: 这项协议为英国与欧洲之间建立更务实的关系带来希望

paragraphs.en: NOWADAYS IT IS commonplace to build walls between countries. But this week a barrier came down. Amid popular celebration, at midnight on July 14th border controls ceased between Gibraltar, a self-governing British sovereign territory, and Spain. Under a treaty signed the same day between the European Union and Britain, demolition of the police and customs posts at the border has started. “The last wall in continental Europe has fallen,” said Pedro Sánchez, Spain’s prime minister, as he visited the site. The treaty marks a tidying up of the mess left by Brexit. And in a region of strategic importance, in sight of the Moroccan coast, optimists hope it may herald a new era of defence co-operation between two NATO allies.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，在国家之间筑墙已是司空见惯。但本周，一道屏障被拆除了。7月14日午夜，在民众的欢庆声中，直布罗陀这个实行自治的英国主权领土与西班牙之间的边境管制停止实施。根据欧盟与英国同日签署的一项条约，边境上的警察和海关岗亭已开始拆除。西班牙首相佩德罗·桑切斯在视察现场时说：“欧洲大陆最后一堵墙倒塌了。”这项条约标志着对英国脱欧留下的混乱局面进行了一次清理。在这个具有战略重要性、可以望见摩洛哥海岸的地区，乐观人士希望它或许预示着两个北约盟友之间防务合作的新时代。

paragraphs.en: With just 40,000 people crammed into narrow strips of land beneath its towering Rock, Gibraltar depends on Spain for many things, including more than 10,000 workers who cross from the town of La Línea each day. Many Gibraltarians have second homes in Spain. Not surprisingly, 96% of Gibraltarians voted to remain in the EU because Brexit implied a hard border. That “would have been catastrophic not just for us but also for the Spanish side”, says John Isola, president of the Gibraltar Chamber of Commerce.
paragraphs.zh: 直布罗陀只有4万人，拥挤地生活在其高耸巨岩下方狭长的土地上，在许多方面都依赖西班牙，包括每天从拉利内亚镇跨境而来的1万多名工人。许多直布罗陀人在西班牙拥有第二套住房。毫不奇怪，96%的直布罗陀人投票支持留在欧盟，因为英国脱欧意味着会出现硬边界。直布罗陀商会主席约翰·伊索拉说，那“将不仅对我们是灾难性的，对西班牙一侧也同样如此”。

paragraphs.en: Under the treaty, whose 1,000-plus pages took more than five years to negotiate, Gibraltar has become a de facto part of the EU’s border-free Schengen area. Non-Gibraltarian passengers arriving at its airport, whose only flights are from Britain, will have their passports checked by Spanish police. Fabian Picardo, Gibraltar’s long-serving chief minister, calls the arrangement “fluidity”, rather than freedom of movement, since citizens of Schengen countries will not have an automatic right of residence in the territory. Gibraltar is also joining the EU’s customs union for goods. It has agreed to European standards and European taxation: having been a tax haven, it will now apply a transaction tax of 15%, rising to 17% by 2028 on goods sold in the territory. It will also set excise duties within six percentage points of Spain’s. The £10 whisky bottles in Gibraltar’s liquor stores will be no more.
paragraphs.zh: 根据这项经过五年多谈判、篇幅超过1000页的条约，直布罗陀已在事实上成为欧盟无边界申根区的一部分。非直布罗陀居民的旅客抵达其机场时，将由西班牙警察检查护照；该机场唯一的航班来自英国。长期担任直布罗陀首席部长的法比安·皮卡多把这一安排称为“流动便利”，而不是迁徙自由，因为申根国家公民不会自动获得在该领土居住的权利。直布罗陀还将加入欧盟货物关税同盟。它已同意采用欧洲标准和欧洲税制：过去作为避税天堂的直布罗陀，现在将对在该领土销售的商品征收15%的交易税，到2028年提高至17%。它还将把消费税设定在与西班牙相差不超过6个百分点的范围内。直布罗陀酒类商店里10英镑一瓶的威士忌将不复存在。

paragraphs.en: Spain has long resented Britain’s control of Gibraltar, acquired in 1713, and its naval base. The treaty parks the dispute over sovereignty in favour of co-operation. “Removing the border was essential for this region to prosper,” says Juan Franco, the mayor of La Línea. Although the area includes the port of Algeciras, across the bay from Gibraltar, it is poorer than most of Spain. The hope is that stability offered by the treaty will attract investment. The region’s poor communications are an obstacle, laments Jesús Verdú of the University of Cádiz. The single-track railway to Algeciras remains closed after landslides last winter. But Gibraltar’s airport might now attract flights from across Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 西班牙长期以来一直不满英国对直布罗陀及其海军基地的控制；英国是在1713年取得直布罗陀的。该条约将主权争端暂时搁置，转而支持合作。拉利内亚市长胡安·弗朗哥说：“取消边境对这个地区的繁荣至关重要。”尽管该地区包括位于直布罗陀海湾对岸的阿尔赫西拉斯港，但它比西班牙大多数地区更贫穷。人们希望，条约带来的稳定将吸引投资。加的斯大学的赫苏斯·贝尔杜感叹说，该地区糟糕的交通联系是一个障碍。通往阿尔赫西拉斯的单轨铁路在去年冬天发生山体滑坡后仍然关闭。但直布罗陀机场现在或许能吸引来自欧洲各地的航班。

paragraphs.en: Some among Spain’s conservative opposition dislike the treaty and think their country lost an opportunity to assert co-sovereignty. But past efforts to coerce Gibraltar, including General Franco’s closure of the border in 1969, only reinforced its separate identity as “Mediterranean British”, as locals say. If the Spanish right wins a general election due next year, as seems likely, “there might be a change in rhetoric, but that doesn’t have to mean a change on the ground”, says Mr Picardo. Importantly, some hope that as well as resolving unfinished Brexit business, the treaty might be a harbinger of a pragmatic reset between Britain and Europe. ■
paragraphs.zh: 西班牙保守派反对党中有些人不喜欢这项条约，认为本国错失了主张共同主权的机会。但过去胁迫直布罗陀的做法，包括佛朗哥将军在1969年关闭边境，只是强化了其作为“地中海英国人”的独立身份，当地人如是说。如果西班牙右翼在明年到期举行的大选中获胜，而这似乎很可能，“措辞上也许会有变化，但这并不一定意味着实地情况会改变”，皮卡多先生说。重要的是，一些人希望，这项条约不仅解决了英国脱欧遗留下来的未竟事务，还可能预示着英国与欧洲之间务实重启关系的到来。■

articles.title_en: Why Berlin’s airport is still completely hopeless
articles.title_zh: 为什么柏林机场仍然彻底没救
articles.rubric_en: Blame passengers, lobbying, history and geography
articles.rubric_zh: 要怪乘客、游说、历史和地理

paragraphs.en: What is it with Berlin and airports? First came Tempelhof, once a repository for Nazi megalomania, later the site of the Berlin airlift, an early cold-war drama, and now a vast and unusual city park. Then it was Tegel, a charming spot adored by locals but bursting at the seams by the end of its life. Finally came the tragicomedy of Berlin Brandenburg Airport, or ber. It opened in 2020, nine years late, €5bn over budget and a homage to Germany’s mishandling of megaprojects.
paragraphs.zh: 柏林和机场到底是怎么回事？最早是滕珀尔霍夫机场，它一度承载着纳粹的狂妄野心，后来成为柏林空运的地点，那是冷战早期的一场戏剧性事件，如今则是一座广阔而不同寻常的城市公园。接着是泰格尔机场，一个深受当地人喜爱的迷人地方，但到寿命末期已不堪重负。最后登场的是柏林勃兰登堡机场（ber）这出悲喜剧。它于2020年启用，比原计划晚了九年，超支50亿欧元，也成了德国处理大型项目失当的一座纪念碑。

paragraphs.en: That should have been that. But locals flocking to ber as the summer holidays began last week may still have felt short-changed. The airport currently has flights to just five long-haul destinations. Heathrow has around 100; even Warsaw has close to 20. Passengers flying beyond Europe must usually transfer elsewhere. “Berlin doesn’t have a capital-city airport,” said Tim Clark, president of Emirates, during a visit last month. “It’s a really sad situation.”
paragraphs.zh: 事情本该到此为止。但上周暑假开始时涌向ber的当地人，可能仍会觉得自己吃了亏。目前这座机场只有飞往五个长途目的地的航班。希思罗机场约有100个；就连华沙也接近20个。飞往欧洲以外地区的乘客通常必须在别处转机。“柏林没有一座首都机场，”阿联酋航空总裁蒂姆·克拉克上个月到访时说。“这真是令人难过的局面。”

paragraphs.en: Germany’s most populous city remains the only big European capital without a home-based long-haul airline. And attempts to entice the likes of Emirates to ber have been grounded by lobbying by Frankfurt-based Lufthansa, Germany’s biggest airline, which says its Gulf rivals are unfairly subsidised. Berlin’s champions regard this as protectionist tosh, but have lost the argument.
paragraphs.zh: 德国人口最多的城市，仍是欧洲唯一没有本地主基地长途航空公司的大首都。而试图吸引阿联酋航空等公司进驻ber的努力，也因总部位于法兰克福的汉莎航空游说而停飞。汉莎是德国最大的航空公司，它称自己的海湾地区竞争对手获得了不公平补贴。柏林的支持者认为这套说法是保护主义胡扯，但他们在这场争论中输了。

paragraphs.en: Worse, passenger numbers at ber last year were one-quarter down on the total at Berlin’s airports before the pandemic. Budget airlines have slashed flights. In April Ryanair said it would close its operating base at the airport, calling it “the worst-recovering major gateway in Europe”. Lufthansa has expanded its Frankfurt and Munich hubs, but hesitated in Berlin, blaming its fees and taxes. ber posts vast losses.
paragraphs.zh: 更糟的是，ber去年的旅客人数比疫情前柏林各机场的总数少了四分之一。廉价航空公司大幅削减了航班。4月，瑞安航空表示将关闭其在该机场的运营基地，并称其为“欧洲复苏最差的主要门户机场”。汉莎扩大了其法兰克福和慕尼黑枢纽，却在柏林犹豫不前，并将原因归咎于ber的费用和税收。ber录得巨额亏损。

paragraphs.en: Geography and history may simply have overwhelmed the city. German airlines were banned from flying to West Berlin during the cold war, letting Frankfurt and Munich become hubs. Germany’s industrial heartland is in its south; the densest population is in the west. Stranded far from other industrial or commercial centres, Berlin does not have the catchment area of its rivals and struggles to attract corporate custom. “When they moved the capital from Bonn to Berlin, they should have moved the airport from Frankfurt too“, jokes Cord Schellenberg, a consultant. That would have been a flight of fancy.■
paragraphs.zh: 地理和历史也许就是压垮这座城市的原因。冷战期间，德国航空公司被禁止飞往西柏林，这让法兰克福和慕尼黑得以发展成枢纽。德国的工业心脏地带在南部；人口最密集的地区在西部。柏林远离其他工业或商业中心，处境孤立，没有竞争对手那样的客源腹地，也难以吸引企业客户。“他们把首都从波恩迁到柏林时，也该把机场从法兰克福一起搬过来，”顾问科德·舍伦贝格开玩笑说。那本来就是异想天开。■

articles.title_en: In praise of Scandinavia’s risky and dirty playgrounds
articles.title_zh: 赞美斯堪的纳维亚那些有风险、脏兮兮的游乐场
articles.rubric_en: Kids stand to gain from a few bumps, bruises and mud-splatters
articles.rubric_zh: 孩子们会从几次磕碰、淤青和满身泥点中受益

paragraphs.en: A visitor to the playground at Valbyparken, a green expanse in Copenhagen, is in for three surprises. The first is that the sprawling public children’s park is built on what was once a rubbish tip for the Danish capital. The second is the free coffee on offer for harried parents. But perhaps the most unexpected is the eagerness of the playground’s staff to hand out knives to children. Or axes, if they prefer. Or even, for tots bored with mere weaponry, some fire-starting equipment. Off you go, kids, and do be careful (oh and parents please keep an eye on them). Sprogs of an age that in most countries would barely be allowed onto a swing unattended scamper away to whittle sticks and soon imagine themselves carving their own magic wand, or the oar of a Viking longship.
paragraphs.zh: 到访哥本哈根一片绿色开阔地瓦尔比公园游乐场的人，会遇到三件意外之事。第一，这座占地广阔的公共儿童公园建在丹麦首都昔日的一处垃圾填埋场上。第二，这里为焦头烂额的父母提供免费咖啡。但也许最出人意料的是，游乐场工作人员很乐意把刀发给孩子们。要是他们愿意，也可以给斧头。甚至，如果小不点们觉得单纯的“武器”已经无聊，还可以给他们一些生火设备。去吧，孩子们，小心点（哦，家长也请看着他们）。在大多数国家，这个年纪的孩子恐怕连无人看管地上秋千都未必被允许；可他们已经蹦蹦跳跳地跑开，去削木棍，并很快想象自己正在雕刻一根魔杖，或是一艘维京长船的船桨。

paragraphs.en: The knife-dispensing strategy may appear to some as an oversight that keeps the local hospital bustling with injured kids in summer. On the contrary. Injecting a dose of risk into children’s free-ranging fun is a feature, not a bug. The nature-based playground offers a litany of ways for children to get bruised, bumped, scratched and dirty. There are trees to climb, brambly branches to rearrange, tricycles to ride (often into each other), a play pond to stumble into, then trails to get lost in far from nosy parents. Adult chaperones may fret about lurking hazards, but their peace of mind is secondary. “It’s children who need to be aware of every step they take,” says Helle Nebelong, a former Copenhagen city official who designed the place at the turn of the century. “They must take responsibility for their own safety.” A cadre of young staff are there to catalyse play rather than stymie it.
paragraphs.zh: 这种发刀策略在一些人看来，或许像是一项疏忽，会让当地医院在夏天忙着接诊受伤的孩子。恰恰相反。给孩子自由撒欢的乐趣中注入一点风险，是功能，不是漏洞。这个以自然为本的游乐场，提供了一长串让孩子撞青、磕碰、擦伤、弄脏自己的方式。这里有树可爬，有带刺的枝条可搬来搬去，有三轮车可骑（还常常撞到彼此），有一个可供失足跌入的游玩池塘，还有远离爱管闲事的父母、可让人迷路的小径。成年陪同者也许会为潜伏的危险担忧，但他们的安心并不是首要事项。“需要留意自己每一步的是孩子，”海勒·内贝隆说。她曾是哥本哈根市政府官员，在本世纪初设计了这座游乐场。“他们必须为自己的安全负责。”一群年轻工作人员在那里，是为了催化玩耍，而不是阻碍玩耍。

paragraphs.en: Playgrounds the world over have done their utmost to eliminate risk. Why expose children to injuries, even minor ones, if they can be avoided? From Tokyo to Toronto, childhood play has thus become enveloped in well-meaning bubble-wrap. Any adult who has visited a play area they frequented as a child can see how anything that might cause so much as a grazed knee or a soiled skirt has been done away with. Asphalt and dirt have been replaced by vast expanses of rubberised carpets. Tree branches that might tempt intrepid youths have been cut down. In their stead, standardised equipment picked out of a catalogue—a few tame slides, some swings and a climbing frame—are erected. This cookie-cutter approach delights fretful municipal officials fearing lawsuits and criticism from health-and-safety bureaucrats. Alas, it bores kids to tears. No wonder they beg for screens instead.
paragraphs.zh: 世界各地的游乐场都竭尽全力消除风险。如果伤害可以避免，哪怕只是轻伤，为什么还要让孩子暴露在其中？于是，从东京到多伦多，童年游戏被善意的气泡膜层层包裹。任何一个成年人，只要去过自己小时候常玩的游乐区，就能看出凡是可能导致擦破膝盖或弄脏裙子的东西，都已经被清除。沥青和泥地被大片橡胶地垫取代。那些可能诱惑无畏少年的树枝被砍掉。取而代之的是从目录里挑出的标准化器械：几条温顺的滑梯、几个秋千和一个攀爬架。这种千篇一律的做法，让那些担心诉讼、害怕卫生安全官僚批评的焦虑市政官员很高兴。可惜，它让孩子们无聊到想哭。难怪他们转而央求要看屏幕。

paragraphs.en: Mariana Brussoni, a childhood expert at the University of British Columbia, decries “an epidemic of anxiety-based parenting” for the trend towards duller and safer play. In past generations families with a dozen kids had little choice but to let them roam free. These days smaller broods are the norm, and so more adult attention has been bestowed on fewer kids. This has led to an outbreak of “parenting” whereby children need to be cultivated rather than left to their own devices (no, not smartphones). In well-off circles, giving sprogs time and space to just be themselves or mess about with friends—ie play—is seen as an indulgence. Instead, they must be shuttled from school to ballet classes to fencing, before homework. What time is allocated to unstructured fun is mostly spent in playgrounds under watchful grown-ups’ eyes.
paragraphs.zh: 英属哥伦比亚大学的童年问题专家玛丽安娜·布鲁索尼谴责说，游戏变得更乏味、更安全的趋势，源于“一场以焦虑为基础的育儿流行病”。在过去几代人里，家里有十来个孩子时，父母别无选择，只能让他们自由乱跑。如今，较小的子女规模已成常态，于是更少的孩子得到了更多成年人的关注。这导致一种“育儿”现象泛滥：孩子需要被精心栽培，而不是被放手让他们自己去琢磨（不，不是让他们玩智能手机）。在富裕圈层里，给孩子时间和空间，让他们只是做自己，或和朋友瞎折腾，也就是玩耍，被视为一种纵容。相反，他们必须被接送着从学校去芭蕾课，再去击剑课，然后做作业。分配给无结构乐趣的那点时间，多半是在游乐场里、在成年人警惕的目光下度过的。

paragraphs.en: This avoids a few scratches and bumps along the way—but at what cost? “If you protect children too much, you rob them of experiences that will make them safer and happier,” says Ellen Beate Hansen Sandseter at Queen Maud University College in Trondheim, Norway. Kids are well equipped to know what daring swing or lunge would thrill them most, and to learn from their mistakes. Playground safety should be about the sturdiness of the equipment, not the way it comes to be used. As for a little dirt, what harm can it do? In fact, recent research from Finland shows wallowing in soil has wondrous effects on small immune systems.
paragraphs.zh: 这样一路上确实避免了一些擦伤和磕碰，可代价是什么？“如果你过度保护孩子，就剥夺了那些会让他们更安全、更快乐的经历，”挪威特隆赫姆莫德王后大学学院的埃伦·贝娅特·汉森·桑德塞特说。孩子们很有能力知道怎样大胆地荡秋千或猛冲最令他们兴奋，也能从错误中学习。游乐场安全应关乎设备是否坚固，而不是设备最终会被怎样使用。至于一点泥土，又能有什么害处？事实上，芬兰最近的研究显示，在泥土里打滚对小小免疫系统有奇妙效果。

paragraphs.en: Giving children this benefit of the doubt still prevails in Scandinavia. There, playgrounds are seen as a practice arena in which kids get “the tickle in their stomach” from taking risks, as Prof Sandseter puts it—whether it be riding a bike a bit too fast, climbing up to the next branch or playing with knives. Sometimes the approach boggles foreign minds. “Construction playgrounds” sprang up in Denmark after the second world war, with open-air areas filled with discarded junk that children could shape into multi-storey houses, with saws or nails or whatever they saw fit.
paragraphs.zh: 在斯堪的纳维亚，给孩子这种信任仍然占主流。在那里，游乐场被视为一个练习场，孩子们通过冒险获得桑德塞特教授所说的“肚子里痒痒的感觉”——无论是把自行车骑得稍快一点，爬到更高一根树枝上，还是玩刀。有时，这种做法会让外国人难以理解。第二次世界大战后，“建筑游乐场”在丹麦兴起，露天场地里堆满被丢弃的杂物，孩子们可以用锯子、钉子或任何他们认为合适的东西，把这些杂物搭成多层房屋。

paragraphs.en: Two traditions anchor a culture of Nordic risky play. The first is the ancestral habit of playing in nature—by its essence a place of untamed risks. But more recent social norms have a role, too. Stay-at-home parents are rare, leaving fewer mothers to interfere with kid-only time. It is frowned upon to ferry even the youngest to school if they can walk or cycle there themselves. Across Europe, but unlike America, there is no culture of litigation in case risky play results in a broken arm. Free health care means a broken bone is a nuisance, not a financial burden.
paragraphs.zh: 两种传统支撑着北欧风险游戏文化。第一是祖祖辈辈在自然中玩耍的习惯，而自然本质上就是一个充满未驯服风险的地方。但较近代的社会规范也起了作用。全职在家的父母很少，因此较少有母亲会干涉孩子们独处的时间。即使是最小的孩子，只要能自己走路或骑车上学，家长还接送他们去学校就会遭人侧目。在欧洲各地，与美国不同，如果冒险游戏导致手臂骨折，并没有诉讼文化。免费医疗意味着骨折是一件麻烦事，而不是经济负担。

paragraphs.en: Keen to explore these Scandinavian ways, The Economist secured the services of four playground afficionados—your columnist’s three boys and their cousin, aged eight to 12. Left to their own wits in Valbyparken while their father disappeared to interview Ms Nebelong, the knife-wielding gang soon secured equipment to cook flatbreads on an open-flame pit. Later in a construction playground across town they deftly navigated rusty nails and discarded furniture in search of new adventures (some sawing required). Might injuries have resulted, a pinched finger or a sprained ankle? Possibly. But the day offered an opportunity to learn about the real world, too, which these youngsters will one day need to navigate. “It beats the old swing, swing, slide, swing, swing playground,” opined the eldest among them. It might even beat a screen.■
paragraphs.zh: 为了探究这些斯堪的纳维亚做法，《经济学人》请来了四位游乐场爱好者——本专栏作者的三个儿子和他们的表兄弟，年龄在8岁到12岁之间。在瓦尔比公园，当他们的父亲消失去采访内贝隆女士时，这群持刀小队被留在那里自凭本事，很快就弄到了设备，在明火坑上烤扁面包。后来，在城另一头的一处建筑游乐场里，他们熟练地绕开生锈的钉子和被丢弃的家具，寻找新的冒险（其中需要锯一些东西）。会不会受伤，比如夹到手指或扭伤脚踝？有可能。但这一天也提供了一个了解真实世界的机会，而这些年轻人终有一天需要在真实世界中摸索前行。“这可比老一套的秋千、秋千、滑梯、秋千、秋千游乐场强多了，”他们中年纪最大的一个评论道。它甚至可能比屏幕还强。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订阅者可以订阅我们的 Opinion 新闻通讯，其中汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。

articles.title_en: Can Andy Burnham keep his own MPs under control?
articles.title_zh: 安迪·伯纳姆能管住自己的议员吗？
articles.rubric_en: Britain’s prime-minister-to-be faces a big task to maintain his authority
articles.rubric_zh: 这位英国准首相面临一项艰巨任务：维持自己的权威

paragraphs.en: “I keep the troops in line. I put a bit of stick about. I make ’em jump.” Francis Urquhart, anti-hero of the 1990s BBC thriller “House of Cards”, embodies the popular perception of Westminster “whipping” as a brutal way for governments to force their will on MPs. In the series he rises from chief whip to prime minister through lies, manipulation and, eventually, murder.
paragraphs.zh: “我让部队守规矩。我会动点硬手段。我让他们紧张起来。”弗朗西斯·厄克特是1990年代BBC惊悚剧《纸牌屋》的反英雄人物，他体现了公众对威斯敏斯特“党鞭”制度的印象：政府用一种粗暴方式把自己的意志强加给议员。在剧中，他通过谎言、操纵，最终甚至谋杀，从首席党鞭一路爬到首相之位。

paragraphs.en: The more pedestrian reality is that the whips use a mix of cajoling and threats to keep party discipline. But during his time as prime minister, Sir Keir Starmer gave the impression that he sympathised with the Urquhart method. Members of the whips’ office showed little tolerance of dissent; 13 backbenchers were ejected from the parliamentary party, at least temporarily, because they opposed Sir Keir’s policies.
paragraphs.zh: 更平淡的现实是，党鞭们会把劝说和威胁混合使用，以维持党内纪律。但基尔·斯塔默爵士担任首相期间给人的印象是，他同情厄克特式做法。党鞭办公室的成员几乎不能容忍异议；有13名后座议员因为反对基尔爵士的政策，被逐出议会党团，至少是暂时如此。

paragraphs.en: It did not work. For all his policy failures and unpopularity with voters, the reason Sir Keir is leaving office on July 20th is that he lost the confidence of his own MPs. The enormous majority he won in the 2024 general election was largely squandered: his attempts to force through contentious policies, such as cuts to benefits, through coercion rather than persuasion resulted in embarrassing climbdowns.
paragraphs.zh: 这并没有奏效。尽管他的政策失败、在选民中也不受欢迎，但基尔爵士将于7月20日离任的原因，是他失去了自己党内议员的信任。他在2024年大选中赢得的巨大多数优势基本被挥霍掉了：他试图通过胁迫而非说服来强推有争议的政策，例如削减福利，结果只能尴尬让步。

paragraphs.en: Andy Burnham, who will replace Sir Keir as prime minister, has promised to take a different approach. In the run-up to the Labour leadership contest, in which he was the only candidate, he was coy about his policy plans but open about the need to make MPs feel better about themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 将接替基尔爵士出任首相的安迪·伯纳姆承诺采取不同做法。在工党党魁竞选前夕，他是唯一候选人；他对自己的政策计划讳莫如深，却坦率表示有必要让议员们对自己感觉更好。

paragraphs.en: He has retreated from his most radical notion: in a book published in 2024, he suggested that whipping should be scrapped altogether because it “makes good people seem like frauds”. It was a recipe for disaster. “Abolish the whip and the Commons would fall apart pretty quickly,” says Sebastian Whale, the author of a book on whipping. Not only do most MPs appreciate being told what their party line is on complex pieces of legislation, but the whips are a crucial conduit between ministers, backbenchers and opposition parties.
paragraphs.zh: 他已经从自己最激进的想法上后退：在2024年出版的一本书中，他曾建议彻底废除党鞭制度，因为它“让好人看起来像骗子”。那会是一剂灾难药方。“废除党鞭，下议院很快就会乱套，”一本党鞭制度著作的作者塞巴斯蒂安·惠尔说。多数议员不仅乐于有人告诉他们，本党在复杂立法上的路线是什么；党鞭还是部长、后座议员和反对党之间至关重要的沟通渠道。

paragraphs.en: Mr Burnham’s message to MPs now is vaguer, but more realistic. He has sworn off “using the whip system to create fear or close down debate”. Instead, the whips’ office will be Labour’s “HR department”. And the prime-minister-to-be says that he and the rest of the cabinet will regularly attend votes in the Commons so that colleagues can chat to them in person, a stark contrast to Sir Keir, who cast a vote on less than 7% of the occasions he could have done.
paragraphs.zh: 伯纳姆如今给议员们传递的信息更模糊，但也更现实。他发誓不再“利用党鞭制度制造恐惧或压制辩论”。相反，党鞭办公室将成为工党的“人力资源部门”。这位准首相还说，他和内阁其他成员会定期参加下议院投票，让同僚能当面同他们交谈；这与基尔爵士形成鲜明对比，后者在本可以投票的场合中，实际投票比例不到7%。

paragraphs.en: Turning up to work and being nice is not enough. It is not clear that Mr Burnham has fully reckoned with the challenges he will face as he tries to establish his authority. The first is a culture of chaos which has taken hold of Westminster over the past decade: the current government has suffered rebellions in more Commons votes than any other newly elected administration since the second world war (apart from the two-party coalition which took power in 2010), according to an analysis by Philip Cowley of Queen Mary University of London. The churn of prime ministers, and policies, has taught MPs that if they hold out they may get their way.
paragraphs.zh: 上班露面、态度友善并不够。伯纳姆是否已充分认识到自己在树立权威时将面临的挑战，目前并不清楚。第一个挑战，是过去十年间在威斯敏斯特扎根的一种混乱文化：伦敦玛丽女王大学的菲利普·考利分析称，现政府在下议院投票中遭遇议员反叛的次数，超过二战以来任何其他新当选政府，2010年上台的两党联合政府除外。首相和政策不断更替，让议员们学会了：只要坚持不让步，他们也许就能如愿。

paragraphs.en: Another difficulty will be dealing with those who are disappointed in their hopes of advancement. For now, Labour MPs are united in their acclaim for Mr Burnham, even if they were not previously fans; more than nine out of ten of them have signed his nomination papers and none can be found to utter a word of public criticism. That will surely change once he has appointed his ministerial team, with sacked Starmer loyalists joining the backbenches alongside those who were passed over for a job. Simon Hart, who was chief whip in the last Conservative government, also warns that as the next general election—due by summer 2029—approaches, it will become harder to keep colleagues in line with vague promises of future promotion.
paragraphs.zh: 另一个困难，是如何应对那些晋升希望落空的人。眼下，工党议员一致称赞伯纳姆，即使他们此前并不是他的拥护者；超过九成议员已在他的提名文件上签名，而且找不到任何人公开批评一句。但一旦他任命了部长团队，这种局面肯定会改变：被免职的斯塔默忠臣将与那些没有获得职位的人一起坐到后座。上一届保守党政府的首席党鞭西蒙·哈特还警告说，随着下一次大选临近，最迟须在2029年夏季前举行，用未来晋升的模糊承诺来约束同僚将变得更加困难。

paragraphs.en: Labour MPs will inevitably divide over some of the tricky dilemmas which confront Mr Burnham. His supporters range from left-wing Corbynites to Blairite centrists; he has sought to persuade them all that he shares their instincts, but that cannot last. Looming problems both big (welfare spending, defence) and relatively small (a plan to reduce the number of jury trials, the future of North Sea oil) will leave one side or the other unhappy.
paragraphs.zh: 面对伯纳姆的一些棘手难题，工党议员不可避免会产生分歧。他的支持者从左翼的科尔宾派到布莱尔派中间派都有；他一直试图说服所有人相信，他与他们的本能立场一致，但这种状态不可能持久。迫在眉睫的问题有大的，例如福利支出、国防，也有相对小的，例如减少陪审团审判数量的计划、北海石油的未来，这些都会让某一方不满。

paragraphs.en: The combination of these factors—a rotten political culture, individual ambition and knotty policy problems—has created a parliamentary Labour Party which is allergic to compromise. Sir Keir sought to mould an army of “Starmtroopers” on the backbenches who would support his every move, but instead there are dozens of MPs who won their seats by an unusually small margin, fear defeat next time and are unwilling to weather tough decisions (see chart). “The same tensions that were difficult for Keir are going to be re-emerging,” predicts a serving minister.
paragraphs.zh: 这些因素结合在一起，即腐坏的政治文化、个人野心和棘手的政策问题，造就了一个对妥协过敏的议会工党。基尔爵士曾试图在后座塑造一支“斯塔默冲锋队”，支持他的每一个举动，但现实却是，有几十名议员以异常微弱的优势赢得席位，他们担心下次落败，也不愿承受艰难决定带来的压力（见图表）。“那些让基尔感到棘手的同样紧张关系将会重新浮现，”一名现任部长预测。

paragraphs.en: Luckily, there are reasons to think that the next prime minister has a good shot at being more successful than the last. He has in spades the personal skills that Sir Keir lacked: even some ministers complain that Sir Keir never had a single substantial conversation with them, whereas Mr Burnham has spent the past few weeks schmoozing MPs. He understands the value of gesture in politics, for example telling the Guardian that the party’s Middle East stance had been insufficiently pro-Palestine, without promising any changes to the policy. As mayor of Greater Manchester, he worked cross-party and with businesses in a way Sir Keir has struggled to do.
paragraphs.zh: 幸运的是，有理由认为下一任首相比上一任更有成功机会。他拥有基尔爵士所缺乏的丰富个人技巧：甚至有些部长抱怨说，基尔爵士从未与他们有过一次实质性的谈话，而伯纳姆过去几周一直在同议员们套近乎。他懂得政治姿态的价值，例如告诉《卫报》，该党在中东问题上的立场还不够亲巴勒斯坦，但又没有承诺对政策作出任何改变。作为大曼彻斯特市长，他能够跨党派并与企业合作，而这正是基尔爵士一直难以做到的。

paragraphs.en: The best way to create loyal followers is to be a strong leader. Mr Burnham is showing some signs of that: his team insist that while he intends to listen to MPs and involve them in his policy plans, he will not cave at every sign of rebellion—for example, he will press ahead with a crackdown on immigration which dozens of backbenchers oppose. But he has little experience of being disliked, and there is a risk he soon crumbles in the face of dissent. “They’ve got MPs and most of the media crawling all over them, saying they’re brilliant,” says a member of Sir Keir’s inner circle. “It’s going to be a big shock when that suddenly goes into reverse.” ■
paragraphs.zh: 培养忠诚追随者的最佳方式，是成为一名强势领导人。伯纳姆正表现出一些这方面迹象：他的团队坚称，虽然他打算倾听议员意见，并让他们参与自己的政策计划，但他不会一有反叛迹象就退让，例如，他将推进打击移民的行动，尽管有几十名后座议员反对。但他几乎没有不受欢迎的经验，而且存在一种风险：面对异议时，他很快就会垮下来。“他们现在有议员和大多数媒体围着他们转，说他们很了不起，”基尔爵士核心圈子的一名成员说。“一旦这种局面突然反转，对他们会是很大的冲击。” ■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 想阅读更多关于英国重大新闻的专家分析，请订阅Blighty，我们面向订阅者的每周新闻通讯。

articles.title_en: Lessons from a cat about to outlast yet another prime minister
articles.title_zh: 一只猫即将又熬走一位首相，它给人的启示
articles.rubric_en: Number 10’s canny feline can teach Andy Burnham a thing or two
articles.rubric_zh: 唐宁街10号这只精明的猫，能教安迪·伯纳姆一两招

paragraphs.en: Around Westminster he is known as self-serving and territorial. “A little shit”, is how one minister has described him. Larry the cat, “Chief Mouser to the Cabinet Office” (to give him his official title), has been in post for 15 years and is on his sixth prime minister. As he prepares to turn his bottom to his seventh, Larry has lessons to offer his new housemate.
paragraphs.zh: 在威斯敏斯特一带，它以自利、地盘意识强而闻名。“一个小混蛋”，一位大臣曾这样形容它。拉里这只猫，“内阁办公厅首席捕鼠官”（这是它的正式头衔），已经在任15年，经历了六任首相。眼看它又要把屁股转向第七位首相，拉里有几条经验可以传授给这位新室友。

paragraphs.en: Start with a good backstory. Like many an effective political operator, Larry came from the wrong side of the tracks. His redemption began when he was selected for office from Battersea Dogs and Cats Home during David Cameron’s tenure as prime minister; he was the first to get the title he now holds.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，要有一个漂亮的背景故事。和许多高明的政治操盘手一样，拉里出身并不光鲜。它的逆袭始于戴维·卡梅伦担任首相期间，当时它从巴特西猫狗之家被选中上任；它也是第一个获得如今这个头衔的猫。

paragraphs.en: Next, either do the job well or maintain the perception that you are. Larry is streetwise and known as “a bit of a bruiser”. But his mousing instincts are much debated. Media stationed outside the famous black door have confirmed two recent kills. Behind closed doors there is scepticism. His official government page notes his solution to the rodent problem remains in the “tactical planning stage” and that he often tests “antique furniture for napping quality”.
paragraphs.zh: 其次，要么把工作干好，要么维持住你干得很好的印象。拉里很懂街头生存之道，还以“有点好斗”著称。但它捕鼠的本能一直颇有争议。驻守在那扇著名黑门外的媒体已证实它最近有两次捕杀记录。可在闭门之后，人们对此仍有怀疑。英国政府官网上关于它的页面写道，它解决鼠患的方案仍处于“战术规划阶段”，而且它经常测试“古董家具是否适合打盹”。

paragraphs.en: Third, be media-savvy. Larry keeps journalists at tail’s length, slinking away from any who approach. But he knows how to exploit media opportunities, having photo-bombed world leaders like Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine. In 2019 he stole headlines by sheltering from rain under “The Beast”, Donald Trump’s armoured limousine.
paragraphs.zh: 第三，要懂媒体。拉里与记者保持一尾之距，凡是有人靠近，它就溜走。但它知道如何利用媒体机会，曾抢镜多国领导人，比如乌克兰总统弗拉基米尔·泽连斯基。2019年，它躲在唐纳德·特朗普的装甲豪华轿车“野兽”下面避雨，抢走了新闻头条。

paragraphs.en: Finally, suffer no challengers. A long-running feud with Palmerston, the former chief mouser at the Foreign Office, culminated in a fight. Larry reportedly lost a collar; Palmerston hurt his ear.
paragraphs.zh: 最后，不容挑战者。拉里与外交部前首席捕鼠官帕默斯顿长期不和，最终以一场打斗收场。据报道，拉里丢了一只项圈；帕默斯顿伤了耳朵。

paragraphs.en: Such canny political manoeuvring has blessed Larry with public-approval ratings that most politicians would sell their cat for. He has over 900,000 followers on his unofficial X account and is frequently sent gifts from fans.
paragraphs.zh: 这种精明的政治腾挪，让拉里拥有了多数政客愿意“卖猫求得”的公众支持率。它的非官方X账号拥有超过90万名关注者，也经常收到粉丝寄来的礼物。

paragraphs.en: But the whiskers are greying. In cat years, Larry is about 92 (Winston Churchill was 80 when he left office). Unlike many British leaders, the chief mouser looks set to exit on his own terms.■
paragraphs.zh: 不过，它的胡须已经灰白。按猫的年龄算，拉里大约92岁了（温斯顿·丘吉尔离任时80岁）。与许多英国领导人不同，这位首席捕鼠官看来将按自己的方式退场。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.

articles.title_en: Glasgow, rescuer of the Commonwealth Games, needs saving too
articles.title_zh: 格拉斯哥拯救了英联邦运动会，自己也需要被拯救
articles.rubric_en: The Scottish Nationalists are keen to take power from London but not to hand any to Glasgow
articles.rubric_zh: 苏格兰民族党人热衷于从伦敦夺权，却不愿把任何权力交给格拉斯哥

paragraphs.en: When the event that stands as a living legacy of Britain’s imperial past was on its knees, it was the place once known as the empire’s second city that came to the rescue. The 2026 Commonwealth Games, a sporting jamboree of the countries that once made up the empire (plus some others), found itself without a host after the Australian state of Victoria backed out because of soaring costs. Glasgow, the host city in 2014, saved the day.
paragraphs.zh: 当这个作为英国帝国往昔鲜活遗产的赛事陷入绝境时，出手相救的是那座曾被称为“帝国第二城”的地方。2026年英联邦运动会是一场由曾经组成帝国的国家（另加一些其他国家）参加的体育盛会；在澳大利亚维多利亚州因成本飙升退出后，它一度找不到主办方。2014年的主办城市格拉斯哥救了场。

paragraphs.en: But after these slimmed-down games, which open on July 23rd, Glasgow itself will need saving. It looks tired. The pink livery on a tower block welcoming people to the 2014 games has faded. Roadworks clog the streets. Blocks of flats lie half-built after investors pulled out amid rent-control fears. The international airport still lacks a rail link to the centre. In March the city lost a listed Victorian building in a blaze that started in a vape shop.
paragraphs.zh: 但在这届精简版运动会于7月23日开幕后，格拉斯哥自身也需要被拯救。它显得疲惫不堪。一栋塔楼上欢迎人们参加2014年运动会的粉色标识已经褪色。道路施工堵塞街道。由于投资者担心租金管制而撤出，一些公寓楼半途停工。国际机场仍然没有通往市中心的铁路连接。今年3月，该市一栋列入保护名录的维多利亚时代建筑在一场从电子烟商店起火的大火中被毁。

paragraphs.en: The challenges facing a post-industrial city such as Glasgow are not unique. Others are coping better. Manchester has trams and a large chunk of the BBC. The average cost of a home there has grown twice as fast as in Glasgow since 2004.
paragraphs.zh: 像格拉斯哥这样的后工业城市所面临的挑战并非独有。其他城市应对得更好。曼彻斯特有有轨电车，还有英国广播公司（BBC）的一大部分业务。自2004年以来，那里的平均房价涨幅是格拉斯哥的两倍。

paragraphs.en: One big reason is that, since 2017, Manchester has had a mayor, who can approve big transport projects, levy small taxes and oversee ambitious developments. Glasgow does not. As mayor of Greater Manchester Andy Burnham, soon to be Britain’s prime minister, took control of the city’s buses. By contrast, Scotland’s local-transport authorities have to get approval from a panel convened by an official appointed by the transport secretary in Whitehall if they want to franchise theirs—a requirement introduced by the supposedly pro-independence Scottish National Party (SNP).
paragraphs.zh: 一个重要原因是，自2017年以来，曼彻斯特有了市长，可以批准大型交通项目、征收小额税费，并监督雄心勃勃的开发项目。格拉斯哥没有。大曼彻斯特市长安迪·伯纳姆即将成为英国首相，他在任市长期间接管了该市的公交车。相比之下，苏格兰的地方交通主管机构如果想把本地公交纳入特许经营，就必须获得一个小组的批准；该小组由白厅交通大臣任命的一名官员召集。这个要求是由号称支持独立的苏格兰民族党（SNP）引入的。

paragraphs.en: It was not always like this. From 1975 to 1996, Glasgow and its surrounding area were run by a juggernaut called the Strathclyde Regional Council. It presided over half of Scotland’s population and employed 85,000 workers. It oversaw water, transport, policing, education and roads. It modernised the Glasgow Subway—the one metro system in the world that has never been expanded beyond its original route.
paragraphs.zh: 情况并非一直如此。1975年至1996年，格拉斯哥及其周边地区由一个名为斯特拉斯克莱德地区委员会的庞然大物管理。它管辖着苏格兰一半的人口，雇用8.5万名工作人员。它负责供水、交通、治安、教育和道路。它对格拉斯哥地铁进行了现代化改造；这是世界上唯一一个从未在原始线路之外扩建过的地铁系统。

paragraphs.en: But this monster of local government was seen as a threat. Britain’s Conservative government killed it in 1996 and (devolution be damned) the SNP has had no desire to bring it back to life. Strathclyde’s police and fire services were folded into Scotland-wide bodies in 2012. Its water company is now Scottish Water. “Glasgow has always been too big for Scotland,” says a (Labour) member of the Scottish Parliament.
paragraphs.zh: 但这个地方政府巨兽被视为一种威胁。英国保守党政府于1996年将其废除，而苏格兰民族党（权力下放见鬼去吧）也毫无让它复活的意愿。斯特拉斯克莱德的警察和消防服务于2012年并入全苏格兰机构。它的自来水公司如今是苏格兰水务公司。“格拉斯哥对苏格兰来说一直都太大了，”一名（工党）苏格兰议会议员说。

paragraphs.en: Glasgow has ample strengths. These include several universities, museums, great architecture and a big graduate population. Run properly, it could thrive.
paragraphs.zh: 格拉斯哥有充足的优势。其中包括多所大学、博物馆、出色的建筑，以及庞大的毕业生群体。如果治理得当，它本可以繁荣起来。

paragraphs.en: A mayor would not be a panacea. “Devolution on its own”, says David Phillips of the Institute for Fiscal Studies, a think-tank, “doesn’t create more money.” The Birmingham region got a mayor in 2017, and its GDP per head grew more slowly than Glasgow’s in the next five years.
paragraphs.zh: 市长并不是万能药。智库财政研究所的戴维·菲利普斯说：“权力下放本身并不会创造更多资金。”伯明翰地区在2017年有了市长，但此后五年，其人均GDP增长速度低于格拉斯哥。

paragraphs.en: But having a go-to person would surely help. Bradley Mitchell, a Glaswegian developer, says he’s banging his head against the wall trying to get permission to launch a ferry carrying passengers down the Clyde because the city council is taking so long to reach a verdict. “You can’t find out who makes the decision,” he moans. With a mayor, he says, things would be far easier.
paragraphs.zh: 但有一个明确的负责人肯定会有帮助。格拉斯哥开发商布拉德利·米切尔说，他想获准开通一条沿克莱德河载客的渡轮，却因为市议会迟迟不作决定而碰得头破血流。“你根本弄不清是谁在做决定，”他抱怨道。他说，如果有市长，事情会容易得多。

paragraphs.en: That is unlikely to come soon. Mr Burnham supports the idea of an elected mayor, but Scotland’s SNP first minister, John Swinney, does not. The party that pioneered devolution of power in the last century is intent on holding on to it now. ■
paragraphs.zh: 这不太可能很快实现。伯纳姆支持设立民选市长的想法，但苏格兰民族党籍苏格兰首席部长约翰·斯温尼并不支持。这个在上世纪率先推动权力下放的政党，如今却一心要把权力攥在自己手里。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需阅读更多关于英国重大新闻的专家分析，请订阅《Blighty》，这是我们每周一期、仅面向订户的新闻简报。

articles.title_en: Violence is reshaping British political life
articles.title_zh: 暴力正在重塑英国政治生活
articles.rubric_en: The shocking murder of Ann Widdecombe will only reinforce the trend
articles.rubric_zh: 安·威德科姆遇害的震惊事件只会强化这一趋势

paragraphs.en: IT is often said after an atrocity that terrorists will not prevail. Those who practise politics by peaceful means, the mantra goes, will triumph over those who choose violence. If only it were true. The bleaker reality is that, in Britain, the threat of violence against parliamentarians is having the effects that at least some of its perpetrators desire: driving people from public life and shrinking the sphere of debate.
paragraphs.zh: 每当暴行发生后，人们常说恐怖分子不会得逞。那套常见说法是，通过和平方式从事政治的人，终将战胜选择暴力的人。要是真的如此就好了。更阴暗的现实是，在英国，针对议员的暴力威胁正在产生至少部分施害者想要的效果：把人赶出公共生活，并压缩辩论的空间。

paragraphs.en: More than a quarter of candidates at the last general election in 2024 said they had considered not standing because of concerns about harassment and intimidation, according to a survey of candidates by the parliamentary authorities. Some 58% said they had been physically attacked or touched against their will; 77% had suffered intimidating acts such as damage to property or being followed in the street. A quarter said it led them to hesitate before making decisions; more than two in five said they avoid getting involved in some policy debates altogether.
paragraphs.zh: 据议会当局对候选人进行的一项调查，在2024年上一次大选中，超过四分之一的候选人表示，由于担心骚扰和恐吓，他们曾考虑不参选。约58%的人说自己曾遭到人身攻击或被违背意愿地触碰；77%的人遭遇过恐吓行为，例如财产受损或在街上被跟踪。四分之一的人说，这导致他们在作出决定前会犹豫；超过五分之二的人说，他们干脆避免参与某些政策辩论。

paragraphs.en: Those figures will surely rise. On July 9th Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former Tory MP who became a member of the populist-right Reform UK, was found dead in her remote bungalow in Devon. Police later concluded that she had been murdered in a “targeted attack”. A 28-year-old white British man from Rotherham, 430km away, has been arrested on suspicion of terrorism offences. The motive is unclear.
paragraphs.zh: 这些数字几乎肯定会上升。7月9日，78岁的前保守党议员安·威德科姆被发现死在她位于德文郡偏僻地区的平房里。她后来加入了民粹右翼政党英国改革党。警方随后认定，她是在一次“有针对性的袭击”中被谋杀的。一名来自430公里外罗瑟勒姆的28岁英国白人男子，因涉嫌恐怖主义罪行被捕。作案动机尚不清楚。

paragraphs.en: Ms Widdecombe was the third prominent politician in Britain to be murdered in little over a decade. Jo Cox, a Labour MP, was killed in the street by a far-right extremist in 2016. David Amess, a Conservative lawmaker, was stabbed at a constituency surgery in 2021 by a radical Islamist.
paragraphs.zh: 威德科姆是十多年里英国第三位遭谋杀的知名政治人物。2016年，工党议员乔·考克斯在街头被一名极右翼极端分子杀害。2021年，保守党议员戴维·埃默斯在一次选区接待选民活动中被一名激进伊斯兰主义者刺死。

paragraphs.en: The phenomenon is not new. From the 1970s to the 1990s, Irish republicans targeted mostly senior Conservatives, killing four MPs and narrowly missing the entire cabinet. Yet such acts at least were governed by a degree of organisation and strategy that gave the security services a chance to predict and disrupt them.
paragraphs.zh: 这种现象并不新鲜。从20世纪70年代到90年代，爱尔兰共和派主要把资深保守党人作为目标，杀害了四名议员，并险些杀死整个内阁。然而，这类行动至少还受到一定组织和战略的约束，使安全部门有机会预测并破坏它们。

paragraphs.en: Today’s threat is diffuse and unpredictable, the work of individuals rather than groups. Ms Widdecombe seems an improbable target: a Conservative minister in the 1990s, she left Parliament in 2010. When not on “Strictly Come Dancing”, a reality-TV show, or giving interviews about her garden, she spoke on immigration and justice for Reform. Amess’s killer had researched over 250 MPs before attacking the mild-mannered backbencher. Cox had been in Parliament little over a year before she was killed. If they could be targets, so could almost anyone in public life.
paragraphs.zh: 如今的威胁则分散且难以预测，更多是个人所为，而非团体行动。威德科姆似乎是个不太可能的目标：她曾在20世纪90年代担任保守党大臣，2010年离开议会。除了参加真人秀节目《舞动奇迹》，或接受关于她花园的采访外，她还会代表改革党就移民和司法问题发声。杀害埃默斯的人在袭击这位性情温和的后座议员前，曾研究过250多名议员。考克斯遇害时，进入议会才一年多。如果他们都可能成为目标，那么公共生活中的几乎任何人也都可能成为目标。

paragraphs.en: MPs feel under siege. It is impossible to tell the difference between unpleasant cranks and potential murderers. Penny Mordaunt, a former Tory minister, said this week that she had received more than 700 threats to kill or rape her; there hadn’t been a day for four years without a live police investigation or court case into her pursuers.
paragraphs.zh: 议员们感到自己处于围困之中。令人讨厌的怪人和潜在杀人犯之间根本无法区分。保守党前大臣彭妮·莫当特本周表示，她收到过700多次威胁，要杀死她或强奸她；过去四年里，没有一天没有针对跟踪骚扰她的人进行中的警方调查或法庭案件。

paragraphs.en: Protecting MPs has become a significant operation. The task is split between the Parliamentary Security Department and the Metropolitan Police’s Parliamentary Liaison and Investigation Team, which investigates crimes against lawmakers and co-ordinates with provincial police forces. A specialist Security Information and Risk Analysis Service weighs intelligence. MPs’ homes and constituency offices are routinely fitted with arson-proof letter boxes and panic rooms.
paragraphs.zh: 保护议员已经成为一项庞大的行动。这项任务由议会安全部和伦敦警察厅的议会联络与调查小组分担，后者负责调查针对议员的犯罪，并与地方警察部队协调。一个专门的安全信息与风险分析服务机构负责评估情报。议员的住所和选区办公室通常会安装防纵火信箱和安全屋。

paragraphs.en: The question of what protection MPs are entitled to has become bitterly partisan. On July 14th a man was arrested for threatening on social media to shoot Nigel Farage, Reform’s leader. “I am going to shoot you in the head if you win,” he wrote. Reform accuses the police and government of purposefully denying its leaders adequate security for political reasons, a charge the authorities deny. It has an interest in having such a row: the party claimed that a previously undeclared £5m ($6.7m) donation to Mr Farage, now subject to intense scrutiny, was for the purpose of private security arrangements.
paragraphs.zh: 议员有权获得何种保护，这个问题已经变得党派对立尖锐。7月14日，一名男子因在社交媒体上威胁要枪击改革党领袖奈杰尔·法拉奇而被捕。他写道：“如果你赢了，我就朝你头部开枪。”改革党指责警方和政府出于政治原因，故意不给其领导人提供足够的安保；当局否认这一指控。改革党有意挑起这样的争吵：该党声称，此前未申报的一笔给法拉奇的500万英镑（670万美元）捐款，是用于私人安保安排的，而这笔捐款如今正受到严密审查。

paragraphs.en: It is often said, after an attack, that violence will not change a country’s way of life. But the fear of violence is now woven deeply into the fabric of British politics. ■
paragraphs.zh: 袭击发生后，人们常说，暴力不会改变一个国家的生活方式。但如今，对暴力的恐惧已经深深织入英国政治的肌理之中。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需阅读更多关于英国重大新闻的专家分析，请订阅Blighty，我们每周一期、仅面向订阅者的新闻简报。

articles.title_en: Britain’s bookies have weathered a tricky World Cup
articles.title_zh: 英国博彩公司挺过了一届棘手的世界杯
articles.rubric_en: But bigger problems are stacking up
articles.rubric_zh: 但更大的问题正在越积越多

paragraphs.en: “We were lucky today,” said England’s manager, Thomas Tuchel, after his team’s nerve-shredding 2-1 win over Norway on July 11th. Britain’s bookmakers enjoyed their own good fortune: England reached the semi-finals of the World Cup, but for betting purposes the match was a draw. By convention, match wagers are settled on the 90-minute scoreline (1-1). England’s winning goal came in extra time, saving the bookies millions of pounds.
paragraphs.zh: 7月11日，英格兰队以2比1惊险战胜挪威队后，主教练托马斯·图赫尔说：“我们今天很幸运。”英国博彩公司也有自己的好运：英格兰进入了世界杯半决赛，但从投注结算的角度看，这场比赛算作平局。按照惯例，比赛投注按90分钟比分结算（1比1）。英格兰的制胜球是在加时赛打进的，为博彩公司省下了数百万英镑。

paragraphs.en: Across this expanded 48-team World Cup, favourites have won 63% of matches in normal time, compared with 53% on average in the previous three. Underdogs prevailed in only 10%, against an average of 23% previously. Such results would once have perturbed British bookies, which do well when favourites don’t.
paragraphs.zh: 在这届扩军至48支球队的世界杯中，热门球队在常规时间内赢下了63%的比赛，而此前三届的平均比例为53%。冷门球队获胜的比例只有10%，此前平均为23%。这样的结果放在过去会让英国博彩公司不安，因为热门球队不赢时，它们才更赚钱。

paragraphs.en: Not this time. Bookies have steered many customers towards “bet builders” (or “same-game parlays” in America). At Kambi, a sportsbook supplier, these made up more than 30% of bets at recent football tournaments. Punters might bet on, say, England to win, Jude Bellingham to score, and the match to have over nine corners and more than two bookings, all in one wager. In the Norway game, only one of those outcomes occurred in regular time. Such bets help the bookies’ profits. Trading data shared with The Economist by four of them suggest that, despite match results generally favouring customers, all remain broadly on course to achieve their usual margins.
paragraphs.zh: 但这一次不是这样。博彩公司已把许多客户引向“串关组合投注”（在美国称为“同场串关”）。体育博彩供应商Kambi的数据显示，在最近几届足球锦标赛中，这类投注占比超过30%。赌客可能会把英格兰获胜、裘德·贝林厄姆进球、全场角球超过9个、黄牌等警告超过2次等选项放在同一注里。挪威那场比赛中，这些结果在常规时间内只发生了一个。这类投注有助于提高博彩公司利润。四家博彩公司向《经济学人》分享的交易数据显示，尽管比赛结果总体上有利于客户，但它们大体上仍有望实现通常的利润率。

paragraphs.en: Yet a decent World Cup will not dispel the industry’s broader worries. Tax and licensing reforms in the early 2000s were intended to allow gambling to flourish in the internet age. They succeeded, until the political wind changed. Last year’s budget raised the tax on online casino-style profits from 21% to 40%. From April 2027 most online sports betting will also face a tax increase, from 15% to 25%.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，一届还不错的世界杯并不能消除该行业更广泛的忧虑。21世纪初的税收和牌照改革，原本是为了让博彩业在互联网时代蓬勃发展。它们确实奏效了，直到政治风向发生变化。去年的预算把在线赌场类利润的税率从21%提高到40%。从2027年4月起，大多数在线体育博彩也将面临增税，税率从15%升至25%。

paragraphs.en: And the Gambling Commission, the industry regulator, has pressed ahead with “financial risk assessments”, intended to identify customers at risk of gambling harm. Bookies argue that the checks are intrusive and are driving customers to unlicensed operators. Anthony Kaminskas, founder of AK Bets, says 78% of his customers refuse to provide financial records when asked.
paragraphs.zh: 同时，行业监管机构赌博委员会推进了“财务风险评估”，旨在识别有赌博伤害风险的客户。博彩公司认为，这些审查侵犯隐私，并正在把客户推向无牌经营者。AK Bets创始人安东尼·卡明斯卡斯说，他的客户中有78%在被要求提供财务记录时拒绝配合。

paragraphs.en: Gambling’s new growth story is America. Flutter, the world’s largest online-betting company, and owner of British brands including Betfair and Sky Bet, will delist from the London Stock Exchange next month, making New York its sole stock-market home. Britain’s bookies have cracked the problem of predictable football results. Their bigger challenge lies in Westminster. ■
paragraphs.zh: 博彩业新的增长故事在美国。全球最大的在线博彩公司Flutter拥有Betfair和Sky Bet等英国品牌，它将于下个月从伦敦证券交易所退市，使纽约成为其唯一的股票市场所在地。英国博彩公司已经破解了足球赛果过于可预测这一难题。它们更大的挑战在威斯敏斯特。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需更多关于英国重大新闻的专家分析，请订阅Blighty，这是我们的每周订阅者专享通讯。

articles.title_en: London’s private members’ clubs are proliferating
articles.title_zh: 伦敦私人会员俱乐部正大量涌现
articles.rubric_en: But building a new community is harder than it looks
articles.rubric_zh: 但打造一个新社群比看起来更难

paragraphs.en: ON A WARM afternoon at Fulham Pier in west London, the Thames glints in the sun and members of Lighthouse Social, a private club, drift between coffee, meetings and glasses of Aperol spritz on the terrace. The relaxed mood is no accident. Much effort has gone into assembling the right mix of members in the right space. “It’s like whispers of smoke,” says Glen Sutton, the Pier’s director. “You can’t grab what makes a club. But you know when you’re in one that isn’t working.”
paragraphs.zh: 在伦敦西部富勒姆码头一个温暖的下午，泰晤士河在阳光下波光粼粼；私人俱乐部 Lighthouse Social 的会员们在露台上喝咖啡、开会，或端着一杯杯阿佩罗橙光鸡尾酒来回走动。这种放松的氛围并非偶然。为了在合适的空间里聚合合适的会员组合，背后下了不少功夫。“这就像一缕缕烟，”码头负责人 Glen Sutton 说。“你抓不住一个俱乐部真正成立的东西。但当你身处一个运转不灵的俱乐部时，你会知道。”

paragraphs.en: London has long been a city of clubs. From the 18th century the grand houses of St James’s offered a home from home for aristocrats and politicians. More recently came the “anti-clubs”: the Groucho, opened in 1985, drew a rowdy creative set; Soho House followed a decade later, aimed at a similar crowd. Both ended up full of bankers. Debauchery was dialled down, but revenue went up.
paragraphs.zh: 伦敦长期以来就是一座俱乐部之城。从18世纪起，圣詹姆斯区的豪华宅邸就为贵族和政客提供了一个家外之家。更近一些，又出现了“反俱乐部”：1985年开业的 Groucho 吸引了一群喧闹的创意人士；十年后 Soho House 跟进，面向相似的人群。两者最后都挤满了银行家。放纵程度降低了，但收入上升了。

paragraphs.en: Now, London clubs are proliferating again, part of a wider trend: a report in 2024 by Knight Frank, a property consultancy, pointed to an acceleration of club openings in Europe and America. Today London has over 130 members’ clubs (excluding those that are primarily gyms or sports clubs), roughly double the number in 1985, according to Seth Thévoz, a historian of clubland. In a city where working and social lives are less tied to offices, clubs offer a place to spend time—and money—throughout the day.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，伦敦的俱乐部又在激增，这是更广泛趋势的一部分：房地产咨询公司莱坊在2024年的一份报告指出，欧洲和美国的俱乐部开业速度正在加快。据俱乐部文化史学家 Seth Thévoz 统计，如今伦敦有130多家会员俱乐部（不包括主要作为健身房或体育俱乐部的场所），约为1985年数量的两倍。在一座工作和社交生活不再那么依附于办公室的城市里，俱乐部提供了一个让人全天消磨时间并花钱的地方。

paragraphs.en: Jamie Caring, a club consultant, says roughly three-quarters of the inquiries he gets about new clubs now include a wellness element. Many are extensions of existing businesses—restaurants, hotels or co-working spaces—with the trappings of a club bolted on. Lighthouse Social forms part of a redevelopment around Fulham Football Club’s Riverside Stand, to generate activity beyond match days.
paragraphs.zh: 俱乐部顾问 Jamie Caring 说，如今他收到的关于新俱乐部的咨询中，大约四分之三都包含健康养生元素。许多俱乐部是现有业务的延伸，比如餐厅、酒店或共享办公空间，再加上一套俱乐部的门面配置。Lighthouse Social 是富勒姆足球俱乐部河畔看台周边重建项目的一部分，目的是在比赛日之外也带来人气。

paragraphs.en: During the pandemic Londoners spent more time in their local areas and many have stuck with those habits. “People have become much more invested in their neighbourhoods,” says Mr Sutton. His club’s members largely walk in from surrounding streets. Families are welcome, and there is an on-site crèche. The small-scale feel is deliberate: too much growth, he argues, would turn it into “an airline lounge”, devoid of community.
paragraphs.zh: 疫情期间，伦敦人在自己居住的社区花了更多时间，许多人也保留了这些习惯。“人们对自己的街区投入得多得多了，”Sutton 先生说。他的俱乐部会员大多从周边街道步行而来。家庭也受欢迎，现场还设有托儿所。这种小规模感是有意为之：他认为，扩张过多会把它变成“机场贵宾室”，失去社群感。

paragraphs.en: At the other end of the spectrum sits the Pembroke. It is due to open later this year in a former prime ministerial residence overlooking Buckingham Palace Garden, and is an exercise in scale. Backed by the Oman Investment Authority, it will include multiple restaurants, lounges and a late-night bar with a £1m ($1.3m) sound system and a DJ booth carved from Italian marble. A job advert in Country Life for a “butter sommelier” with an “encyclopedic knowledge of toast” was posted partly in jest; the volume of applications helped turn it into a real hire. Membership costs £3,250 a year, or £60,000 for life. The club says it has already sold more than ten life memberships; perks include a “life loo”, exclusive use of one of the 55 lavatories.
paragraphs.zh: 光谱的另一端是 Pembroke。它计划于今年晚些时候在一处俯瞰白金汉宫花园的前首相官邸开业，是一场规模化实验。它由阿曼投资局支持，将设有多家餐厅、休息室和一家深夜酒吧，酒吧配备价值100万英镑（130万美元）的音响系统，还有一个由意大利大理石雕成的 DJ 台。它曾在《Country Life》上刊登招聘广告，寻找一名拥有“百科全书般吐司知识”的“黄油侍酒师”，部分是开玩笑；大量申请让这个职位变成了真正的招聘。会员费为每年3,250英镑，或终身60,000英镑。俱乐部称已经售出十多个终身会员资格；会员福利包括一个“终身专用厕所”，即55间洗手间中一间的独家使用权。

paragraphs.en: The Pembroke sits among embassies and mansions in Belgravia. Yet its director, Will Woodhams, like his counterpart in Fulham, says he is targeting a local crowd. As some of the international wealth that sustained Mayfair’s clubs has ebbed, newer clubs are courting Londoners who stayed put. Many will use the Pembroke as a workspace: Mr Woodhams jokes he will bring a mini martini to members who close their laptops by 4pm.
paragraphs.zh: Pembroke 位于贝尔格莱维亚的大使馆和豪宅之间。不过，它的负责人 Will Woodhams 和富勒姆那位同行一样，说自己的目标是本地人群。随着支撑梅费尔俱乐部的一部分国际财富减少，新兴俱乐部正在争取留在伦敦的本地人。许多人会把 Pembroke 当作办公空间使用：Woodhams 先生开玩笑说，如果会员在下午4点前合上笔记本电脑，他会给他们端上一小杯马提尼。

paragraphs.en: A third model is the scalable club brand. Several London-born concepts are expanding abroad, not least to New York. One, 67 Pall Mall, a wine-focused club founded in St James’s in 2015, now has three foreign locations and plans for more. Such clubs are not rooted in a single neighbourhood but offer a familiar experience replicated across cities. The template is Soho House, which has around 50 sites worldwide but also saw its 200,000-plus members dismayed by overcrowding and rising fees. Its recent move back into private ownership, after a spell as a public company, was widely read as a reminder of how hard it is to scale exclusivity.
paragraphs.zh: 第三种模式是可复制扩张的俱乐部品牌。几个诞生于伦敦的概念正在向海外扩张，尤其是纽约。其中之一是67 Pall Mall，这是一家以葡萄酒为核心的俱乐部，2015年创立于圣詹姆斯区，如今已有三处海外网点，并计划开设更多。这类俱乐部并不扎根于单一社区，而是在不同城市复制一种熟悉的体验。其模板是 Soho House，后者在全球拥有约50处场所，但其20多万名会员也曾因过度拥挤和费用上涨而失望。它在上市一段时间后最近重新转为私人所有，这被广泛解读为一个提醒：要把排他性规模化有多难。

paragraphs.en: Mr Thévoz says the success of traditional London clubs relied on the ability of members to mix easily with one another. Shared tables and common rooms encouraged “clubbability”. Whereas older clubs filtered by class or profession, newer ones aim for a broader mix. Those hungrier for subscriptions may be less selective still: if you can pay, you’re in. One commentator warns of “ersatz clubs”: places that look like clubs but operate as hospitality businesses. Members, less interested in community, behave more like customers—readier to complain when dissatisfied.
paragraphs.zh: Thévoz 先生说，伦敦传统俱乐部的成功依赖于会员能够轻松相互交往的能力。共用餐桌和公共休息室鼓励了“适合俱乐部交往”的气质。老式俱乐部按阶层或职业筛选会员，而新式俱乐部则追求更广泛的混合。那些更渴求会费收入的俱乐部可能更不挑剔：只要你付得起钱，就能加入。一位评论人士警告称，正在出现“仿冒俱乐部”：一些地方看起来像俱乐部，实际却按酒店餐饮服务业来运营。会员对社群不那么感兴趣，表现得更像顾客，一不满意就更容易投诉。

paragraphs.en: Clubs remain a precarious business. Mr Thévoz estimates that around 90% fail, often within three years. High up-front capital costs can leave operators burdened with debt that modest annual revenues struggle to service. London’s older clubs have advantages here. Many own their buildings. They have also adapted quietly, relaxing dress codes, improving facilities and, in some cases, admitting women.
paragraphs.zh: 俱乐部仍是一门不稳当的生意。Thévoz 先生估计，大约90%的俱乐部会失败，往往是在三年内。高昂的前期资本成本可能让经营者背上债务，而适中的年度收入难以偿还这些债务。伦敦较老的俱乐部在这方面有优势。许多拥有自己的建筑。它们也悄然作出了调整，放宽着装规定，改善设施，并在某些情况下接纳女性。

paragraphs.en: London has reinvented its clubs before. The coffee houses of the 17th century gave way to the gentlemen’s clubs of Pall Mall; the smoky dining rooms of the post-war era yielded to the creative hangouts of Soho. Today’s boom may have a mix of models, but all borrow the language of community. The history of clubland suggests it is the hardest thing to build.■
paragraphs.zh: 伦敦的俱乐部并非第一次自我重塑。17世纪的咖啡馆让位于蓓尔美尔街的绅士俱乐部；战后时代烟雾缭绕的餐厅又让位于 Soho 的创意人士聚点。今天这波热潮或许混合了多种模式，但它们都借用了社群的语言。俱乐部世界的历史表明，这正是最难打造的东西。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in Britain, sign up to Blighty, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需更多关于英国重大新闻的专家分析，请订阅 Blighty，我们每周一期、仅面向订阅者的新闻简报。

articles.title_en: Sir Keir’s bucket list
articles.title_zh: 基尔爵士的遗愿清单
articles.rubric_en: The prime minister’s final few weeks sum him up
articles.rubric_zh: 这位首相最后几周的作为概括了他这个人

paragraphs.en: To see how someone lived, examine how they died. The final few weeks of Sir Keir Starmer’s strange political life say much about the man.
paragraphs.zh: 若想看清一个人如何活过，就看他如何谢幕。基尔·斯塔默爵士这段奇特政治生涯的最后几周，很能说明其人。

paragraphs.en: Outstanding affairs have been put in order. Sir Keir hammered out an increase in defence spending of £15bn ($20bn) over four years. The bulk of it was paid for with cuts to capital-spending budgets. Roads will go unbuilt. Energy projects will be canned. You could call it a “sticking-plaster” approach to public investment. Bad for business investment. Bad for living standards. Bad for growth. And, in the end, bad for the public finances as well. Those words may sound familiar. They are Sir Keir’s, when attacking the Conservatives in 2023 for doing what he has now done: raiding capital budgets to pay for spending elsewhere.
paragraphs.zh: 未了之事已经一一安排妥当。基尔爵士敲定了在四年内增加150亿英镑（200亿美元）国防开支的方案。其中大部分资金来自削减资本支出预算。道路将不会开建。能源项目将被砍掉。你可以称之为公共投资上的“创可贴”做法。对商业投资不利。对生活水平不利。对增长不利。而且到头来，对公共财政同样不利。这些话听起来也许耳熟。它们正是基尔爵士在2023年攻击保守党时说过的话，当时保守党做了他如今也做的事：挪用资本预算，去支付其他领域的开支。

paragraphs.en: Others will have to deal with the inheritance. Even with the capital cuts, Sir Keir leaves behind about £5bn in unfunded spending for whoever is lucky enough to be Andy Burnham’s chancellor. When making way for someone in their own party, a prime minister should act as a sin-eater, choking down the consequences of the errors made in government, allowing their successor the benefit of purity. When it comes to defence spending, Sir Keir is less sin-eating than sin-chewing-and-spitting-into-a-napkin.
paragraphs.zh: 这笔遗产将由别人来收拾。即便削减了资本开支，基尔爵士仍给那位有幸成为安迪·伯纳姆财政大臣的人留下了约50亿英镑没有资金来源的支出。首相若是给本党内的人让路，就应当扮演“替罪羊”的角色，咽下政府犯错带来的后果，让继任者得以保持清白。就国防开支而言，基尔爵士与其说是在吞罪，不如说是把罪嚼了嚼又吐进餐巾纸里。

paragraphs.en: There was a final trip to Paris. Where else? Sir Keir was hosted by Emmanuel Macron at a meeting of the “coalition of the willing” of Ukraine allies. The French president led plaudits and awarded him the Légion d’honneur as a sign of the improved relations between both countries. If patriotism is the last refuge of a scoundrel, foreign policy is the last refuge of an unsuccessful prime minister. Sir Keir, more than any recent occupant, loved it, attracting plaudits for deft diplomacy even when pilloried at home.
paragraphs.zh: 最后一次出访去了巴黎。还能去哪儿呢？基尔爵士受到埃马纽埃尔·马克龙接待，出席乌克兰盟友“志愿联盟”会议。法国总统带头致以赞誉，并授予他荣誉军团勋章，以示两国关系改善。如果说爱国主义是无赖最后的避难所，那么外交政策就是不成功首相最后的避难所。基尔爵士比近来的任何一位唐宁街主人都更钟爱外交，即便在国内遭到猛烈抨击，也能凭借娴熟外交赢得喝彩。

paragraphs.en: Beneath the diplomatic pomp, personal relationships matter less than many assume. Where Britain was useful, it was welcomed. The coalition of the willing would have been far less potent without Britain, which is still one of only two serious hard powers in western Europe. Where Britain was weak, it was bullied. Under Sir Keir, Britain struggled to win concessions from the eu—often because of the man handing over the Légion d’honneur to a beaming prime minister.
paragraphs.zh: 在外交排场之下，个人关系并没有许多人以为的那么重要。英国有用之处，就受到欢迎。若没有英国，志愿联盟的力量会小得多，因为英国仍是西欧仅有的两个严肃硬实力国家之一。英国软弱之处，就会受人欺压。在基尔爵士任内，英国很难从欧盟那里争取到让步，往往正是因为那位把荣誉军团勋章交给笑容满面的首相的人。

paragraphs.en: Back in London, Sir Keir gathered the people he always spoke for: victims. He saw politics through their eyes. On a warm afternoon on July 14th, he welcomed a collection of them to the Downing Street garden: one mother whose son was killed by a ninja sword, and another whose child perished in the Manchester Arena attacks in 2017. Also in attendance were parents of those killed in the Hillsborough disaster, in which 97 Liverpool Football Club fans died due to police incompetence before being slandered by a malevolent state.
paragraphs.zh: 回到伦敦后，基尔爵士召集了他始终代言的人群：受害者。他透过他们的眼睛看政治。7月14日一个温暖的下午，他在唐宁街花园欢迎了一批受害者：一位母亲的儿子死于忍者刀之下，另一位母亲的孩子在2017年曼彻斯特体育馆袭击中丧生。到场的还有希尔斯堡惨案遇难者的父母；那场灾难中，97名利物浦足球俱乐部球迷因警方无能而死亡，随后又遭一个恶意国家机器的污蔑。

paragraphs.en: Thanks to Sir Keir’s government, each now has a law named after their tragedies. For a man with few guiding principles, victims provided a moral map. The job of the Labour Party was “to look directly into the eyes of their suffering”, said Sir Keir. It is a humane response. But governing is not a humane job. Was banning ninja swords, already illegal to carry in public, a good use of legislative time? Is it proportionate to require a church hall to have an anti-terror plan? Will the “duty of candour” that the Hillsborough law introduces lead to openness, or to public servants becoming strategically uninformed about brewing trouble? A government must be clear-eyed, and grief makes people blind.
paragraphs.zh: 由于基尔爵士政府的作为，如今每一场悲剧都有了一部以其命名的法律。对于一个几乎没有指导原则的人来说，受害者提供了一张道德地图。基尔爵士说，工党的职责是“直视他们苦难的眼睛”。这是一种富有人性的回应。但治理国家并不是一份富有人性的工作。禁止忍者刀，是否值得占用立法时间，何况这种刀本来就已禁止在公共场所携带？要求一座教堂大厅制定反恐计划，是否合乎比例？希尔斯堡法引入的“坦诚义务”，究竟会带来公开透明，还是会让公职人员在麻烦酝酿时策略性地保持不知情？政府必须眼明心亮，而悲痛会让人失明。

paragraphs.en: Cabinet ministers gathered for the final time on July 15th to send off a prime minister regarded by all but a few of them with contempt. They gave him a carriage clock—the symbol of an unthinking retirement gift. Still, it was a serene end. Good prime ministers tend to enjoy messy departures, argued Andrew Adonis, a former Labour minister, in a note to his then soon-to-be-departing boss, Sir Tony Blair. By contrast, Sir Keir has enjoyed a form of political euthanasia, put out of his misery by people he once considered friends. It is a dignity he did not extend to the public after he failed to pass a euthanasia bill, although it was one of the few things in which he sincerely believed.
paragraphs.zh: 7月15日，内阁大臣们最后一次聚集，为一位除寥寥数人外几乎人人鄙视的首相送行。他们送给他一座马车钟，这是不假思索的退休礼物的象征。不过，这仍是一个平静的结局。前工党大臣安德鲁·阿多尼斯曾在写给当时即将离任的上司托尼·布莱尔爵士的一份备忘录中说，优秀的首相往往会有混乱的离场。相比之下，基尔爵士享受了一种政治安乐死，被他曾经视为朋友的人结束了痛苦。这份体面，他在未能通过安乐死法案后并没有给予公众，尽管那是他少数真心相信的事情之一。

paragraphs.en: Since Sir Keir’s fate has been settled officially for nearly a month (and unofficially for far longer), the prime minister has had time to offer interviews about his legacy. What policy does he want to be remembered for? Why, scrapping the two-child limit on benefits, of course, which lifted hundreds of thousands of children out of poverty. It is the sort of thing Labour should do. Unmentioned was the fact Sir Keir fought such a move for almost two years, even sacking seven mps for calling for it. It could have been announced on day one. Instead, it happened on day 509.
paragraphs.zh: 由于基尔爵士的命运已正式尘埃落定近一个月（非正式地说则早得多），这位首相有时间接受采访，谈论自己的政治遗产。他希望哪项政策被人铭记？当然是取消福利中的二孩上限，因为这让数十万儿童摆脱了贫困。这是工党本该做的事。没有提到的是，基尔爵士为阻止此举抗拒了将近两年，甚至因有7名议员呼吁这样做而将他们开除。这本可以在第一天宣布。结果却发生在第509天。

paragraphs.en: Sir Keir would have spent his final few moments in power 5,500km away in New Jersey, had Argentina not ended England’s hopes in the World Cup. By a remarkable coincidence, the football-mad prime minister had picked the day after the final as the earliest possible moment for the handover. Sir Keir began his reign in a furore about free football tickets he enjoyed while leader of the opposition. Why change the habit? England in a World Cup final would have been more justifiable than Arsenal away at Newcastle.
paragraphs.zh: 如果不是阿根廷终结了英格兰在世界杯上的希望，基尔爵士本会在离权力交接还有最后片刻时，身处5500公里外的新泽西。极为巧合的是，这位痴迷足球的首相把决赛次日选定为最早可能的交接时刻。基尔爵士执政伊始，就因担任反对党领袖期间享受免费足球票而陷入风波。何必改变这个习惯呢？英格兰参加世界杯决赛，总比阿森纳客场对阵纽卡斯尔更说得过去。

paragraphs.en: Some prime ministers work to the last second. Others simply let death take them. In one of those final interviews, Sir Keir was asked for his proudest moment. He chose walking up Downing Street the day after the general election. It was winning for the sake of winning. Why he wanted the job, Britain never quite learned. Sir Keir composed his own epitaph before he had even come to power, during one of Labour’s interminable internal rows: “There is no such thing as Starmerism, and there never will be.” How right he was.■
paragraphs.zh: 有些首相工作到最后一秒。另一些则只是任由死亡带走自己。在最后几次采访中的一次，基尔爵士被问及最自豪的时刻。他选择的是大选次日走上唐宁街的那一刻。那是为了胜利而胜利。英国始终没有真正明白，他为什么想要这份工作。基尔爵士甚至还没上台，就已经在工党没完没了的一场内部争吵中为自己写好了墓志铭：“不存在所谓斯塔默主义，也永远不会有。”他说得多么正确。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订阅用户可以订阅我们的 Opinion 新闻通讯，其中汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。

articles.title_en: Sovereign AI, independent of America and China, is a pipe dream
articles.title_zh: 独立于美国和中国的“主权AI”仍是幻想
articles.rubric_en: But a degree of protection from coercion remains possible
articles.rubric_zh: 但一定程度上摆脱胁迫仍有可能

paragraphs.en: WHEN THE leaders of the biggest Western economies gathered last month in the French Alps for the G7 summit, it was not just presidents and prime ministers in attendance. The bosses of America’s leading artificial-intelligence firms—Sam Altman of OpenAI, Sir Demis Hassabis of Google DeepMind and Dario Amodei of Anthropic—were seated alongside the political bigwigs. Even the stewards of the mightiest economies, it turns out, are buttering up the potentates of AI.
paragraphs.zh: 上个月，西方主要经济体领导人齐聚法国阿尔卑斯山参加七国集团峰会时，到场的不仅是总统和首相。美国头部人工智能公司的掌门人也坐在他们身边：OpenAI 的萨姆·奥特曼、Google DeepMind 的戴米斯·哈萨比斯爵士，以及 Anthropic 的达里奥·阿莫代伊。事实证明，即便是最强大的经济体掌舵者，也在讨好 AI 这群“新权贵”。

paragraphs.en: Days before the summit, America’s government had barred Anthropic from making its most advanced model available to foreigners. OpenAI soon imposed similar restrictions on some of its frontier systems. The move turned a simmering concern into a burning one: might countries without their own, home-grown AI firms end up excluded from the world-changing technology? Emmanuel Macron, France’s president, warned that no one would buy American AI if it could “turn off the switch” at will. The American AI labs’ Chinese rivals offer only faint reassurance. On July 7th Reuters reported that Chinese officials, too, were considering restricting foreigners’ access to leading AI models.
paragraphs.zh: 峰会前几天，美国政府已禁止 Anthropic 将其最先进的模型提供给外国机构。OpenAI 随后也对其部分前沿系统施加了类似限制。这一举动将长期存在的担忧推向焦点：没有自有 AI 企业的国家，是否会被排除在改变世界的技术之外？法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙警告说，如果美国可以随时“关闭开关”，就没人会购买美国的 AI。美国 AI 机构的中国竞争者也只能提供微弱安慰。7月7日，路透社报道，中国官员也在考虑限制外国人获取领先 AI 模型。

paragraphs.en: Caught between the world’s two AI superpowers, governments are increasingly pinning their hopes on “sovereign AI”: the idea that countries should limit their dependence on foreign providers of AI by actively developing domestic AI firms and infrastructure. Announcements of such initiatives are proliferating. The European Union has unveiled a technology-sovereignty package encompassing semiconductors, AI and cloud computing. Canada has launched its own plan to reduce reliance on American technology providers. India, Japan and Singapore are backing domestic AI infrastructure and models. The Centre for a New American Security (CNAS), a think-tank in Washington, estimates that the number of state-backed AI projects outside America and China grew fivefold over the course of 2024 and 2025 (see chart 1). Governments have announced investments of $70bn-plus in such initiatives, according to CNAS’s tally.
paragraphs.zh: 处于世界两大 AI 强权之间，政府越来越多地把希望寄托在“主权 AI”上：即通过积极发展本国 AI 企业和基础设施，减少对外部供应商的依赖。这类倡议的声明正不断增加。欧盟推出了一个技术主权方案包，涵盖半导体、人工智能与云计算。加拿大也推出了降低对美国技术供应商依赖的计划。印度、日本和新加坡都在支持本土 AI 基础设施和模型。华盛顿智库“美国新安全中心”（CNAS）估计，2024年至2025年间，美中之外由国家支持的 AI 项目数量增长了五倍（见图表 1）。CNAS 的统计显示，政府已宣布在此类倡议上投资逾 700 亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Yet turning these ambitions into reality will be extremely difficult. No matter what, most countries will still rely on American chips, Chinese open-source models or both to build their AI systems. Even then, most governments will find it prohibitively expensive to achieve anything remotely resembling AI sovereignty.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，把这些愿景变为现实会异常困难。无论如何，大多数国家仍将需要依赖美国芯片、中国开源模型，或两者兼用来构建 AI 系统。即便如此，大多数政府要想实现接近“AI 主权”的状态，也会发现成本高得几乎无法承受。

paragraphs.en: The incentive to seek it, however, is clear and compelling. Pablo Chavez of CNAS puts the restrictions on Anthropic in a “long line of events” demonstrating America’s willingness to use its technological dominion as a weapon. In the dying days of Joe Biden’s presidency in 2025, America proposed sweeping export controls that would have rationed access to advanced chips through a complex licensing regime. When Donald Trump became president a few weeks later he dropped the plan. But he has not hesitated to use America’s lead in AI as leverage in trade negotiations. The Department of Commerce is considering rules that would allow it to vet all sales of AI chips designed by American firms anywhere in the world. It might also insist that buyers allow inspections or monitoring to make sure chips are being used as promised. Another possible requirement could be to invest in American AI infrastructure in exchange for access.
paragraphs.zh: 寻求主权的动机却十分清晰而有说服力。CNAS 的帕布罗·查韦斯把对 Anthropic 的限制放在一系列“先例”中，认为这体现了美国愿意将技术主导权当作武器。在 2025 年乔·拜登任期末期，美国提出了覆盖面广泛的出口管制方案，拟通过复杂的许可制度配给先进芯片。几周后，唐纳德·特朗普上任后撤回了该方案。但他并未迟疑于在贸易谈判中把美国 AI 领先地位当作筹码。美国商务部正在考虑规定，让其有权审核美国公司在全球范围内出售 AI 芯片的所有交易。美国还可能要求买方接受检查或监测，以确保芯片按承诺用途使用。另一种可能的要求是，作为换取访问权的条件，要投资美国的 AI 基础设施。

paragraphs.en: Protecting themselves from coercion is not the only reason governments want to build their own “AI stack”, the layers of hardware and software on which AI depends. National security is another: it is awkward to use foreign, commercial AI for top-secret purposes, especially of a military nature. Governments also like the idea of models trained on their own languages, laws and norms. They worry, with some justification, about users’ sensitive, personal data falling into foreign hands. And policymakers want to reap the economic benefits of AI. They have seen how previous technological innovations—the internet, smartphones and cloud computing—entranced consumers and reshaped their economies while generating riches for American tech firms. They are keen to avoid a repeat.
paragraphs.zh: 政府想打造自己的“AI 技术栈”（即 AI 所依赖的硬件和软件层）并非只为防止被胁迫。国家安全也是原因：对最高机密、尤其军事用途，使用外国产品的商业 AI 非常不便。政府也希望拥有基于本国语言、法律和规范训练的模型。它们也有一定道理地担心敏感、个人数据落入外国手中。政策制定者还希望获得 AI 的经济收益。他们看到过往技术创新——互联网、智能手机和云计算——如何让消费者着迷并重塑经济，同时为美国科技公司带来巨额财富。他们不想重演一遍。

paragraphs.en: The Anthropic cut-off was instructive, says Arthur Mensch of Mistral AI, a French model-maker. In an interview for The Economist’s Inside Tech video series, he argues that the imperative to develop sovereign AI runs much deeper than the risk of diplomatic strong-arming. The scale of the outflow of cash to America and China in a world where the pair continue to dominate AI would be so vast it would create “enormous instabilities”, he argues. Countries will find that unacceptable and so will be forced to invest in the ability to serve at least some demand for AI domestically.
paragraphs.zh: 法国模型公司 Mistral AI 的 Arthur Mensch 认为，Anthropic 的断供很有启发性。在《经济学人》Inside Tech 视频访谈中，他指出，发展主权 AI 的紧迫性远深于外交施压的风险。若未来美国和中国继续主导 AI，向两国流出的资金规模将大到造成“巨大不稳定”。各国会认为这是不可接受的，因此被迫投资于在国内至少满足部分 AI 需求的能力。

paragraphs.en: Mr Mensch estimates (conservatively, he says) that within the next five or ten years, the European Union will be spending the equivalent of a tenth of its current bill for labour on AI tokens, or about around €1trn ($1.1trn) a year. If things stay as they are, the vast majority of that expenditure will flow directly to American cloud providers. Such outflows would hit the euro, increasing the cost yet more. That, he believes, would create an opportunity for a local enterprise to compete directly, even if its product is not of the same quality. Every country, he predicts, will need to make sure that whatever happens, “The AI can continue to run.”
paragraphs.zh: Mensch 保守地估计，在未来五到十年内，欧盟在 AI 代币上的年度支出将达到其当前劳动成本约十分之一，约合 1 万亿欧元（约 1.1 万亿美元）。如果现状不变，这笔开支中的绝大多数都将直接流向美国云服务商。如此一来，资金外流会打击欧元，进一步抬高代价。Mensch 认为，这将给本地企业带来竞争机会，即使其产品质量不及对手。

paragraphs.en: Sovereignty looks different at each layer of the stack. Start at the bottom: computing power (“compute”, in the jargon), largely in the form of AI chips. The market for those chips is dominated by Nvidia, whose processors account for two-thirds of the world’s AI compute. Chips designed by Google, AMD and Amazon, all American firms, make up much of the remainder.
paragraphs.zh: 在栈的每一层，“主权”看起来都不同。先从最底层开始：算力，也就是业内常说的“计算力”，主要以 AI 芯片的形式存在。该市场由英伟达主导，其处理器占全球 AI 算力的三分之二。谷歌、AMD 和亚马逊这几家美国企业设计的芯片，占据了剩下的大部分。

paragraphs.en: The alternatives are limited. Chinese firms such as Huawei and Cambricon have made impressive strides despite being cut off by American trade restrictions from the most advanced chipmaking equipment. But they remain some distance from the frontier. Huawei’s Ascend 910C, one of China’s most advanced AI chips, provides roughly a fifth of the performance of Nvidia’s latest offering. Performance is not the only constraint. Chinese manufacturers are prioritising domestic customers, and demand still exceeds available capacity. Even if Chinese chips continue to improve, shortages are likely to persist for years.
paragraphs.zh: 可替代方案并不多。华为、寒武纪等中国企业在被美国贸易限制切断最先进芯片制造设备后，仍取得了令人瞩目的进展。但它们与最前沿仍有一定差距。华为的 Ascend 910C 作为中国最先进的 AI 芯片之一，其性能大约只有英伟达最新产品的五分之一。性能并非唯一限制。中国制造商优先服务国内客户，且需求仍超出现有产能。即便中国芯片持续进步，短缺很可能还会持续多年。

paragraphs.en: Aiming for self-sufficiency in hardware is therefore unrealistic. Kevin Xu of Interconnected Capital, an AI-focused hedge fund, argues that sovereignty should be understood less as independence than as control. If the machines being used to run AI models sit within a country’s borders, governments retain a degree of autonomy even if future access to chips is restricted. “Is your factory going to still run if the US government decides that suddenly you can’t?” asks Mr Mensch. The latest AI chips have a lifespan of at least five or six years.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，追求硬件完全自给并不现实。Interconnected Capital 的 AI 对冲基金负责人徐凯文（Kevin Xu）认为，主权应理解为“可控”，而非“独立”。如果运行 AI 模型的机器位于本国领土内，即便将来芯片访问受到限制，政府仍可保留一定自主权。“如果美国政府突然决定禁止，你的工厂还能继续运行吗？”Mensch 问道。

paragraphs.en: Chips are only part of the equation, however. Running AI places huge demands on the electricity grid. According to the International Energy Agency, an intergovernmental forecaster, a typical AI data centre consumes as much electricity as 100,000 households; the largest may use around 20 times as much.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，芯片只是方程式的一部分。运行 AI 对电网提出了巨大的要求。根据跨政府的能源预测机构——国际能源署的数据，典型 AI 数据中心的电力消耗相当于 10 万户家庭；最大型的数据中心可能高达其 20 倍。

paragraphs.en: Countries like Norway, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) look like attractive places to build data centres because they have abundant, reliable power. The reverse is true in most of Europe, however: grids are congested, there are long waits for connections and electricity prices are high. For many countries, producing enough power for AI will be as difficult as securing enough chips.
paragraphs.zh: 挪威、沙特阿拉伯和阿联酋看起来是建设数据中心的理想地点，因为它们拥有充足且稳定的电力。大多数欧洲则相反：电网拥塞，接入排期漫长，电价高。对许多国家而言，生产足够的 AI 电力和拿到足够的芯片一样困难。

paragraphs.en: Move one layer higher and the picture improves. Most sovereign-AI projects are built on open-source models. These are appealing not simply because they cost less, but also because they offer a higher degree of control. Users can modify them, host them locally and avoid dependence on the proprietary systems of firms such as OpenAI and Anthropic, which might be ordered by Uncle Sam to revoke or restrict access. Although open-source models are not at the cutting edge, the gap is narrow enough not to matter for many applications. Chinese firms have become particularly competitive. On OpenRouter, a marketplace for AI models, Chinese ones have overtaken American ones in usage. The day after the restrictions on Anthropic took effect, Z.ai, a Chinese AI company, released GLM-5.2, an open-source model that quickly climbed to fourth place on Artificial Analysis, a benchmarking site.
paragraphs.zh: 向上看一层，情况开始好转。大多数主权 AI 项目都建立在开源模型上。之所以受青睐，不仅因为更省钱，还因为它们提供更高程度的掌控。用户可以对其进行修改、在本地托管，避免依赖 OpenAI、Anthropic 等公司专有系统，而这些公司可能被华盛顿命令收回或限制访问。尽管开源模型并非最前沿，但与主流应用相关的性能差距已小到可以忽略。中国企业尤其具备竞争力。在 AI 模型市场 OpenRouter 上，中国模型的使用量已超越美国模型。Anthropic 限制生效的次日，中国 AI 公司 Z.ai 发布了开源模型 GLM-5.2，迅速登上了基准平台 Artificial Analysis 的第四位。

paragraphs.en: Yet if China were to impose the same sort of export controls on its most capable models as America has, users of Chinese AI would simply be exchanging one form of dependency for another. In June Joe Tsai, chairman of Alibaba, was asked at a conference how Europe could trust China not to follow America’s lead in cutting off access to frontier models. “You can’t,” he replied candidly. “But here’s the thing. Right now, all of your eggs are in one basket. Why not get a second basket?”
paragraphs.zh: 但如果中国也像美国一样对最强模型实施同类出口管制，那么使用中国 AI 的用户就只是把一种依赖换成了另一种。6月，在一次会议上，阿里巴巴董事长蔡崇信被问到欧洲如何相信中国不会效仿美国切断前沿模型访问时，他坦率回答道：“你不能。但问题是，现在你们所有鸡蛋都放在一个篮子里。为什么不再放一个篮子？”

paragraphs.en: As AI becomes ever more powerful, Chinese officials appear to be thinking about the strategic advantages prowess in AI can confer, just like their American counterparts, and also the risks of its falling into the wrong hands. Reuters reported that Chinese regulators had been discussing the possibility of export bans on future cutting-edge models with Chinese AI firms. It also claimed that they were considering making unauthorised disclosures of intellectual property related to AI models an offence under China’s draconian national-security laws.
paragraphs.zh: 随着 AI 越来越强大，中国官员似乎也像美国同行一样在思考 AI 带来的战略优势，也在关注它落入错误主体之手的风险。路透社报道称，中国监管机构正在与国内 AI 企业讨论对未来前沿模型实施出口禁令的可能性。报道还称，他们也在研究将 AI 模型相关知识产权的非法泄露纳入中国严厉的国家安全法制裁范围。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps even harder than acquiring the necessary hardware and software for sovereign AI is getting hold of the people needed to operate them. Mr Xu argues that this layer of the stack receives the least attention. Hardware can be imported and software downloaded. Building a workforce that can install, maintain and optimise increasingly complex AI systems is much harder. Training such engineers takes years. Hiring them from abroad is possible, but only at huge cost, as governments vie with deep-pocketed technology firms to recruit from a tiny pool of specialists.
paragraphs.zh: 也许比获得主权 AI 所需的硬件和软件更难的是，获得能够运行它们的人才。徐凯文认为，栈的这一层往往最容易被忽视。硬件可以进口，软件可以下载；而能够安装、维护和优化日益复杂 AI 系统的劳动力却难以快速建立。培养这类工程师需要多年。向国外招聘是可能的，但代价高昂，因为各国政府与资金雄厚的科技公司在一小撮高端人才市场里展开激烈竞争。

paragraphs.en: That, in turn, hints at the biggest constraint of all: money. Governments trying to conjure up sovereign AI have adopted a range of financing models. In the Gulf the state is the builder. Saudi Arabia has entrusted the task to Humain, a government-backed company created to develop AI infrastructure and data centres. In the UAE the task has fallen to G42, an AI firm partly owned by Mubadala, the country’s sovereign-wealth fund. The two countries are planning to invest more than $100bn.
paragraphs.zh: 这又指向最根本的瓶颈：资金。各国在构建主权 AI 时采用了多种融资模式。在海湾地区，国家是建设者。沙特将任务交给 Humain，这是一家由政府支持、负责开发 AI 基础设施和数据中心的公司。阿联酋则由 G42 承担该任务，G42 是由该国主权财富基金 Mubadala 部分持有的 AI 企业。这两国计划投入超过 1000 亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Close state involvement helps secure the necessary hardware. During Donald Trump’s visit to the Middle East last year, governments in the Gulf put access to chips high on the agenda. Saudi Arabia said it planned to buy “several hundred thousand” of Nvidia’s top-end processors over the following five years. The UAE is reportedly aiming far higher, at half a million chips a year. On July 10th the Trump administration removed export controls on sales of advanced chips to the UAE, citing its support for America’s war with Iran.
paragraphs.zh: 强力的国家参与有助于锁定必要硬件。去年唐纳德·特朗普访问中东时，海湾各国把芯片供应列入议程高优先级。沙特表示，未来五年内计划购买“数十万”台英伟达高端处理器。阿联酋据称规模更大，目标是每年 50 万块芯片。7月10日，特朗普政府取消了对向阿联酋出口先进芯片的限制，以其对美国对伊朗战争的支持为由。

paragraphs.en: Elsewhere governments are acting more as catalysts than as builders. France is backing Mistral AI, a home-grown but private model-developer, by helping recruit state-backed investors and foreign partners. In May SoftBank, a Japanese conglomerate, pledged to invest up to $85bn to build Europe’s biggest AI facility in France. Britain has launched a £500m ($670m) sovereign-AI fund to foster domestic startups. It has also set up a widely admired safety agency, the AI Security Institute, which has helped keep the country involved in the industry.
paragraphs.zh: 在其他地区，政府更多扮演催化者而非建设者。法国通过帮助吸引国家支持的投资者和海外合作伙伴，在支持本土但私有的模型开发商 Mistral AI。5月，日本财团软银承诺投资高达 850 亿美元，在法国建设欧洲最大的 AI 设施。英国推出了 5 亿英镑（约 6.7 亿美元）的主权 AI 基金，以扶持本土初创公司。英国还建立了受广泛好评的安全机构——AI 安全研究所，这有助于该国在该行业中保持参与。

paragraphs.en: India is attempting a “third way” by focusing on providing AI cheaply, with the aim of solving local problems in local languages. It is channeling these ambitions through a mix of state-linked firms and private partnerships.. The government has allocated about $1bn to domestic AI infrastructure, Indian-language models and public data sets.
paragraphs.zh: 印度正尝试一条“第三条道路”，以低成本提供 AI，目标是用本地语言解决本地问题。其战略是通过国家关联企业与私营合作模式推进。政府已向本土 AI 基础设施、印度语种模型和公共数据集拨款约 10 亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Whether the capital is public or private, however, it will be difficult to muster enough of it. AI infrastructure is ruinously expensive. A one-gigawatt (GW) data centre—the scale at which advanced AI is increasingly conducted—costs roughly $50bn. Over two-thirds of that bill comes from computing equipment, chiefly chips.
paragraphs.zh: 无论资本是公有还是私有，要筹措到足够资金都很难。AI 基础设施代价高昂。一座一吉瓦（GW）级数据中心——当前高性能 AI 主要运行的规模——的成本大约是 500 亿美元，其中超过三分之二的支出来自计算设备，尤其是芯片。

paragraphs.en: Gartner, a consultancy, estimates that between 2026 and 2030 countries outside America and China could add more than 50GW of data-centre capacity, much of it for AI (see chart 2). That would entail investment of $2trn or more. Yet the combined capital expenditure of all providers bar Chinese and American firms, including sovereign-AI projects and neoclouds, which rent out compute, will amount to only around $800bn over the same period.
paragraphs.zh: 咨询公司 Gartner 估计，从 2026 年到 2030 年，除美国和中国之外的国家可能新增 50GW 以上的数据中心容量，其中很大一部分用于 AI（见图表 2）。这将意味着至少 2 万亿美元的投资。然而，排除中美厂商后，所有供应商包括主权 AI 项目与“新云”提供者的合计资本支出，在同一时期也只有约 8000 亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Someone else will have to foot the bill. America’s hyperscalers look like the obvious candidates. Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft and Oracle are expected to invest around $5trn by the end of the decade, most of it on AI infrastructure.
